text.NRSV:
1:1 Happy are they who have not walked in the counsel of the wicked, nor lingered in the way of sinners, nor sat in the seats of the scornful!
1:2 Their delight is in the law of the Lord, and they meditate on his law day and night.
1:3 They are like trees planted by streams of water, bearing fruit in due season, with leaves that do not wither; everything they do shall prosper.
1:4 It is not so with the wicked; they are like chaff which the wind blows away.
1:5 Therefore the wicked shall not stand upright when judgment comes, nor the sinner in the council of the righteous.
1:6 For the Lord knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked is doomed.
text.Coverdale:

text.KJV:
1:1 Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsell of the vngodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornefull.
1:2 But his delight is in the Law of the Lord, and in his Law doeth he meditate day and night.
1:3 And he shalbe like a tree planted by the riuers of water, that bringeth foorth his fruit in his season, his leafe also shall not wither, and whatsoeuer he doeth, shall prosper.
1:4 The vngodly are not so: but are like the chaffe, which the winde driueth away.
1:5 Therefore the vngodly shall not stand in the iudgement, nor sinners in the Congregation of the righteous.
1:6 For the Lord knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the vngodly shall perish.
text.JPS1985:
1:1 Happy is the man who has not followed the counsel of the wicked, or taken the path of sinners, or joined the company of the insolent;
1:2 rather, the teaching of the LORD is his delight, and he studies that teaching day and night.
1:3 He is like a tree planted beside streams of water, which yields its fruit in season, whose foliage never fades, and whatever it produces thrives.
1:4 Not so the wicked; rather, they are like chaff that wind blows away.
1:5 Therefore the wicked will not survive judgment, nor will sinners, in the assembly of the righteous.
1:6 For the LORD cherishes the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked is doomed.
text.Grail1963:
1 Happy indeed is the man
who follows not the counsel of the wicked,
nor lingers in the way of sinners
nor sits in the company of scorners,
2 but whose delight is the law of the Lord
and who ponders his law day and night.
3 He is like a tree that is planted
beside the flowing waters,
that yields its fruit in due season
and whose leaves shall never fade;
and all that he does shall prosper.
4 Not so are the wicked, not so!
For they like winnowed chaff
shall be driven away by the wind.
5 When the wicked are judged they shall not stand,
nor find room among those who are just;
6 for the Lord guards the way of the just
but the way of the wicked leads to doom.
text.Rotherham:
1:1 How happy the man, who hath not walked in the counsel of the lawless,—and, in the way of sinners, hath not stood, and, in the seat of scoffers, hath not sat;
1:2 But, in the law of Yahweh, is his delight,—and, in his law, doth he talk with himself day and night.
1:3 So doth he become like a tree planted by streams of waters,—that yieldeth, its fruit, in its season, whose leaf, also doth not wither, and, whatsoever he doeth, prospereth.
1:4 Not so, the lawless,—but as chaff which is driven about by the wind:
1:5 For this cause, shall the lawless not stand in the judgment,—nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.
1:6 For Yahweh doth acknowledge the way of the righteous; but, the way of the lawless, shall vanish.
text.NABRE:
1:1 First Book—Psalms 1–41 Psalm 1 True Happiness in God’s Law I - Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, Nor stand in the way of sinners, nor sit in company with scoffers.
1:2 Rather, the law of the LORD is his joy; and on his law he meditates day and night.
1:3 He is like a tree planted near streams of water, that yields its fruit in season; Its leaves never wither; whatever he does prospers.
1:4 II - But not so are the wicked, not so! They are like chaff driven by the wind.
1:5 Therefore the wicked will not arise at the judgment, nor will sinners in the assembly of the just.
1:6 Because the LORD knows the way of the just, but the way of the wicked leads to ruin.
text.DRB:
1:1 Blessed is the man, that hath not gone in the counsel of the impious, & hath not stood in the way of sinners, and hath not sit in the chair of pestilence:
1:2 But his will is in the way of our Lord, and in his law he will meditate day and night.
1:3 And he shall be as a tree, that is planted nigh to the streams of waters, which shall give his fruit in his time. And his leaf shall not fall: and all things whatsoever he shall do, shall prosper.
1:4 The impious not so, not so: but as dust, which the wind driveth from the face of the earth.
1:5 Therefore the impious shall not rise again in judgment: nor sinners in the Council of the just.
1:6 For our Lord knoweth the way of the just, and the way of the impious shall perish.

text.NRSV:
2:1 Why are the nations in an uproar? Why do the peoples mutter empty threats?
2:2 The kings of the earth rise up in revolt, and the princes plot together, against the Lord and against his Anointed.
2:3 'Let us break their yoke,' they say; 'let us cast off their bonds from us.'
2:4 He whose throne is in heaven is laughing; the Lord has them in derision.
2:5 Then he speaks to them in his wrath, and his rage fills them with terror.
2:6 'I myself have set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.'
2:7 Let me announce the decree of the Lord: he said to me, 'You are my Son; this day have I begotten you.
2:8 Ask of me, and I will give you the nations for your inheritance and the ends of the earth for your possession.
2:9 You shall crush them with an iron rod and shatter them like a piece of pottery.'
2:10 And now, you kings, be wise; be warned, you rulers of the earth.
2:11 Submit to the Lord with fear, and with trembling bow before him;
2:12 Lest he be angry and you perish; for his wrath is quickly kindled.
2:13 Happy are they all who take refuge in him!

text.KJV:
2:1 Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vaine thing?
2:2 The Kings of the earth set themselues, and the rulers take counsell together, against the Lord, and against his Anoynted, saying,
2:3 Let vs breake their bandes asunder, and cast away their cords from vs.
2:4 Hee that sitteth in the heauens shal laugh: the Lord shall haue them in derision.
2:5 Then shall hee speake vnto them in his wrath, and vexe them in his sore displeasure.
2:6 Yet haue I set my King vpon my holy hill of Sion.
2:7 I will declare the decree: the Lord hath said vnto mee, Thou art my sonne, this day haue I begotten thee.
2:8 Aske of me, and I shall giue thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the vttermost parts of the earth for thy possession.
2:9 Thou shalt breake them with a rod of iron, thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potters vessell.
2:10 Bee wise now therefore, O yee Kings: be instructed ye Iudges of the earth.
2:11 Serue the Lord with feare, and reioyce with trembling.
2:12 Kisse the Sonne lest he be angry, and ye perish from the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little: Blessed are all they that put their trust in him.
text.JPS1985:
2:1 Why do nations assemble, and peoples plot vain things;
2:2 kings of the earth take their stand, and regents intrigue together against the LORD and against His anointed?
2:3 “Let us break the cords of their yoke, shake off their ropes from us! ”
2:4 He who is enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord mocks at them.
2:5 Then He speaks to them in anger, terrifying them in His rage,
2:6 “But I have installed My king on Zion, My holy mountain! ”
2:7 Let me tell of the decree: the LORD said to me, “You are My son, I have fathered you this day.
2:8 Ask it of Me, and I will make the nations your domain; your estate, the limits of the earth.
2:9 You can smash them with an iron mace, shatter them like potter’s ware.”
2:10 So now, O kings, be prudent; accept discipline, you rulers of the earth!
2:11 Serve the LORD in awe; tremble with fright,
2:12 pay homage in good faith, lest He be angered, and your way be doomed in the mere flash of His anger. Happy are all who take refuge in Him.
text.Grail1963:
1 Why this tumult among nations,
among peoples this useless murmuring?
2 They arise, the kings of the earth,
princes plot against the Lord and his Anointed.
3 “Come, let us break their fetters,
come, let us cast off their yoke."
4 He who sits in the heavens laughs;
the Lord is laughing them to scorn.
5 Then he will speak in his anger,
his rage will strike them with terror.
6 "It is I who have set up my king on Zion,
my holy mountain."
7 (I will announce the decree of the Lord:)
The Lord said to me: "You are my Son.
It is I who have begotten you this day.
8 Ask and I will shall bequeath you the nations,
put the ends of the earth in your possession.
9 With a rod of iron you will break them,
shatter them like a potter's jar."
10 Now, O kings, understand,
take warning, rulers of the earth;
11 serve the Lord with awe
and trembling, pay him your homage
12 lest he be angry and you perish;
for suddenly his anger will blaze.

Blessed are they who put their trust in God.
text.Rotherham:
2:1 Wherefore have nations assembled in tumult? Or should, peoples, mutter an empty thing?
2:2 The kings of earth take their station, and, grave men, have met by appointment together,—against Yahweh, and against his Anointed One [saying] :
2:3 Let us break asunder their bonds,—and cast from us their cords!
2:4 He that sitteth in the heavens, will laugh,—My Lord, will mock at them:
2:5 Then, will he speak unto them in his anger, and, in his wrath, confound them:
2:6 Yet, I, have installed my king,—on Zion my holy mountain.
2:7 Let me tell of a decree,—Yahweh, hath said unto me, My son, thou art, I, to-day, have begotten thee:
2:8 Ask of me, and let me give nations as thine inheritance, and, as thy possession, the ends of the earth:
2:9 Thou shalt shepherd them with a sceptre of iron,—as a potter’s vessel, shalt thou dash them in pieces.
2:10 Now, therefore, ye kings, show your prudence, Be admonished, ye judges of earth:
2:11 Serve Yahweh with reverence, and exult with trembling:
2:12 Kiss the son, lest he be angry, and ye perish by the way, for soon might be kindled his anger,—How happy are all who seek refuge in him!
text.NABRE:
2:1 Psalm 2 A Psalm for a Royal Coronation - Why do the nations protest and the peoples conspire in vain?
2:2 Kings on earth rise up and princes plot together against the LORD and against his anointed one:
2:3 “Let us break their shackles and cast off their chains from us!”
2:4 The one enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord derides them,
2:5 Then he speaks to them in his anger, in his wrath he terrifies them:
2:6 “I myself have installed my king on Zion, my holy mountain.”
2:7 I will proclaim the decree of the LORD, he said to me, “You are my son; today I have begotten you.
2:8 Ask it of me, and I will give you the nations as your inheritance, and, as your possession, the ends of the earth.
2:9 With an iron rod you will shepherd them, like a potter’s vessel you will shatter them.”
2:10 And now, kings, give heed; take warning, judges on earth.
2:11 Serve the LORD with fear; exult with trembling, Accept correction lest he become angry and you perish along the way when his anger suddenly blazes up. Blessed are all who take refuge in him!
text.DRB:
2:1 Why did the Gentiles rage, and peoples meditate vain things?
2:2 The kings of the earth stood up, and the Princes came together in one against our Lord, and against his Christ.
2:3 Let us break their bonds a-sunder: and let us cast away their yoke from us.
2:4 He that dwelleth in the heavens shall laugh at them: and our Lord shall scorn them.
2:5 Then shall he speak to them in his wrath, & in his fury he shall trouble them.
2:6 But I am appointed king by him over Sion his holy hill, preaching his precept.
2:7 The Lord said to me: Thou art my Son, I this day have begotten thee.
2:8 Ask of me, and I will give thee the Gentiles, for thine inheritance, and thy possession the ends of the earth.
2:9 Thou shalt rule them in a rod of iron, and as a potter's vessel thou shalt break them in pieces.
2:10 And now ye kings understand: take instruction you that judge the earth.
2:11 Serve our Lord in fear: and rejoice to him with trembling.
2:12 Apprehend discipline lest sometime our Lord be wrath, and you perish out of the just way.
2:13 when his wrath shall burn in short time, blessed are all that trust in him.

text.NRSV:
3:1 Lord, how many adversaries I have; how many there are who rise up against me.
3:2 How many there are who say of me, 'There is no help for him in his God.'
3:3 But you, O Lord, are a shield about me; you are my glory, the one who lifts up my head.
3:4 I call aloud upon the Lord, and he answers me from his holy hill.
3:5 I lie down and go to sleep; I wake again, because the Lord sustains me.
3:6 I do not fear the multitudes of people who set themselves against me all around.
3:7 Rise up, O Lord; set me free, O my God; surely you will strike all my enemies across the face, you will break the teeth of the wicked.
3:8 Deliverance belongs to the Lord. Your blessing be upon your people!

text.KJV:
3:1 Lord, how are they increased that trouble mee? many are they that rise vp against me.
3:2 Many there bee which say of my soule, There is no helpe for him in God. Selah.
3:3 But thou, O Lord, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter vp of mine head.
3:4 I cryed vnto the Lord with my voyce, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.
3:5 I layd me downe and slept; I awaked, for the Lord sustained me.
3:6 I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that haue set themselues against me round about.
3:7 Arise, O Lord, saue mee, O my God; for thou hast smitten all mine enemies vpon the cheeke bone: thou hast broken the teeth of the vngodly.
3:8 Saluation belongeth vnto the Lord: thy blessing is vpon thy people. Selah.
text.JPS1985:
3:1 A psalm of David when he fled from his son Absalom.
3:2 O LORD, my foes are so many! Many are those who attack me;
3:3 many say of me, “There is no deliverance for him through God.” Selah.
3:4 But You, O LORD, are a shield about me, my glory, He who holds my head high.
3:5 I cry aloud to the LORD, and He answers me from His holy mountain. Selah.
3:6 I lie down and sleep and wake again, for the LORD sustains me.
3:7 I have no fear of the myriad forces arrayed against me on every side.
3:8 Rise, O LORD! Deliver me, O my God! For You slap all my enemies in the face; You break the teeth of the wicked.
3:9 Deliverance is the LORD’s; Your blessing be upon Your people! Selah.
text.Grail1963:
.2 How many are my foes, O Lord!
How many are rising up against me!
.3 How many are saying about me:
"There is no help for him in God."
.4 But you, Lord, are a shield about me,
my glory, who lift up my head.
.5 I cry aloud to the Lord.
He answers from his holy mountain.
.6 I lie down to rest, and I sleep.
I wake, for the Lord upholds me.
.7 I will not fear even thousands of people
who are ranged on every side against me.
.8 Arise, Lord; save me, my God,
you who strike my foes on the mouth,
you who break the teeth of the wicked!
.9 O Lord of salvation, bless your people!
text.Rotherham:
3:1 Yahweh! how have mine adversaries multiplied, Multitudes, are rising against me;
3:2 Multitudes, are saying of my soul,—No salvation for him in God. [Selah.]
3:3 But, thou, Yahweh, art a shield about me, my glory, and the lifter up of my head.
3:4 With my voice—unto Yahweh, do I cry, and he hath answered me out of his holy mountain. [Selah.]
3:5 I, laid me down and slept,—I awoke, Surely, Yahweh, sustaineth me!
3:6 I will not be afraid of myriads of people, who, round about, have set themselves against me.
3:7 Rise! Yahweh, Save me, my God. Surely thou hast smitten all my foes on the cheekbone, The teeth of the lawless, hast thou broken.
3:8 To Yahweh, belongeth Salvation! Upon thy people, be thy blessing. [Selah.]
text.NABRE:
3:1 Psalm 3 Threatened but Trusting - A psalm of David, when he fled from his son Absalom.
3:2 I - How many are my foes, LORD! How many rise against me!
3:3 How many say of me, “There is no salvation for him in God.” Selah
3:4 But you, LORD, are a shield around me; my glory, you keep my head high.
3:5 II - With my own voice I will call out to the LORD, and he will answer me from his holy mountain. Selah
3:6 I lie down and I fall asleep, [and] I will wake up, for the LORD sustains me.
3:7 I do not fear, then, thousands of people arrayed against me on every side.
3:8 III - Arise, LORD! Save me, my God! For you strike the cheekbone of all my foes; you break the teeth of the wicked.
3:9 Salvation is from the LORD! May your blessing be upon your people! Selah
text.DRB:
3:1 The Psalm of David, when he fled from the face of Absalom his son.
3:2 Lord why are they multiplied that trouble me? Many rise up against me.
3:3 Many say to my soul: There is no salvation for him in his God.
3:4 But thou Lord art my protector, my glory, & exalting my head.
3:5 With my voice I have cried to our Lord: and he hath heard me from his holy hill.
3:6 I have slept, and have been at rest; and have risen up, because our lord hath taken me.
3:7 I will not fear thousands of people compassing me: arise Lord, save me my God.
3:8 Because thou hast strucken all that are my adversaries without cause: thou hast broken the teeth of sinners.
3:9 Salvation is our Lord's: and thy blessing upon thy people.

text.NRSV:
4:1 Answer me when I call, O God, defender of my cause; you set me free when I am hard-pressed; have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
4:2 You mortals, how long will you dishonor my glory; how long will you worship dumb idols and run after false gods?
4:3 Know that the Lord does wonders for the faithful; when I call upon the Lord, he will hear me.
4:4 Tremble, then, and do not sin; speak to your heart in silence upon your bed.
4:5 Offer the appointed sacrifices and put your trust in the Lord.
4:6 Many are saying, 'Oh, that we might see better times!' Lift up the light of your countenance upon us, O Lord.
4:7 You have put gladness in my heart, more than when grain and wine and oil increase.
4:8 I lie down in peace; at once I fall asleep; for only you, Lord, make me dwell in safety.
text.Coverdale:

      you set me free when I am hard-pressed;
      have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
4:2 "You mortals, how long will you dishonor my glory; *
      how long will you worship dumb idols
      and run after false gods?"
4:3 Know that the LORD does wonders for the faithful; *
      when I call upon the LORD, he will hear me.
4:4 Tremble, then, and do not sin; *
      speak to your heart in silence upon your bed.
4:5 Offer the appointed sacrifices *
      and put your trust in the LORD.
4:6 Many are saying, "Oh, that we might see better times!" *
      Lift up the light of your countenance upon us, O LORD.
4:7 You have put gladness in my heart, *
      more than when grain and wine and oil increase.
4:8 I lie down in peace; at once I fall asleep; *

text.KJV:
4:1 Heare me, when I call, O God of my righteousnesse: thou hast inlarged mee when I was in distresse, haue mercy vpon me, and heare my prayer.
4:2 O ye sonnes of men, how long will yee turne my glory into shame? how long will yee loue vanitie, and seeke after leasing? Selah.
4:3 But know that the Lord hath set apart him that is godly, for himselfe: the Lord will heare when I call vnto him.
4:4 Stand in awe, and sinne not: commune with your owne heart vpon your bed, and be still. Selah.
4:5 Offer the sacrifices of righteousnesse, and put your trust in the Lord.
4:6 There be many that say, Who wil shew vs any good? Lord lift thou vp the light of thy countenance vpon vs.
4:7 Thou hast put gladnesse in my heart, more then in the time that their corne and their wine increased.
4:8 I will both lay mee downe in peace, and sleepe: for thou Lord only makest me dwell in safetie.
text.JPS1985:
4:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A psalm of David.
4:2 Answer me when I call, O God, my vindicator! You freed me from distress; have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
4:3 You men, how long will my glory be mocked, will you love illusions, have recourse to frauds? Selah.
4:4 Know that the LORD singles out the faithful for Himself; the LORD hears when I call to Him.
4:5 So tremble, and sin no more; ponder it on your bed, and sigh.
4:6 Offer sacrifices in righteousness and trust in the LORD.
4:7 Many say, “O for good days! ” Bestow Your favor on us, O LORD.
4:8 You put joy into my heart when their grain and wine show increase.
4:9 Safe and sound, I lie down and sleep, for You alone, O LORD, keep me secure.
text.Grail1963:
2 When I call, answer me, O God of justice;
from anguish you released me, have mercy and hear me!
3 O men, how long will your hearts be closed,
will you love what is futile and seek what is false?
4 It is the Lord who grants favors to those whom he loves;
the Lord hears me whenever I call him.
5 Fear him; do not sin: ponder on your bed and be still
6 Make justice your sacrifice, and trust in the Lord.
7 "What can bring us happiness?" many say.
Let the light of your face shine on us, O Lord.
8 You have put into my heart a greater joy
than they have from abundance of corn and new wine.
9 I will lie down in peace and sleep comes at once
for you alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety.
text.Rotherham:
4:1 When I cry, answer me, O mine own righteous God, In a strait place, thou hast made room for me, Show me favour, and hear my prayer.
4:2 Ye sons of the great! how long, turning my glory to contempt, will ye love emptiness, will ye seek falsehood? [Selah.]
4:3 Know ye, then, that Yahweh hath set apart the man of lovingkindness for himself: Yahweh, will hear, when I cry to him.
4:4 Be deeply moved, but do not sin,—Ponder in your own heart upon your bed, and be silent. [Selah.]
4:5 Sacrifice the sacrifices of righteousness; and put your trust in Yahweh.
4:6 Multitudes, are saying, Who will show us prosperity? Lift thou upon us the light of thy countenance, O Yahweh.
4:7 Thou hast put gladness in my heart,—beyond the season when, their corn and their new wine, have increased.
4:8 In peace, will I lay me down and at once sleep; for, thou, Yahweh alone, wilt cause me, in security, to dwell.
text.NABRE:
4:1 Psalm 4 Trust in God - For the leader; with stringed instruments. A psalm of David.
4:2 I - Answer me when I call, my saving God. When troubles hem me in, set me free; take pity on me, hear my prayer.
4:3 II - How long, O people, will you be hard of heart? Why do you love what is worthless, chase after lies? Selah
4:4 Know that the LORD works wonders for his faithful one; the LORD hears when I call out to him.
4:5 Tremble and sin no more; weep bitterly within your hearts, wail upon your beds,
4:6 Offer fitting sacrifices and trust in the LORD.
4:7 III - Many say, “May we see better times! LORD, show us the light of your face!” Selah
4:8 But you have given my heart more joy than they have when grain and wine abound.
4:9 In peace I will lie down and fall asleep, for you alone, LORD, make me secure.
text.DRB:
4:1 Unto the end in songs, the Psalm of David.
4:2 When I invocated, the God of my justice heard me: in tribulation thou hast enlarged to me. Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.
4:3 Ye sons of men how long are you of heavy heart? Why love you vanity, and seek lying?
4:4 And know ye that our Lord hath made his holy one marvelous: our Lord will hear me, when I shall cry to him.
4:5 Be ye angry, and sin not: the things that you say in your hearts, in your chambers be ye sorry for.
4:6 Sacrifice ye the sacrifice of justice, and hope in our Lord. Many say: Who sheweth us good things?
4:7 The light of thy countenance, o Lord is signed upon us: thou hast given gladness in my heart.
4:8 By the fruit of their corn, and wine, and oil, they are multiplied.
4:9 In peace in the self same I will sleep, and rest:
4:10 Because thou Lord hast singularly settled me in hope.

text.NRSV:
5:1 Give ear to my words, O Lord; consider my meditation.
5:2 Hearken to my cry for help, my King and my God, for I make my prayer to you.
5:3 In the morning, Lord, you hear my voice; early in the morning I make my appeal and watch for you.
5:4 For you are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness, and evil cannot dwell with you.
5:5 Braggarts cannot stand in your sight; you hate all those who work wickedness.
5:6 You destroy those who speak lies; the bloodthirsty and deceitful, O Lord, you abhor.
5:7 But as for me, through the greatness of your mercy, I will go into your house; I will bow down toward your holy temple in awe of you.
5:8 Lead me, O Lord, in your righteousness, because of those who lie in wait for me; make your way straight before me.
5:9 For there is no truth in their mouth; there is destruction within them.
5:10 Their throat is an open grave; they flatter with their tongue.
5:11 Declare them guilty, O God; let them fall by their own devices.
5:12 Because of their many transgressions cast them out, for they have rebelled against you.
5:13 But let all who take refuge in you be glad; let them ever sing out their joy with your loving care.
5:14 Shake them that they may be joyful in you, those who love your Name.
5:15 For you, O Lord, will bless the righteous; you will defend them with your favor as with a shield.

text.KJV:
5:1 Giue eare to my words, O Lord, consider my meditation.
5:2 Hearken vnto the voice of my crie, my King, and my God: for vnto thee will I pray.
5:3 My voyce shalt thou heare in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer vnto thee, and will looke vp.
5:4 For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickednesse: neither shall euill dwell with thee.
5:5 The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest al workers of iniquity
5:6 Thou shalt destroy them that speake leasing: the Lord will abhorre the bloodie and deceitfull man.
5:7 But as for me, I will come into thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy feare will I worship toward thy holy temple.
5:8 Lead me O Lord, in thy righteousnesse, because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.
5:9 For there is no faithfulnes in their mouth, their inward part is very wickednesse: their throat is an open sepulchre, they flatter with their tongue.
5:10 Destroy thou them, O God, let them fall by their owne counsels: cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions, for they haue rebelled against thee.
5:11 But let all those that put their trust in thee, reioyce: let them euer shout for ioy; because thou defendest them: let them also that loue thy name, be ioyfull in thee.
5:12 For thou, Lord, wilt blesse the righteous: with fauour wilt thou compasse him as with a shield.
text.JPS1985:
5:1 For the leader; on neḥiloth. A psalm of David.
5:2 Give ear to my speech, O LORD; consider my utterance.
5:3 Heed the sound of my cry, my king and God, for I pray to You.
5:4 Hear my voice, O LORD, at daybreak; at daybreak I plead before You, and wait.
5:5 For You are not a God who desires wickedness; evil cannot abide with You;
5:6 wanton men cannot endure in Your sight. You detest all evildoers;
5:7 You doom those who speak lies; murderous, deceitful men the LORD abhors.
5:8 But I, through Your abundant love, enter Your house; I bow down in awe at Your holy temple.
5:9 O LORD, lead me along Your righteous [path] because of my watchful foes; make Your way straight before me.
5:10 For there is no sincerity on their lips; their heart is [filled with] malice; their throat is an open grave; their tongue slippery.
5:11 Condemn them, O God; let them fall by their own devices; cast them out for their many crimes, for they defy You.
5:12 But let all who take refuge in You rejoice, ever jubilant as You shelter them; and let those who love Your name exult in You.
5:13 For You surely bless the righteous man, O LORD, encompassing him with favor like a shield.

text.Rotherham:
5:1 To my words, give ear, O Yahweh, Understand thou my softly murmured prayer:
5:2 Attend to the voice of my cry, my King and my God, for, unto thee, do I pray.
5:3 O Yahweh! in the morning, shalt thou hear my voice, In the morning, will I set in order unto thee, and keep watch;
5:4 For, not a GOD finding pleasure in lawlessness, art thou, and wrong can be no guest of thine:
5:5 Boasters, shall not station themselves, before thine eyes,—Thou hatest all workers of iniquity:
5:6 Thou wilt destroy them who speak falsehood,—The man of bloodshed and of deceit, Yahweh abhorreth.
5:7 But, I, in the abounding of thy lovingkindness, will enter thy house, I will bow down towards thy holy temple, in reverence of thee:
5:8 O Yahweh! lead me in thy righteousness, because of mine adversaries, Make even, before me, thy way:
5:9 For in his mouth is nothing worthy of trust, Their inward purpose, is engulphing ruin,—An opened sepulchre, is their throat, With their tongue, speak they smooth things.
5:10 Declare them guilty, O God, Let them fall by their own counsels,—Into the throng of their own transgressions, thrust them down, For they have rebelled against thee:—
5:11 That all may rejoice who seek refuge in thee, to times age-abiding, may shout in triumph, that thou wilt protect them, and they may leap for joy in thee who are lovers of thy Name.
5:12 For, thou, wilt bless the righteous man, O Yahweh, As with an all-covering shield—with good pleasure, wilt thou encompass him.
text.NABRE:
5:1 Psalm 5 Prayer for Divine Help - For the leader; with wind instruments. A psalm of David.
5:2 I - Give ear to my words, O LORD; understand my sighing.
5:3 Attend to the sound of my cry, my king and my God! For to you I will pray, LORD;
5:4 in the morning you will hear my voice; in the morning I will plead before you and wait.
5:5 II - You are not a god who delights in evil; no wicked person finds refuge with you;
5:6 the arrogant cannot stand before your eyes. You hate all who do evil;
5:7 you destroy those who speak falsely. A bloody and fraudulent man the LORD abhors.
5:8 III - But I, through the abundance of your mercy, will enter into your house. I will bow down toward your holy sanctuary out of fear of you.
5:9 LORD, guide me in your justice because of my foes; make straight your way before me.
5:10 IV - For there is no sincerity in their mouth; their heart is corrupt. Their throat is an open grave; on their tongue are subtle lies.
5:11 Declare them guilty, God; make them fall by their own devices. Drive them out for their many sins; for they have rebelled against you.
5:12 V - Then all who trust in you will be glad and forever shout for joy. You will protect them and those will rejoice in you who love your name.
5:13 For you, LORD, bless the just one; you surround him with favor like a shield.
text.DRB:
5:1 Unto the end, for her that obtaineth the inheritance. The Psalm of David.
5:2 Receive, o Lord my words with thine ears, understand my cry.
5:3 Attend to the voice of my prayer, my King and my God.
5:4 Because I will pray to thee: Lord in the morning thou wilt hear my voice.
5:5 In the morning I will stand by thee and will see: because thou art not a God that wilt iniquity.
5:6 Neither shall the malignant dwell near thee: neither shall the unjust abide before thine eyes.
5:7 Thou hatest all that work iniquity: thou wilt destroy all that speak lie. The bloody and deceitful man our Lord will abhor:
5:8 But I in the multitude of thy mercy. I will enter into thy house: I will adore toward thy holy temple in thy fear.
5:9 Lord conduct me in thy justice: because of mine enemies direct my way in thy sight.
5:10 Because there is no truth in their mouth: their heart is vain.
5:11 Their throat is an open sepulchre, they did deceitfully with their tongues, judge them o God . Let them fail of their cogitations, according to the multitude of their impieties expel them, because they have provoked thee o Lord.
5:12 And let all be glad, that hope in thee, they shall rejoice for ever: and thou shalt dwell in them. And all that love thy name shall glory in thee,
5:13 because thou wilt bless the just. Lord, as with a shield of thy good will, thou hast crowned us.

text.NRSV:
6:1 O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger, or discipline me in your wrath.
6:2 Be gracious to me, O Lord, for I am languishing; O Lord, heal me, for my bones are shaking with terror.
6:3 My soul also is shaking with terror; how long, O Lord, how long?
6:4 Turn, O Lord, save my life; deliver me for the sake of your steadfast love.
6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol who can give you praise?
6:6 I am weary with my moaning; every night I flood my bed with tears; I drench my couch with my weeping.
6:7 My eyes waste away because of grief; they grow weak because of all my foes.
6:8 Depart from me, all you workers of evil, for the Lord has heard the sound of my weeping.
6:9 The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord accepts my prayer.
6:10 All my enemies shall be ashamed and struck with terror; they shall turn back, and in a moment be put to shame.
text.Coverdale:
6:1 LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger; *
      do not punish me in your wrath.
6:2 Have pity on me, LORD, for I am weak; *
      heal me, LORD, for my bones are racked.
6:3 My spirit shakes with terror; *
      how long, O LORD, how long?
6:4 Turn, O LORD, and deliver me; *
      save me for your mercy's sake.
6:5 For in death no one remembers you; *
      and who will give you thanks in the grave?
6:6 I grow weary because of my groaning; *
      every night I drench my bed
      and flood my couch with tears.
6:7 My eyes are wasted with grief *
      and worn away because of all my enemies.
6:8 Depart from me, all evildoers, *
      for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
6:9 The LORD has heard my supplication; *
      the LORD accepts my prayer.
6:10 All my enemies shall be confounded and quake with fear; *
      they shall turn back and suddenly be put to shame.
text.KJV:
6:1 O Lord, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
6:2 Haue mercy vpon me, O Lord, for I am weake: O Lord heale mee, for my bones are vexed.
6:3 My soule is also sore vexed: but thou, O Lord, how long?
6:4 Returne, O Lord, deliuer my soule: oh saue mee, for thy mercies sake.
6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of thee: in the graue who shall giue thee thankes?
6:6 I am weary with my groning, all the night make I my bed to swim: I water my couch with my teares.
6:7 Mine eie is consumed because of griefe; it waxeth olde because of all mine enemies.
6:8 Depart from me, all yee workers of iniquitie: for the Lord hath heard the voice of my weeping.
6:9 The Lord hath heard my supplication; the Lord will receiue my prayer.
6:10 Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them returne and be ashamed suddainly.
text.JPS1985:
6:1 For the leader; with instrumental music on the sheminith. A psalm of David.
6:2 O LORD, do not punish me in anger, do not chastise me in fury.
6:3 Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I languish; heal me, O LORD, for my bones shake with terror.
6:4 My whole being is stricken with terror, while You, LORD —O, how long!
6:5 O LORD, turn! Rescue me! Deliver me as befits Your faithfulness.
6:6 For there is no praise of You among the dead; in Sheol, who can acclaim You?
6:7 I am weary with groaning; every night I drench my bed, I melt my couch in tears.
6:8 My eyes are wasted by vexation, worn out because of all my foes.
6:9 Away from me, all you evildoers, for the LORD heeds the sound of my weeping.
6:10 The LORD heeds my plea, the LORD accepts my prayer.
6:11 All my enemies will be frustrated and stricken with terror; they will turn back in an instant, frustrated.
text.Rotherham:
6:1 O Yahweh! do not, in thine anger, correct me, nor, in thy wrath, chastise me.
6:2 Show me favour, O Yahweh, for languishing am I: Heal me, O Yahweh,—for dismayed are my bones:
6:3 Yea, my soul, is dismayed greatly, Thou, then, O Yahweh—how long?
6:4 Return, O Yahweh, deliver my soul, Save me, for the sake of thy lovingkindness;
6:5 For, in death, is no remembrance of thee,—In hades, who shall give thanks unto thee?
6:6 I am weary with my sighing, I flood, through the whole night, my couch,—With my tears, I cause, my bed, to dissolve:
6:7 My face, is all sunken with sorrow,—it hath aged, because of all mine adversaries.
6:8 Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity, for Yahweh hath heard the voice of my weeping:
6:9 Yahweh, hath heard my supplication, Yahweh, will receive, my prayer.
6:10 Let all my foes, turn pale and be greatly dismayed, Again let them turn pale in a moment.
text.NABRE:
6:1 Psalm 6 Prayer in Distress - For the leader; with stringed instruments, “upon the eighth.” A psalm of David.
6:2 I - Do not reprove me in your anger, LORD, nor punish me in your wrath.
6:3 Have pity on me, LORD, for I am weak; heal me, LORD, for my bones are shuddering.
6:4 My soul too is shuddering greatly— and you, LORD, how long…?
6:5 Turn back, LORD, rescue my soul; save me because of your mercy.
6:6 For in death there is no remembrance of you. Who praises you in Sheol?
6:7 II - I am wearied with sighing; all night long I drench my bed with tears; I soak my couch with weeping.
6:8 My eyes are dimmed with sorrow, worn out because of all my foes.
6:9 III - Away from me, all who do evil! The LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
6:10 The LORD has heard my plea; the LORD will receive my prayer.
6:11 My foes will all be disgraced and will shudder greatly; they will turn back in sudden disgrace.
text.DRB:
6:1 Unto the end in songs, the Psalm of David for the octave.
6:2 Lord, rebuke me not in thy fury; nor chastise me in thy wrath.
6:3 Have mercy on me Lord, because I am weak: heal me Lord, because all my bones be troubled.
6:4 And my soul is troubled exceedingly: but thou Lord how long?
6:5 Turn thee, o Lord, and deliver my soul: save me for thy mercy.
6:6 Because there is not in death that is mindful of thee: and in hell who shall confess to thee?
6:7 I have laboured in my sighing, I will every night wash my bed; I will water my couch with my tears.
6:8 My eye is troubled for fury: I have waxen old among all mine enemies.
6:9 Depart from me all ye that work iniquity: because our Lord hath heard the voice of my weeping.
6:10 Our Lord hath heard my petition, our Lord hath received my prayer.
6:11 Let all mine enemies be ashamed, & very sore troubled: let them be converted and ashamed very quickly.

text.NRSV:
7:1 O Lord my God, in you I take refuge; save me from all my pursuers, and deliver me,
7:2 or like a lion they will tear me apart; they will drag me away, with no one to rescue.
7:3 O Lord my God, if I have done this, if there is wrong in my hands,
7:4 if I have repaid my ally with harm or plundered my foe without cause,
7:5 then let the enemy pursue and overtake me, trample my life to the ground, and lay my soul in the dust. Selah
7:6 Rise up, O Lord, in your anger; lift yourself up against the fury of my enemies; awake, O my God; you have appointed a judgment.
7:7 Let the assembly of the peoples be gathered around you, and over it take your seat on high.
7:8 The Lord judges the peoples; judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness and according to the integrity that is in me.
7:9 O let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous, you who test the minds and hearts, O righteous God.
7:10 God is my shield, who saves the upright in heart.
7:11 God is a righteous judge, and a God who has indignation every day.
7:12 If one does not repent, God will whet his sword; he has bent and strung his bow;
7:13 he has prepared his deadly weapons, making his arrows fiery shafts.
7:14 See how they conceive evil, and are pregnant with mischief, and bring forth lies.
7:15 They make a pit, digging it out, and fall into the hole that they have made.
7:16 Their mischief returns upon their own heads, and on their own heads their violence descends.
7:17 I will give to the Lord the thanks due to his righteousness, and sing praise to the name of the Lord, the Most High.
text.Coverdale:
7:1 O LORD my God, I take refuge in you; *
      save and deliver me from all who pursue me;
7:2 Lest like a lion they tear me in pieces *
      and snatch me away with none to deliver me.
7:3 O LORD my God, if I have done these things: *
      if there is any wickedness in my hands,
7:4 If I have repaid my friend with evil, *
      or plundered him who without cause is my enemy;
7:5 Then let my enemy pursue and overtake me, *
      trample my life into the ground,
      and lay my honor in the dust.
7:6 Stand up, O LORD, in your wrath; *
      rise up against the fury of my enemies.
7:7 Awake, O my God, decree justice; *
      let the assembly of the peoples gather round you.
7:8 Be seated on your lofty throne, O Most High; *
      O LORD, judge the nations.
7:9 Give judgment for me according to my righteousness, O LORD, *
      and according to my innocence, O Most High.
7:10 Let the malice of the wicked come to an end,
      but establish the righteous; *
      for you test the mind and heart, O righteous God.
7:11 God is my shield and defense; *
      he is the savior of the true in heart.
7:12 God is a righteous judge; *
      God sits in judgment every day.
7:13 If they will not repent, God will whet his sword; *
      he will bow his bow and make it ready.
7:14 He has prepared his weapons of death; *
      he makes his arrows shafts of fire.
7:15 Look at those who are in labor with wickedness, *
      who conceive evil, and give birth to a lie.
7:16 They dig a pit and make it deep *
      and fall into the hole that they have made.
7:17 Their malice turns back upon their own head; *
      their violence falls on their own scalp.
7:18 I will bear witness that the LORD is righteous; *
      I will praise the Name of the LORD Most High.
text.KJV:
7:1 O Lord, my God, in thee doe I put my trust: saue me from all them that persecute me, and deliuer me.
7:2 Least hee teare my soule like a lyon, renting it in pieces, while there is none to deliuer.
7:3 O Lord my God, if I haue done this; if there be iniquitie in my hands:
7:4 If I haue rewarded euill vnto him that was at peace with me: (yea I haue deliuered him that without cause is mine enemie.)
7:5 Let the enemie persecute my soule, and take it, yea let him tread downe my life vpon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust. Selah.
7:6 Arise, O Lord, in thine anger, lift vp thy selfe, because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the iudgement that thou hast commanded.
7:7 So shall the congregation of the people compasse thee about: for their sakes therefore returne thou on high.
7:8 The Lord shal iudge the people: iudge me, O Lord, according to my righteousnesse, and according to mine integritie that is in me.
7:9 Oh let the wickednes of the wicked come to an end, but establish the iust: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reines.
7:10 My defence is of God, which saueth the vpright in heart.
7:11 God iudgeth the righteous, and God is angrie with the wicked euery-day.
7:12 If he turne not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bowe, and made it ready.
7:13 He hath also prepared for him the instruments of death; he ordaineth his arrowes against the persecutors.
7:14 Behold, he trauelleth with iniquitie, and hath conceiued mischiefe, and brought forth falshood.
7:15 He made a pit and digged it, and is fallen into the ditch which he made.
7:16 His mischiefe shall returne vpon his owne head, and his violent dealing shall come downe vpon his owne pate.
7:17 I will praise the Lord according to his righteousnesse: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
text.JPS1985:
7:1 Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD, concerning Cush, a Benjaminite.
7:2 O LORD, my God, in You I seek refuge; deliver me from all my pursuers and save me,
7:3 lest, like a lion, they tear me apart, rending in pieces, and no one save me.
7:4 O LORD, my God, if I have done such things, if my hands bear the guilt of wrongdoing,
7:5 if I have dealt evil to my ally— I who rescued my foe without reward —
7:6 then let the enemy pursue and overtake me; let him trample my life to the ground, and lay my body in the dust. Selah.
7:7 Rise, O LORD, in Your anger; assert Yourself against the fury of my foes; bestir Yourself on my behalf; You have ordained judgment.
7:8 Let the assembly of peoples gather about You, with You enthroned above, on high.
7:9 The LORD judges the peoples; vindicate me, O LORD, for the righteousness and blamelessness that are mine.
7:10 Let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; he who probes the mind and conscience is God the righteous.
7:11 I look to God to shield me; the deliverer of the upright.
7:12 God vindicates the righteous; God pronounces doom each day.
7:13 If one does not turn back, but whets his sword, bends his bow and aims it,
7:14 then against himself he readies deadly weapons, and makes his arrows sharp.
7:15 See, he hatches evil, conceives mischief, and gives birth to fraud.
7:16 He has dug a pit and deepened it, and will fall into the trap he made.
7:17 His mischief will recoil upon his own head; his lawlessness will come down upon his skull.
7:18 I will praise the LORD for His righteousness, and sing a hymn to the name of the LORD Most High.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Lord God, I take refuge in you.
From my pursuer save me and rescue me,
.3 lest he tear me to pieces like a lion
and drag me off with no one to rescue me.
.4 Lord God, if my hands have done wrong,
.5 if I have paid back evil for good,
I who saved my unjust oppressor:
.6 then let my foe pursue me and seize me,
let him trample my life to the ground
and lay my soul in the dust.
* * *
.7 Lord, rise up in your anger,
rise against the fury of my foes;
my God, awake! You will give judgment.
.8 Let the company of nations gather round you,
taking your seat above them on high.
.9 (The Lord is judge of the peoples.)
Give judgment for me, Lord; I am just
and innocent of heart.
.10 Put an end to the evil of the wicked!
Make the just stand firm,
you who test mind and heart,
O just God!
.11 God is the shield that protects me,
who saves the upright of heart.
.12 God is a just judge
slow to anger;
but he threatens the wicked every day,
.13 men who will not repent.
* * *

God will sharpen his sword;
he has braced his bow and taken aim.
.14 For them he has prepared deadly weapons;
he barbs his arrows with fire.
.15 Here is one who is pregnant with malice,
who conceives evil and brings forth lies.
.16 He digs a pitfall, digs it deep;
and in the trap he has made he will fall.
.17 His malice will recoil on himself;
on his own head his violence will fall.
* * *
.18 I will thank the Lord for his justice:
I will sing to the Lord, the Most High.
text.Rotherham:
7:1 O Yahweh my God, in thee, have I sought refuge,—Save me from all my pursuers, and deliver me:
7:2 Lest one tear, as a lion, my soul,—and there be no deliverer to rescue.
7:3 O Yahweh my God, If I have done this, If there hath been perversity in my hands:
7:4 If I have requited my friend with wrong,—or have oppressed mine adversary without need,
7:5 Let an enemy pursue my soul, and overtake it, that he may tread down, to the earth, my life,—and, mine honour—in the dust, he may cause to dwell. [Selah.]
7:6 Rise! Yahweh! in thine anger, Lift thyself up, because of the haughty outbursts of mine adversaries, Stir up for me the justice thou hast commanded:
7:7 When, the assembly of peoples, gather round thee, then, above it—on high, do thou return!
7:8 Yahweh, will judge the peoples,—Do me justice, O Yahweh, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity upon me,
7:9 Let the wrong of the lawless, I pray thee, come to an end, and establish thou him that is righteous,—for, a trier of hearts and reins, is God the righteous one.
7:10 My shield, is held by God, who is ready to save the upright in heart:
7:11 A God to vindicate the righteous,—And yet a God to be indignant throughout every day.
7:12 If he turn not, his sword, will he whet,—his bow, hath he bent, and made ready:
7:13 But, for himself, hath he made ready the weapons so deadly, His arrows, he, so fiery, would make:
7:14 Lo! he gendereth trouble, and conceiveth mischief, but giveth birth to a disappointment:
7:15 A pit, he cut out, and digged it, and then fell into the ditch he had made:
7:16 His mischief, turneth back, on his own head,—and, on his own crown, his violence, descendeth.
7:17 I will praise Yahweh according to his righteousness,—and will praise in song the Name of Yahweh Most High.
text.NABRE:
7:1 Psalm 7 God the Vindicator - A plaintive song of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning Cush, the Benjaminite.
7:2 I - LORD my God, in you I trusted; save me; rescue me from all who pursue me,
7:3 Lest someone maul me like a lion, tear my soul apart with no one to deliver.
7:4 II - LORD my God, if I have done this, if there is guilt on my hands,
7:5 If I have maltreated someone treating me equitably— or even despoiled my oppressor without cause—
7:6 Then let my enemy pursue and overtake my soul, trample my life to the ground, and lay my honor in the dust. Selah
7:7 III - Rise up, LORD, in your anger; be aroused against the outrages of my oppressors. Stir up the justice, my God, you have commanded.
7:8 Have the assembly of the peoples gather about you; and return on high above them,
7:9 the LORD will pass judgment on the peoples. Judge me, LORD, according to my righteousness, and my integrity.
7:10 Let the malice of the wicked end. Uphold the just one, O just God, who tries hearts and minds.
7:11 IV - God is a shield above me saving the upright of heart.
7:12 God is a just judge, powerful and patient, not exercising anger every day.
7:13 If one does not repent, God sharpens his sword, strings and readies the bow,
7:14 Prepares his deadly shafts, makes arrows blazing thunderbolts.
7:15 V - Consider how one conceives iniquity; is pregnant with mischief, and gives birth to deception.
7:16 He digs a hole and bores it deep, but he falls into the pit he has made.
7:17 His malice turns back upon his head; his violence falls on his own skull.
7:18 VI - I will thank the LORD in accordance with his justice; I will sing the name of the LORD Most High.
text.DRB:
7:1 The Psalm of David which he sung to our Lord, for the words of Chusi the son of Jemini.
7:2 O Lord my God I have hoped in thee: save me from all that persecute me, and deliver me.
7:3 Lest sometime he as a Lion violently take my soul, whilst there is none to redeem, nor to save.
7:4 O Lord my God if I have done this, if there be iniquity in my hands:
7:5 If I have rendered to them that repayed me evils, let me worthily fall empty from mine enemies.
7:6 Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it, and tread down my life in the earth, and bring down my glory into the dust.
7:7 Arise Lord in thy wrath: and be exalted in the coasts of mine enemies.
7:8 And arise, o Lord my God, in the precept which thou hast commanded: and a synagogue of peoples shall compass thee.
7:9 And for it return on high: our Lord judgeth peoples. Judge me, o Lord, according to my justice, and according to my innocency upon me.
7:10 The wickedness of sinners shall be consumed, and thou shalt direct the just, which searchest the heart and reins, o God.
7:11 My just help is from our Lord, who saveth those that be right of heart.
7:12 God is a just judge, strong, & patient: is he angry every day?
7:13 Unless you will be converted, he shall shake his sword, he hath bent his bow, and prepared it.
7:14 And in it he hath prepared the vessels of death: he hath made his arrows for them that burn.
7:15 Behold he hath bred with injustice: he hath conceived sorrow, and brought forth iniquity.
7:16 He hath opened a pit, and digged it up: and he is fallen into the ditch, which he made.
7:17 His sorrow shall be turned upon his head: and his iniquity shall descend upon his crown.
7:18 I will confess to our Lord according to his justice: and will sing to the name of our Lord most high.

text.NRSV:
8:1 O Lord, our Sovereign, how majestic is your name in all the earth! You have set your glory above the heavens.
8:2 Out of the mouths of babes and infants you have founded a bulwark because of your foes, to silence the enemy and the avenger.
8:3 When I look at your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and the stars that you have established;
8:4 what are human beings that you are mindful of them, mortals that you care for them?
8:5 Yet you have made them a little lower than God, and crowned them with glory and honor.
8:6 You have given them dominion over the works of your hands; you have put all things under their feet,
8:7 all sheep and oxen, and also the beasts of the field,
8:8 the birds of the air, and the fish of the sea, whatever passes along the paths of the seas.
8:9 O Lord, our Sovereign, how majestic is your name in all the earth!
text.Coverdale:
8:1 O LORD our Governor, *
      how exalted is your Name in all the world!
8:2 Out of the mouths of infants and children *
      your majesty is praised above the heavens.
8:3 You have set up a stronghold against your adversaries, *
      to quell the enemy and the avenger.
8:4 When I consider your heavens, the work of your fingers, *
      the moon and the stars you have set in their courses,
8:5 What is man that you should be mindful of him? *
      the son of man that you should seek him out?
8:6 You have made him but little lower than the angels; *
      you adorn him with glory and honor;
8:7 You give him mastery over the works of your hands; *
      you put all things under his feet:
8:8 All sheep and oxen, *
      even the wild beasts of the field,
8:9 The birds of the air, the fish of the sea, *
      and whatsoever walks in the paths of the sea.
8:10 O LORD our Governor, *
      how exalted is your Name in all the world!
text.KJV:
8:1 O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory aboue the heauens.
8:2 Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength, because of thine enemies, that thou mightest still the enemie and the auenger.
8:3 When I consider thy heauens, the worke of thy fingers, the moone and the starres which thou hast ordained;
8:4 What is man, that thou art mindfull of him? and the sonne of man, that thou visitest him?
8:5 For thou hast made him a little lower then the Angels; and hast crowned him with glory and honour.
8:6 Thou madest him to haue dominion ouer the workes of thy hands; thou hast put all things vnder his feete.
8:7 All sheepe and oxen, yea and the beasts of the field.
8:8 The foule of the aire, and the fish of the sea, and whatsoeuer passeth through the paths of the seas.
8:9 O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
text.JPS1985:
8:1 For the leader; on the gittith. A psalm of David.
8:2 O LORD, our Lord, How majestic is Your name throughout the earth, You who have covered the heavens with Your splendor!
8:3 From the mouths of infants and sucklings You have founded strength on account of Your foes, to put an end to enemy and avenger.
8:4 When I behold Your heavens, the work of Your fingers, the moon and stars that You set in place,
8:5 what is man that You have been mindful of him, mortal man that You have taken note of him,
8:6 that You have made him little less than divine, and adorned him with glory and majesty;
8:7 You have made him master over Your handiwork, laying the world at his feet,
8:8 sheep and oxen, all of them, and wild beasts, too;
8:9 the birds of the heavens, the fish of the sea, whatever travels the paths of the seas.
8:10 O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name throughout the earth!
text.Grail1963:
.2 How great is your name, O Lord our God,
through all the earth!
Your majesty is praised above the heavens;
.3 on the lips of children and of babes
you have found praise to foil your enemy,
to silence the foe and the rebel.
.4 When I see the heavens, the work of your hands,
the moon and the stars which you arranged,
.5 what is man that you should keep him in mind,
mortal man that you care for him?
.6 Yet you have made him little less than a god;
with glory and honor you crowned him,
.7 gave him power over the works of your hands,
put all things under his feet.
.8 All of them, sheep and cattle,
yes, even the savage beasts,
.9 birds of the air, and fish
that make their way through the waters.
.10 How great is your name, O Lord our God
through all the earth!
text.Rotherham:
8:1 O Yahweh, our Lord! How majestic is thy Name, in all the earth, Who hast set thy splendour upon the heavens.
8:2 Out of the mouth of children and sucklings, hast thou laid a foundation of strength,—because of thine adversaries, to make foe and avenger be still.
8:3 When I view thy heavens, the work, of thy fingers, moon and stars, which thou hast established,
8:4 What was weak man, that thou shouldst make mention of him? or the son of the earthborn, that thou shouldst set him in charge?
8:5 That thou shouldst make him little less than messengers of God, with glory and honour, shouldst crown him?
8:6 Shouldst give him dominion over the works of thy hands,—All things, shouldst have put under his feet:
8:7 Sheep and oxen, all of them,—yea even the beasts of the field;
8:8 The bird of the heavens, and the fishes of the sea,— the passer-by on the paths of the seas?
8:9 O Yahweh, our Lord! How majestic is thy Name, in all the earth.
text.NABRE:
8:1 Psalm 8 Divine Majesty and Human Dignity - For the leader; “upon the gittith.” A psalm of David.
8:2 O LORD, our Lord, how awesome is your name through all the earth! I will sing of your majesty above the heavens
8:3 with the mouths of babes and infants. You have established a bulwark against your foes, to silence enemy and avenger.
8:4 When I see your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and stars that you set in place—
8:5 What is man that you are mindful of him, and a son of man that you care for him?
8:6 Yet you have made him little less than a god, crowned him with glory and honor.
8:7 You have given him rule over the works of your hands, put all things at his feet:
8:8 All sheep and oxen, even the beasts of the field,
8:9 The birds of the air, the fish of the sea, and whatever swims the paths of the seas.
8:10 O LORD, our Lord, how awesome is your name through all the earth!
text.DRB:
8:1 Unto the end for presses, the Psalm of David.
8:2 O Lord our Lord, how marvelous is thy name in the whole earth! Because thy magnificence is elevated above the heavens.
8:3 Out of the mouth of infants & sucklings, thou hast perfected praise because of thine enemies, that thou mayest destroy the enemy & revenger.
8:4 Because I shall see thy heavens, the works of thy fingers: the moon and the stars, which thou hast founded.
8:5 What is man, that thou art mindful of him? Or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
8:6 Thou hast minished him a little less than Angels; with glory and honour thou hast crowned him:
8:7 And hast appointed him over the works of thy hands.
8:8 Thou hast subjected all things under his feet, all sheep and oxen: moreover also the beasts of the field.
8:9 The birds of the air, & fishes of the sea; that walk the paths of the sea.
8:10 O Lord our Lord, how marvelous is thy name in the whole earth!

text.NRSV:
9:1 I will give thanks to you, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will tell of all your marvelous works.
9:2 I will be glad and exult in you; I will sing psalms to your Name, O Most High.
9:3 When my enemies are driven back, they will stumble and perish at your presence.
9:4 For you have maintained my right and my cause; you are seated on your throne judging righteously.
9:5 You have rebuked the ungodly and destroyed the wicked; you have blotted out their name for ever and ever.
9:6 As for the enemy, they are finished, in perpetual ruin, their cities plowed under, the very memory of them has perished.
9:7 But the Lord enthroned for ever; he has set up his throne for judgment.
9:8 It is he who rules the world with righteousness; he judges the peoples with equity.
9:9 The Lord will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in time of trouble.
9:10 Those who know your Name will put their trust in you, for you never forsake those who seek you, O Lord.
9:11 Sing psalms to the Lord who dwells in Zion; proclaim among the peoples the things he has done.
9:12 The Avenger of blood will remember them; he will not forget the cry of the afflicted.
9:13 Have pity on me, O Lord; see the misery I suffer from those who hate me, O you who lift me up from the gates of death;
9:14 So that I may tell of all your praises and rejoice in your salvation in the gates of the city of Zion.
9:15 The ungodly have fallen into the pit they dug, and in the snare they set is their own foot caught.
9:16 The Lord is known by his acts of justice; the wicked are trapped in the works of their own hands.
9:17 The wicked shall be given over to the grave, and also all the peoples that forget God.
9:18 For the needy shall not always be forgotten, and the hope of the poor shall not perish for ever.
9:19 Rise up, O Lord, let not the ungodly have the upper hand; let them be judged before you.
9:20 Put them in fear, O Lord; let the ungodly know they are but mortal.

text.KJV:
9:1 I wil praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart: I will shewe foorth all thy maruellous workes.
9:2 I will bee glad and reioyce in thee: I will sing prayse to thy name, O thou most High.
9:3 When mine enemies are turned backe, they shall fall and perish at thy presence.
9:4 For thou hast maintained my right, and my cause: thou satest in the throne iudging right.
9:5 Thou hast rebuked the heathen, thou hast destroyed the wicked; thou hast put out their name for euer and euer.
9:6 O thou enemie, destructions are come to a perpetuall end; and thou hast destroyed cities, their memoriall is perished with them.
9:7 But the Lord shall endure for euer: he hath prepared his throne for iudgement.
9:8 And hee shall iudge the world in righteousnesse; he shall minister iudgement to the people in vprightnesse.
9:9 The Lord also will bee a refuge for the oppressed: a refuge, in times of trouble.
9:10 And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou Lord hast not forsaken them that seeke thee.
9:11 Sing praises to the Lord, which dwelleth in Sion: declare among the people his doings.
9:12 When he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembreth them: he forgetteth not the crie of the humble.
9:13 Haue mercie vpon me O Lord, consider my trouble which I suffer of them that hate me, thou that liftest mee vp from the gates of death:
9:14 That I may shew foorth all thy prayse in the gates of the daughter of Sion: I will reioyce in thy saluation.
9:15 The heathen are sunke downe in the pit that they made: in the net which they hid, is their own foot taken.
9:16 The Lord is knowen by the iudgement which he executeth: the wicked is snared in the worke of his owne hands. Higgaion. Selah.
9:17 The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.
9:18 For the needie shall not alway be forgotten: the expectation of the poore shall not perish for euer.
9:19 Arise, O Lord, let not man preuaile: let the heathen bee iudged in thy sight.
9:20 Put them in feare, O Lord: that the nations may know themselues to be but men. Selah.
text.JPS1985:
9:1 For the leader; ‘almuth labben. A psalm of David.
9:2 I will praise You, LORD, with all my heart; I will tell all Your wonders.
9:3 I will rejoice and exult in You, singing a hymn to Your name, O Most High.
9:4 When my enemies retreat, they stumble to their doom at Your presence.
9:5 For You uphold my right and claim, enthroned as righteous judge.
9:6 You blast the nations; You destroy the wicked; You blot out their name forever.
9:7 The enemy is no more— ruins everlasting; You have torn down their cities; their very names are lost.
9:8 But the LORD abides forever; He has set up His throne for judgment;
9:9 it is He who judges the world with righteousness, rules the peoples with equity.
9:10 The LORD is a haven for the oppressed, a haven in times of trouble.
9:11 Those who know Your name trust You, for You do not abandon those who turn to You, O LORD.
9:12 Sing a hymn to the LORD, who reigns in Zion; declare His deeds among the peoples.
9:13 For He does not ignore the cry of the afflicted; He who requites bloodshed is mindful of them.
9:14 Have mercy on me, O LORD; see my affliction at the hands of my foes, You who lift me from the gates of death,
9:15 so that in the gates of Fair Zion I might tell all Your praise, I might exult in Your deliverance.
9:16 The nations sink in the pit they have made; their own foot is caught in the net they have hidden.
9:17 The LORD has made Himself known: He works judgment; the wicked man is snared by his own devices. Higgaion. Selah.
9:18 Let the wicked be in Sheol, all the nations who ignore God!
9:19 Not always shall the needy be ignored, nor the hope of the afflicted forever lost.
9:20 Rise, O LORD! Let not men have power; let the nations be judged in Your presence.
9:21 Strike fear into them, O LORD; let the nations know they are only men. Selah.
text.Grail1963:
.2 I will praise you, Lord, with all my heart;
I will recount all your wonders.
.3 I will rejoice in you and be glad,
and sing psalms to your name, O Most High.

.4 See how my enemies turn back,
how they stumble and perish before you.
.5 You upheld the justice of my cause;
you sat enthroned, judging with justice.
.6 You have checked the nations, destroyed the wicked;
you have wiped out their name for ever and ever.
.7 The foe is destroyed, eternally ruined.
You uprooted their cities; their memory has perished.
.8 But the Lord sits enthroned for ever.
He has set up his throne for judgment;
.9 he will judge the world with justice,
he will judge the peoples with his truth.
.10 For the oppressed let the Lord be a stronghold,
a stronghold in times of distress.
.11 Those who know your name will trust you;
you will never forsake those who seek you.
.12 Sing psalms to the Lord who dwells in Zion.
Proclaim his mighty works among the peoples,
.13 for the Avenger of blood has remembered them,
has not forgotten the cry of the poor.
.14 Have pity on me, Lord, see my sufferings,
you who save me from the gates of death;
.15 that I may recount all your praise
at the gates of the city of Zion
and rejoice in your saving help.
.16 The nations have fallen in the pit which they made,
their feet caught in the snare they laid.
.17 The Lord has revealed himself, and given judgment.
The wicked are snared in the work of their own hands.
.18 Let the wicked go down among the dead,
all the nations forgetful of God;
.19 for the needy shall not always be forgotten
nor the hopes of the poor be in vain.
.20 Arise, Lord, let men not prevail!
Let the nations be judged before you.
.21 Lord, strike them with terror,
let the nations know they are but men.
text.Rotherham:
9:1 I will praise Yahweh with all my heart, I will recount all thy wonderful doings:
9:2 I will rejoice and exult in thee, I will praise in song, thy Name, O most High.
9:3 When mine enemies turned back, they stumbled and perished from before thee;
9:4 For thou hast executed my right and my cause, Thou hast sat on the throne, judging righteously:
9:5 Thou hast rebuked the nations, Thou hast destroyed the lawless one, Their name, hast thou wiped out, to times age-abiding and beyond.
9:6 O enemy! complete are the desolations, evermore,—even cities, hast thou uprooted, The memory of, them, hath perished.
9:7 But, Yahweh, unto times age-abiding, will sit, Ready for judgment, is his throne;
9:8 And, he, will judge the world in righteousness, He will minister judgment to peoples, in uprightness.
9:9 Thus be Yahweh a refuge for the crushed one, a refuge for times of destitution:
9:10 Thus let them who know thy Name, put confidence in thee, that thou hast not forsaken the searchers for thee, O Yahweh.
9:11 Sing ye praise to Yahweh, who is seated in Zion, Tell among the peoples his doings.
9:12 When he was making inquisition for blood, of them, had he remembrance, he forgat not the outcry of the oppressed.
9:13 Show me favour, O Yahweh! Behold my humiliation due to them who hate me, Lift me on high out of the gates of death;
9:14 That I may recount all thy praises, In the gates of the daughter of Zion, may exult in thy salvation.
9:15 The nations, have sunk, in the pit they had made, In the net which they had hidden, is caught their own foot.
9:16 To be known is Yahweh, by the sentence he hath executed, By the doing of his own hands, is he about to strike down the lawless one. [Resounding music. Selah.]
9:17 The lawless, shall return, to hades, all nations forgetful of God.
9:18 For, not always, shall the needy, be forgotten,—[nor,] the hope of the oppressed, perish for ever.
9:19 Rise! Yahweh! let man not prevail, let the nations be judged before thee.
9:20 Appoint, O Yahweh, a terror for them,—Let the nations know [that] they are men. [Selah.]
text.NABRE:
9:1 Psalm 9 Thanksgiving for Victory and Prayer for Justice - For the leader; according to Muth Labben. A psalm of David.
9:2 I - I will praise you, LORD, with all my heart; I will declare all your wondrous deeds.
9:3 I will delight and rejoice in you; I will sing hymns to your name, Most High.
9:4 When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish before you.
9:5 II - For you upheld my right and my cause, seated on your throne, judging justly.
9:6 You rebuked the nations, you destroyed the wicked; their name you blotted out for all time.
9:7 The enemies have been ruined forever; you destroyed their cities; their memory has perished.
9:8 III - The LORD rules forever, has set up his throne for judgment.
9:9 It is he who judges the world with justice, who judges the peoples with fairness.
9:10 The LORD is a stronghold for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble.
9:11 Those who know your name trust in you; you never forsake those who seek you, LORD.
9:12 IV - Sing hymns to the LORD enthroned on Zion; proclaim his deeds among the nations!
9:13 For the avenger of bloodshed remembers, does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
9:14 V - Be gracious to me, LORD; see how my foes afflict me! You alone can raise me from the gates of death.
9:15 Then I will declare all your praises, sing joyously of your salvation in the gates of daughter Zion.
9:16 VI - The nations fall into the pit they dig; in the snare they hide, their own foot is caught.
9:17 The LORD is revealed in making judgments: by the deeds they do the wicked are trapped. Higgaion. Selah
9:18 VII - To Sheol the wicked will depart, all the nations that forget God.
9:19 For the needy will never be forgotten, nor will the hope of the afflicted ever fade.
9:20 Arise, LORD, let no mortal prevail; let the nations be judged in your presence.
9:21 Strike them with terror, LORD; show the nations they are only human. Selah
text.DRB:
9:1 Unto the end, for the secrets of the son, the Psalm of David.
9:2 I will confess to thee, o Lord, with all my heart: I will tell all thy marvelous things.
9:3 I will be glad and rejoice in thee: I will sing to thy name, o most High.
9:4 In turning mine enemy backward: they shall be weakened, and perish before my face.
9:5 Because thou hast done my judgment and my cause: thou hast sit upon the throne which judgest justice.
9:6 Thou hast rebuked the Gentiles, and the impious hath perished: their name thou hast destroyed for ever, and for ever and ever.
9:7 The swords of the enemy have failed unto the end: and their cities thou hast destroyed.
9:8 Their memory hath perished with a sound: and our Lord abideth for ever. He hath prepared his throne in judgment:
9:9 and he will judge the whole world in equity, he will judge the people in justice.
9:10 And our Lord is made a refuge for the poor: an helper in opportunities, in tribulation.
9:11 And let them hope in thee that know thy name: because thou hast not forsaken them that seek thee, o Lord.
9:12 Sing to our Lord, which dwelleth in Sion: declare his studies among the Gentiles.
9:13 Because requiring blood he hath remembered them: he hath not forgotten the cry of the poor.
9:14 Have mercy on me, o Lord: See my humiliation by my enemies.
9:15 Which exaltest me from the gates of death, that I may declare all thy praises in the gates of the daughter of Sion.
9:16 I will rejoice in thy salvation: the Gentiles are fastened in the destruction, which they made. In this snare, which they hid, is their foot taken.
9:17 Our Lord shall be known doing judgments: the sinner is taken in the works of his own hands.
9:18 Let sinners be turned into hell, all nations that forget God.
9:19 Because to the end there shall not be oblivion of the poor man: the patience of the poor, shall not perish in the end.
9:20 Arise Lord, let not man be strengthened: Let the Gentiles be judged in thy sight.
9:21 Appoint Lord a Law-giver over them: that the Gentiles may know that they be men.
9:22 Why Lord hast thou departed far off, despisest in opportunities, in tribulation?
9:23 Whilst the impious is proud, the poor is set on fire: they are caught in the counsels which they devise.
9:24 Because the sinner is praised in the desires of his soul: and the unjust man is blessed.
9:25 The sinner hath exasperated our Lord, according to the multitude of his wrath he shall not seek.
9:26 There is no God in his sight: his ways are defiled at all time. Thy judgments are taken away from his face: he shall rule over all his enemies.
9:27 For he hath said in his heart: I will not be moved from generation unto generation without evil.
9:28 Whose mouth is full of cursing, and bitterness, and guile: under his tongue labour and sorrow.
9:29 He sitteth in wait with the rich in secret places, to kill the innocent.
9:30 His eyes look upon the poor: he lieth in wait in secret, as a lion in his den.
9:31 He lieth in wait to take the poor man violently, violently to take the poor man whilst he draweth him. In his snare he will humble himself, and shall fall when he shall have dominion over the poor.
9:32 For he hath said in his heart: God hath forgotten, he hath turned away his face not to see for ever.
9:33 Arise Lord God, let thy hand be exalted: forget not the poor.
9:34 Wherefore hath the impious provoked God? For he hath said in his heart: He will not inquire.
9:35 Thou seest, that thou considerest labour & sorrow: that thou mayest deliver them into thy hands. To thee is the poor left: to the orphan thou wilt be an helper.
9:36 Break the arm of the sinner and malignant: his sin shall be sought, and shall not be found.
9:37 Our Lord shall reign for ever, and for ever and ever: ye Gentiles shall perish from his land.
9:38 Our Lord hath heard the desire of the poor: thy ear hath heard the preparation of their heart.
9:39 To judge for the pupil and the humble, that man add no more to magnify himself upon the earth.

text.NRSV:
10:1 Why do you stand so far off, O Lord, and hide yourself in time of trouble?
10:2 The wicked are so proud that they persecute the poor; let them be caught in the schemes they have devised.
10:3 For the wicked boast of their heart's desire and prosper; they curse the Lord and spurn him.
10:4 The wicked are so proud that they care not for God; their only thought is, 'There is no God.'
10:5 Their ways are prosperous at all times; your judgments are on high out of their sight; they scoff at all their enemies.
10:6 They say in their heart, 'I shall not be shaken; from age to age I shall not come to misfortune.'
10:7 Their mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and fraud; under their tongue are mischief and wrong.
10:8 They lurk in abandoned places and in secret places they murder the innocent; their eyes haunt the needy.
10:9 They lie in wait like a lion in a covert; they lie in wait to catch the helpless; they catch the helpless when they draw them into their net.
10:10 The helpless are crushed and sink down; they fall into the power of their wretchedness.
10:11 They say in their heart, 'God has forgotten; he hides his face; he will never see it.'
10:12 Rise up, O Lord; O God, lift up your hand; do not forget the afflicted.
10:13 Why should the wicked spurn God? Why should they say in their heart, 'You will not call to account'?
10:14 Surely you behold trouble and misery; you see it and take it into your own hand.
10:15 The helpless commits himself to you, for you are the helper of the orphans.
10:16 Break the power of the wicked and evil man; search out his wickedness until you find none.
10:17 The Lord is King for ever and ever; the ungodly shall perish from his land.
10:18 The Lord will hear the desire of the humble; you will strengthen their heart and your ear shall hear;
10:19 To give justice to the orphan and oppressed, so that mere mortals may strike terror no more.
text.Coverdale:
10:1 Why do you stand so far off, O LORD, *
      and hide yourself in time of trouble?
10:2 The wicked arrogantly persecute the poor, *
      but they are trapped in the schemes they have devised.
10:3 The wicked boast of their heart's desire; *
      the covetous curse and revile the LORD.
10:4 The wicked are so proud that they care not for God; *
      their only thought is, "God does not matter."
10:5 Their ways are devious at all times;
      your judgments are far above out of their sight; *
      they defy all their enemies.
10:6 They say in their heart, "I shall not be shaken; *
      no harm shall happen to me ever."
10:7 Their mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and oppression; *
      under their tongue are mischief and wrong.
10:8 They lurk in ambush in public squares
      and in secret places they murder the innocent; *
      they spy out the helpless.
10:9 They lie in wait, like a lion in a covert;
      they lie in wait to seize upon the lowly; *
      they seize the lowly and drag them away in their net.
10:10 The innocent are broken and humbled before them; *
      the helpless fall before their power.
10:11 They say in their heart, "God has forgotten; *
      he hides his face; he will never notice."
10:12 Rise up, O LORD;
      lift up your hand, O God; *
      do not forget the afflicted.
10:13 Why should the wicked revile God? *
      why should they say in their heart, "You do not care"?
10:14 Surely, you behold trouble and misery; *
      you see it and take it into your own hand.
10:15 The helpless commit themselves to you, *
      for you are the helper of orphans.
10:16 Break the power of the wicked and evil; *
      search out their wickedness until you find none.
10:17 The LORD is King for ever and ever; *
      the ungodly shall perish from his land.
10:18 The LORD will hear the desire of the humble; *
      you will strengthen their heart and your ears shall hear;
10:19 To give justice to the orphan and oppressed, *
