text.KJV:
10:1 Why standest thou afarre off, O Lord? why hidest thou thy selfe in times of trouble?
10:2 The wicked in his pride doeth persecute the poore: let them be taken in the deuices that they haue imagined.
10:3 For the wicked boasteth of his hearts desire, and blesseth the couetous, whom the Lord abhorreth.
10:4 The wicked through the pride of his countenance will not seeke after God: God is not in all his thoughts.
10:5 His wayes are alwayes grieuous, thy iudgements are farre aboue out of his sight: as for all his enemies, he puffeth at them.
10:6 He hath said in his heart, I shall not be moued: for I shall neuer be in aduersitie.
10:7 His mouth is full of cursing, and deceit, and fraud: vnder his tongue is mischiefe and vanitie.
10:8 He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doeth he murder the innocent: his eyes are priuily set against the poore.
10:9 He lieth in waite secretly as a lyon in his denne, he lieth in wait to catch the poore: he doth catch the poore when he draweth him into his net.
10:10 He croucheth, and humbleth himselfe, that the poore may fall by his strong ones.
10:11 Hee hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face, hee will neuer see it.
10:12 Arise, O Lord, O God lift vp thine hand: forget not the humble.
10:13 Wherefore doeth the wicked contemne God? he hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require it.
10:14 Thou hast seene it, for thou beholdest mischiefe and spite to requite it with thy hand: the poore committeth himselfe vnto thee, thou art the helper of the fatherlesse.
10:15 Breake thou the arme of the wicked, and the euill man: seeke out his wickednes, till thou finde none.
10:16 The Lord is King for euer and euer: the heathen are perished out of his land.
10:17 Lord, thou hast heard the desire of the humble: thou wilt prepare their heart, thou wilt cause thine eare to heare,
10:18 To iudge the fatherlesse and the oppressed, that the man of the earth may no more oppresse.
text.JPS1985:
10:1 Why, O LORD, do You stand aloof, heedless in times of trouble?
10:2 The wicked in his arrogance hounds the lowly— may they be caught in the schemes they devise!
10:3 The wicked crows about his unbridled lusts; the grasping man reviles and scorns the LORD.
10:4 The wicked, arrogant as he is, in all his scheming [thinks], “He does not call to account; God does not care.”
10:5 His ways prosper at all times; Your judgments are far beyond him; he snorts at all his foes.
10:6 He thinks, “I shall not be shaken, through all time never be in trouble.”
10:7 His mouth is full of oaths, deceit, and fraud; mischief and evil are under his tongue.
10:8 He lurks in outlying places; from a covert he slays the innocent; his eyes spy out the hapless.
10:9 He waits in a covert like a lion in his lair; waits to seize the lowly; he seizes the lowly as he pulls his net shut;
10:10 he stoops, he crouches, and the hapless fall prey to his might.
10:11 He thinks, “God is not mindful, He hides His face, He never looks.”
10:12 Rise, O LORD! Strike at him, O God! Do not forget the lowly.
10:13 Why should the wicked man scorn God, thinking You do not call to account?
10:14 You do look! You take note of mischief and vexation! To requite is in Your power. To You the hapless can entrust himself; You have ever been the orphan’s help.
10:15 O break the power of the wicked and evil man, so that when You look for his wickedness You will find it no more.
10:16 The LORD is king for ever and ever; the nations will perish from His land.
10:17 You will listen to the entreaty of the lowly, O LORD, You will make their hearts firm; You will incline Your ear
10:18 to champion the orphan and the downtrodden, that men who are of the earth tyrannize no more.
text.Grail1963:
1 Lord, why do you stand afar off
and hide yourself in times of distress?
2 The poor man is devoured by the pride of the wicked:
he is caught in the schemes that others have made.
3 For the wicked man boasts of his heart's desires;
the covetous blasphemes and spurns the Lord.

4 In his pride the wicked says: "He will not punish.
There is no God." Such are his thoughts.
5 His path is ever untroubled;
your judgment is far from his mind.
His enemies he regards with contempt.
6 He thinks: "Never shall I falter:
misfortune shall never be my lot."
7 His mouth is full of cursing, guile, oppression;
mischief and deceit are under his tongue.
8 He lies in wait among the reeds;
the innocent he murders in secret.
His eyes are on the watch for the helpless man.
9 He lurks in hiding like a lion in his lair;
he lurks in hiding to seize the poor;
he seizes the poor man and drags him away.
10 He crouches, preparing to spring,
and the helpless fall beneath such strength.
11 He thinks in his heart: "God forgets,
he hides his face, he does not see."
12 Arise then, Lord, lift up your hand!
O God, do not forget the poor!
13 Why should the wicked spurn the Lord
and think in his heart: "God will not punish"?
14 But you have seen the trouble and sorrow,
you note it, you take it in hand.
The helpless trusts himself to you;
for you are the helper of the orphan.
15 Break the power of the wicked and the sinner!
Punish their wickedness till nothing remains!
16 The Lord is king for ever and ever.
The heathen shall perish from the land he rules.
17 Lord, you hear the prayer of the poor;
you strengthen their hearts; you turn your ear
18 to protect the rights of the orphan and oppressed:
so that mortal man may strike terror no more.
text.Rotherham:
10:1 Wherefore, O Yahweh, shouldst thou stand afar off? [Wherefore] hide thyself, in times of destitution?
10:2 In the pride of the lawless one, he hotly pursueth the poor, let them be caught in the plots which they have devised!
10:3 For the lawless one hath boasted over the longing of his soul, and, the robber, hath blasphemed Yahweh.
10:4 The lawless one, in the loftiness of his countenance, will not enquire, God is not in any of his plots:
10:5 His ways are firm at all times, On high, are thy righteous regulations, out of his sight, As for all his adversaries, he puffeth at them:
10:6 He hath said in his heart, I shall not be shaken,—From generation to generation, shall I be in no misfortune.
10:7 With cursing, his mouth is full, and with deceptions and oppression, Under his tongue, are trouble and mischief:
10:8 He abideth in the lurking-place of villages, In the hiding-places, he murdereth the innocent,—His eyes, for the unfortunate, are on the watch:
10:9 He lieth in wait in a hiding-place, like a lion in his covert, He lieth in wait to catch the humbled, He catcheth the humbled, by drawing him into his net:
10:10 He croucheth, he lieth down, then falleth he with his strong claws upon the unfortunate.
10:11 He hath said in his heart, GOD hath forgotten,—He hath veiled his face, Oh he hath never seen!
10:12 Arise! Yahweh! O GOD! raise thy hand,—Do not forget the patient!
10:13 Wherefore hath the lawless one blasphemed God? He hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require [it] !
10:14 Thou hast seen! For, thou, mischief and misery, dost discern, to requite with thine own hand, Unto thee, doth, the unfortunate one, give himself up, To the fatherless, thou thyself, hast become a helper.
10:15 Shatter thou the arm of the lawless one, And, as for the wrongful, wilt thou not enquire for his lawlessness—wilt thou not find [it] ?
10:16 Yahweh, is king, to times age-abiding and beyond, The nations have perished out of his land.
10:17 The longing of the patient, thou hast heard, O Yahweh, Thou wilt establish their heart, Thou wilt make attentive thine ear:
10:18 To vindicate the fatherless and the crushed, A man of the earth, shall, no further, cause terror!
text.NABRE:
10:1 Psalm 10 I - Why, LORD, do you stand afar and pay no heed in times of trouble?
10:2 Arrogant scoundrels pursue the poor; they trap them by their cunning schemes.
10:3 II - The wicked even boast of their greed; these robbers curse and scorn the LORD.
10:4 In their insolence the wicked boast: “God does not care; there is no God.”
10:5 Yet their affairs always succeed; they ignore your judgment on high; they sneer at all who oppose them.
10:6 They say in their hearts, “We will never fall; never will we see misfortune.”
10:7 Their mouths are full of oaths, violence, and lies; discord and evil are under their tongues.
10:8 They wait in ambush near towns; their eyes watch for the helpless to murder the innocent in secret.
10:9 They lurk in ambush like lions in a thicket, hide there to trap the poor, snare them and close the net.
10:10 The helpless are crushed, laid low; they fall into the power of the wicked,
10:11 Who say in their hearts, “God has forgotten, shows no concern, never bothers to look.”
10:12 III - Rise up, LORD! God, lift up your hand! Do not forget the poor!
10:13 Why should the wicked scorn God, say in their hearts, “God does not care”?
10:14 But you do see; you take note of misery and sorrow; you take the matter in hand. To you the helpless can entrust their cause; you are the defender of orphans.
10:15 Break the arm of the wicked and depraved; make them account for their crimes; let none of them survive.
10:16 IV - The LORD is king forever; the nations have vanished from his land.
10:17 You listen, LORD, to the needs of the poor; you strengthen their heart and incline your ear.
10:18 You win justice for the orphaned and oppressed; no one on earth will cause terror again.
text.DRB:
10:1 Unto the end the psalm of David. I Trust in our Lord, how say ye to my soul: Pass over unto the mountain as a sparrow?
10:2 For behold sinners have bent the bow, they have prepared their arrows in the quiver, that they may shoot in the dark, at them that be right of heart.
10:3 For they have destroyed the things, which thou didst perfit: but the just what hath he done?
10:4 Our Lord is in his holy temple, our Lord his seat is in heaven.
10:5 His eyes have respect unto the poor: his eye-lids examine the sons of men.
10:6 Our Lord examineth the just and the impious but he that loveth iniquity, hateth his own soul.
10:7 He shall rain snares upon sinners: fire and brimstone, and blast of storms the portion of their cup.
10:8 Because our Lord is just and hath loved justice: his countenance hath seen equity.

text.NRSV:
11:1 In the Lord have I taken refuge; how then can you say to me, 'Fly like a bird to the hilltop;
11:2 For see, the wicked bend the bow and fit the arrow to the string, to shoot from ambush at the true of heart.
11:3 When the foundations are being destroyed, what can the righteous do?'
11:4 The Lord is in his holy temple; the Lord's throne is in heaven.
11:5 His eyes behold the inhabited world; his gazing examines humankind.
11:6 The Lord examines the righteous and the wicked; he abhors those who love violence.
11:7 He shall rain coals of fire and brimstone upon the wicked; a scorching wind shall be their lot to drink.
11:8 For the Lord is righteous; he delights in righteous deeds; and the upright shall see his face.
text.Coverdale:
11:1 In the LORD have I taken refuge; *
      how then can you say to me,
      "Fly away like a bird to the hilltop;
11:2 For see how the wicked bend the bow
      and fit their arrows to the string, *
      to shoot from ambush at the true of heart.
11:3 When the foundations are being destroyed, *
      what can the righteous do?"
11:4 The LORD is in his holy temple; *
      the LORD's throne is in heaven.
11:5 His eyes behold the inhabited world; *
      his piercing eye weighs our worth.
11:6 The LORD weighs the righteous as well as the wicked, *
      but those who delight in violence he abhors.
11:7 Upon the wicked he shall rain coals of fire and burning sulphur; *
      a scorching wind shall be their lot.
11:8 For the LORD is righteous;
      he delights in righteous deeds; *
      and the just shall see his face.
text.KJV:
11:1 In the Lord put I my trust: how say yee to my soule, Flee as a bird to your mountaine?
11:2 For loe, the wicked bende their bow, they make ready their arrow vpon the string: that they may priuily shoote at the vpright in heart.
11:3 If the foundations bee destroyed: what can the righteous doe?
11:4 The Lord is in his holy Temple, the Lords Throne is in heauen: his eyes beholde, his eye lids trie the children of men.
11:5 The Lord trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loueth violence, his soule hateth.
11:6 Upon the wicked hee shall raine snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest: this shall be the portion of their cup.
11:7 For the righteous Lord loueth righteousnesse: his countenance doeth behold the vpright.
text.JPS1985:
11:1 For the leader. Of David. In the LORD I take refuge; how can you say to me, “Take to the hills like a bird!
11:2 For see, the wicked bend the bow, they set their arrow on the string to shoot from the shadows at the upright.
11:3 When the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous man do? ”
11:4 The LORD is in His holy palace; the LORD —His throne is in heaven; His eyes behold, His gaze searches mankind.
11:5 The LORD seeks out the righteous man, but loathes the wicked one who loves injustice.
11:6 He will rain down upon the wicked blazing coals and sulfur; a scorching wind shall be their lot.
11:7 For the LORD is righteous; He loves righteous deeds; the upright shall behold His face.
text.Grail1963:
1 In the Lord I have taken my refuge.
How can you say to my soul:
"Fly like a bird to its mountain.
2 See the wicked bracing their bow;
they are fixing their arrows on the string
to shoot upright men in the dark.
3 Foundations once destroyed, what can the just do?"

4 The Lord is in his holy temple,
the Lord, whose throne is in heaven.
His eyes look down on the world;
his gaze tests mortal men.
5 The Lord tests the just and the wicked;
the lover of violence he hates.
6 He sends fire and brimstone on the wicked;
he sends a scorching wind as their lot.
7 The Lord is just and loves justice;
the upright shall see his face.
text.Rotherham:
11:1 In Yahweh, have I sought refuge. How can ye say to my soul, Flee to a mountain like a little bird;
11:2 For lo! the lawless, bend the bow They have fixed their arrow upon the string, To shoot in the darkness at the upright in heart:
11:3 When the pillars are overthrown, What could, a righteous man, do?
11:4 Yahweh, is in his holy temple As for Yahweh, in the heavens, is his throne, His eyes, behold—His eyelashes test the sons of men.
11:5 Yahweh, putteth, the righteous, to the test,—But the lawless one and the lover of violence, his soul doth hate.
11:6 He will rain upon the lawless live-coals, Fire and brimstone and a burning wind, are the portion of their cup.
11:7 For righteous is Yahweh Righteousness, he loveth, the upright, shall behold his face.
text.NABRE:
11:1 Psalm 11 Confidence in the Presence of God - For the leader. Of David. I - In the LORD I take refuge; how can you say to me, “Flee like a bird to the mountains!
11:2 See how the wicked string their bows, fit their arrows to the string to shoot from the shadows at the upright of heart.
11:3 If foundations are destroyed, what can the just one do?”
11:4 II - The LORD is in his holy temple; the LORD’s throne is in heaven. God’s eyes keep careful watch; they test the children of Adam.
11:5 The LORD tests the righteous and the wicked, hates those who love violence,
11:6 And rains upon the wicked fiery coals and brimstone, a scorching wind their allotted cup.
11:7 The LORD is just and loves just deeds; the upright will see his face.
text.DRB:
11:1 Unto the end for the octave, the Psalm of David.
11:2 Save me Lord, because the holy hath failed because verities are diminished from among the children of men.
11:3 They have spoken vain things every one to his neighbour, deceitful lips, they have spoken in heart and heart.
11:4 Our Lord destroy all deceitful lips, & the tongue that speaketh great things.
11:5 Which have said: We will magnify our tongue, our lips are of us, who is our Lord?
11:6 For the misery of the needy, and mourning of the poor, now will I arise, saith our Lord: I will put in a salvation, I will do confidently in him.
11:7 Words of our Lord be chaste words: silver examined by fire, tried from the earth, purged seven fold.
11:8 Thou Lord wilt preserve us: and keep us from this generation for ever.
11:9 The impious walk round about: according to thy highness thou hast multiplied the children of men.

text.NRSV:
12:1 Help me, Lord, for there is no godly one left; the faithful have vanished from among us.
12:2 Everyone speaks falsely with his neighbor; with a smooth tongue they speak from a double heart.
12:3 Oh, that the Lord would cut off all smooth tongues, and close the lips that utter proud boasts!
12:4 Those who say, 'With our tongue will we prevail; our lips are our own: who is lord over us?'
12:5 'Because the needy are oppressed, and the poor cry out in misery, I will rise up,' says the Lord, 'and give them the help they long for.'
12:6 The words of the Lord are pure words, like silver refined from ore and purified seven times in the fire.
12:7 O Lord, protect us from this generation for ever.
12:8 The wicked prowl on every side, and that which is worthless is highly prized by everyone.

      the faithful have vanished from among us.
12:2 Everyone speaks falsely with his neighbor; *
      with a smooth tongue they speak from a double heart.
12:3 Oh, that the LORD would cut off all smooth tongues, *
      and close the lips that utter proud boasts!
12:4 Those who say, "With our tongue will we prevail; *
      our lips are our own; who is lord over us?"
12:5 "Because the needy are oppressed,
      and the poor cry out in misery, *
      I will rise up," says the LORD,
      "and give them the help they long for."
12:6 The words of the LORD are pure words, *
      like silver refined from ore

text.KJV:
12:1 Helpe Lord, for the godly man ceaseth; for the faithfull faile from among the children of men.
12:2 They speake vanitie euery one with his neighbour: with flattering lips, and with a double heart do they speake.
12:3 The Lord shall cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things.
12:4 Who haue said, with our tongue wil we preuaile, our lips are our owne: who is Lord ouer vs?
12:5 For the oppression of the poore, for the sighing of the needy, now will I arise (saith the Lord,) I will set him in safetie from him that puffeth at him.
12:6 The wordes of the Lord are pure wordes: as siluer tried in a fornace of earth purified seuen times.
12:7 Thou shalt keepe them, (O Lord,) thou shalt preserue them, from this generation for euer.
12:8 The wicked walke on euery side, when the vilest men are exalted.
text.JPS1985:
12:1 For the leader; on the sheminith. A psalm of David.
12:2 Help, O LORD! For the faithful are no more; the loyal have vanished from among men.
12:3 Men speak lies to one another; their speech is smooth; they talk with duplicity.
12:4 May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, every tongue that speaks arrogance.
12:5 They say, “By our tongues we shall prevail; with lips such as ours, who can be our master? ”
12:6 “Because of the groans of the plundered poor and needy, I will now act, ” says the LORD. “I will give help, ” He affirms to him.
12:7 The words of the LORD are pure words, silver purged in an earthen crucible, refined sevenfold.
12:8 You, O LORD, will keep them, guarding each from this age evermore.
12:9 On every side the wicked roam when baseness is exalted among men.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Help, O Lord, for good men have vanished;
truth has gone from the sons of men.
.3 Falsehood they speak one to another,
with lying lips, with a false heart.
.4 May the Lord destroy all lying lips,
the tongue that speaks highsounding words,
.5 those who say: "Our tongue is our strength;
our lips are our own, who is our master?"
.6 "For the poor who are oppressed and the needy who groan
I myself will arise," says the Lord,
"I will grant them the salvation for which they thirst."
.7 The words of the Lord are words without alloy,
silver from the furnace, seven times refined.
.8 It is you, O Lord, who will take us in your care
and protect us for ever from this generation.
.9 See how the wicked prowl on every side,
while the worthless are prized highly by the sons of men.
text.Rotherham:
12:1 O save Yahweh, for the man of lovingkindness, is no more, for the faithful, have vanished, from among the sons of men.
12:2 Deception, speak they, every one with his neighbour,—with lips uttering smooth things—with a heart and a heart, do they speak.
12:3 May Yahweh cut off All the lips that utter smooth things,—the tongue that speaketh swelling words;
12:4 Them who say—With our tongue, will we prevail, our lips, are our own, who is our master?
12:5 Because of violence done to the poor, because of the crying of the needy, Now, will I arise! O may Yahweh say,—I will place [him] in safety—let him puff at him!
12:6 The words of Yahweh, are words, that are pure, silver refined in a crucible of earth, purified seven times!
12:7 Thou, O Yahweh, wilt keep them,—Thou wilt guard him, from this generation unto times age-abiding.
12:8 On every side, the lawless, march about,—when worthlessness is exalted by the sons of men.
text.NABRE:
12:1 Psalm 12 Prayer Against Evil Tongues - For the leader; “upon the eighth.” A psalm of David.
12:2 I - Help, LORD, for no one loyal remains; the faithful have vanished from the children of men.
12:3 They tell lies to one another, speak with deceiving lips and a double heart.
12:4 II - May the LORD cut off all deceiving lips, and every boastful tongue,
12:5 Those who say, “By our tongues we prevail; when our lips speak, who can lord it over us?”
12:6 III - “Because they rob the weak, and the needy groan, I will now arise,” says the LORD; “I will grant safety to whoever longs for it.”
12:7 IV - The promises of the LORD are sure, silver refined in a crucible, silver purified seven times.
12:8 You, O LORD, protect us always; preserve us from this generation.
12:9 On every side the wicked roam; the shameless are extolled by the children of men.
text.DRB:
12:1 Unto the end, the Psalm of David. How long, o Lord wilt thou forget me unto the end? How long doest thou turn away thy face from me?
12:2 How long shall I put counsels in my soul, sorrow in my heart by day?
12:3 How long shall mine enemies be exalted over me? Regard and hear me, o Lord my God.
12:4 Illuminate mine eyes that I sleep not in death at any time: lest sometime mine enemy say: I have prevailed against him.
12:5 They that trouble me, will rejoice if I be moved: but I have hoped in thy mercy.
12:6 My heart shall rejoice in thy salvation: I will sing to our Lord, which giveth me good things: and I will sing to the name of our Lord most high.

text.NRSV:
13:1 How long, O Lord? will you forget me for ever? how long will you hide your face from me?
13:2 How long shall I have perplexity in my mind, and grief in my heart, day after day? how long shall my enemy triumph over me?
13:3 Look upon me and answer me, O Lord my God; give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death;
13:4 Lest my enemy say, 'I have prevailed over him,' and my foes rejoice that I have fallen.
13:5 But I put my trust in your mercy; my heart is joyful because of your saving help.
13:6 I will sing to the Lord, for he has dealt with me richly; I will praise the Name of the Lord Most High.
text.Coverdale:

      will you forget me for ever? *

13:2 How long shall I have perplexity in my mind,
      and grief in my heart, day after after day? *
      how long shall my enemy triumph over me?
13:3 Look upon me and answer me, O LORD my God; *

13:5 But I put my trust in your mercy; *

13:6 I will sing to the LORD, for he has dealt with me richly; *

text.KJV:
13:1 How long wilt thou forget mee (O Lord) for euer? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
13:2 How long shall I take counsel in my soule, hauing sorrow in my heart dayly? how long shall mine enemie be exalted ouer me?
13:3 Consider and heare me, O Lord my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleepe of death.
13:4 Least mine enimie say, I haue preuailed against him: and those that trouble mee, reioyce, when I am moued.
13:5 But I haue trusted in thy mercy, my heart shall reioyce in thy saluation.
13:6 I will sing vnto the Lord, because hee hath dealt bountifully with mee.
text.JPS1985:
13:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
13:2 How long, O LORD; will You ignore me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
13:3 How long will I have cares on my mind, grief in my heart all day? How long will my enemy have the upper hand?
13:4 Look at me, answer me, O LORD, my God! Restore the luster to my eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
13:5 lest my enemy say, “I have overcome him, ” my foes exult when I totter.
13:6 But I trust in Your faithfulness, my heart will exult in Your deliverance. I will sing to the LORD, for He has been good to me.
text.Grail1963:
.2 How long, O Lord, will you forget me?
How long will you hide your face?
.3 How long must I bear grief in my soul,
this sorrow in my heart day and night?
How long shall my enemy prevail?
.4 Look at me, answer me, Lord my God!
Give light to my eyes lest I fall asleep in death,
.5 lest my enemy say: "I have overcome him";
lest my foes rejoice to see my fall.
.6 As for me, I trust in your merciful love.
Let my heart rejoice in your saving help:

text.Rotherham:
13:1 How long, O Yahweh, wilt thou wholly forget me? How long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
13:2 How long shall I lay up cares within my soul, sorrow in my heart, day by day? How long shall mine enemy lift himself up over me?
13:3 Have regard! answer me O Yahweh my God,—Light up mine eyes, lest I sleep on into death:
13:4 Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed over him! and, mine adversaries, exult, that I totter.
13:5 But, I, in thy lovingkindness, have put my trust, My heart shall exult in thy salvation:
13:6 I will sing to Yahweh, for he hath dealt beautifully with me.
text.NABRE:
13:1 Psalm 13 Prayer for Help - For the leader. A psalm of David.
13:2 I - How long, LORD? Will you utterly forget me? How long will you hide your face from me?
13:3 How long must I carry sorrow in my soul, grief in my heart day after day? How long will my enemy triumph over me?
13:4 II - Look upon me, answer me, LORD, my God! Give light to my eyes lest I sleep in death,
13:5 Lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed,” lest my foes rejoice at my downfall.
13:6 III - But I trust in your mercy. Grant my heart joy in your salvation, I will sing to the LORD, for he has dealt bountifully with me!
text.DRB:
13:1 Unto the end, a Psalm of David. The fool hath said in his heart: There is no God. They are corrupt, and are become abominable in their studies: there is not that doth good no not one.
13:2 Our Lord hath looked forth from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there be that understandeth, and seeketh after God.
13:3 All have declined, they are become unprofitable together: there is not that doth good, no not one. Their throat is an open sepulchre, with their tongues they did deceitfully, the poison of asps under their lips. Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness: their feet swift to shed blood. Destruction and infelicity in their ways, and the way of peace they have not known: there is no fear of God before their eyes.
13:4 Shall not all they know that work iniquity, that devour my people as food of bread?
13:5 They have not invocated our Lord, there have they trembled for fear, where no fear was.
13:6 Because our Lord is in the just generation, you have confounded the counsel of the poor man: because our Lord is his hope.
13:7 Who will give from Sion the salvation of Israel? When our Lord shall have turned away the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

text.NRSV:
14:1 The fool has said in his heart, 'There is no God.' All are corrupt and commit abominable acts; there is none who does any good.
14:2 The Lord looks down from heaven upon us all, to see if there is any who is wise, if there is one who seeks after God.
14:3 Everyone has proved faithless; all alike have turned bad; there is none who does good; no, not one.
14:4 Have they no knowledge, all those evildoers who eat up my people like bread and do not call upon the Lord?
14:5 See how they tremble with fear, because God is in the company of the righteous.
14:6 Their aim is to confound the plans of the afflicted, but the Lord is their refuge.
14:7 Oh, that Israel's deliverance would come out of Zion! when the Lord restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice and Israel be glad.

      All are corrupt and commit abominable acts;
      there is none who does any good.
14:2 The LORD looks down from heaven upon us all, *
      to see if there is any who is wise,

14:3 Every one has proved faithless;
      all alike have turned bad; *

text.KJV:
14:1 The foole hath sayd in his heart, There is no God: they are corrupt, they haue done abominable workes, there is none that doeth good.
14:2 The Lord looked downe from heauen vpon the children of men; to see if there were any that did vnderstand and seeke God.
14:3 They are all gone aside, they are all together become filthy: there is none that doeth good, no not one.
14:4 Haue all the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eate vp my people as they eate bread, and call not vpon the Lord.
14:5 There were they in great feare; for God is in the generation of the righteous.
14:6 You haue shamed the counsell of the poore; because the Lord is his refuge.
14:7 O that the saluation of Israel were come out of Sion! when the Lord bringeth backe the captiuitie of his people, Iacob shall reioyce, and Israel shalbe glad.
text.JPS1985:
14:1 For the leader. Of David. The benighted man thinks, “God does not care.” Man’s deeds are corrupt and loathsome; no one does good.
14:2 The LORD looks down from heaven on mankind to find a man of understanding, a man mindful of God.
14:3 All have turned bad, altogether foul; there is none who does good, not even one.
14:4 Are they so witless, all those evildoers, who devour my people as they devour food, and do not invoke the LORD?
14:5 There they will be seized with fright, for God is present in the circle of the righteous.
14:6 You may set at naught the counsel of the lowly, but the LORD is his refuge.
14:7 O that the deliverance of Israel might come from Zion! When the LORD restores the fortunes of His people, Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.
text.Grail1963:
1 The fool has said in his heart:
"There is no God above."
Their deeds are corrupt, depraved;
not a good man is left.
2 From heaven the Lord looks down
on the sons of men
to see if any are wise,
if any seek God.
3 All have left the right path,
depraved, every one;
there is not a good man left,
no, not even one.
4 Will the evildoers not understand?
They eat up my people
as though they were eating bread:
they never pray to the Lord.
5 See how they tremble with fear
without cause for fear:
for God is with the just.
6 You may mock the poor man's hope,
but his refuge is the Lord.
7 O that Israel's salvation might come from Zion!
When the Lord delivers his people from bondage,
then Jacob will be glad and Israel rejoice.
text.Rotherham:
14:1 The impious hath said in his heart—There is no God! They have acted corruptly, they have done an abominable deed, there is none that doeth good.
14:2 Yahweh, out of the heavens, looked down over the sons of men,—to see whether there was one that showed wisdom, enquiring after God:—
14:3 The whole, have turned aside, Together have they become tainted,—There is none that doeth good, not so much as, one!
14:4 Are all the workers of iniquity, without knowledge? devouring my people [as] they devour food! Upon Yahweh, have they not called.
14:5 There have they been in great dread, because, God, is in the circle of the righteous man.
14:6 The purpose of the poor, ye would put to shame, because, Yahweh, is his refuge.
14:7 Oh that, out of Zion, were granted the salvation of Israel! When Yahweh bringeth back the captives of his people, Jacob, shall exult, Israel, be glad.
text.NABRE:
14:1 Psalm 14 A Lament over Widespread Corruption - For the leader. Of David. I - The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” Their deeds are loathsome and corrupt; not one does what is good.
14:2 The LORD looks down from heaven upon the children of men, To see if even one is wise, if even one seeks God.
14:3 All have gone astray; all alike are perverse. Not one does what is good, not even one.
14:4 II - Will these evildoers never learn? They devour my people as they devour bread; they do not call upon the LORD.
14:5 They have good reason, then, to fear; God is with the company of the just.
14:6 They would crush the hopes of the poor, but the poor have the LORD as their refuge.
14:7 III - Oh, that from Zion might come the salvation of Israel! Jacob would rejoice, and Israel be glad when the LORD restores his people!
text.DRB:
14:1 The Psalm of David. Lord who shall dwell in thy tabernacle? Or who shall rest in thy holy hill?
14:2 He that walketh without spot, and worketh justice.
14:3 He that speaketh truth in his heart, that hath not done guile in his tongue. Nor hath done evil to his neighbour, and hath not taken reproach against his neighbour.
14:4 The malignant is brought to nothing in his sight: but them that fear our Lord, he glorifieth. He that sweareth to his neighbour, and deceiveth not,
14:5 That hath not given his money to usury, and hath not taken gifts upon the innocent, He that doth these things, shall not be moved for ever.

text.NRSV:
15:1 Lord, who may dwell in your tabernacle? Who may abide upon your holy hill?
15:2 Whoever leads a blameless life and does what is right, who speaks the truth from his heart.
15:3 There is no guile upon his tongue; he does no evil to his friend; he does not heap contempt upon his neighbor.
15:4 In his sight the wicked is rejected, but he honors those who fear the Lord.
15:5 He has sworn to do no wrong and does not take back his word.
15:6 He does not give his money in hope of gain, nor does he take a bribe against the innocent.
15:7 Whoever does these things shall never be overthrown.
text.Coverdale:
15:1 LORD, who may dwell in your tabernacle? *
      who may abide upon your holy hill?
15:2 Whoever leads a blameless life and does what is right, *
      who speaks the truth from his heart.
15:3 There is no guile upon his tongue;

text.KJV:
15:1 Lord, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?
15:2 Hee that walketh vprightly, and worketh righteousnesse, and speaketh the trueth in his heart.
15:3 Hee that backbiteth not with his tongue, nor doth euill to his neighbour, nor taketh vp a reproach against his neighbour.
15:4 In whose eies a vile person is contemned; but he honoureth them that feare the Lord: he that sweareth to his owne hurt, and changeth not.
15:5 He that putteth not out his money to vsury, nor taketh reward against the innocent: he that doth these things, shall neuer be moued.
text.DRB:
15:1 The inscription of the title to David himself. Preserve me, o Lord, because I have hoped in thee.
15:2 I have said to our Lord: Thou art my God, because thou needest not my goods.
15:3 To the Saints that are in his land, he hath made all my wills marvelous in them.
15:4 Their infirmities were multiplied: afterward they made haste. I will not assemble their conventicles of blood: neither will I be mindful of their names by my lips.
15:5 Our Lord the portion of mine inheritance, and of my cup: thou art he, that will restore mine inheritance unto me.
15:6 Cords are fallen to me in goodly places: for mine inheritance is goodly unto me.
15:7 I will bless our Lord, who hath given me understanding: moreover also even till night my veins have rebuked me.
15:8 I foresaw our Lord in my sight always: because he is at my right hand, that I be not moved.
15:9 For this thing my heart hath been glad, and my tongue hath rejoiced: moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope.
15:10 Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell: neither wilt give thy holy one to see corruption . Thou hast made the ways of life known to me, thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance: delectations on thy right hand, even to the end.

text.NRSV:
16:1 Protect me, O God, for I take refuge in you; I have said to the Lord, 'You are my Lord, my good above all other.'
16:2 All my delight is upon the godly who are in the land, upon those who are noble among the people.
16:3 But those who run after other gods shall have their troubles multiplied.
16:4 Their libations of blood I will not offer, nor take the names of their gods upon my lips.
16:5 O Lord, you are my portion and my cup; it is you who uphold my lot.
16:6 My boundaries enclose a pleasant land; indeed, I have a goodly heritage.
16:7 I will bless the Lord who gives me counsel; my heart teaches me, night after night.
16:8 I have set the Lord always before me; because he is at my right hand, I shall not fall.
16:9 My heart, therefore, is glad, and my spirit rejoices; my body also shall rest in hope.
16:10 For you will not abandon me to the grave, nor let your holy one see the Pit.
16:11 You will show me the path of life; in your presence there is fullness of joy, and in your right hand are pleasures for evermore.

text.KJV:
16:1 Preserue me, O God: for in thee doe I put my trust.
16:2 O my soule, thou hast sayd vnto the Lord, Thou art my Lord: my goodnes extendeth not to thee:
16:3 But to the Saints, that are in the earth, and to the excellent, in whom is all my delight.
16:4 Their sorrowes shalbe multiplied, that hasten after another God: their drinke offerings of blood will I not offer, nor take vp their names into my lippes.
16:5 The Lord is the portion of mine inheritance, and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot.
16:6 The lines are fallen vnto mee in pleasant places; yea, I haue a goodly heritage.
16:7 I will blesse the Lord, who hath giuen me counsell: my reines also instruct me in the night seasons.
16:8 I haue set the Lord alwaies before me: because hee is at my right hand, I shall not be moued.
16:9 Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory reioyceth: my flesh also shall rest in hope.
16:10 For thou wilt not leaue my soule in hell; neither wilt thou suffer thine holy one to see corruption.
16:11 Thou wilt shewe me the path of life: in thy presence is fulnesse of ioy, at thy right hand there are pleasures for euermore.
text.JPS1985:
16:1 A michtam of David. Protect me, O God, for I seek refuge in You.
16:2 I say to the LORD, “You are my Lord, my benefactor; there is none above You.”
16:3 As to the holy and mighty ones that are in the land, my whole desire concerning them is that
16:4 those who espouse another [god] may have many sorrows! I will have no part of their bloody libations; their names will not pass my lips.
16:5 The LORD is my allotted share and portion; You control my fate.
16:6 Delightful country has fallen to my lot; lovely indeed is my estate.
16:7 I bless the LORD who has guided me; my conscience admonishes me at night.
16:8 I am ever mindful of the LORD’s presence; He is at my right hand; I shall never be shaken.
16:9 So my heart rejoices, my whole being exults, and my body rests secure.
16:10 For You will not abandon me to Sheol, or let Your faithful one see the Pit.
16:11 You will teach me the path of life. In Your presence is perfect joy; delights are ever in Your right hand.
text.Grail1963:
1 Preserve me, God, I take refuge in you.
2 I say to the Lord: "You are my God.
My happiness lies in you alone."
3 He has put into my heart a marvelous love
for the faithful ones who dwell in his land.
4 Those who choose other gods increase their sorrows.
Never will I offer their offerings of blood.
Never will I take their name upon my lips.
5 O Lord, it is you who are my portion and cup;
it is you yourself who are my prize.
6 The lot marked out for me is my delight:
welcome indeed the heritage that falls to me!
7 I will bless the Lord who gives me counsel,
who even at night directs my heart.
8 I keep the Lord ever in my sight:
since he is at my right hand, I shall stand firm.
9 And so my heart rejoices, my soul is glad;
even my body shall rest in safety.
10 For you will not leave my soul among the dead,
nor let your beloved know decay.
11 You will show me the path of life,
the fullness of joy in your presence,
at your right hand happiness for ever.
text.Rotherham:
16:1 Preserve me, O GOD, for I have sought refuge in thee.
16:2 Thou hast said unto Yahweh, My Lord, art thou! My goodness, mounteth not unto thee.
16:3 It belongeth unto the holy ones, whom, in his own land, Yahweh, ennobleth, In whom is all his delight
16:4 They multiply their idols—another, have they purchased! I will not pour out their drink-offerings of blood, nor will I take up their names on my lips.
16:5 Yahweh, is mine allotted portion and my cup, Thou, art maintaining my lot.
16:6 The lines, have fallen unto me, in pleasant places, Verily an inheritance that delighteth me.
16:7 I will bless Yahweh, who hath counselled me,—Surely, by night, shall mine impulses admonish me.
16:8 I have set Yahweh before me continually, because he is on my right hand, I shall not be shaken!
16:9 Therefore, hath my heart rejoiced, and mine honour exulted, Yea, my flesh, shall settle down securely;
16:10 For thou wilt not abandon my soul to hades, neither wilt thou suffer thy man of lovingkindness, to see corruption:
16:11 Thou wilt cause me to know, the path of life,—Fulness of joys before thee, Pleasures at thy right hand evermore.
text.NABRE:
16:1 Psalm 16 God the Supreme Good - A miktam of David. I - Keep me safe, O God; in you I take refuge.
16:2 I say to the LORD, you are my Lord, you are my only good.
16:3 As for the holy ones who are in the land, they are noble, in whom is all my delight.
16:4 They multiply their sorrows who court other gods. Blood libations to them I will not pour out, nor will I take their names upon my lips.
16:5 LORD, my allotted portion and my cup, you have made my destiny secure.
16:6 Pleasant places were measured out for me; fair to me indeed is my inheritance.
16:7 II - I bless the LORD who counsels me; even at night my heart exhorts me.
16:8 I keep the LORD always before me; with him at my right hand, I shall never be shaken.
16:9 Therefore my heart is glad, my soul rejoices; my body also dwells secure,
16:10 For you will not abandon my soul to Sheol, nor let your devout one see the pit.
16:11 You will show me the path to life, abounding joy in your presence, the delights at your right hand forever.
text.DRB:
16:1 The prayer of David. Hear, o Lord my justice: attend my petition. With thine ears hear my prayer, not in deceitful lips.
16:2 From thy countenance let my judgment proceed: let thine eyes see equities.
16:3 Thou hast proved my heart, and visited it by night: by fire thou hast examined me, and there is no iniquity found in me.
16:4 That my mouth speak not the works of men: for the words of thy lips I have kept the hard ways.
16:5 Perfit my passes in thy paths, that my steps be not moved.
16:6 I have cried, because thou hast heard me, o God: incline thine ear to me, and hear my words.
16:7 Make thy mercies marvelous, which savest them that hope in thee.
16:8 From them that resist thy right hand keep me, as the apple of the eye. Under the shadow of thy wings protect me:
16:9 from the face of the impious, that have afflicted me. Mine enemies have compassed my soul,
16:10 they have shut up their fat: their mouth hath spoken pride.
16:11 Casting me forth now have they compassed me: they have set their eyes to bend them unto the earth.
16:12 They have taken me as a lion ready to the prey: and as a lion's whelp dwelling in hid places.
16:13 Arise Lord, prevent him, and supplant him: deliver my soul from the impious, thy sword
16:14 from the enemies of thy hand. Lord from a few out of the land divide them, in their life: their belly is filled of thy secrets. They are filled with children: and they have left their remnants to their little ones.
16:15 But I in justice shall appear to thy sight: I shall be filled when thy glory shall appear?

text.NRSV:
17:1 Hear my plea of innocence, O Lord; give heed to my cry; listen to my prayer, which does not come from lying lips.
17:2 Let my vindication come forth from your presence; let your eyes be fixed on justice.
17:3 Weigh my heart, summon me by night, melt me down; you will find no impurity in me; I give no offense with my mouth as others do; I have heeded the words of your lips.
17:4 My footsteps hold fast to the ways of your law; in your paths my feet shall not stumble.
17:5 I call upon you, O God, for you will answer me; incline your ear to me and hear my words.
17:6 Show me your marvelous loving-kindness, O Savior of those who take refuge at your right hand from those who rise up against them.
17:7 Keep me as the apple of your eye; hide me under the shadow of your wings,
17:8 From the wicked who assault me, from my deadly enemies who surround me.
17:9 They have closed their heart to pity, and their mouth speaks proud things.
17:10 They press me hard, now they surround me, watching how they may cast me to the ground,
17:11 Like a lion, greedy for its prey, and like a young lion lurking in secret places.
17:12 Rise up, O Lord; confront them and cast them down; deliver me from the wicked by your sword.
17:13 Deliver me, O Lord, by your hand from those whose portion in life is this world;
17:14 Whose bellies you fill with your treasure, who are sated with children and leave their wealth to their little ones.
17:15 As for me, I shall see your face in righteousness; when I awake and behold your likeness, I shall be satisfied.

17:11 They press me hard,
      now they surround me, *
      watching how they may cast me to the ground,
17:12 Like a lion, greedy for its prey, *
      and like a young lion lurking in secret places.
17:13 Rise up, O LORD, confront them and bring them down; *
      deliver me from the wicked by your sword.
17:14 Deliver me, O LORD, by your hand *
      from those whose portion in life is this world;
17:15 Whose bellies you fill with your treasure, *
      who are well supplied with children
      and leave their wealth to their little ones.
17:16 But at my vindication I shall see your face; *
      when I awake, I shall be satisfied, beholding your likeness.
text.KJV:
17:1 Heare the right, O Lord, attend vnto my crie, giue eare vnto my prayer, that goeth not out of fained lips.
17:2 Let my sentence come forth from thy presence: let thine eyes beholde the things that are equall.
17:3 Thou hast prooued mine heart, thou hast visited me in the night, thou hast tried me, and shalt find nothing: I am purposed that my mouth shall not transgresse.
17:4 Concerning the workes of men, by the word of thy lips, I haue kept me from the paths of the destroyer.
17:5 Hold vp my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
17:6 I haue called vpon thee, for thou wilt heare me, O God: incline thine eare vnto me, and heare my speach.
17:7 Shewe thy marueilous louing kindnesse, O thou that sauest by thy right hand, them which put their trust in thee, frō those that rise vp against them.
17:8 Keepe me as the apple of the eye: hide mee vnder the shadowe of thy wings,
17:9 From the wicked that oppresse me, from my deadly enemies, who compasse me about.
17:10 They are inclosed in their owne fat: with their mouth they speake proudly.
17:11 They haue now compassed vs in our steps: they haue set their eyes bowing downe to the earth:
17:12 Like as a lyon that is greedie of his pray, and as it were a yong lyon lurking in secret places.
17:13 Arise, O Lord, disappoint him, cast him downe: deliuer my soule from the wicked, which is thy sword:
17:14 From men which are thy hand, O Lord, from men of the world, which haue their portion in this life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid treasure: They are full of children, and leaue the rest of their substance to their babes.
17:15 As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousnesse: I shall bee satisfied, when I awake, with thy likenesse.
text.JPS1985:
17:1 A prayer of David. Hear, O LORD, what is just; heed my cry, give ear to my prayer, uttered without guile.
17:2 My vindication will come from You; Your eyes will behold what is right.
17:3 You have visited me at night, probed my mind, You have tested me and found nothing amiss; I determined that my mouth should not transgress.
17:4 As for man’s dealings, in accord with the command of Your lips, I have kept in view the fate of the lawless.
17:5 My feet have held to Your paths; my legs have not given way.
17:6 I call on You; You will answer me, God; turn Your ear to me, hear what I say.
17:7 Display Your faithfulness in wondrous deeds, You who deliver with Your right hand those who seek refuge from assailants.
17:8 Guard me like the apple of Your eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings
17:9 from the wicked who despoil me, my mortal enemies who encircle me.
17:10 Their hearts are closed to pity; they mouth arrogance;
17:11 now they hem in our feet on every side; they set their eyes roaming over the land.
17:12 He is like a lion eager for prey, a king of beasts lying in wait.
17:13 Rise, O LORD! Go forth to meet him. Bring him down; rescue me from the wicked with Your sword,
17:14 from men, O LORD, with Your hand, from men whose share in life is fleeting. But as to Your treasured ones, fill their bellies. Their sons too shall be satisfied, and have something to leave over for their young.
17:15 Then I, justified, will behold Your face; awake, I am filled with the vision of You.
text.Grail1963:
1 Lord, hear a cause that is just,
pay heed to my cry.
Turn your ear to my prayer:
no deceit is on my lips.
2 From you may my judgment come forth.
Your eyes discern the truth.
3 You search my heart, you visit me by night.
You test me and you find in me no wrong.
/4 My words are not sinful as are men's words.
I kept from violence because of your word,
5 I kept my feet firmly in your paths;
there was no faltering in my steps.
6 I am here and I call, you will hear me, O God.
Turn your ear to me; hear my words.
7 Display your great love, you whose right hand saves

your friends from those who rebel against them.
8 Guard me as the apple of your eye.
Hide me in the shadow of your wings
9 from the violent attack of the wicked.
My foes encircle me with deadly intent.
10 Their hearts tight shut, their mouths speak proudly.
11 They advance against me, and now they surround me.
Their eyes are watching to strike me to the ground,
12 as though they were lions ready to claw
or like some young lion crouched in hiding.
13 Lord, arise, confront them, strike them down!
Let your sword rescue my soul from the wicked;
14 let your hand, O Lord, rescue me from men,
from men whose reward is in this present life.
You give them their fill of your treasures;
they rejoice in abundance of offspring
and leave their wealth to their children.
15 As for me, in my justice I shall see your face
and be filled, when I awake, with the sight of your glory.
text.Rotherham:
17:1 Hear, O Yahweh, the right, Attend to my loud cry, Give ear unto my prayer, on lips that would not deceive:
17:2 From before thee, let my sentence come forth, Thine eyes, behold with equity.
17:3 Thou hast tested my heart, hast made inspection by night, hast refined me until thou couldst find nothing, Had I devised evil, my mouth should not have transgressed:
17:4 As for the workings of men, By the word of thy lips, have, I, taken heed of the paths of the violent one.
17:5 Thou hast held fast my goings on to thy ways, My footsteps have not been shaken:
17:6 I, have called upon thee, for thou wilt answer me, O GOD,—Incline thine ear unto me, Hear thou my speech:
17:7 Let thy lovingkindness be distinguished, thou Saviour of such as seek refuge from them who lift themselves up against thy right hand.
17:8 Guard me, as the pupil of the eye,—Under the shadow of thy wings, wilt thou hide me:
17:9 From the face of lawless ones who have treated me with violence, the foes of my soul, who come round against me:
17:10 Their own fat [heart], have they shut up,—With their mouth, have they spoken proudly.
17:11 As for our own goings, now, have they surrounded us,—Their eyes, they fix, bending to the earth:
17:12 His likeness, is as a lion, that longeth to rend, and as a young lion, lurking in secret places.
17:13 Rise, Yahweh! Confront his face, Bring him down, Deliver my soul from the lawless one [who is] thy sword:
17:14 From men [who are] thy hand, O Yahweh, From the men of this age, whose portion, is among the living, and, with thy treasure, thou fillest their bosom,—They must be satisfied with sons, And must leave their abundance to their children:—
17:15 I, in righteousness, shall behold thy face, Shall be satisfied when awakened by a vision of thee.
text.NABRE:
17:1 Psalm 17 Prayer for Rescue from Persecutors - A prayer of David. I - Hear, LORD, my plea for justice; pay heed to my cry; Listen to my prayer from lips without guile.
17:2 From you let my vindication come; your eyes see what is right.
17:3 You have tested my heart, searched it in the night. You have tried me by fire, but find no malice in me. My mouth has not transgressed
17:4 as others often do. As your lips have instructed me, I have kept from the way of the lawless.
17:5 My steps have kept to your paths; my feet have not faltered.
17:6 II - I call upon you; answer me, O God. Turn your ear to me; hear my speech.
17:7 Show your wonderful mercy, you who deliver with your right arm those who seek refuge from their foes.
17:8 Keep me as the apple of your eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings
17:9 from the wicked who despoil me. III - My ravenous enemies press upon me;
17:10 they close their hearts, they fill their mouths with proud roaring.
17:11 Their steps even now encircle me; they watch closely, keeping low to the ground,
17:12 Like lions eager for prey, like a young lion lurking in ambush.
17:13 Rise, O LORD, confront and cast them down; rescue my soul from the wicked.
17:14 Slay them with your sword; with your hand, LORD, slay them; snatch them from the world in their prime. Their bellies are being filled with your friends; their children are satisfied too, for they share what is left with their young.
17:15 I am just—let me see your face; when I awake, let me be filled with your presence.
text.DRB:
17:1 Unto the end, to the servant of our Lord David, who spake to our Lord the words of this canticle, in the day, that our Lord delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies, and out of the hand of Saul, and he said: I will love thee o Lord my strength:
17:2 Our Lord is my firmament, and my refuge, and my deliverer.
17:3 My God is my helper, and I will hope in him. My protector and the horn of my salvation, and my receiver.
17:4 Praising I will invocate our Lord: & I shall be saved from mine enemies.
17:5 The sorrows of death have compassed me: and torrents of iniquity have troubled me.
17:6 The sorrows of hell have compassed me: the snares of death have prevented me.
17:7 In my tribulation I have invocated our Lord, and have cried to my God: And he hath heard my voice from his holy temple: and my cry in his sight, hath entered into his ears.
17:8 The earth was shaken & trembled: the foundations of mountains were troubled, and were moved, because he was wrath with them.
17:9 Smoke arose in his wrath: and fire flamed up from his face: coals were kindled from him.
17:10 He bowed the heavens, and descended: and darkness under his feet.
17:11 And he ascended upon the cherubs, and flew: he flew upon the wings of winds.
17:12 And he put darkness his covert, his tabernacle is round about him: darksome water in the clouds of the air.
17:13 Because of the brightness in his sight the clouds passed, hail and coals of fire.
17:14 And our Lord thundered from heaven, and the Highest gave his voice: hail and coals of fire.
17:15 And he shot his arrows, and dissipated them: he multiplied lightnings, and troubled them.
17:16 And the fountains of waters appeared, and the foundations of the world were revealed. At thy rebuke, o Lord, at the blast of the spirit of thy wrath.
17:17 He sent from on high, and took me: and he received me out of many waters.
17:18 He delivered me from my most strong enemies, and from them that hated me: because they were made strong over me.
17:19 They prevented me in the day of mine affliction: and our Lord was made my protector.
17:20 And he brought me out into largeness: he saved me, because he would me.
17:21 And our Lord will reward me according to my justice, and according to the purity of my hands he will reward me.
17:22 Because I have kept the ways of our Lord, neither have I done impiously from my God.
17:23 Because all his judgments are in my sight: and his justices I have not repelled from me.
17:24 And I shall be immaculate with him, and shall keep me from mine iniquity.
17:25 And our Lord will reward me according to my justice: & according to the purity of my hands in the sight of his eyes.
17:26 With the holy thou shalt be holy, and with the innocent man thou shalt be innocent.
17:27 And with the elect thou shalt be elect: and with the perverse thou shalt be perverted.
17:28 Because thou wilt save the humble people: and the eyes of the proud thou wilt humble.
17:29 Because thou dost illuminate my lamp o Lord: my God illuminate my darkness.
17:30 Because in thee I shall be delivered from tentation, and in my God I shall go over the wall.
17:31 My God his way is unpolluted: the words of our Lord are examined by fire: he is protector of all that hope in him.
17:32 For who is God but our Lord? Or who is God but our God?
17:33 God that girded me with strength: and made my way immaculate.
17:34 That perfited my feet as it were of harts: and setting me upon high things.
17:35 That teacheth my hands to battle: and hast put mine arms as a bow of brass.
17:36 And hast given me the protection of thy salvation: & thy right hand hath received me. And thy discipline hath corrected me unto the end: and thy discipline, the same shall teach me.
17:37 Thou hast enlarged my passes under me: and my steps are not weakened:
17:38 I will pursue mine enemies, and overtake them: and will not return till they fail.
17:39 I will break them, neither shall they be able to stand: they shall fall under my feet.
17:40 And thou hast girded me with strength to battle: and hast supplanted them that rise against me under me.
17:41 And mine enemies thou hast given me their back, & them that hate me thou hast destroyed.
17:42 They cried, neither was there that would save them, to our Lord, neither did he hear them.
17:43 And I will break them to powder, as the dust before the face of wind: as the durt of the streets I will destroy them.
17:44 Thou wilt deliver me from the contradictions of the people: thou wilt appoint me to be head of the Gentiles.
17:45 Apeople which I knew not hath served me: in the hearing of the ear it hath obeyed me.
17:46 The children being aliens have lied to me, the children aliens are inveterated, and have halted from their paths.
17:47 Our Lord liveth, and blessed be my God, and the God of my salvation be exalted.
17:48 O God which givest me revenges, & subduest peoples under me, my deliverer from mine angry enemies.
17:49 And from them that rise up against me thou wilt exalt me: from the unjust man thou wilt deliver me.
17:50 Therefore will I confess to thee among nations, o Lord: and will say a Psalm to thy name.
17:51 Magnifying the salvations of his king, and doing mercy to his Christ David, and to his seed for ever.

text.NRSV:
18:1 I love you, O Lord my strength,
18:2 O Lord my stronghold, my crag, and my haven; my God, my rock in whom I put my trust, my shield, the horn of my salvation, and my refuge; you are worthy of praise.
18:3 I will call upon the Lord, and so shall I be saved from my enemies.
18:4 The breakers of death rolled over me, and the torrents of oblivion made me afraid.
18:5 The cords of hell entangled me, and the snares of death were set for me.
18:6 I called upon the Lord in my distress and cried out to my God for help.
18:7 He heard my voice from his heavenly dwelling; my cry of anguish came to his ears.
18:8 The earth reeled and rocked; the roots of the mountains shook; they reeled because of his anger.
18:9 Smoke rose from his nostrils and a consuming fire out of his mouth; hot burning coals blazed forth from him.
18:10 He parted the heavens and came down with a storm cloud under his feet.
18:11 He rode upon a cherub and flew; he came swiftly on the wings of the wind.
18:12 He wrapped darkness about him; he made dark waters and thick clouds his pavilion.
18:13 From the brightness of his presence, through the clouds, burst hailstones and coals of fire.
18:14 The Lord thundered out of heaven; the Most High uttered his voice.
18:15 He loosed his arrows and scattered them; he hurled thunderbolts and routed them.
18:16 The beds of the seas were uncovered, and the foundations of the world laid bare, at your battle cry, O Lord, at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.
18:17 He reached down from on high and grasped me; he drew me out of great waters.
18:18 He delivered me from my strong enemies and from those who hated me; for they were too mighty for me.
18:19 They confronted me in the day of my disaster; but the Lord was my support.
18:20 He brought me out into an open place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
18:21 The Lord rewarded me because of my righteous dealing; because my hands were clean he rewarded me;
18:22 For I have kept the ways of the Lord and have not offended against my God;
18:23 For all his judgments are before my eyes, and his decrees I have not put away from me;
18:24 For I have been blameless with him and have kept myself from iniquity;
18:25 Therefore the Lord rewarded me according to my righteous dealing, because of the cleanness of my hands in his sight.
18:26 With the faithful you show yourself faithful, O God; with the forthright you show yourself forthright.
18:27 With the pure you show yourself pure, but with the crooked you are wily.
18:28 You will save a lowly people, but you will humble the haughty eyes.
18:29 You, O Lord, are my lamp; my God, you make my darkness bright.
18:30 With you I will break down an enclosure; with the help of my God I will scale any wall.
18:31 As for God, his ways are perfect; the words of the Lord are tried in the fire; he is a shield to all who trust in him.
18:32 For who is God, but the Lord? who is the Rock, except our God?
18:33 It is God who girds me about with strength and makes my way secure.
18:34 He makes me sure-footed like a deer and lets me stand firm on the heights.
18:35 He trains my hands for battle, and my arms for bending even a bow of bronze.
18:36 You have given me your shield of victory; your right hand also sustains me; your loving care makes me great.
18:37 You lengthen my stride beneath me, and my ankles do not give way.
18:38 I pursue my enemies and overtake them; I will not turn back till I have destroyed them.
18:39 I strike them down, and they cannot rise; they fall defeated at my feet.
18:40 You have girded me with strength for the battle; you have cast down my adversaries beneath me; you have made my enemies turn their backs upon me, and I destroyed those who hated me.
18:41 They cried for help, but there was none to save them; they cried to the Lord, but he did not answer.
18:42 I beat them small like dust before the wind; I trampled them like mud in the streets.
18:43 You delivered me from strife with the peoples; you made me head over nations; people whom I had not known served me.
18:44 As soon as they heard of me, they obeyed me; foreigners came cringing to me.
18:45 Foreigners lost heart and came trembling out of their strongholds.
18:46 The Lord lives! Blessed is my rock! Exalted is the God of my salvation!
18:47 He is the God who gave me victory and cast down the peoples beneath me.
18:48 You rescued me from the fury of my enemies; you exalted me above those who rose against me; you saved me from my deadly foe.
18:49 Therefore will I extol you among the nations, O Lord, and sing praises to your Name.
18:50 He multiplies the victories of his king; he shows loving-kindness to his anointed, to David and his descendants for ever.

      he shows loving-kindness to his anointed,
