text.NABRE:
23:1 Psalm 23 The Lord, Shepherd and Host - A psalm of David. I - The LORD is my shepherd; there is nothing I lack.
23:2 In green pastures he makes me lie down; to still waters he leads me;
23:3 he restores my soul. He guides me along right paths for the sake of his name.
23:4 Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff comfort me.
23:5 II - You set a table before me in front of my enemies; You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.
23:6 Indeed, goodness and mercy will pursue me all the days of my life; I will dwell in the house of the LORD for endless days.
text.DRB:
23:1 The first of the Sabbath, the Psalm of David. The earth is our Lord's, and the fullness thereof: the round world, and all that dwell therein.
23:2 Because he hath founded it upon the seas: and upon the rivers hath prepared it.
23:3 Who shall ascend into the mount of our Lord? Or who shall stand in his holy place?
23:4 The innocent of hands, and of clean heart, that hath not taken his soul in vain, nor sworn to his neighbour in guile.
23:5 He shall receive blessing of our Lord: and mercy of God his Saviour.
23:6 This is the generation of them that seek him, of them that seek the face of the God of Jacob.
23:7 Lift up your gates ye Princes, and be ye lifted up, o eternal gates: and the King of glory shall enter in.
23:8 Who is this King of glory? Our Lord strong & mighty, our Lord mighty in battle.
23:9 Lift up your gates ye Princes, and be ye lifted up, o eternal gates: and the King of glory shall enter in.
23:10 Who is this King of glory? The Lord of powers he is the King of glory.

text.NRSV:
24:1 The earth is the Lord's and all that is in it, the world and all who dwell therein.
24:2 For it is he who founded it upon the seas and made it fast upon the great deep.
24:3 Who can ascend the hill of the Lord? and who can stand in his holy place?
24:4 Those who have clean hands and a pure heart, who have not pledged themselves to falsehood, nor sworn by what is a fraud.
24:5 They shall receive a blessing from the Lord and a just reward from the God of their salvation.
24:6 Such is the generation of those who seek him, of those who seek your face, O God of Jacob.
24:7 Lift up your heads, O gates; lift them high, O everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
24:8 'Who is this King of glory?' 'The Lord, strong and mighty, the Lord, mighty in battle.'
24:9 Lift up your heads, O gates; lift them high, O everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
24:10 'Who is he, this King of glory?' 'The Lord of hosts, he is the King of glory.'

text.KJV:
24:1 The earth is the Lords, and the fulnesse thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
24:2 For he hath founded it vpon the seas, and established it vpon the floods.
24:3 Who shall ascend into the hill of the Lord? and who shall stand in his holy place?
24:4 He that hath cleane hands, and a pure heart; who hath not lift vp his soule vnto vanitie, nor sworne deceitfully.
24:5 Hee shall receiue the blessing from the Lord, and righteousnesse from the God of his saluation.
24:6 This is the generation of them that seeke him: that seeke thy face, O Iacob. Selah.
24:7 Lift vp your heads, O yee gates, and be ye lift vp ye euerlasting doores; and the King of glory shall come in.
24:8 Who is this king of glory? the Lord strong & mightie, the Lord mighty in battell.
24:9 Lift vp your heads, O ye gates, euen lift them vp, ye euerlasting doores; and the king of glory shall come in.
24:10 Who is this king of glory? the Lord of hostes, he is the king of glory. Selah.
text.JPS1985:
24:1 Of David. A psalm. The earth is the LORD’s and all that it holds, the world and its inhabitants.
24:2 For He founded it upon the ocean, set it on the nether-streams.
24:3 Who may ascend the mountain of the LORD? Who may stand in His holy place? —
24:4 He who has clean hands and a pure heart, who has not taken a false oath by My life or sworn deceitfully.
24:5 He shall carry away a blessing from the LORD, a just reward from God, his deliverer.
24:6 Such is the circle of those who turn to Him, Jacob, who seek Your presence. Selah.
24:7 O gates, lift up your heads! Up high, you everlasting doors, so the King of glory may come in!
24:8 Who is the King of glory? — the LORD, mighty and valiant, the LORD, valiant in battle.
24:9 O gates, lift up your heads! Lift them up, you everlasting doors, so the King of glory may come in!
24:10 Who is the King of glory? — the LORD of hosts, He is the King of glory! Selah.
text.Grail1963:
1 The Lord's is the earth and its fullness,
the world and all its peoples.
2 It is he who set it on the seas;
on the waters he made it firm.
3 Who shall climb the mountain of the Lord?
Who shall stand in his holy place?
4 The man with clean hands and pure heart,
who desires not worthless things,
(who have not sworn so as to deceive their neighbor.)
5 He shall receive blessings from the Lord
and reward from the God who saves him .
6 Such are the men who seek him,
seek the face of the God of Jacob.
* * *
7 O gates, lift high your heads;
grow higher, ancient doors.
Let him enter, the king of glory!
8 Who is the king of glory?
The Lord, the mighty, the valiant,
the Lord, the valiant in war.
9 O gates, lift high your heads;
grow higher, ancient doors.
Let him enter, the king of glory!

He, the Lord of armies,
he is the king of glory.
text.Rotherham:
24:1 To Yahweh, belongeth, The earth and the fulness thereof, The world, and they who dwell therein;
24:2 For, he, upon the seas, hath founded it, and upon the currents, doth make it firm.
24:3 Who shall ascend the mountain of Yahweh? And who shall stand in his holy place?
24:4 The clean of hands, and pure of heart,—who hath not uplifted, to falsehood, his soul, nor sworn deceitfully,
24:5 Shall bear away a blessing from Yahweh, and righteousness, from his delivering God.
24:6 This, is the generation of them who inquire of him, who seek thy face, O God of Jacob. [Selah.]
24:7 Lift up, O ye gates, your heads, and lift yourselves up, ye age-abiding doors, That the king of glory may come in.
24:8 Who is the king of glory? Yahweh, strong and mighty, Yahweh, mighty in war.
24:9 Lift up, O ye gates, your heads, yea lift [them] up, ye age-abiding doors, That the king of glory may come in.
24:10 Who then is the king of glory? Yahweh of hosts, He, is the king of glory. [Selah.]
text.NABRE:
24:1 Psalm 24 The Glory of God in Procession to Zion - A psalm of David. I - The earth is the LORD’s and all it holds, the world and those who dwell in it.
24:2 For he founded it on the seas, established it over the rivers.
24:3 II - Who may go up the mountain of the LORD? Who can stand in his holy place?
24:4 “The clean of hand and pure of heart, who has not given his soul to useless things, what is vain.
24:5 He will receive blessings from the LORD, and justice from his saving God.
24:6 Such is the generation that seeks him, that seeks the face of the God of Jacob.” Selah
24:7 III - Lift up your heads, O gates; be lifted, you ancient portals, that the king of glory may enter.
24:8 Who is this king of glory? The LORD, strong and mighty, the LORD, mighty in war.
24:9 Lift up your heads, O gates; rise up, you ancient portals, that the king of glory may enter.
24:10 Who is this king of glory? The LORD of hosts, he is the king of glory. Selah
text.DRB:
24:1 Unto the end, the Psalm of David. To thee, O Lord, I have lifted up my soul:
24:2 my God in thee is my confidence, let me not be ashamed.
24:3 Neither let mine enemies scorn me: for all that expect thee, shall not be confounded.
24:4 Let all be confounded that do unjust things in vain. Lord shew me thy ways: and teach me thy paths.
24:5 Direct me in thy truth, and teach me: because thou art God my Saviour, and thee have I expected all the day.
24:6 Remember, O Lord, thy commiserations, and thy mercies: that are from the beginning of the world.
24:7 The sins of my youth, and my ignorances do not remember. According to thy mercy remember thou me: for thy goodness, O Lord.
24:8 Our Lord is sweet, and righteous: for this cause he will give a law to them that sin in the way.
24:9 He will direct the mild in judgment: he will teach the meek his ways.
24:10 All the ways of our Lord be mercy and truth, to them that seek after his testament and his testimonies.
24:11 For thy name, o Lord, thou wilt be propitious to my sin: for it is much.
24:12 Who is the man that feareth our Lord? He appointeth him a law in the way that he hath chosen.
24:13 His soul shall abide in good things: and his seed shall inherit the land.
24:14 Our Lord is a firmament to them that fear him: & his testament that it may be made manifest to them.
24:15 Mine eyes are always to our Lord: because he will pluck my feet out of the snare.
24:16 Have respect to me, and have mercy on me: because I am alone and poor.
24:17 The tribulations of my heart are multiplied: deliver me from my necessities.
24:18 See my humiliation and my labour, and forgive all my sins.
24:19 Behold mine enemies, because they are multiplied, and with unjust hatred hated me.
24:20 Keep my soul, and deliver me: I shall not be ashamed, because I hoped in thee.
24:21 The innocent and righteous have cleaved to me: because I expected thee.
24:22 Deliver Israel, o God, out of all his tribulations.

text.NRSV:
25:1 To you, O Lord, I lift up my soul; my God, I put my trust in you; let me not be humiliated, nor let my enemies triumph over me.
25:2 Let none who look to you be put to shame; let the treacherous be disappointed in their schemes.
25:3 Show me your ways, O Lord, and teach me your paths.
25:4 Lead me in your truth and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation; in you have I trusted all the day long.
25:5 Remember, O Lord, your compassion and love, for they are from everlasting.
25:6 Remember not the sins of my youth and my transgressions; remember me according to your love and for the sake of your goodness, O Lord.
25:7 Gracious and upright is the Lord; therefore he teaches sinners in his way.
25:8 He guides the humble in doing right and teaches his way to the lowly.
25:9 All the paths of the Lord are love and faithfulness to those who keep his covenant and his testimonies.
25:10 For your Name's sake, O Lord, forgive my sin, for it is great.
25:11 Who are they who fear the Lord? he will teach them the way that they should choose.
25:12 They shall dwell in prosperity, and their offspring shall inherit the land.
25:13 The Lord is a friend to those who fear him, and he will show them his covenant.
25:14 My eyes are ever looking to the Lord, for he shall pluck my feet out of the net.
25:15 Turn to me and have pity on me, for I am left alone and in misery.
25:16 The sorrows of my heart have multiplied; deliver me out of my distress.
25:17 Look upon my adversity and misery and forgive me all my sin.
25:18 Look upon my enemies, for they are many, and they bear a violent hatred against me.
25:19 Protect my person and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I have trusted in you.
25:20 Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, for my hope has been in you.
25:21 Deliver Israel, O God, out of all his troubles.

text.KJV:
25:1 Vnto thee, O Lord, doe I lift vp my soule.
25:2 O my God, I trust in thee, let me not be ashamed: let not mine enemies triumph ouer me.
25:3 Yea let none that waite on thee, be ashamed: let them bee ashamed which transgresse without cause.
25:4 Shewe mee thy wayes, O Lord: teach me thy pathes.
25:5 Lead me in thy trueth, and teach me: for thou art the God of my saluation, on thee doe I waite all the day.
25:6 Remember, O Lord, thy tender mercies, and thy louing kindnesses: for they haue beene euer of old.
25:7 Remember not the sinnes of my youth, nor my transgressions: according to thy mercie remember thou me, for thy goodnesse sake, O Lord.
25:8 Good and vpright is the Lord: therefore will hee teach sinners in the way.
25:9 The meeke will he guide in iudgement: and the meeke will he teach his way.
25:10 All the pathes of the Lord are mercy and truth: vnto such as keepe his couenant, and his testimonies.
25:11 For thy names sake, O Lord, pardon mine iniquitie: for it is great.
25:12 What man is he that feareth the Lord? him shall he teach in the way that he shall chuse.
25:13 His soule shall dwell at ease: and his seede shall inherite the earth.
25:14 The secret of the Lord is with them that feare him: and he will shew them his couenant.
25:15 Mine eyes are euer towards the Lord: for hee shall plucke my feete out of the net.
25:16 Turne thee vnto me, and haue mercy vpon me: for I am desolate and afflicted.
25:17 The troubles of my heart are inlarged: O bring thou me out of my distresses.
25:18 Looke vpon mine affliction, aud my paine, and forgiue all my sinnes.
25:19 Consider mine enemies: for they are many, and they hate me with cruell hatred.
25:20 O keepe my soule and deliuer me: let me not bee ashamed, for I put my trust in thee.
25:21 Let integritie and vprightnesse preserue me: for I wait on thee.
25:22 Redeeme Israel, O God, out of all his troubles.
text.JPS1985:
25:1 Of David. O LORD, I set my hope on You;
25:2 my God, in You I trust; may I not be disappointed, may my enemies not exult over me.
25:3 O let none who look to You be disappointed; let the faithless be disappointed, empty-handed.
25:4 Let me know Your paths, O LORD; teach me Your ways;
25:5 guide me in Your true way and teach me, for You are God, my deliverer; it is You I look to at all times.
25:6 O LORD, be mindful of Your compassion and Your faithfulness; they are old as time.
25:7 Be not mindful of my youthful sins and transgressions; in keeping with Your faithfulness consider what is in my favor, as befits Your goodness, O LORD.
25:8 Good and upright is the LORD; therefore He shows sinners the way.
25:9 He guides the lowly in the right path, and teaches the lowly His way.
25:10 All the LORD’s paths are steadfast love for those who keep the decrees of His covenant.
25:11 As befits Your name, O LORD, pardon my iniquity though it be great.
25:12 Whoever fears the LORD, he shall be shown what path to choose.
25:13 He shall live a happy life, and his children shall inherit the land.
25:14 The counsel of the LORD is for those who fear Him; to them He makes known His covenant.
25:15 My eyes are ever toward the LORD, for He will loose my feet from the net.
25:16 Turn to me, have mercy on me, for I am alone and afflicted.
25:17 My deep distress increases; deliver me from my straits.
25:18 Look at my affliction and suffering, and forgive all my sins.
25:19 See how numerous my enemies are, and how unjustly they hate me!
25:20 Protect me and save me; let me not be disappointed, for I have sought refuge in You.
25:21 May integrity and uprightness watch over me, for I look to You.
25:22 O God, redeem Israel from all its distress.
text.Grail1963:

do not let my enemies triumph.

Lord, teach me your paths.

7c because of your goodness, O Lord.

forgive my guilt, for it is great.

he will show him the path he should choose.

for I am lonely and poor.
17 Relieve the anguish of my heart

text.Rotherham:
25:1 Unto thee, O Yahweh, my soul, would I lift:
25:2 O my God, in thee, have I put my trust, Let me not be put to shame, Let not my foe exult over me:
25:3 Yea let, none who wait for thee, be put to shame, Let them be put to shame who act covertly without cause!
25:4 Thy ways, O Yahweh, let me know, Thy paths, teach thou me:
25:5 Guide me into thy truth and teach me, for, thou, art my delivering God, For thee, have I waited all the day:
25:6 Remember thy compassions, O Yahweh, and thy lovingkindnesses, For, from age-past times, have they been.
25:7 The sins of my youth, and my transgressions, do not thou call to mind,—According to thine own lovingkindness, remember thou me, for the sake of thine own goodness, O Yahweh.
25:8 Good and upright, is Yahweh, For this cause, will he direct sinners into the way.
25:9 May he guide patient wronged-ones to be righted, and teach such oppressed-ones his way.
25:10 All the paths of Yahweh, are lovingkindness and faithfulness, to such as keep his covenant, and his testimonies.
25:11 For the sake of thy Name, O Yahweh, Therefore wilt thou pardon mine iniquity, for great it is.
25:12 Who then is the man that revereth Yahweh? Let him direct him into the way he should choose.
25:13 His soul, with prosperity, shall tarry, and, his seed, shall possess the land.
25:14 Intimacy with Yahweh, have they who revere him, His covenant also, he letteth them know.
25:15 Mine eyes, are continually unto Yahweh,—for, he, bringeth, out of the net, my feet.
25:16 Turn thou unto me, and show me favour, for, alone and oppressed, I am.
25:17 The distresses of my heart, hath he relieved,—and, out of my straits, brought me forth.
25:18 Behold my humiliation and my pain, and take away all my sins.
25:19 Behold my foes, for they abound,—and, with the hatred of violence, do they hate me.
25:20 Oh keep my soul, and rescue me, Let me not be put to shame, for I have sought refuge in thee.
25:21 Let, blamelessness and uprightness, watch over me, because I have waited for thee.
25:22 Redeem Israel, O God,—out of all his distresses.
text.NABRE:
25:1 Psalm 25 Confident Prayer for Forgiveness and Guidance - Of David. I - To you, O LORD, I lift up my soul,
25:2 my God, in you I trust; do not let me be disgraced; do not let my enemies gloat over me.
25:3 No one is disgraced who waits for you, but only those who are treacherous without cause.
25:4 Make known to me your ways, LORD; teach me your paths.
25:5 Guide me by your fidelity and teach me, for you are God my savior, for you I wait all the day long.
25:6 Remember your compassion and your mercy, O LORD, for they are ages old.
25:7 Remember no more the sins of my youth; remember me according to your mercy, because of your goodness, LORD.
25:8 II - Good and upright is the LORD, therefore he shows sinners the way,
25:9 He guides the humble in righteousness, and teaches the humble his way.
25:10 All the paths of the LORD are mercy and truth toward those who honor his covenant and decrees.
25:11 For the sake of your name, LORD, pardon my guilt, though it is great.
25:12 Who is the one who fears the LORD? God shows him the way he should choose.
25:13 He will abide in prosperity, and his descendants will inherit the land.
25:14 The counsel of the LORD belongs to those who fear him; and his covenant instructs them.
25:15 My eyes are ever upon the LORD, who frees my feet from the snare.
25:16 III - Look upon me, have pity on me, for I am alone and afflicted.
25:17 Relieve the troubles of my heart; bring me out of my distress.
25:18 Look upon my affliction and suffering; take away all my sins.
25:19 See how many are my enemies, see how fiercely they hate me.
25:20 Preserve my soul and rescue me; do not let me be disgraced, for in you I seek refuge.
25:21 Let integrity and uprightness preserve me; I wait for you, O LORD.
25:22 Redeem Israel, O God, from all its distress!
text.DRB:
25:1 Unto the end the Psalm of David. Judge me, o Lord, because I have walked in my innocency: and hoping in our Lord I shall not be weakened.
25:2 Prove me Lord, and tempt me: burn my reins and my heart.
25:3 Because thy mercy is before mine eyes: and I am well pleased in thy truth.
25:4 I have not sitten with the Council of vanity: and with them that do unjust things I will not enter in.
25:5 I have hated the Church of the malignant: and with the impious I will not sit.
25:6 I will wash my hands among innocents: and will compass thy altar, o Lord:
25:7 That I may hear the voice of praise, and shew forth all thy marvelous works.
25:8 Lord I have loved the beauty of thy house, and the place of the habitation of thy glory.
25:9 Destroy not, o God, my soul with the impious, and my life with bloody men.
25:10 In whose hands are iniquities: their right hand is replenished with gifts.
25:11 But I have walked in mine innocency: redeem me, and have mercy on me.
25:12 My foot hath stood in the direct way: in the Churches I will bless thee o Lord.

text.NRSV:
26:1 Give judgment for me, O Lord, for I have lived with integrity; I have trusted in the Lord and have not faltered.
26:2 Test me, O Lord, and try me; examine my heart and my mind.
26:3 For your love is before my eyes; I have walked in your truth.
26:4 I have not sat with the worthless, nor do I consort with the deceitful.
26:5 I have hated the company of the evildoers; I will not sit down with the wicked.
26:6 I will wash my hands in innocence, O Lord, that I may go in procession round your altar;
26:7 Adoring with the voice of thanksgiving and declaring all your wonderful deeds.
26:8 Lord, I love the house in which you dwell and the place where your glory abides.
26:9 Do not sweep me away with sinners, nor my life with those who thirst for blood,
26:10 Whose hands are full of evil plots, and their right hand full of bribes.
26:11 As for me, I will live with integrity; redeem me, O Lord, and have pity on me.
26:12 My foot stands on level ground; in the full assembly I will bless the Lord.

text.KJV:
26:1 Iudge me, O Lord, for I haue walked in mine integritie: I haue trusted also in the Lord: therfore I shall not slide.
26:2 Examine me, O Lord, and proue me; try my reines and my heart.
26:3 For thy louing kindnesse is before mine eyes: and I haue walked in thy trueth.
26:4 I haue not sate with vaine persons, neither will I goe in with dissemblers.
26:5 I haue hated the congregation of euill doers: and will not sit with the wicked.
26:6 I will wash mine hands in innocencie: so will I compasse thine Altar, O Lord:
26:7 That I may publish with the voyce of thankesgiuing, and tell of all thy wonderous workes.
26:8 Lord, I haue loued the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth.
26:9 Gather not my soule with sinners, nor my life with bloody men.
26:10 In whose hands is mischiefe: and their right hand is full of bribes.
26:11 But as for mee, I will walke in mine integritie: redeeme me, and bee mercifull vnto me.
26:12 My foot standeth in an euen place: in the congregations will I blesse the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
26:1 Of David. Vindicate me, O LORD, for I have walked without blame; I have trusted in the LORD; I have not faltered.
26:2 Probe me, O LORD, and try me, test my heart and mind;
26:3 for my eyes are on Your steadfast love; I have set my course by it.
26:4 I do not consort with scoundrels, or mix with hypocrites;
26:5 I detest the company of evil men, and do not consort with the wicked;
26:6 I wash my hands in innocence, and walk around Your altar, O LORD,
26:7 raising my voice in thanksgiving, and telling all Your wonders.
26:8 O LORD, I love Your temple abode, the dwelling-place of Your glory.
26:9 Do not sweep me away with sinners, or [snuff out] my life with murderers,
26:10 who have schemes at their fingertips, and hands full of bribes.
26:11 But I walk without blame; redeem me, have mercy on me!
26:12 My feet are on level ground. In assemblies I will bless the LORD.
text.Grail1963:
1 Give judgment for me, O Lord:
for I walk the path of perfection.
I trust in the Lord; I have not wavered.
2 Examine me, Lord, and try me;
O test my heart and my mind,
3 for your love is before my eyes
and I walk according to your truth.
4 I never take my place with liars
and with hypocrites I shall not go.
5 I hate the evildoer's company:
I will not take my place with the wicked.
6 To prove my innocence I wash my hands
and take my place around your altar,
7 singing a song of thanksgiving,
proclaiming all your wonders.
8 O Lord, I love the house where you dwell,
the place where your glory abides.
9 Do not sweep me away with sinners,
nor my life with bloodthirsty men
10 in whose hands are evil plots,
whose right hands are filled with gold.
11 As for me, I walk the path of perfection.

text.NABRE:
26:1 Psalm 26 Prayer of Innocence - Of David. I - Judge me, LORD! For I have walked in my integrity. In the LORD I trust; I do not falter.
26:2 Examine me, Lord, and test me; search my heart and mind.
26:3 Your mercy is before my eyes; I walk guided by your faithfulness.
26:4 II - I do not sit with worthless men, nor with hypocrites do I mingle.
26:5 I hate an evil assembly; with the wicked I do not sit.
26:6 I will wash my hands in innocence so that I may process around your altar, Lord,
26:7 To hear the sound of thanksgiving, and recount all your wondrous deeds.
26:8 Lord, I love the refuge of your house, the site of the dwelling-place of your glory.
26:9 III - Do not take me away with sinners, nor my life with the men of blood,
26:10 In whose hands there is a plot, their right hands full of bribery.
26:11 But I walk in my integrity; redeem me, be gracious to me!
26:12 My foot stands on level ground; in assemblies I will bless the LORD.
text.DRB:
26:1 The Psalm of David before he was anointed. Our Lord is my illumination, and my salvation, whom shall I fear? Our Lord is the protector of my life, of whom shall I be afraid?
26:2 Whilst the harmful approach upon me, to eat my flesh. Mine enemies that trouble me, themselves are weakened and are fallen.
26:3 If camps stand together against me, my heart shall not fear. If battle rise up against me, in this will I hope.
26:4 One thing I have asked of our Lord, this will I seek for, that I may dwell in the house of our Lord all the days of my life. That I may see the pleasantness of our Lord, and visit his temple.
26:5 Because he hath hid me in his tabernacle: in the day of evils he hath protected me, in the secret of his tabernacle.
26:6 In a rock he hath exalted me: and now he hath exalted my head over mine enemies. I have gone round about, and have immolated in his tabernacle an host of jubilation: I will sing, and say a Psalm to our Lord.
26:7 Hear, o Lord, my voice, wherewith I have cried to thee: have mercy on me, and hear me.
26:8 My heart hath said to thee, my face hath sought thee out: thy face, o Lord, I will seek.
26:9 Turn not away thy face from me: decline not in wrath from thy servant. Be thou my helper: forsake me not, neither despise me, o God my Saviour.
26:10 because my father and my mother have forsaken me: but our Lord hath taken me.
26:11 Give me a law, o Lord, in thy way: and direct me in the right path, because of mine enemies.
26:12 Deliver me not into the souls of them that trouble me: because unjust witnesses have risen up against me: and iniquity hath lied to it-self.
26:13 I believe to see the good things of our Lord, in the land of the living.
26:14 Expect our Lord, do manfully: and let thy heart take courage, and expect thou our Lord.

text.NRSV:
27:1 The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
27:2 When evildoers assail me to eat up my flesh, my adversaries and foes, they shall stumble and fall.
27:3 Though an army encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; though war rise up against me, yet I will be confident.
27:4 One thing I asked of the Lord, that will I seek after: to live in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the Lord, and to inquire in his temple.
27:5 For he will hide me in his shelter in the day of trouble; he will conceal me under the cover of his tent; he will set me high on a rock.
27:6 Now my head is lifted up above my enemies all around me, and I will offer in his tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and make melody to the Lord.
27:7 Hear, O Lord, when I cry aloud; be gracious to me and answer me!
27:8 'Come,' my heart says, 'seek his face!' Your face, Lord, do I seek.
27:9 Do not hide your face from me. Do not turn your servant away in anger, you who have been my help. Do not cast me off, do not forsake me, O God of my salvation!
27:10 If my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will take me up.
27:11 Teach me your way, O Lord, and lead me on a level path because of my enemies.
27:12 Do not give me up to the will of my adversaries, for false witnesses have risen against me, and they are breathing out violence.
27:13 I believe that I shall see the goodness of the Lord in the land of the living.
27:14 Wait for the Lord; be strong, and let your heart take courage; wait for the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
27:1 The LORD is my light and my salvation;
      whom then shall I fear? *
      the LORD is the strength of my life;
      of whom then shall I be afraid?
27:2 When evildoers came upon me to eat up my flesh, *
      it was they, my foes and my adversaries, who stumbled and fell.
27:3 Though an army should encamp against me, *
      yet my heart shall not be afraid;
27:4 And though war should rise up against me, *
      yet will I put my trust in him.
27:5 One thing have I asked of the LORD;
      one thing I seek; *
      that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life;
27:6 To behold the fair beauty of the LORD *
      and to seek him in his temple.
27:7 For in the day of trouble he shall keep me safe in his shelter; *
      he shall hide me in the secrecy of his dwelling
      and set me high upon a rock.
27:8 Even now he lifts up my head *
      above my enemies round about me.
27:9 Therefore I will offer in his dwelling an oblation
      with sounds of great gladness; *
      I will sing and make music to the LORD.
27:10 Hearken to my voice, O LORD, when I call; *
      have mercy on me and answer me.
27:11 You speak in my heart and say, "Seek my face." *
      Your face, LORD, will I seek.
27:12 Hide not your face from me, *
      nor turn away your servant in displeasure.
27:13 You have been my helper;
      cast me not away; *
      do not forsake me, O God of my salvation.
27:14 Though my father and my mother forsake me, *
      the LORD will sustain me.
27:15 Show me your way, O LORD; *
      lead me on a level path, because of my enemies.
27:16 Deliver me not into the hand of my adversaries, *

text.KJV:
27:1 The Lord is my light, and my saluation, whome shal I feare? the Lord is the strength of my life, of whō shall I be afraid?
27:2 When the wicked, euen mine enemies and my foes came vpon me to eat vp my flesh, they stumbled and fell.
27:3 Though an host should encampe against me, my heart shall not feare: though warre should rise against me, in this will I be confident.
27:4 One thing haue I desired of the Lord, that will I seeke after: that I may dwel in the house of the Lord, all the dayes of my life, to behold the beautie of the Lord, and to inquire in his temple.
27:5 For in the time of trouble he shall hide me in his pauilion: in the secret of his tabernacle shall he hide me, hee shall set me vp vpon a rocke.
27:6 And now shall mine head be lifted vp aboue mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of ioy, I will sing, yea, I will sing praises vnto the Lord.
27:7 Heare, O Lord, when I crie with my voice: haue mercie also vpon mee, and answere me.
27:8 When thou saidst, Seeke ye my face, my heart said vnto thee, Thy face, Lord, will I seeke.
27:9 Hide not thy face farre frō me, put not thy seruant away in anger: thou hast bin my helpe, leaue me not, neither forsake me, O God of my saluation.
27:10 When my father and my mother forsake me, then the Lord will take me vp.
27:11 Teach me thy way, O Lord, and leade me in a plaine path, because of mine enemies.
27:12 Deliuer me not ouer vnto the will of mine enemies: for false witnesses are risen vp against me, and such as breath out crueltie.
27:13 I had fainted, vnlesse I had beleeued to see the goodnesse of the Lord in the land of the liuing.
27:14 Wait on the Lord: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
27:1 Of David. The LORD is my light and my help; whom should I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life, whom should I dread?
27:2 When evil men assail me to devour my flesh — it is they, my foes and my enemies, who stumble and fall.
27:3 Should an army besiege me, my heart would have no fear; should war beset me, still would I be confident.
27:4 One thing I ask of the LORD, only that do I seek: to live in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to gaze upon the beauty of the LORD, to frequent His temple.
27:5 He will shelter me in His pavilion on an evil day, grant me the protection of His tent, raise me high upon a rock.
27:6 Now is my head high over my enemies round about; I sacrifice in His tent with shouts of joy, singing and chanting a hymn to the LORD.
27:7 Hear, O LORD, when I cry aloud; have mercy on me, answer me.
27:8 In Your behalf my heart says: “Seek My face! ” O LORD, I seek Your face.
27:9 Do not hide Your face from me; do not thrust aside Your servant in anger; You have ever been my help. Do not forsake me, do not abandon me, O God, my deliverer.
27:10 Though my father and mother abandon me, the LORD will take me in.
27:11 Show me Your way, O LORD, and lead me on a level path because of my watchful foes.
27:12 Do not subject me to the will of my foes, for false witnesses and unjust accusers have appeared against me.
27:13 Had I not the assurance that I would enjoy the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living…
27:14 Look to the LORD; be strong and of good courage! O look to the LORD!

text.Rotherham:
27:1 Yahweh, is my light and my salvation, Of whom shall I be afraid? Yahweh, is the refuge of my life, Of whom shall I be in dread?
27:2 When there drew near against me evil-doers to devour my flesh, mine adversaries, and mine enemies—mine!—they, stumbled and fell!
27:3 Though there pitch against me—a camp, my heart shall not fear,—Though there come up against me—a battle, still, am I confident.
27:4 One thing, have I asked of Yahweh, That, will I seek to secure,—That I may dwell in the house of Yahweh, all the days of my life, To view the delightfulness of Yahweh, And to contemplate in his temple.
27:5 For he will hide me in his pavilion, in the day of calamity,—He will conceal me, in the secrecy of his tent, Within a rock, will he set me on high.
27:6 Now, therefore, shall my head be raised high above my foes round about me, and I will sacrifice in his tent the sacrifices of triumphant joy, I will sing and touch the strings, to Yahweh.
27:7 Hear, O Yahweh, With my voice, do I cry, Oh then show me favour and answer me.
27:8 To thee, said my heart—[When thou saidst] Seek ye my face—Thy face, O Yahweh, will I seek!
27:9 Do not hide thy face from me, Do not repulse, in thine anger, thine own servant,—My help, thou hast been, Do not send me away nor forsake me, O my saving God!
27:10 When, my own father and mother, had forsaken me, Then, Yahweh, took me up!
27:11 Point out to me, O Yahweh, thy way,—And guide me in a level path, because of mine adversaries.
27:12 Do not give me up unto the desire of mine adversaries, for there have risen up against me witnesses that are false, and he that fanneth up violence.
27:13 I believe that I shall see—The goodness of Yahweh in the land of the living,
27:14 Wait thou for Yahweh,—Be strong, and let thy heart be bold, Wait, then, for Yahweh!
text.NABRE:
27:1 Psalm 27 Trust in God - Of David. A - IThe LORD is my light and my salvation; whom should I fear? The LORD is my life’s refuge; of whom should I be afraid?
27:2 When evildoers come at me to devour my flesh, These my enemies and foes themselves stumble and fall.
27:3 Though an army encamp against me, my heart does not fear; Though war be waged against me, even then do I trust.
27:4 II One thing I ask of the LORD; this I seek: To dwell in the LORD’s house all the days of my life, To gaze on the LORD’s beauty, to visit his temple.
27:5 For God will hide me in his shelter in time of trouble, He will conceal me in the cover of his tent; and set me high upon a rock.
27:6 Even now my head is held high above my enemies on every side! I will offer in his tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and chant praise to the LORD.
27:7 B - I Hear my voice, LORD, when I call; have mercy on me and answer me.
27:8 “Come,” says my heart, “seek his face”; your face, LORD, do I seek!
27:9 Do not hide your face from me; do not repel your servant in anger. You are my salvation; do not cast me off; do not forsake me, God my savior!
27:10 Even if my father and mother forsake me, the LORD will take me in.
27:11 II LORD, show me your way; lead me on a level path because of my enemies.
27:12 Do not abandon me to the desire of my foes; malicious and lying witnesses have risen against me.
27:13 I believe I shall see the LORD’s goodness in the land of the living.
27:14 Wait for the LORD, take courage; be stouthearted, wait for the LORD!
text.DRB:
27:1 Apsalm to David himself. To thee, o Lord, I will cry, my God keep not silence from me: lest at any time thou hold thy peace from me, & I shall be like to them that go down into the lake.
27:2 Hear, o Lord, the voice of my petition whilst I pray to thee: whilst I lift up my hands to thy holy temple.
27:3 Draw me not together with sinners: and with them that work iniquity destroy me not. Which speak peace with their neighbour, but evils in their hearts.
27:4 Give them according to their works, & according to the wickedness of their inventions. According to the works of their hands give unto them: render them their retribution.
27:5 Because they have not understood the works of our Lord, and in the works of his hands thou shalt destroy them, & not build them up.
27:6 blessed be our Lord: because he hath heard the voice of my petition.
27:7 Our Lord is my helper, and my protector: in him my heart hath hoped, and I was holpen. And my flesh flourished again: & with my will I will confess to him.
27:8 Our Lord is the strength of his people: and he is the protector of the salvations of his anointed.
27:9 Save thy people, o Lord, and bless thine inheritance: and rule them, and extol them for ever.

text.NRSV:
28:1 To you, O Lord, I call; my rock, do not refuse to hear me, for if you are silent to me, I shall be like those who go down to the Pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my supplication, as I cry to you for help, as I lift up my hands toward your most holy sanctuary.
28:3 Do not drag me away with the wicked, with those who are workers of evil, who speak peace with their neighbors, while mischief is in their hearts.
28:4 Repay them according to their work, and according to the evil of their deeds; repay them according to the work of their hands; render them their due reward.
28:5 Because they do not regard the works of the Lord, or the operation of his hands, he will break them down and build them up no more.
28:6 Blessed be the Lord, for he has heard the sound of my pleadings.
28:7 The Lord is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusts; so I am helped, and my heart exults, and with my song I give thanks to him.
28:8 The Lord is the strength of his people; he is the saving refuge of his anointed.
28:9 O save your people, and bless your heritage; be their shepherd, and carry them for ever.
text.Coverdale:
28:1 O LORD, I call to you;
      my Rock, do not be deaf to my cry; *
      lest, if you do not hear me,
      I become like those who go down to the Pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my prayer when I cry out to you, *
      when I lift up my hands to your holy of holies.
28:3 Do not snatch me away with the wicked or with the evildoers, *
      who speak peaceably with their neighbors,
      while strife is in their hearts.
28:4 Repay them according to their deeds, *
      and according to the wickedness of their actions.
28:5 According to the work of their hands repay them, *
      and give them their just deserts.
28:6 They have no understanding of the LORD's doings,
      nor of the works of his hands; *
      therefore he will break them down and not build them up.
28:7 Blessed is the LORD! *
      for he has heard the voice of my prayer.
28:8 The LORD is my strength and my shield; *
      my heart trusts in him, and I have been helped;
28:9 Therefore my heart dances for joy, *
      and in my song will I praise him.
28:10 The LORD is the strength of his people, *
      a safe refuge for his anointed.
28:11 Save your people and bless your heritage; *
      shepherd them and carry them for ever.
text.KJV:
28:1 Vnto thee will I cry, O Lord, my rocke, be not silent to mee: lest if thou be silent to me, I become like them that goe downe into the pit.
28:2 Heare the voyce of my supplications, when I cry vnto thee: when I lift vp my handes toward thy holy Oracle.
28:3 Draw me not away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquitie: which speake peace to their neighbors, but mischiefe is in their hearts.
28:4 Giue them according to their deedes, and according to the wickednes of their endeuours: giue them after the worke of their handes, render to them their desert.
28:5 Because they regard not the workes of the Lord, nor the operation of his hands, he shal destroy them, and not build them vp.
28:6 Blessed be the Lord, because he hath heard the voyce of my supplications.
28:7 The Lord is my strength, and my shield, my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly reioyceth, and with my song will I praise him.
28:8 The Lord is their strength, and hee is the sauing strength of his Anointed.
28:9 Saue thy people, and blesse thine inheritance, feede them also, and lift them vp for euer.
text.JPS1985:
28:1 Of David. O LORD, I call to You; my rock, do not disregard me, for if You hold aloof from me, I shall be like those gone down into the Pit.
28:2 Listen to my plea for mercy when I cry out to You, when I lift my hands toward Your inner sanctuary.
28:3 Do not count me with the wicked and evildoers who profess goodwill toward their fellows while malice is in their heart.
28:4 Pay them according to their deeds, their malicious acts; according to their handiwork pay them, give them their deserts.
28:5 For they do not consider the LORD’s deeds, the work of His hands. May He tear them down, never to rebuild them!
28:6 Blessed is the LORD, for He listens to my plea for mercy.
28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in Him. I was helped, and my heart exulted, so I will glorify Him with my song.
28:8 The LORD is their strength; He is a stronghold for the deliverance of His anointed.
28:9 Deliver and bless Your very own people; tend them and sustain them forever.
text.Grail1963:

If you do not heed I shall become
like those in the grave.
2 Hear the voice of my pleading
as I call for help,
as I lift up my hands in prayer
to your holy place.
3 Do not drag me away with the wicked,
with the evildoers,

who speak words of peace to their neighbors
but with evil in their hearts.
4 Repay them as their actions deserve
and the malice of their deeds.
Repay them for the works of their hands;
give them their desserts.
5 For they ignore the deeds of the Lord
and the work of his hands.
(May he ruin them and never rebuild them.)

text.Rotherham:
28:1 Unto thee, O Yahweh, do I cry, O my Rock! do not turn in silence from me,—lest, if thou turn from me in silence, I be like them who go down into the pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my supplication, when I cry to thee for help, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy shrine.
28:3 Do not drag me away—with the lawless, or with the workers of iniquity,—who speak peaceably with their neighbours, but, wrong, is in their heart.
28:4 Give them, according to their deed, and, according to the wrong of their practices,—According to the work of their own hands, give thou unto them, Bring back their own dealing to themselves.
28:5 Because they heed not The Deeds of Yahweh, or the Work of his hands, he will pull them down, and not build them.
28:6 Blessed be Yahweh, Because he hath heard the voice of my supplication.
28:7 Yahweh, is my strength and my shield, In him, hath trusted my heart, and I have found help,—Therefore hath my heart danced for joy, and, with my song, do I praise him.
28:8 Yahweh, is strength to his people,—And, the all-saving refuge of his Anointed One, is he!
28:9 Oh save thy people, and bless thine inheritance, Tend them also, and carry them, Unto times age-abiding.
text.NABRE:
28:1 Psalm 28 Petition and Thanksgiving - Of David. I - To you, LORD, I call; my Rock, do not be deaf to me, Do not be silent toward me, so that I join those who go down to the pit.
28:2 Hear the sound of my pleading when I cry to you for help when I lift up my hands toward your holy place.
28:3 Do not drag me off with the wicked, with those who do wrong, Who speak peace to their neighbors though evil is in their hearts.
28:4 Repay them for their deeds, for the evil that they do. For the work of their hands repay them; give them what they deserve.
28:5 Because they do not understand the LORD’s works, the work of his hands, He will tear them down, never to rebuild them.
28:6 II - Blessed be the LORD, who has heard the sound of my pleading.
28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield, in whom my heart trusts. I am helped, so my heart rejoices; with my song I praise him.
28:8 III - LORD, you are a strength for your people, the saving refuge of your anointed.
28:9 Save your people, bless your inheritance; pasture and carry them forever!
text.DRB:
28:1 The Psalm of David, in the consummation of the tabernacle. Bring to our Lord ye children of God: bring to our Lord the sons of rams.
28:2 Bring to our Lord glory and honour, bring to our Lord glory unto his name: adore ye our Lord in his holy court.
28:3 The voice of our Lord upon waters, the God of majesty hath thundered: Our Lord upon many waters.
28:4 The voice of our Lord in power: the voice of our Lord in magnificence.
28:5 The voice of our Lord breaking cedars: and our Lord shall break the cedars of Libanus:
28:6 And he shall break them in pieces as a calf of Libanus. And the beloved as the son of unicorns.
28:7 The voice of our Lord dividing the flame of fire.
28:8 The voice of our Lord shaking the desert, and our Lord shall move the desert of Cades.
28:9 The voice of our Lord preparing harts, and he shall discover thick woods : and in his temple all shall say glory.
28:10 Our Lord maketh to inhabit the flood: and our Lord shall sit King for ever.
28:11 Our Lord will give strength to his people: our Lord will bless his people in peace.

text.NRSV:
29:1 Ascribe to the Lord, O heavenly beings, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
29:2 Ascribe to the Lord the glory of his name; worship the Lord in holy splendor.
29:3 The voice of the Lord is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the Lord, over mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the Lord is powerful; the voice of the Lord is full of majesty.
29:5 The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars; the Lord breaks the cedars of Lebanon.
29:6 He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, and Sirion like a young wild ox.
29:7 The voice of the Lord flashes forth flames of fire.
29:8 The voice of the Lord shakes the wilderness; the Lord shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
29:9 The voice of the Lord causes the oaks to whirl, and strips the forest bare; and in his temple all say, 'Glory!'
29:10 The Lord sits enthroned over the flood; the Lord sits enthroned as king forever.
29:11 May the Lord give strength to his people! May the Lord bless his people with peace!

text.KJV:
29:1 Giue vnto the Lord (O ye mighty) giue vnto the Lord glory and strength.
29:2 Giue vnto the Lord the glory due vnto his Name; worship the Lord in the beautie of holinesse.
29:3 The voice of the Lord is vpon the waters: the God of glory thundreth, the Lord is vpon many waters.

29:6 He maketh them also to skip like a calfe: Lebanon, and Sirion like a yong Unicorne.

29:11 The Lord will giue strength vnto his people; the Lord will blesse his people with peace.
text.JPS1985:

29:3 The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the LORD, over the mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the LORD is power; the voice of the LORD is majesty;
29:5 the voice of the LORD breaks cedars; the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.

29:8 the voice of the LORD convulses the wilderness; the LORD convulses the wilderness of Kadesh;

text.NABRE:
29:1 Psalm 29 The Lord of Majesty Acclaimed as King of the World - A psalm of David. I - Give to the LORD, you sons of God, give to the LORD glory and might;
29:2 Give to the LORD the glory due his name. Bow down before the LORD’s holy splendor!
29:3 II - The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the LORD, over the mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the LORD is power; the voice of the LORD is splendor.
29:5 The voice of the LORD cracks the cedars; the LORD splinters the cedars of Lebanon,
29:6 Makes Lebanon leap like a calf, and Sirion like a young bull.
29:7 The voice of the LORD strikes with fiery flame;
29:8 the voice of the LORD shakes the desert; the LORD shakes the desert of Kadesh.
29:9 The voice of the LORD makes the deer dance and strips the forests bare. All in his Temple say, “Glory!”
29:10 III - The LORD sits enthroned above the flood! The LORD reigns as king forever!
29:11 May the LORD give might to his people; may the LORD bless his people with peace!
text.DRB:
29:1 A Psalm of Canticle, in the dedication of David's house.
29:2 I will exalt thee, o Lord because thou hast received me: neither hast delighted mine enemies over me.
29:3 O Lord my God I have cried to thee, and thou hast healed me.
29:4 Lord thou hast brought forth my soul out of hell: thou hast saved me from them that go down into the lake.
29:5 Sing to our Lord ye his Saints: and confess to the memory of his holiness.
29:6 Because wrath is in his indignation: and life in his will. At evening shall weeping abide: and in the morning gladness.
29:7 And I said in my aboundance: I will not be moved for ever.
29:8 O Lord in thy will thou hast given strength to my beauty. Thou hast turned away thy face from me, and I became troubled.
29:9 To thee o Lord I will cry: and I will pray to my God.
29:10 What profit is in my blood, whilst I descend into corruption? Shall dust confess to thee, or declare thy truth?
29:11 Our Lord hath heard, and had mercy on me: our Lord is become my helper.
29:12 Thou hast turned my mourning into joy unto me: thou hast cut my sackcloth, and hast compassed me with gladness.
29:13 That my glory may sing to thee, and I be not compunct: Lord my God for ever will I confess to thee.

text.NRSV:
30:1 I will exalt you, O Lord, because you have lifted me up and have not let my enemies triumph over me.
30:2 O Lord my God, I cried out to you, and you restored me to health.
30:3 You brought me up, O Lord, from the dead; you restored my life as I was going down to the grave.
30:4 Sing to the Lord, you servants of his; give thanks for the remembrance of his holiness.
30:5 For his wrath endures but the twinkling of an eye, his favor for a lifetime.
30:6 Weeping may spend the night, but joy comes in the morning.
30:7 While I felt secure, I said, 'I shall never be disturbed; you, Lord, with your favor, made me as strong as the mountains.'
30:8 Then you hid your face, and I was filled with fear.
30:9 I cried to you, O Lord; I pleaded with the Lord, saying,
30:10 'What profit is there in my blood, if I go down to the Pit? will the dust praise you or declare your faithfulness?
30:11 Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me; O Lord, be my helper.'
30:12 You have turned my wailing into dancing; you have put off my sackcloth and clothed me with joy.
30:13 Therefore my heart sings to you without ceasing; O Lord my God, I will give you thanks for ever.

text.KJV:
30:1 I wil extol thee, O Lord, for thou hast lifted me vp; and hast not made my foes to reioyce ouer me.
30:2 O Lord my God, I cried vnto thee, and thou hast healed me.
30:3 O Lord, thou hast brought vp my soule from the graue: thou hast kept me aliue, that I should not goe downe to the pit.
30:4 Sing vnto the Lord, (O yee Saints of his) and giue thanks at the remembrance of his holinesse.
30:5 For his anger endureth but a moment; in his fauour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but ioy commeth in the morning.
30:6 And in my prosperitie I said, I shall neuer be mooued.
30:7 Lord, by thy fauour thou hast made my mountaine to stand strong: Thou didst hide thy face, and I was troubled.
30:8 I cried to thee, O Lord: and vnto the Lord I made supplication.
30:9 What profit is there in my blood, when I goe downe to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy trueth?
30:10 Heare, O Lord, and haue mercie vpon me: Lord be thou my helper.
30:11 Thou hast turned for mee my mourning into dauncing: thou hast put off my sackecloth, and girded mee with gladnesse:
30:12 To the end that my glory may sing prayse to thee, and not be silent: O Lord my God, I will giue thankes vnto thee for euer.
text.JPS1985:
30:1 A psalm of David. A song for the dedication of the House.
30:2 I extol You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up, and not let my enemies rejoice over me.
30:3 O LORD, my God, I cried out to You, and You healed me.
30:4 O LORD, You brought me up from Sheol, preserved me from going down into the Pit.
30:5 O you faithful of the LORD, sing to Him, and praise His holy name.
30:6 For He is angry but a moment, and when He is pleased there is life. One may lie down weeping at nightfall; but at dawn there are shouts of joy.
30:7 When I was untroubled, I thought, “I shall never be shaken, ”
30:8 for You, O LORD, when You were pleased, made [me] firm as a mighty mountain. When You hid Your face, I was terrified.
30:9 I called to You, O LORD; to my Lord I made appeal,
30:10 “What is to be gained from my death, from my descent into the Pit? Can dust praise You? Can it declare Your faithfulness?
30:11 Hear, O LORD, and have mercy on me; O LORD, be my help! ”
30:12 You turned my lament into dancing, you undid my sackcloth and girded me with joy,
30:13 that [my] whole being might sing hymns to You endlessly; O LORD my God, I will praise You forever.
text.Grail1963:
.2 I will praise you, Lord, you have rescued me
and have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
* * *
.3 O Lord, I cried to you for help
and you, my God, have healed me.
.4 O Lord, you have raised my soul from the dead,
restored me to life from those who sink into the grave.
.5 Sing psalms to the Lord, you who love him,
give thanks to his holy name.
.6 His anger lasts a moment; his favor all through life.
At night there are tears, but joy comes with dawn.

"Nothing will ever disturb me."
.8 Your favor had set me on a mountain fastness,
then you hid your face and I was put to confusion.
.9 To you, Lord, I cried,
to my God I made appeal:
.10 "What profit would my death be, my going to the grave?
Can dust give you praise or proclaim your truth?
.11 The Lord listened and had pity.
The Lord came to my help.
.12 For me you have changed my mourning into dancing,
you removed my sackcloth and clothed me with joy.
.13 So my soul sings psalms to you unceasingly.
O Lord my God, I will thank you for ever.
text.Rotherham:
30:1 I will extol thee, O Yahweh, for thou hast drawn me up,—and not suffered my foes to rejoice over me.
30:2 O Yahweh, my God, I cried for help unto thee, and thou hast healed me.
30:3 O Yahweh! thou hast lifted, out of hades, my soul, Thou hast brought me back to life, from among those who were going down to the pit.
30:4 Sweep the strings to Yahweh, ye his men of lovingkindness, and give ye praise at the mention of his holiness.
30:5 For there is, a Moment, in his anger, a Life-time, in his good-pleasure, In the evening, cometh Weeping to lodge, But, by the morning, ’tis a Shout of Triumph!
30:6 But, I, said, in my tranquility, I shall not be shaken to times age-abiding!
30:7 O Yahweh, in thy good-pleasure, hadst thou caused my mountain to stand, strong,—Thou didst hide thy face—I was dismayed!
30:8 Unto thee, O Yahweh, do I cry,—and, unto My Lord, do I make supplication:
30:9 What profit in my blood? in my going down into the pit? Can dust praise thee? Can it declare thy faithfulness?
30:10 Hear, O Yahweh, and show me favour, O Yahweh! become thou a helper unto me.
30:11 Thou hast turned my lamentation, into a dance for me,—Thou hast torn off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness:
30:12 To the end that mine honour should make melody unto thee, and not be silent. O Yahweh, my God! Unto times age-abiding, will I praise thee.
text.NABRE:
30:1 Psalm 30 Thanksgiving for Deliverance - A psalm. A song for the dedication of the Temple. Of David.
30:2 I - I praise you, LORD, for you raised me up and did not let my enemies rejoice over me.
30:3 O LORD, my God, I cried out to you for help and you healed me.
30:4 LORD, you brought my soul up from Sheol; you let me live, from going down to the pit.
30:5 II - Sing praise to the LORD, you faithful; give thanks to his holy memory.
30:6 For his anger lasts but a moment; his favor a lifetime. At dusk weeping comes for the night; but at dawn there is rejoicing.
30:7 III - Complacent, I once said, “I shall never be shaken.”
30:8 LORD, you showed me favor, established for me mountains of virtue. But when you hid your face I was struck with terror.
30:9 To you, LORD, I cried out; with the Lord I pleaded for mercy:
30:10 “What gain is there from my lifeblood, from my going down to the grave? Does dust give you thanks or declare your faithfulness?
30:11 Hear, O LORD, have mercy on me; LORD, be my helper.”
30:12 IV - You changed my mourning into dancing; you took off my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness.
30:13 So that my glory may praise you and not be silent. O LORD, my God, forever will I give you thanks.
text.DRB:
30:1 Unto the end, the Psalm of David, for excess of mind.
30:2 In thee o Lord have I hoped, let me not be confounded for ever: in thy justice deliver me.
30:3 Incline thine ear to me, make haste to deliver me. Be unto me for a God protector: and for a house of refuge, that thou mayst save me.
30:4 Because thou art my strength, and my refuge: and for thy name thou wilt conduct me, and wilt nourish me.
30:5 Thou wilt bring me out of this snare, which they have hid for me: because thou art my protector.
30:6 Into thy hands I commend my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, o Lord God of truth.
30:7 Thou hast hated them that observe vanities, unprofitably. But I have hoped in our Lord:
30:8 Ishall rejoice and be joyful in thy mercy. Because thou hast respected my humility, thou hast saved my soul out of necessities.
30:9 Neither hast thou shut me up in the hands of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large place.
30:10 Have mercy upon me, o Lord, because I am in tribulation: mine eye is troubled for wrath, my soul, and my belly:
30:11 Because my life is decayed for sorrow: and my years for groanings. My strength is weakened for poverty; and my bones are troubled.
30:12 Above all mine enemies I am made a reproach, both to my neighbours exceedingly: and a fear to my acquaintance. They that saw me, fled forth from me:
30:13 I am forgotten, from the heart as one dead. I am made as a vessel destroyed:
30:14 because I have heard the reprehension of many that abide round about: In that whilst, they assembled together against me, they consulted to take my soul.
30:15 But I have hoped in thee. O Lord: I said, Thou art my God:
30:16 my lots are in thy hands. Deliver me out of the hands of my enemies, and from them that persecute me.
30:17 Illustrate thy face upon thy servant, save me in thy mercy:
30:18 Lord let me not be confounded, because I have invocated thee. Let the impious be ashamed, and brought down into hell:
30:19 let the deceitful lips be made mute. Which speak iniquity against the just, in pride and abuse.
30:20 How great is the multitude of thy sweetness, o Lord which thou hast hid for them that fear thee. Thou hast perfited it for them that hope in thee, in the sight of the children of men.
30:21 Thou shalt hide them in the secret of thy face from the disturbance of men. Thou shall protect them in thy tabernacle from the contradiction of tongues.
30:22 Blessed be our Lord: because he hath made his mercy marvelous to me in the fenced city.
30:23 But I have said in the excess of my mind: I am cast away from the sight of thine eyes. Therefore thou hast heard the voice of my prayer, whilst I cried to thee.
30:24 Love our Lord all ye his Saints: because our Lord will require truth, & will repay them aboundantly that do proudly.
30:25 Do ye manfully, and let your heart take courage, all ye that hope in our Lord.

text.NRSV:
31:1 In you, O Lord, have I taken refuge; let me never be put to shame; deliver me in your righteousness.
31:2 Incline your ear to me; make haste to deliver me.
31:3 Be my strong rock, a castle to keep me safe, for you are my crag and my stronghold;
31:4 For the sake of your Name, lead me and guide me.
31:5 Take me out of the net that they have secretly set for me, for you are my tower of strength.
31:6 Into your hands I commend my spirit, for you have redeemed me, O Lord, O God of truth.
31:7 I hate those who hold to worthless idols, but I trust in the Lord.
31:8 I will boast and be glad because of your mercy; for you have seen my affliction; you have known my distress.
31:9 You have not abandoned me into the hands of the enemy; you have set my feet in an open place.
31:10 Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am in trouble; my eye is consumed with sorrow, and also my throat and my belly.
31:11 For my life is wasted with grief, and my years with sighing; my strength fails me because of affliction, and my bones are consumed.
31:12 I have become a reproach to all my enemies and even to my neighbors, a dismay to those of my acquaintance; when they see me in the street they avoid me.
31:13 I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind; I am as useless as a broken pot.
31:14 For I have heard the whispering of the crowd; fear is all around; they put their heads together against me; they plot to take my life.
31:15 But as for me, I have trusted in you, O Lord. I have said, 'You are my God.'
31:16 My times are in your hand; rescue me from the hand of my enemies and from those who persecute me.
31:17 Make your face to shine upon your servant, and in your loving-kindness save me.
31:18 Lord, let me not be ashamed for having called upon you; rather, let the wicked be put to shame; let them be silent in the grave.
31:19 Let the lying lips be silenced which speak against the righteousness, insolently, disdainfully, and with contempt.
31:20 How great is your goodness, O Lord! which you have laid up for those who fear you; which you have done in the sight of all for those who put their trust in you.
31:21 You hide them in the covert of your presence from those who confederate against them; you keep them in your shelter from the strife of tongues.
31:22 Blessed be the Lord! for he has shown me the wonders of his love in a besieged city.
31:23 Yet I said in my alarm, 'I am cut off from the sight of your eyes.' Nevertheless, you heard the voice of my prayer when I cried out to you.
31:24 Love the Lord, all you who worship him; the Lord protects the faithful, but repays to the full the proud.
31:25 Be strong and let your heart take courage, all you who wait for the Lord.
