text.KJV:
31:1 In thee, O Lord, doe I put my trust, let me neuer be ashamed: deliuer me in thy righteousnesse.
31:2 Bowe downe thine eare to me, deliuer me speedily: be thou my strong rocke, for an house of defence to saue me.
31:3 For thou art my rocke and my fortresse: therfore for thy names sake lead me, and guide me.
31:4 Pull me out of the net, that they haue layd priuily for me: for thou art my strength.
31:5 Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed mee, O Lord God of trueth.
31:6 I haue hated them that regard lying vanities: but I trust in the Lord.
31:7 I will be glad, and reioyce in thy mercie: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast knowen my soule in aduersities;
31:8 And hast not shut me vp into the hand of the enemie: thou hast set my feete in a large roome.
31:9 Haue mercy vpon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble; mine eie is consumed with griefe, yea my soule and my belly.
31:10 For my life is spent with griefe, and my yeeres with sighing: my strength faileth, because of mine iniquitie, and my bones are consumed.
31:11 I was a reproch among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a feare to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without, fled from me.
31:12 I am forgotten as a dead man out of minde: I am like a broken vessell.
31:13 For I haue heard the slaunder of many, feare was on euery side: while they tooke counsell together against me, they deuised to take away my life.
31:14 But I trusted in thee, O Lord: I sayd, Thou art my God.
31:15 My times are in thy hand: deliuer me from the hand of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me.
31:16 Make thy face to shine vpon thy seruant: saue me for thy mercies sake.
31:17 Let mee not be ashamed, O Lord, for I haue called vpon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be silent in the graue.
31:18 Let the lying lippes be put to silence: which speake grieuous things proudly and contemptuously against the righteous.
31:19 O how great is thy goodnesse, which thou hast layd vp for them that feare thee: which thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee, before the sonnes of men!
31:20 Thou shalt hide them in the secret of thy presence, from the pride of man: thou shalt keepe them secretly in a pauilion, from the strife of tongues.
31:21 Blessed be the Lord; for hee hath shewed me his maruellous kindnesse, in a strong citie.
31:22 For I sayd in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eies: Neuerthelesse thou heardest the voice of my supplications, when I cryed vnto thee.
31:23 O loue the Lord, all yee his Saints: for the Lord preserueth the faithfull, and plentifully rewardeth the proud doer.
31:24 Be of good courage, and hee shall strenghten your heart: all ye that hope in the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
31:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
31:2 I seek refuge in You, O LORD; may I never be disappointed; as You are righteous, rescue me.
31:3 Incline Your ear to me; be quick to save me; be a rock, a stronghold for me, a citadel, for my deliverance.
31:4 For You are my rock and my fortress; You lead me and guide me as befits Your name.
31:5 You free me from the net laid for me, for You are my stronghold.
31:6 Into Your hand I entrust my spirit; You redeem me, O LORD, faithful God.
31:7 I detest those who rely on empty folly, but I trust in the LORD.
31:8 Let me exult and rejoice in Your faithfulness when You notice my affliction, are mindful of my deep distress,
31:9 and do not hand me over to my enemy, but grant me relief.
31:10 Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eyes are wasted by vexation, my substance and body too.
31:11 My life is spent in sorrow, my years in groaning; my strength fails because of my iniquity, my limbs waste away.
31:12 Because of all my foes I am the particular butt of my neighbors, a horror to my friends; those who see me on the street avoid me.
31:13 I am put out of mind like the dead; I am like an object given up for lost.
31:14 I hear the whisperings of many, intrigue on every side, as they scheme together against me, plotting to take my life.
31:15 But I trust in You, O LORD; I say, “You are my God! ”
31:16 My fate is in Your hand; save me from the hand of my enemies and pursuers.
31:17 Show favor to Your servant; as You are faithful, deliver me.
31:18 O LORD, let me not be disappointed when I call You; let the wicked be disappointed; let them be silenced in Sheol;
31:19 let lying lips be stilled that speak haughtily against the righteous with arrogance and contempt.
31:20 How abundant is the good that You have in store for those who fear You, that You do in the full view of men for those who take refuge in You.
31:21 You grant them the protection of Your presence against scheming men; You shelter them in Your pavilion from contentious tongues.
31:22 Blessed is the LORD, for He has been wondrously faithful to me, a veritable bastion.
31:23 Alarmed, I had thought, “I am thrust out of Your sight”; yet You listened to my plea for mercy when I cried out to You.
31:24 So love the LORD, all you faithful; the LORD guards the loyal, and more than requites him who acts arrogantly.
31:25 Be strong and of good courage, all you who wait for the LORD.
text.Grail1963:
.2 In you, O Lord, I take refuge.
Let me never be put to shame.
In your justice, set me free,
.3 hear me and speedily rescue me.
Be a rock of refuge for me,
a mighty stronghold to save me,
.4 for you are my rock, my stronghold.
For your name's sake, lead me and guide me.
.5 Release me from the snares they have hidden
for you are my refuge, Lord.
.6 Into your hands I commend my spirit.
It is you who will redeem me, Lord.
/.7 O God of truth, you detest

those who worship false and empty gods.
.8 As for me, I trust in the Lord:
let me be glad and rejoice in your love.
You who have seen my affliction
and taken heed of my soul's distress,
.9 have not handed me over to the enemy,
but set my feet at large.
* * *
.10 Have mercy on me, O Lord,
for I am in distress.
Tears have wasted my eyes,
my throat and my heart.
.11 For my life is spent with sorrow
and my years with sighs.
Affliction has broken down my strength
and my bones waste away.
.12 In the face of all my foes
I am a reproach,
an object of scorn to my neighbors
and of fear to my friends.
Those who see me in the street
run far away from me.
.13 I am like a dead man, forgotten,
like a thing thrown away.
.14 I have heard the slander of the crowd,
fear is all around me,
as they plot together against me,
as they plan to take my life.
.15 But as for me, I trust in you, Lord;
I say: "You are my God.
.16 My life is in your hands, deliver me
from the hands of those who hate me.
.17 Let your face shine on your servant.
Save me in your love.
.18 Let me not be put to shame for I call you,
let the wicked be shamed!
Let them be silenced in the grave,
.19 let lying lips be dumb,
that speak haughtily against the just
with pride and contempt."
* * *
.20 How great is the goodness, Lord,
that you keep for those who fear you,
that you show to those who trust you
in the sight of men.

.21 You hide them in the shelter of your presence
from the plotting of men;
you keep them safe within your tent
from disputing tongues.
.22 Blessed be the Lord who has shown me
the wonders of his love
in a fortified city.
.23 "I am far removed from your sight"
I said in my alarm.
Yet you heard the voice of my plea
when I cried for help.
.24 Love the Lord, all you saints.
He guards his faithful
but the Lord will repay to the full
those who act with pride.
.25 Be strong, let your heart take courage,
all who hope in the Lord.
text.Rotherham:
31:1 In thee, O Yahweh, have I sought refuge, Let me not be ashamed to times age-abiding, In thy righteousness, deliver me:
31:2 Incline unto me, thine ear, Speedily rescue me,—Become to me a Rock of refuge, a Place of security, For saving me.
31:3 Because, my mountain crag and my stronghold, thou art, Therefore, for the sake of thine own Name, wilt thou lead me and guide me?
31:4 Wilt thou bring me forth, out of the net which they have hidden for me? For, thou, art my refuge.
31:5 Into thy hand, do I commit my spirit—Thou hast ransomed me, O Yahweh, GOD most faithful.
31:6 I hate such as give heed to false vanities, I, then, in Yahweh, have set my hope.
31:7 I will indeed exult and rejoice, in thy lovingkindness,—In that thou hast looked upon my humiliation, thou hast taken note that in distresses was my life;
31:8 And hast not shut me up in the hand of the foe, Thou hast given standing, in a roomy place, unto my feet.
31:9 Show me favour, O Yahweh, for in distress am I,—Wasted with vexation, is mine eye—my soul and my body;
31:10 For, consumed with sorrow, is my life, and my years with sighing,—My strength hath staggered with my humiliation, and, my bones, are without marrow.
31:11 By reason of all mine adversaries, have I become a reproach. Even to my neighbours, altogether, and a dread to mine acquaintance,—They who have seen me abroad, have fled from me:
31:12 I have been forgotten, like one dead—out of mind, I have been as a missing vessel.
31:13 For I have heard the whispering of many—A terror round about! When they have sat in conclave together against me, to take away my life, have they intrigued.
31:14 I, therefore. in thee, have put my trust, O Yahweh, I have said, My God, thou art!
31:15 In thy hand, are my times, Rescue me from the hand of my foes and from my pursuers!
31:16 Cause thy face to shine upon thy servant, Save me in thy lovingkindness.
31:17 O Yahweh! let me not be ashamed, For I have called upon thee, Let the lawless be ashamed, Go down in silence to hades!
31:18 Let false lips be made dumb,—which are speaking—against a righteous one—arrogantly, with pride and contempt.
31:19 How great is thy goodness, which thou hast hidden away for them who revere thee,—Thou hast wrought for them who seek refuge in thee, in sight of the sons of men.
31:20 Thou wilt conceal them, in the secrecy of thine own presence, from the conspiracies of men,—Thou wilt hide them in a pavilion, from the strife of tongues.
31:21 Blessed be Yahweh, for he hath made wonderful his lovingkindness for me, in a fortified city.
31:22 But, I, had said in mine alarm, I am cut off from before thine eyes,—But, indeed, thou didst hear the voice of my supplication, when I cried for help unto thee.
31:23 Love Yahweh, all ye his men of lovingkindness,—Faithfulness, doth Yahweh observe, but repayeth abundantly him that worketh proudly.
31:24 Be strong, and let your heart be bold, all ye who are waiting for Yahweh.
text.NABRE:
31:1 Psalm 31 Prayer in Distress and Thanksgiving for Escape - For the leader. A psalm of David.
31:2 I - In you, LORD, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame. In your righteousness deliver me;
31:3 incline your ear to me; make haste to rescue me! Be my rock of refuge, a stronghold to save me.
31:4 For you are my rock and my fortress; for your name’s sake lead me and guide me.
31:5 Free me from the net they have set for me, for you are my refuge.
31:6 Into your hands I commend my spirit; you will redeem me, LORD, God of truth.
31:7 You hate those who serve worthless idols, but I trust in the LORD.
31:8 I will rejoice and be glad in your mercy, once you have seen my misery, [and] gotten to know the distress of my soul.
31:9 You will not abandon me into enemy hands, but will set my feet in a free and open space.
31:10 II - Be gracious to me, LORD, for I am in distress; affliction is wearing down my eyes, my throat and my insides.
31:11 My life is worn out by sorrow, and my years by sighing. My strength fails in my affliction; my bones are wearing down.
31:12 To all my foes I am a thing of scorn, and especially to my neighbors a horror to my friends. When they see me in public, they quickly shy away.
31:13 I am forgotten, out of mind like the dead; I am like a worn-out tool.
31:14 I hear the whispers of the crowd; terrors are all around me. They conspire together against me; they plot to take my life.
31:15 But I trust in you, LORD; I say, “You are my God.”
31:16 My destiny is in your hands; rescue me from my enemies, from the hands of my pursuers.
31:17 Let your face shine on your servant; save me in your mercy.
31:18 Do not let me be put to shame, for I have called to you, LORD. Put the wicked to shame; reduce them to silence in Sheol.
31:19 Strike dumb their lying lips, which speak arrogantly against the righteous in contempt and scorn.
31:20 III - How great is your goodness, Lord, stored up for those who fear you. You display it for those who trust you, in the sight of the children of Adam.
31:21 You hide them in the shelter of your presence, safe from scheming enemies. You conceal them in your tent, away from the strife of tongues.
31:22 Blessed be the LORD, marvelously he showed to me his mercy in a fortified city.
31:23 Though I had said in my alarm, “I am cut off from your eyes.” Yet you heard my voice, my cry for mercy, when I pleaded with you for help.
31:24 Love the LORD, all you who are faithful to him. The LORD protects the loyal, but repays the arrogant in full.
31:25 Be strong and take heart, all who hope in the LORD.
text.DRB:
31:1 To David himself understanding. Blessed are they, whose iniquities are forgiven: and whose sins be covered.
31:2 Blessed is the man, to whom our Lord hath not imputed sin, neither is there guile in his spirit.
31:3 Because I held my peace, my bones are inveterated, whilst I cried all the day.
31:4 Because day & night thy hand is made heavy upon me: I am turned in my anguish, whilst the thorn is fastened.
31:5 I have made my sin known to thee: and my injustice I have not hid. I said: I will confess against me my injustice to our Lord: and thou hast forgiven the impiety of my sin.
31:6 For this shall every holy one pray to thee, in time convenient. But yet in the flood of many waters, they shall not approach to him.
31:7 Thou art my refuge from tribulation, which hath compassed me: my exultation, deliver me from them that compass me.
31:8 I will give thee understanding, and will instruct thee in the way, that thou shalt go: I will fasten mine eyes upon thee.
31:9 Do not become as horse and mule, which have no understanding. In bit and bridle bind fast their cheeks that approach not to thee.
31:10 Many are the scourges of a sinner, but him that hopeth in our Lord mercy shall compass.
31:11 Be joyful in our Lord and rejoice ye just, and glory all ye right of heart.

text.NRSV:
32:1 Happy are those whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
32:2 Happy are those to whom the Lord imputes no iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
32:3 While I kept silence, my body wasted away through my groaning all day long.
32:4 For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength was dried up as by the heat of summer. Selah
32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to you, and I did not hide my iniquity; I said, 'I will confess my transgressions to the Lord,' and you forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah
32:6 Therefore let all who are faithful offer prayer to you; at a time of distress, the rush of mighty waters shall not reach them.
32:7 You are a hiding place for me; you preserve me from trouble; you surround me with glad cries of deliverance. Selah
32:8 I will instruct you and teach you the way you should go; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
32:9 Do not be like a horse or a mule, without understanding, whose temper must be curbed with bit and bridle, else it will not stay near you.
32:10 Many are the torments of the wicked, but steadfast love surrounds those who trust in the Lord.
32:11 Be glad in the Lord and rejoice, O righteous, and shout for joy, all you upright in heart.
text.Coverdale:
32:1 Happy are they whose transgressions are forgiven, *
      and whose sin is put away!
32:2 Happy are they to whom the LORD imputes no guilt, *
      and in whose spirit there is no guile!
32:3 While I held my tongue, my bones withered away, *
      because of my groaning all day long.
32:4 For your hand was heavy upon me day and night; *
      my moisture was dried up as in the heat of summer.
32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to you, *
      and did not conceal my guilt.
32:6 I said, "I will confess my transgressions to the LORD." *
      Then you forgave me the guilt of my sin.
32:7 Therefore all the faithful will make their prayers to you in time of trouble; *
      when the great waters overflow, they shall not reach them.
32:8 You are my hiding-place;
      you preserve me from trouble; *
      you surround me with shouts of deliverance.
32:9 "I will instruct you and teach you in the way that you should go; *
      I will guide you with my eye.
32:10 Do not be like horse or mule, which have no understanding; *
      who must be fitted with bit and bridle,
      or else they will not stay near you."
32:11 Great are the tribulations of the wicked; *
      but mercy embraces those who trust in the LORD.
32:12 Be glad, you righteous, and rejoice in the LORD; *
      shout for joy, all who are true of heart.
text.KJV:
32:1 Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiuen, whose sinne is couered.
32:2 Blessed is the man vnto whom the Lord imputeth not iniquitie: and in whose spirit there is no guile.
32:3 When I kept silence, my bones waxed old; through my roaring all the day long.
32:4 For day and night thy hand was heauy vpon me: my moisture is turned into the drought of summer. Selah.
32:5 I acknowledged my sin vnto thee, and mine iniquitie haue I not hid: I said, I will confesse my transgressions vnto the Lord; and thou forgauest the iniquitie of my sinne. Selah.
32:6 For this shall euery one that is godly pray vnto thee, in a time when thou mayest bee found: surely in the floods of great waters, they shall not come nigh vnto him.
32:7 Thou art my hiding place, thou shalt preserue mee from trouble: thou shalt compasse me about with songs of deliuerance. Selah.
32:8 I will instruct thee, and teach thee in the way which thou shalt goe: I will guide thee with mine eye.
32:9 Be yee not as the horse, or as the mule which haue no vnderstanding: whose mouth must be held in with bit and bridle, least they come neere vnto thee.
32:10 Many sorrowes shall be to the wicked: but he that trusteth in the Lord, mercy shall compasse him about.
32:11 Be glad in the Lord, and reioyce yee righteous: and shout for ioy all ye that are vpright in heart.
text.JPS1985:
32:1 Of David. A maskil. Happy is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered over.
32:2 Happy the man whom the LORD does not hold guilty, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
32:3 As long as I said nothing, my limbs wasted away from my anguished roaring all day long.
32:4 For night and day Your hand lay heavy on me; my vigor waned as in the summer drought. Selah.
32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to You; I did not cover up my guilt; I resolved, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD, ” and You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah.
32:6 Therefore let every faithful man pray to You upon discovering [his sin], that the rushing mighty waters not overtake him.
32:7 You are my shelter; You preserve me from distress; You surround me with the joyous shouts of deliverance. Selah.
32:8 Let me enlighten you and show you which way to go; let me offer counsel; my eye is on you.
32:9 Be not like a senseless horse or mule whose movement must be curbed by bit and bridle; far be it from you!
32:10 Many are the torments of the wicked, but he who trusts in the LORD shall be surrounded with favor.
32:11 Rejoice in the LORD and exult, O you righteous; shout for joy, all upright men!
text.Grail1963:
1 Happy the man whose offense is forgiven,
whose sin is remitted.
2 O happy the man to whom the Lord
imputes no guilt,
in whose spirit is no guile.
3 I kept it secret and my frame was wasted.
I groaned all day long,
4 for night and day your hand was heavy upon me.
Indeed my strength was dried up
as by the summer's heat.
5 But now I have acknowledged my sins;
my guilt I did not hide.
I said: "I will confess
my offense to the Lord."
And you, Lord, have forgiven
the guilt of my sin.
6 So let every good man pray to you
in the time of need.
The floods of water may reach high
but him they shall not reach.
7 You are my hiding place, O Lord;
you save me from distress.
(You surround me with cries of deliverance.)
* * *
8 I will instruct you and teach you

the way you should go;
I will give you counsel
with my eye upon you.
9 Be not like horse and mule, unintelligent,
needing bridle and bit
else they will not approach you.
10 Many sorrows has the wicked
but he who trusts in the Lord,
loving mercy surrounds him.
* * *
11 Rejoice, rejoice in the Lord,
exult, you just!
O come, ring out your joy,
all you upright of heart.
text.Rotherham:
32:1 How happy is he whose transgression is forgiven! whose sin is pardoned!
32:2 How happy the son of earth, to whom Yahweh will not reckon iniquity! and in whose spirit is no guile!
32:3 When I kept silence, my bones became worn out, Through my groaning all the day;
32:4 For, day and night, heavy upon me, was thy hand,—Changed was my life-sap into the drought of summer. [Selah.]
32:5 My sin, would I own unto thee, and, mine iniquity, not hide, I said, I will confess my transgressions unto Yahweh, And, thou, didst forgive the iniquity of my sin. [Selah.]
32:6 For this cause, will every man of lovingkindness pray unto thee, in time to obtain,—Surely, in the overflow of many waters, unto him, shall they not reach.
32:7 Thou, art a hiding-place for me, From distress, wilt thou preserve me,—With shouts of deliverance, wilt thou compass me about. [Selah.]
32:8 I will make thee discreet, I will point out to thee the way which thou must go, I will fix upon thee mine eye.
32:9 Do not ye become like a horse, like a mule, without discernment,—With the bit and bridle of his mouth, [hast thou] to restrain him,—He will not come near unto thee.
32:10 Many pains, hath the lawless one,—But, he that trusteth in Yahweh, Lovingkindness, shall compass him about.
32:11 Rejoice in Yahweh and exult, O ye righteous, Yea, shout in triumph, all ye upright in heart!
text.NABRE:
32:1 Psalm 32 Remission of Sin - Of David. A maskil. I - Blessed is the one whose fault is removed, whose sin is forgiven.
32:2 Blessed is the man to whom the LORD imputes no guilt, in whose spirit is no deceit.
32:3 II - Because I kept silent, my bones wasted away; I groaned all day long.
32:4 For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength withered as in dry summer heat. Selah
32:5 Then I declared my sin to you; my guilt I did not hide. I said, “I confess my transgression to the LORD,” and you took away the guilt of my sin. Selah
32:6 Therefore every loyal person should pray to you in time of distress. Though flood waters threaten, they will never reach him.
32:7 You are my shelter; you guard me from distress; with joyful shouts of deliverance you surround me. Selah
32:8 III - I will instruct you and show you the way you should walk, give you counsel with my eye upon you.
32:9 Do not be like a horse or mule, without understanding; with bit and bridle their temper is curbed, else they will not come to you.
32:10 IV - Many are the sorrows of the wicked one, but mercy surrounds the one who trusts in the LORD.
32:11 Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous; exult, all you upright of heart.
text.DRB:
32:1 The Psalm of David. Rejoice ye just in our Lord; praising becometh the righteous.
32:2 Confess ye to our Lord on the harp: on a psalter of ten strings sing to him.
32:3 Sing ye to him a new song: sing well to him in jubilation.
32:4 Because the word of our Lord is right, and all his works are in faith.
32:5 He loveth mercy and judgment: the earth is full of the mercy of our Lord.
32:6 By the word of our Lord the heavens are established: and by the spirit of his mouth all the power of them,
32:7 Gathering together the waters of the sea, as it were in a bottle: putting the depths in treasures.
32:8 Let all the earth fear our Lord: and let all the inhabitants of the world be moved at him.
32:9 Because he said, and they were made: he commanded and they were created,
32:10 Our Lord dissipateth the counsels of nations: and he reproveth the cogitations of people, and he reproveth the counsels of princes.
32:11 But the counsel of our Lord abideth for ever, the cogitations of his heart in generation and generation.
32:12 Blessed is the nation, whose God is our Lord: the people whom he hath chosen for his inheritance.
32:13 Our Lord hath looked from heaven: he hath seen all the children of men.
32:14 From his prepared habitation he hath looked upon all that inhabit the earth
32:15 Who made their hearts severally: who understandeth all their works.
32:16 The king is not saved by much power: and the giant shall not be saved in the multitude of his strength.
32:17 The horse faileth to safety, and in the aboundance of his force he shall not be saved.
32:18 Behold the eyes of our Lord be upon them that fear him: and on them, that hope upon his mercy.
32:19 That he may deliver their souls from death: and nourish them in famine.
32:20 Our soul expecteth our Lord: because he is our helper and protector.
32:21 Because in him our heart shall rejoice: and we have trusted in his holy name.
32:22 Let thy mercy, o Lord, be made upon us, as we have hoped in thee.

text.NRSV:
33:1 Rejoice in the Lord, O you righteous. Praise befits the upright.
33:2 Praise the Lord with the lyre; make melody to him with the harp of ten strings.
33:3 Sing to him a new song; play skillfully on the strings, with loud shouts.
33:4 For the word of the Lord is upright, and all his work is done in faithfulness.
33:5 He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the steadfast love of the Lord.
33:6 By the word of the Lord the heavens were made, and all their host by the breath of his mouth.
33:7 He gathers the waters of the sea as in a bottle; he puts the deeps in storehouses.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the Lord; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
33:9 For he spoke, and it came to be; he commanded, and it stood firm.
33:10 The Lord brings the counsel of the nations to nothing; he frustrates the plans of the peoples.
33:11 The counsel of the Lord stands forever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
33:12 Happy is the nation whose God is the Lord, the people whom he has chosen as his heritage.
33:13 The Lord looks down from heaven; he sees all humankind.
33:14 From where he sits enthroned he watches all the inhabitants of the earth—
33:15 he who fashions the hearts of them all, and observes all their deeds.
33:16 A king is not saved by his great army; a warrior is not delivered by his great strength.
33:17 The war horse is a vain hope for victory, and by its great might it cannot save.
33:18 Truly the eye of the Lord is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love,
33:19 to deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
33:20 Our soul waits for the Lord; he is our help and our shield.
33:21 Our heart is glad in him, because we trust in his holy name.
33:22 Let your steadfast love, O Lord, be upon us, even as we hope in you.
text.Coverdale:
33:1 Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous; *
      it is good for the just to sing praises.
33:2 Praise the LORD with the harp; *
      play to him upon the psaltery and lyre.
33:3 Sing for him a new song; *
      sound a fanfare with all your skill upon the trumpet.
33:4 For the word of the LORD is right, *
      and all his works are sure.
33:5 He loves righteousness and justice; *
      the loving-kindness of the LORD fills the whole earth.
33:6 By the word of the LORD were the heavens made, *
      by the breath of his mouth all the heavenly hosts.
33:7 He gathers up the waters of the ocean as in a water-skin *
      and stores up the depths of the sea.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; *
      let all who dwell in the world stand in awe of him.
33:9 For he spoke, and it came to pass; *
      he commanded, and it stood fast.
33:10 The LORD brings the will of the nations to naught; *
      he thwarts the designs of the peoples.
33:11 But the LORD's will stands fast for ever, *
      and the designs of his heart from age to age.
33:12 Happy is the nation whose God is the LORD! *
      happy the people he has chosen to be his own!
33:13 The LORD looks down from heaven, *
      and beholds all the people in the world.
33:14 From where he sits enthroned he turns his gaze *
      on all who dwell on the earth.
33:15 He fashions all the hearts of them *
      and understands all their works.
33:16 There is no king that can be saved by a mighty army; *
      a strong man is not delivered by his great strength.
33:17 The horse is a vain hope for deliverance; *
      for all its strength it cannot save.
33:18 Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon those who fear him, *
      on those who wait upon his love,
33:19 To pluck their lives from death, *
      and to feed them in time of famine.
33:20 Our soul waits for the LORD; *
      he is our help and our shield.
33:21 Indeed, our heart rejoices in him, *
      for in his holy Name we put our trust.
33:22 Let your loving-kindness, O LORD, be upon us, *
      as we have put our trust in you.
text.KJV:
33:1 Reioyce in the Lord, O yee righteous: for prayse is comely for the vpright.
33:2 Praise the Lord with harp: sing vnto him with the Psalterie, and an instrument of ten strings.
33:3 Sing vnto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud noise.
33:4 For the word of the Lord is right: and all his workes are done in trueth.
33:5 Hee loueth righteousnesse and iudgement: the earth is ful of the goodnesse of the Lord.
33:6 By the word of the Lord were the heauens made: and all the host of them, by the breath of his mouth.
33:7 He gathereth the waters of the sea together, as an heape: he layeth vp the depth in storehouses.
33:8 Let all the earth feare the Lord: let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
33:9 For he spake, and it was done: he commanded, and it stood fast.
33:10 The Lord bringeth the counsell of the heathen to nought: he maketh the deuices of the people, of none effect.
33:11 The counsaile of the Lord standeth for euer, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
33:12 Blessed is the nation, whose God is the Lord: and the people, whom he hath chosen for his owne inheritance.
33:13 The Lord looketh from heauen: he beholdeth all the sonnes of men.
33:14 From the place of his habitation, he looketh vpon all the inhabitants of the earth.
33:15 He fashioneth their hearts alike: he considereth all their workes.
33:16 There is no king saued by the multitude of an hoste: a mightie man is not deliuered by much strength.
33:17 An horse is a vaine thing for safetie: neither shall he deliuer any by his great strength.
33:18 Behold, the eye of the Lord is vpon them that feare him: vpon them that hope in his mercy:
33:19 To deliuer their soule from death, and to keepe them aliue in famine.
33:20 Our soule waiteth for the Lord: he is our helpe, and our shield.
33:21 For our heart shall reioyce in him: because we haue trusted in his holy name.
33:22 Let thy mercy (O Lord) be vpon vs: according as we hope in thee.
text.JPS1985:
33:1 Sing forth, O you righteous, to the LORD; it is fit that the upright acclaim Him.
33:2 Praise the LORD with the lyre; with the ten-stringed harp sing to Him;
33:3 sing Him a new song; play sweetly with shouts of joy.
33:4 For the word of the LORD is right; His every deed is faithful.
33:5 He loves what is right and just; the earth is full of the LORD’s faithful care.
33:6 By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, by the breath of His mouth, all their host.
33:7 He heaps up the ocean waters like a mound, stores the deep in vaults.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world dread Him.
33:9 For He spoke, and it was; He commanded, and it endured.
33:10 The LORD frustrates the plans of nations, brings to naught the designs of peoples.
33:11 What the LORD plans endures forever, what He designs, for ages on end.
33:12 Happy the nation whose God is the LORD, the people He has chosen to be His own.
33:13 The LORD looks down from heaven; He sees all mankind.
33:14 From His dwelling-place He gazes on all the inhabitants of the earth—
33:15 He who fashions the hearts of them all, who discerns all their doings.
33:16 Kings are not delivered by a large force; warriors are not saved by great strength;
33:17 horses are a false hope for deliverance; for all their great power they provide no escape.
33:18 Truly the eye of the LORD is on those who fear Him, who wait for His faithful care
33:19 to save them from death, to sustain them in famine.
33:20 We set our hope on the LORD, He is our help and shield;
33:21 in Him our hearts rejoice, for in His holy name we trust.
33:22 May we enjoy, O LORD, Your faithful care, as we have put our hope in You.
text.Grail1963:
1 Ring out your joy to the Lord, O you just;
for praise is fitting for loyal hearts.
2 Give thanks to the Lord upon the harp,
with a tenstringed lute sing him songs.
3 O sing him a song that is new,
play loudly, with all your skill.
4 For the word of the Lord is faithful
and all his works to be trusted.
5 The Lord loves justice and right
and fills the earth with his love.
6 By his word the heavens were made,
by the breath of his mouth all the stars.
7 He collects the waves of the ocean;
he stores up the depths of the sea.
8 Let all the earth fear the Lord
all who live in the world revere him.
9 He spoke; and it came to be.
He commanded; it sprang into being.
10 He frustrates the designs of the nations,
he defeats the plans of the peoples.
11 His own designs shall stand for ever,
the plans of his heart from age to age.
12 They are happy, whose God is the Lord,
the people he has chosen as his own.
13 From the heavens the Lord looks forth,
he sees all the children of men.
14 From the place where he dwells he gazes
on all the dwellers on the earth;

15 he who shapes the hearts of them all;
and considers all their deeds.
16 A king is not saved by his army,
nor a warrior preserved by his strength.
17 A vain hope for safety is the horse;
despite its power it cannot save.
18 The Lord looks on those who revere him,
on those who hope in his love,
19 to rescue their souls from death,
to keep them alive in famine.
20 Our soul is waiting for the Lord.
The Lord is our help and our shield.
21 In him do our hearts find joy.
We trust in his holy name.
22 May your love be upon us, O Lord,
as we place all our hope in you.
text.Rotherham:
33:1 Shout for joy, ye righteous, in Yahweh, To the upright, seemly is praise.
33:2 Give ye thanks unto Yahweh with the lyre, With a harp of ten strings, make ye music unto him.
33:3 Sing unto him, a song that is new, With skill, sweep the strings, with loud noise.
33:4 For right is the word of Yahweh, and, all his work, is in faithfulness:
33:5 Who loveth righteousness and justice, With the lovingkindness of Yahweh, the earth is full.
33:6 By the word of Yahweh, the heavens were made, and, by the spirit of his mouth, all their host:
33:7 Who gathered as into a skin-bottle the waters of the sea, Delivering, into treasuries, the roaring deeps.
33:8 Let all the earth, stand in awe of Yahweh, Of him, be in dread, all ye inhabitants of the world;
33:9 For, he, spake, and it was, He, commanded, and it stood forth.
33:10 Yahweh, hath frustrated the counsel of nations, hath brought to nothing the devices of peoples.
33:11 The counsel of Yahweh, to times age-abiding, shall stand, The devices of his heart, from generation to generation.
33:12 How happy the nation whose God is, Yahweh, The people he hath chosen as his own inheritance!
33:13 Out of the heavens, hath Yahweh looked, He hath seen all the sons of men:
33:14 Out of his settled place of abode, hath he fixed his gaze on all the inhabitants of the earth:
33:15 Who fashioneth their heart all together, Who understandeth all their doings.
33:16 Not a king, can be saved by greatness of force, nor, hero, deliver himself by greatness of strength:
33:17 A deception, is the horse, for victory, and, by his great strength, shall he not deliver.
33:18 Lo! the eye of Yahweh, is toward them who revere him, unto such as are waiting for his lovingkindness:
33:19 To rescue, from death, their soul, and to keep them alive in famine.
33:20 Our own soul, hath waited for Yahweh, Our help and our shield, is he!
33:21 For, in him, shall our heart rejoice, For, in his holy Name, have we trusted.
33:22 Be thy lovingkindness, O Yahweh, upon us, According as we have waited for thee.
text.NABRE:
33:1 Psalm 33 Praise of God’s Power and Providence I - Rejoice, you righteous, in the LORD; praise from the upright is fitting.
33:2 Give thanks to the LORD on the harp; on the ten-stringed lyre offer praise.
33:3 Sing to him a new song; skillfully play with joyful chant.
33:4 For the LORD’s word is upright; all his works are trustworthy.
33:5 He loves justice and right. The earth is full of the mercy of the LORD.
33:6 II - By the LORD’s word the heavens were made; by the breath of his mouth all their host.
33:7 He gathered the waters of the sea as a mound; he sets the deep into storage vaults.
33:8 III - Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all who dwell in the world show him reverence.
33:9 For he spoke, and it came to be, commanded, and it stood in place.
33:10 The LORD foils the plan of nations, frustrates the designs of peoples.
33:11 But the plan of the LORD stands forever, the designs of his heart through all generations.
33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people chosen as his inheritance.
33:13 IV - From heaven the LORD looks down and observes the children of Adam,
33:14 From his dwelling place he surveys all who dwell on earth.
33:15 The One who fashioned together their hearts is the One who knows all their works.
33:16 V - A king is not saved by a great army, nor a warrior delivered by great strength.
33:17 Useless is the horse for safety; despite its great strength, it cannot be saved.
33:18 Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon those who fear him, upon those who count on his mercy,
33:19 To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive through famine.
33:20 VI - Our soul waits for the LORD, he is our help and shield.
33:21 For in him our hearts rejoice; in his holy name we trust.
33:22 May your mercy, LORD, be upon us; as we put our hope in you.
text.DRB:
33:1 To David, when he changed his countenance before Abimelech, and he dismist him, and he went away
33:2 I will bless our Lord at all time: his praise always in my mouth.
33:3 In our Lord my soul shall be praised: let the mild hear, and rejoice.
33:4 Magnify ye our Lord with me: and let us exalt his name for ever.
33:5 I have sought out our Lord, and he hath heard me: and from all my tribulations he hath delivered me.
33:6 Come ye to him, and be illuminated: and your faces shall not be confounded.
33:7 This poor man hath cried, and our Lord hath heard him: and from all his tribulations he hath saved him.
33:8 The Angel of our Lord shall put in himself about them that fear him: and shall deliver them.
33:9 Taste ye, and see that our Lord is sweet: blessed is the man, that hopeth in him.
33:10 Fear ye our Lord all ye his Saints: because there is no lack to them that fear him.
33:11 The rich have wanted, and have been hungry: but they that seek after our Lord shall not be diminished of any good.
33:12 Come children, hear me: I will teach you the fear of our Lord.
33:13 who is the man that will have life: loveth to see good days?
33:14 Stay thy tongue from evil: and thy lips that they speak not guile.
33:15 Turn away from evil, and do good: seek after peace and pursue it.
33:16 The eyes of our Lord upon the just: and his ears unto their prayers.
33:17 But the countenance of our Lord is upon them that do evil things: to destroy their memory out of the earth.
33:18 The just have cried, and our Lord hath heard them: and out of all their tribulations he hath delivered them.
33:19 Our Lord is nigh to them, that are of a contrite heart: and the humble of spirit he will save.
33:20 Many are the tribulations of the just: and out of all these our Lord will deliver them.
33:21 Our Lord keepeth all their bones: there shall not one of them be broken.
33:22 The death of sinners is very ill: and they that hate the just shall offend.
33:23 Our Lord will redeem the souls of his servants: and all that hope in him shall not offend.

text.NRSV:
34:1 I will bless the Lord at all times; his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
34:2 My soul makes its boast in the Lord; let the humble hear and be glad.
34:3 O magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
34:4 I sought the Lord, and he answered me, and delivered me from all my fears.
34:5 Look to him, and be radiant; so your faces shall never be ashamed.
34:6 This poor soul cried, and was heard by the Lord, and was saved from every trouble.
34:7 The angel of the Lord encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.
34:8 O taste and see that the Lord is good; happy are those who take refuge in him.
34:9 O fear the Lord, you his holy ones, for those who fear him have no want.
34:10 The young lions suffer want and hunger, but those who seek the Lord lack no good thing.
34:11 Come, O children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
34:12 Which of you desires life, and covets many days to enjoy good?
34:13 Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.
34:14 Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
34:15 The eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears are open to their cry.
34:16 The face of the Lord is against evildoers, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
34:17 When the righteous cry for help, the Lord hears, and rescues them from all their troubles.
34:18 The Lord is near to the brokenhearted, and saves the crushed in spirit.
34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but the Lord rescues them from all of them.
34:20 He keeps all their bones; not one of them will be broken.
34:21 Evil brings death to the wicked, and those who hate the righteous will be condemned.
34:22 The Lord redeems the life of his servants; none of those who take refuge in him will be condemned.

      let the humble hear and rejoice.

      let us exalt his Name together.

      and delivered me out of all my terror.

34:15 The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, *

text.KJV:
34:1 I will blesse the Lord at all times: his prayse shall continually bee in my mouth.
34:2 My soule shall make her boast in the Lord: the humble shall heare thereof, and be glad.
34:3 O magnifie the Lord with me, and let vs exalt his name together.
34:4 I sought the Lord, and hee heard me; and deliuered mee from all my feares.
34:5 They looked vnto him, and were lightned: and their faces were not ashamed.
34:6 This poore man cried, and the Lord heard him; and saued him out of all his troubles.
34:7 The Angel of the Lord encampeth round about them that feare him, and deliuereth them.
34:8 O taste and see that the Lord is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
34:9 O feare the Lord yee his Saints: for there is no want to them that feare him.
34:10 The young lyons doe lacke, and suffer hunger: but they that seeke the Lord, shall not want any good thing.
34:11 Come yee children, hearken vnto me: I will teach you the feare of the Lord.
34:12 What man is hee that desireth life; and loueth many dayes, that he may see good?
34:13 Keepe thy tongue from euill, and thy lippes from speaking guile.
34:14 Depart from euill, and doe good: seeke peace and pursue it.
34:15 The eies of the Lord are vpon the righteous; and his eares are open vnto their crie.
34:16 The face of the Lord is against them that doe euill; to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
34:17 The righteous crie, and the Lord heareth; and deliuereth them out of all their troubles.
34:18 The Lord is nigh vnto them that are of a broken heart: and saueth such as be of a contrite spirit.
34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the Lord deliuereth him out of them all.
34:20 He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
34:21 Euill shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shalbe desolate.
34:22 The Lord redeemech the soule of his seruants: and none of them that trust in him, shalbe desolate.
text.JPS1985:
34:1 Of David, when he feigned madness in the presence of Abimelech, who turned him out, and he left.
34:2 I bless the LORD at all times; praise of Him is ever in my mouth.
34:3 I glory in the LORD; let the lowly hear it and rejoice.
34:4 Exalt the LORD with me; let us extol His name together.
34:5 I turned to the LORD, and He answered me; He saved me from all my terrors.
34:6 Men look to Him and are radiant; let their faces not be downcast.
34:7 Here was a lowly man who called, and the LORD listened, and delivered him from all his troubles.
34:8 The angel of the LORD camps around those who fear Him and rescues them.
34:9 Taste and see how good the LORD is; happy the man who takes refuge in Him!
34:10 Fear the LORD, you His consecrated ones, for those who fear Him lack nothing.
34:11 Lions have been reduced to starvation, but those who turn to the LORD shall not lack any good.
34:12 Come, my sons, listen to me; I will teach you what it is to fear the LORD.
34:13 Who is the man who is eager for life, who desires years of good fortune?
34:14 Guard your tongue from evil, your lips from deceitful speech.
34:15 Shun evil and do good, seek amity and pursue it.
34:16 The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous, His ears attentive to their cry.
34:17 The face of the LORD is set against evildoers, to erase their names from the earth.
34:18 They cry out, and the LORD hears, and saves them from all their troubles.
34:19 The LORD is close to the brokenhearted; those crushed in spirit He delivers.
34:20 Though the misfortunes of the righteous be many, the LORD will save him from them all,
34:21 Keeping all his bones intact, not one of them being broken.
34:22 One misfortune is the deathblow of the wicked; the foes of the righteous shall be ruined.
34:23 The LORD redeems the life of His servants; all who take refuge in Him shall not be ruined.

text.Rotherham:
34:1 Let me bless Yahweh at all times, Continually be his praise in my mouth.
34:2 In Yahweh, boasteth my soul, The patient oppressed-ones shall hear and be glad.
34:3 Ascribe ye greatness unto Yahweh with me, and let us exalt his Name together.
34:4 I enquired of Yahweh, and he hath answered me, And, out of all my terrors, hath he rescued me.
34:5 They looked unto him and were radiant, And, as for their faces, let them not be abashed.
34:6 This, oppressed one cried, and, Yahweh, heard—and, out of all his distresses, saved him.
34:7 The messenger of Yahweh encampeth around them who revere him, Thus hath he delivered them.
34:8 Oh taste and see, that good is Yahweh,—How happy the man who seeketh refuge in him!
34:9 Revere Yahweh, ye his holy ones, For there is no want to them who revere him.
34:10 Young lions, have come short, and suffered hunger, but, they who seek Yahweh, shall not lack any good thing.
34:11 Come, ye children! hearken unto me, The reverence of Yahweh, will I teach you.
34:12 Who is the man that desireth life, Loving days, that he may see good?
34:13 Keep thy tongue from wickedness, And thy lips from speaking deceit:
34:14 Depart from wickedness and do good, Aim at well-being, and pursue it.
34:15 The eyes of Yahweh, are towards the righteous, And, his ears, towards their cry for help:
34:16 the face of Yahweh, is against such as do wickedness, To cut off, from the earth, their memory.
34:17 They made outcry, and Yahweh heard, And, out of all their straits, hath he rescued them.
34:18 Near is Yahweh, to the broken in heart, And, the crushed in spirit, will he save.
34:19 Many are the misfortunes of the righteous, But, out of them all, doth Yahweh rescue him.
34:20 Keeping all his bones, Not, one from among them, is broken.
34:21 Misfortune shall be the death of the lawless one, And, the haters of the righteous man, shall be held guilty.
34:22 Yahweh ransometh the soul of his servants, And none shall be held guilty, who seek refuge in him.
text.NABRE:
34:1 Psalm 34 Thanksgiving to God Who Delivers the Just - Of David, when he feigned madness before Abimelech, who drove him out and he went away.
34:2 I - I will bless the LORD at all times; his praise shall be always in my mouth.
34:3 My soul will glory in the LORD; let the poor hear and be glad.
34:4 Magnify the LORD with me; and let us exalt his name together.
34:5 II - I sought the LORD, and he answered me, delivered me from all my fears.
34:6 Look to him and be radiant, and your faces may not blush for shame.
34:7 This poor one cried out and the LORD heard, and from all his distress he saved him.
34:8 The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear him, and he saves them.
34:9 Taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the stalwart one who takes refuge in him.
34:10 Fear the LORD, you his holy ones; nothing is lacking to those who fear him.
34:11 The rich grow poor and go hungry, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.
34:12 III - Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you fear of the LORD.
34:13 Who is the man who delights in life, who loves to see the good days?
34:14 Keep your tongue from evil, your lips from speaking lies.
34:15 Turn from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
34:16 The eyes of the LORD are directed toward the righteous and his ears toward their cry.
34:17 The LORD’s face is against evildoers to wipe out their memory from the earth.
34:18 The righteous cry out, the LORD hears and he rescues them from all their afflictions.
34:19 The LORD is close to the brokenhearted, saves those whose spirit is crushed.
34:20 Many are the troubles of the righteous, but the LORD delivers him from them all.
34:21 He watches over all his bones; not one of them shall be broken.
34:22 Evil will slay the wicked; those who hate the righteous are condemned.
34:23 The LORD is the redeemer of the souls of his servants; and none are condemned who take refuge in him.
text.DRB:
34:1 To David himself. Judge o Lord them that hurt me: overthrow them that impugn me.
34:2 Take armour and shield: and rise up to help me.
34:3 Bring forth the sword, and shut up against them, that persecute me: say to my soul: I am thy salvation.
34:4 Let them be confounded & ashamed, that seek my soul. Let them be turned backward, and be confounded that think evil against me.
34:5 Be they made as dust before the face of wind; and the Angel of our Lord straitening them.
34:6 Let their way be made darkness and slipperness: and the Angel of our Lord pursuing them.
34:7 Because they have hid the destruction of their snare for me without cause: in vain have they upbraided my soul.
34:8 Let the snare which he knoweth not, come on him; and the net, which he hath hid, catch him: and let him fall into the very same snare.
34:9 But my soul shall rejoice in our Lord: and shall be delighted upon his salvation.
34:10 All my bones shall say: Lord, who is like to thee? Delivering the needy from the hand of them that are stronger than he: the needy and poor from them that spoil him.
34:11 Unjust witnesses rising up, asked me things that I knew not.
34:12 They repayed me evil things for good, sterility to my soul.
34:13 But I when they were troublesome to me, did put on cloth of hair. I humbled my soul in fasting: and my prayer shall be turned into my bosom.
34:14 As a neighbour, as our brother, so did I please: as mourning and sorrowful so was I humbled.
34:15 And they rejoiced against me, and came together: scourges were gathered together upon me, and I was ignorant.
34:16 They were dissipated, and not compunct, they tempted me, they scorned me with scorning: they gnashed upon me with their teeth.
34:17 Lord when wilt thou regard? Restore thou my soul from their malignity, mine only one from the lions.
34:18 I will confess to thee in the great Church, in a grave people I will praise thee.
34:19 Let them not rejoice over me that are my adversaries unjustly: that hate me without cause, and twinkle with the eyes.
34:20 Because they spake indeed peaceably to me: and in the anger of the earth speaking they meant guiles.
34:21 And they opened their mouth awide upon me: they said, Well, well, our eyes have seen.
34:22 Thou hast seen, o Lord, keep not silence: Lord depart not from me.
34:23 Arise and attend to my judgment: my God, and my Lord unto my cause.
34:24 Judge me according to thy justice, o Lord my God, and let them not rejoice over me.
34:25 Let them not say in their hearts: Well, well, to our soul: neither let them say, We have devoured him.
34:26 Let them blush and be ashamed together, that rejoice at my evils. Let them be clothed with confusion and shame, that speak great things upon me.
34:27 Let them rejoice and be glad, that will my justice: and let them say always: Our Lord be magnified, that will the peace of his servant.
34:28 And my tongue shall meditate thy justice, thy praise all the day.

text.NRSV:
35:1 Fight those who fight me, O Lord; attack those who are attacking me.
35:2 Take up shield and armor and rise up to help me.
35:3 Draw the sword and bar the way against those who pursue me; say to my soul, 'I am your salvation.'
35:4 Let those who seek my life be shamed and disgraced; let those who plot my ruin fall back and be dismayed.
35:5 Let them be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the Lord driving them on.
35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery, with the angel of the Lord pursuing them.
35:7 For without cause they have laid a net in a pit for me; without cause they have dug a trap for my life.
35:8 Let ruin come upon them unawares; let them be caught in the net they laid; let them fall into their own pit of ruin.
35:9 Then will I be joyful in the Lord; I will glory in his victory.
35:10 My whole being will say, 'Lord, who is like you? You deliver the poor from those who are too strong for them, the poor and needy from those who rob them.'
35:11 Malicious witnesses rise up against me; they charge me with matters I know nothing about.
35:12 They repay me evil for good, to my intense sorrow.
35:13 As for me, when they were sick, I wore sackcloth and humbled myself by fasting; I prayed with my whole heart.
35:14 I walked about as for my friend or my brother; I went in mourning as one who laments his mother.
35:15 But when I stumbled, they were glad and gathered together; they gathered against me; strangers whom I did not know mocked me and did not cease.
35:16 Like profane mockers at a feast, they gnashed at me with their teeth.
35:17 O Lord, how long will you look on? rescue me from the roaring beasts, and my life from the young lions.
35:18 I will give you thanks in the great assembly; I will praise you in the mighty throng.
35:19 Do not let my treacherous enemies rejoice over me, nor let those who hate me without a cause wink their eyes.
35:20 For they do not speak of peace, but invent deceitful schemes against those who are quiet in the land.
35:21 They opened their mouths at me and said, 'Aha! we saw it with our own eyes.'
35:22 You saw it, O Lord; do not be silent; O Lord, be not far from me.
35:23 Awake, arise, to my cause! to my defense, my God and my Lord!
35:24 Give me justice, O Lord my God, according to your righteousness; let them not triumph over me.
35:25 Let them not say to themselves, 'Aha! just what we wanted!' nor let them say, 'We have swallowed him up.'
35:26 Let all who rejoice at my ruin be shamed and disgraced; let those who boast against me be clothed with dismay and shame.
35:27 Let those who favor my cause shout for joy and be glad; let them ever say, 'Great is the Lord, who desires the prosperity of his servant.'
35:28 And my tongue shall be talking of your righteousness and of your praise all the day long.

text.KJV:
35:1 Plead my cause (O Lord) with them that striue with mee: fight against them that fight against me.
35:2 Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand vp for mine helpe.
35:3 Draw out also the speare, and stop the way against them that persecute me: say vnto my soule, I am thy saluation.
35:4 Let them be confounded and put to shame that seeke after my soule: let them be turned backe and brought to confusion, that deuise my hurt.
35:5 Let them be as chaffe before the wind: and let the Angel of the Lord chase them.
35:6 Let their way be darke and slippery, and let the Angel of the Lord persecute them.
35:7 For without cause haue they hid for me their net in a pit, which without cause they haue digged for my soule.
35:8 Let destruction come vpon him at vnawares, and let his net that hee hath hid, catch himselfe: into that very destruction let him fall.
35:9 And my soule shalbe ioyfull in the Lord: it shall reioyce in his saluation.
35:10 All my bones shall say, Lord, who is like vnto thee which deliuerest the poore from him that is too strong for him, yea the poore and the needy, from him that spoileth him?
35:11 False witnesses did rise vp they layd to my charge things that I knew not.
35:12 They rewarded mee euill for good, to the spoiling of my soule.
35:13 But as for me, when they were sicke, my clothing was sack-cloth: I humbled my soule with fasting, and my prayer returned into mine owne bosome.
35:14 I behaued my selfe as though he had bene my friend, or brother: I bowed downe heauily, as one that mourneth for his mother.
35:15 But in mine aduersitie they reioyced, and gathered themselues together: yea, the abiects gathered themselues together against me, & I knew it not, they did teare me, and ceased not,
35:16 With hypocriticall mockers in feasts: they gnashed vpon mee with their teeth.
35:17 Lord, how long wilt thou looke on? rescue my soule from their destructions, my darling from the lyons.
35:18 I will giue thee thankes in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.
35:19 Let not them that are mine enemies wrongfully, reioyce ouer me: neither let them winke with the eye, that hate me without a cause.
35:20 For they speake not peace: but they deuise deceitfull matters against them that are quiet in the land.
35:21 Yea they opened their mouth wide against me, and saide, Aha, Aha, our eye hath seene it.
35:22 This thou hast seene (O Lord) keepe not silence: O Lord be not farre from me.
35:23 Stirre vp thy selfe and awake to my iudgement, euen vnto my cause, my God and my Lord.
35:24 Iudge me O Lord my God, according to thy righteousnesse, and let them not reioyce ouer me.
35:25 Let them not say in their hearts, Ah, so would we haue it: let them not say, We haue swallowed him vp.
35:26 Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together, that reioyce at mine hurt: let them bee cloathed with shame and dishonour, that magnifie themselues against me.
35:27 Let them shoute for ioy, and bee glad that fauour my righteous cause: yea let them say continually, Let the Lord bee magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his seruant
35:28 And my tongue shall speake of thy righteousnesse, and of thy praise all the day long.
text.JPS1985:
35:1 Of David. O LORD, strive with my adversaries, give battle to my foes,
35:2 take up shield and buckler, and come to my defense;
35:3 ready the spear and javelin against my pursuers; tell me, “I am your deliverance.”
35:4 Let those who seek my life be frustrated and put to shame; let those who plan to harm me fall back in disgrace.
35:5 Let them be as chaff in the wind, the LORD’s angel driving them on.
35:6 Let their path be dark and slippery, with the LORD’s angel in pursuit.
35:7 For without cause they hid a net to trap me; without cause they dug a pit for me.
35:8 Let disaster overtake them unawares; let the net they hid catch them; let them fall into it when disaster [strikes].
35:9 Then shall I exult in the LORD, rejoice in His deliverance.
35:10 All my bones shall say, “LORD, who is like You? You save the poor from one stronger than he, the poor and needy from his despoiler.”
35:11 Malicious witnesses appear who question me about things I do not know.
35:12 They repay me evil for good, [seeking] my bereavement.
35:13 Yet, when they were ill, my dress was sackcloth, I kept a fast— may what I prayed for happen to me!
35:14 I walked about as though it were my friend or my brother; I was bowed with gloom, like one mourning for his mother.
35:15 But when I stumble, they gleefully gather; wretches gather against me, I know not why; they tear at me without end.
35:16 With impious, mocking grimace they gnash their teeth at me.
35:17 O Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue me from their attacks, my precious life, from the lions,
35:18 that I may praise You in a great congregation, acclaim You in a mighty throng.
35:19 Let not my treacherous enemies rejoice over me, or those who hate me without reason wink their eyes.
35:20 For they do not offer amity, but devise fraudulent schemes against harmless folk.
35:21 They open wide their mouths at me, saying, “Aha, aha, we have seen it! ”
35:22 You have seen it, O LORD; do not hold aloof! O Lord, be not far from me!
35:23 Wake, rouse Yourself for my cause, for my claim, O my God and my Lord!
35:24 Take up my cause, O LORD my God, as You are beneficent, and let them not rejoice over me.
35:25 Let them not think, “Aha, just what we wished! ” Let them not say, “We have destroyed him! ”
35:26 May those who rejoice at my misfortune be frustrated and utterly disgraced; may those who vaunt themselves over me be clad in frustration and shame.
35:27 May those who desire my vindication sing forth joyously; may they always say, “Extolled be the LORD who desires the well-being of His servant, ”
35:28 while my tongue shall recite Your beneficent acts, Your praises all day long.
text.Grail1963:
1 O Lord, plead my cause against my foes;
fight those who fight me.
2 Take up your buckler and shield;
arise to help me.
3 Take up the javelin and the spear
against those who pursue me.
O Lord, say to my soul:
"I am your salvation."
4 Let those who seek my life
be shamed and disgraced.
Let those who plan evil against me
be routed in confusion.
5 Let them be like chaff before the wind;
let God's angel scatter them.
6 Let their path be slippery and dark;
let God's angel pursue them.
7 They have hidden a net for me wantonly;
they have dug a pit.
8 Let ruin fall upon them
and take them by surprise.
Let them be caught in the net they have hidden;
let them fall into their pit.
9 But my soul shall be joyful in the Lord
and rejoice in his salvation.
10 My whole being will say:

"Lord, who is like you
who rescue the weak from the strong
and the poor from the oppressor?"
11 Lying witnesses arise
and accuse me unjustly.
12 They repay me evil for good;
my soul is forlorn.
13 When they were sick I went into mourning,
afflicted with fasting.
My prayer was ever on my lips,
14 as for a brother, a friend.
I went as though mourning a mother,
bowed down with grief.
15 Now that I am in trouble they gather,
they gather and mock me.
They take me by surprise and strike me
and tear me to pieces.
16 They provoke me with mockery on mockery
and gnash their teeth.
17 O Lord, how long will you look on?
Come to my rescue!
Save my life from these raging beasts,
my soul from these lions.
18 I will thank you in the great assembly,
amid the throng I will praise you.
19 Do not let my lying foes
rejoice over me.
Do not let those who hate me unjustly
wink eyes at each other.
20 They wish no peace to the peaceful
who live in the land.
They make deceitful plots
21 and with mouths wide open
their cry against me is: "Yes!"
We saw you do it!"
22 O Lord, you have seen, do not be silent,
do not stand afar off!
23 Awake, stir to my defense,
to my cause, O God!
24 Vindicate me, Lord, in your justice,
do not let them rejoice.
25 Do not let them think: "Yes, we have won,
we have brought you to an end!"
26 Let them be shamed and brought to disgrace
who rejoice at my misfortune.
Let them be covered with shame and confusion
who raise themselves against me.

27 Let there be joy for those who love my cause.
Let them say without end:
"Great is the Lord who delights
in the peace of his servant."
28 Then my tongue shall speak of your justice,
and all day long of your praise.
text.Rotherham:
35:1 Contend, O Yahweh, with them who contend with me, Make war upon them who make war upon me.
35:2 Grasp buckler and shield, and arise in my help;
35:3 Then draw the spear, and close up against my pursuers, Say to my soul, Thy salvation, I am!
35:4 Let them be ashamed and confounded, who are seeking my life,—Let them turn back and be put to the blush, who are devising my hurt:
35:5 Let them be as chaff before the wind, with, the messenger of Yahweh, pressing them on:
35:6 Let their way become dark and slippery, with, the messenger of Yahweh, pursuing them.
35:7 For, without cause, have they hid for me, in a ditch, their net,—Without cause, have they digged [a pit] for my life.
35:8 There shall reach him a ruin he could not know,—Yea, his own net which he hath hidden, shall capture him, Into that ruin, shall he fall!
35:9 But, my soul, shall exult in Yahweh,—shall be glad in his salvation.
35:10 All my bones, shall say—O Yahweh, who is like unto thee? Rescuing the oppressed from one stronger than he, Yea the oppressed and the needy, from their spoiler.
35:11 There rise up witnesses helping forward violence and wrong, What I know not, they demand of me:
35:12 They repay me evil for good, Bereaving my soul.
35:13 But, as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth, I humbled, with fasting, my soul, though, my prayer, unto mine own bosom, might return:
35:14 Like as for a friend, like as for a brother of mine own, I walked to and fro, As though mourning for a mother, I gloomily bowed myself down.
35:15 But, in mine overthrow, have they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together,—There gathered themselves together against me foolish men, and I knew it not, They have torn in pieces, and not been silent;
35:16 Amidst profane praters of perversion, have they gnashed upon me with their teeth.
35:17 My Lord! how long wilt thou look on? Bring back my soul out of their raging, from among lions, my solitary self.
35:18 I will thank thee, in the midst of a great convocation,—In the midst of a mighty people, will I praise thee.
35:19 Let not them who are falsely my foes rejoice over me, As for them who hate me without cause, let them not wink the eye!
35:20 For, no salvation, will they utter,—But, against the quiet of the land, deceitful things, do they devise;
35:21 Yea they have opened wide against me their mouth, They have said, Aha! Aha! our own eye, hath seen!
35:22 Thou hast seen, O Yahweh, do not keep silence! O My Lord! be not far from me:
35:23 Bestir thyself and wake up, to my vindication, O my God and My Lord!—to my plea:
35:24 Vindicate me according to thy righteousness, O Yahweh, my God! And let them not rejoice over me:
35:25 Let them not say in their heart, Aha! to our mind! Let them not say, We have swallowed him up!
35:26 Let them turn pale, and then at once blush, who are rejoicing at my misfortune,—Let them be clothed with shame and confusion, who are magnifying themselves against me.
35:27 Let them shout in triumph and rejoice, who are desiring my justification; And let them say continually, Yahweh be magnified, Who hath taken pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.
35:28 Mine own tongue also, shall softly utter thy righteousness,—All the day long,—thy praise!
text.NABRE:
35:1 Psalm 35 Prayer for Help Against Unjust Enemies - Of David. I - Oppose, O LORD, those who oppose me; war upon those who make war upon me.
35:2 Take up the shield and buckler; rise up in my defense.
35:3 Brandish lance and battle-ax against my pursuers. Say to my soul, “I am your salvation.”
35:4 Let those who seek my life be put to shame and disgrace. Let those who plot evil against me be turned back and confounded.
35:5 Make them like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them on.
35:6 Make their way slippery and dark, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
35:7 II - Without cause they set their snare for me; without cause they dug a pit for me.
35:8 Let ruin overtake them unawares; let the snare they have set catch them; let them fall into the pit they have dug.
35:9 Then I will rejoice in the LORD, exult in God’s salvation.
35:10 My very bones shall say, “O LORD, who is like you, Who rescue the afflicted from the powerful, the afflicted and needy from the despoiler?”
35:11 III - Malicious witnesses rise up, accuse me of things I do not know.
35:12 They repay me evil for good; my soul is desolate.
35:13 Yet I, when they were ill, put on sackcloth, afflicted myself with fasting, sobbed my prayers upon my bosom.
35:14 I went about in grief as for my brother, bent in mourning as for my mother.
35:15 Yet when I stumbled they gathered with glee, gathered against me and I did not know it. They slandered me without ceasing;
35:16 without respect they mocked me, gnashed their teeth against me.
35:17 IV - O Lord, how long will you look on? Restore my soul from their destruction, my very life from lions!
35:18 Then I will thank you in the great assembly; I will praise you before the mighty throng.
35:19 Do not let lying foes rejoice over me, my undeserved enemies wink knowingly.
35:20 They speak no words of peace, but against the quiet in the land they fashion deceitful speech.
35:21 They open wide their mouths against me. They say, “Aha! Good! Our eyes have seen it!”
35:22 You see this, LORD; do not be silent; Lord, do not withdraw from me.
35:23 Awake, be vigilant in my defense, in my cause, my God and my Lord.
35:24 Defend me because you are just, LORD; my God, do not let them rejoice over me.
35:25 Do not let them say in their hearts, “Aha! Our soul!” Do not let them say, “We have devoured that one!”
35:26 Put to shame and confound all who relish my misfortune. Clothe with shame and disgrace those who lord it over me.
35:27 But let those who favor my just cause shout for joy and be glad. May they ever say, “Exalted be the LORD who delights in the peace of his loyal servant.”
35:28 Then my tongue shall recount your justice, declare your praise, all the day long.
text.DRB:
35:1 Unto the end, to the servant of our Lord David himself.
35:2 The unjust hath said within himself, that he would sin: there is no fear of God before his eyes.
35:3 Because he hath done deceitfully in his sight: that his iniquity may be found unto hatred.
35:4 The words of his mouth are iniquity, and guile: he would not understand that he might do well.
35:5 He hath meditated iniquity in his bed: he hath set himself on every way not good, and malice he hath not hated.
35:6 Lord thy mercy is in heaven: and thy truth even to the clouds.
35:7 Thy justice as the hills of God: thy judgments are great depth. Men & beasts thou wilt save, o Lord,
35:8 as thou hast multiplied thy mercy, o God. But the children of men shall hope in the covert of thy wings.
35:9 They shall be inebriated with the plenty of thy house: and with the torrent of thy pleasure thou shalt make them drink.
35:10 Because with thee is the fountain of life: and in thy light we shall see light.
35:11 Extend thy mercy to them that know thee, and thy justice to them, that are of a right heart.
35:12 Let not the foot of pride come to me: and let not the hand of a sinner move me.
35:13 There have they fallen that work iniquity: they were expelled, neither could they stand.

text.NRSV:
36:1 There is a voice of rebellion deep in the heart of the wicked; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
36:2 He flatters himself in his own eyes that his abominable sin will not be found out.
36:3 The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful; he has left off acting wisely and doing good.
36:4 He plots wickedness upon his bed and has set himself in no good way; he does not abhor that which is evil.
36:5 Your love, O Lord, reaches to the heavens, and your faithfulness to the clouds.
36:6 Your righteousness is like the strong mountains, your justice like the great deep; you save both man and beast, O Lord.
36:7 How priceless is your love, O God! your people take refuge under the shadow of your wings.
36:8 They feast upon the abundance of your house; you give them drink from the river of your delights.
36:9 For with you is the well of life, and in your light we see light.
36:10 Continue your loving-kindness to those who know you, and your favor to those who are true of heart.
36:11 Let not the foot of the proud come near me, nor the hand of the wicked push me aside.
36:12 See how they are fallen, those who work wickedness! they are cast down and shall not be able to rise.

text.KJV:
36:1 The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, that there is no feare of God before his eyes.
36:2 For he flatterech himselfe in his owne eyes, vntill his iniquitie be found to be hatefull.
36:3 The words of his mouth are iniquitie and deceit: he hath left off to bee wise, and to doe good.
36:4 Hee deuiseth mischiefe vpon his bed, he setteth himselfe in a way that is not good; he abhorreth not euill.
36:5 Thy mercie (O Lord) is in the heauens; and thy faithfulnesse reacheth vnto the cloudes.
36:6 Thy righteousnesse is like the great mountaines; thy iudgements are a great deepe; O Lord, thou preseruest man and beast.
36:7 How excellent is thy louing kindnesse, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust vnder the shadowe of thy wings.
36:8 They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatnesse of thy house: and thou shalt make them drinke of the riuer of thy pleasures.
36:9 For with thee is the fountaine of life: in thy light shall we see light.
36:10 O continue thy louing kindnesse vnto them that know thee; and thy righteousnesse to the vpright in heart.
36:11 Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked remoue me.
36:12 There are the workers of iniquitie fallen: they are cast downe, and shal not be able to rise.
text.JPS1985:
36:1 For the leader. Of the servant of the LORD, of David.
36:2 I know what Transgression says to the wicked; he has no sense of the dread of God,
36:3 because its speech is seductive to him till his iniquity be found out and he be hated.
36:4 His words are evil and deceitful; he will not consider doing good.
36:5 In bed he plots mischief; he is set on a path of no good, he does not reject evil.
36:6 O LORD, Your faithfulness reaches to heaven; Your steadfastness to the sky;
36:7 Your beneficence is like the high mountains; Your justice like the great deep; man and beast You deliver, O LORD.
36:8 How precious is Your faithful care, O God! Mankind shelters in the shadow of Your wings.
36:9 They feast on the rich fare of Your house; You let them drink at Your refreshing stream.
36:10 With You is the fountain of life; by Your light do we see light.
36:11 Bestow Your faithful care on those devoted to You, and Your beneficence on upright men.
36:12 Let not the foot of the arrogant tread on me, or the hand of the wicked drive me away.
36:13 There lie the evildoers, fallen, thrust down, unable to rise.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Sin speaks to the sinner

.6 Your love, Lord, reaches to heaven;

/.8 O Lord, how precious is your love.
