text.KJV:
42:1 As the Hart panteth after the water brookes, so panteth my soule after thee, O God.
42:2 My soule thirsteth for God, for the liuing God: when shall I come and appeare before God?
42:3 My teares haue bene my meate day and night; while they continually say vnto me, Where is thy God?
42:4 When I remember these things, I powre out my soule in mee; for I had gone with the multitude, I went with them to the house of God; with the voyce of ioy and praise, with a multitude that kept holy day.
42:5 Why art thou cast downe, O my soule, and why art thou disquieted in me? hope thou in God, for I shall yet praise him for the helpe of his countenance.
42:6 O my God, my soule is cast downe within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Iordane, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Missar.
42:7 Deepe calleth vnto deepe at the noyse of thy water-spouts: all thy waues, and thy billowes are gone ouer me.
42:8 Yet the Lord will command his louing kindnes in the day time, and in the night his song shalbe with me, and my prayer vnto the God of my life.
42:9 I will say vnto God, My rocke, why hast thou forgotten me? why goe I mourning, because of the oppression of the enemy?
42:10 As with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproch mee: while they say dayly vnto me, Where is thy God?
42:11 Why art thou cast downe, O my soule? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God, for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
text.JPS1985:
42:1 For the leader. A maskil of the Korahites.
42:2 Like a hind crying for water, my soul cries for You, O God;
42:3 my soul thirsts for God, the living God; O when will I come to appear before God!
42:4 My tears have been my food day and night; I am ever taunted with, “Where is your God? ”
42:5 When I think of this, I pour out my soul: how I walked with the crowd, moved with them, the festive throng, to the House of God with joyous shouts of praise.
42:6 Why so downcast, my soul, why disquieted within me? Have hope in God; I will yet praise Him for His saving presence.
42:7 O my God, my soul is downcast; therefore I think of You in this land of Jordan and Hermon, in Mount Mizar,
42:8 where deep calls to deep in the roar of Your cataracts; all Your breakers and billows have swept over me.
42:9 By day may the LORD vouchsafe His faithful care, so that at night a song to Him may be with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
42:10 I say to God, my rock, “Why have You forgotten me, why must I walk in gloom, oppressed by my enemy? ”
42:11 Crushing my bones, my foes revile me, taunting me always with, “Where is your God? ”
42:12 Why so downcast, my soul, why disquieted within me? Have hope in God; I will yet praise Him, my ever-present help, my God.

text.Rotherham:
42:1 As, the hart, cometh panting up to the channels of water, So my soul, panteth for thee, O God.
42:2 My soul thirsteth for God, for a GOD who liveth,—When shall I enter in, and see the face of God?
42:3 My tears have been my food day and night, While it hath been said unto me all the day, Where is thy God?
42:4 These things, I keep calling to mind, and pouring out, over me, my own soul, For I used to cross over with a crowd, Lead them in procession up to the house of God, With the voice of shouting and praise.—a throng keeping festival.
42:5 Why shouldst thou be cast down, O my soul? And [why] shouldst thou moan over me? Wait thou for God, for yet shall I praise him, As the triumph of my presence.
42:6 My God! over myself, my soul is cast down,—For this cause, will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, And the Hermons, from the hill Mizar.
42:7 Roaring deep unto roaring deep, is calling, at the voice of thy cataracts, All thy breakers and thy rolling waves, over me, have passed.
42:8 By day, will Yahweh command his lovingkindness, And, in the night, shall His song be with me, My prayer be to the GOD of my life.
42:9 I will say onto GOD,—my rock, Wherefore hast thou forgotten me? Wherefore in gloom should I go, because of oppression by the enemy?
42:10 With a crushing of my bones, have my adversaries reproached me,—While they keep saying unto me all the day, Where is thy God?
42:11 Why shouldst thou be sat down, O my soul? And why shouldst thou moan over me? Wait thou for God, for yet shall I praise him, As the triumph of my presence and my God.
text.NABRE:
42:1 Second Book—Psalms 42–72 Psalm 42 Longing for God’s Presence in the Temple - For the leader. A maskil of the Korahites.
42:2 I - As the deer longs for streams of water, so my soul longs for you, O God.
42:3 My soul thirsts for God, the living God. When can I enter and see the face of God?
42:4 My tears have been my bread day and night, as they ask me every day, “Where is your God?”
42:5 Those times I recall as I pour out my soul, When I would cross over to the shrine of the Mighty One, to the house of God, Amid loud cries of thanksgiving, with the multitude keeping festival.
42:6 Why are you downcast, my soul; why do you groan within me? Wait for God, for I shall again praise him, my savior and my God.
42:7 II - My soul is downcast within me; therefore I remember you From the land of the Jordan and Hermon, from Mount Mizar,
42:8 Deep calls to deep in the roar of your torrents, and all your waves and breakers sweep over me.
42:9 By day may the LORD send his mercy, and by night may his righteousness be with me! I will pray to the God of my life,
42:10 I will say to God, my rock: “Why do you forget me? Why must I go about mourning with the enemy oppressing me?”
42:11 It shatters my bones, when my adversaries reproach me, when they say to me every day: “Where is your God?”
42:12 Why are you downcast, my soul, why do you groan within me? Wait for God, for I shall again praise him, my savior and my God.
text.DRB:
42:1 A Psalm of David. Judge me, o God, & discern my cause from the nation not holy, from the unjust and deceitful man deliver me.
42:2 Because thou art God my strength: why hast thou repelled me? And why go I sorrowful whilst the enemy afflicteth me.
42:3 Send forth thy light and thy truth: they have conducted me, and have brought me into thy holy hill, and into thy tabernacles.
42:4 And I will go in to the altar of God: to God, which maketh my youth joyful. I will confess to thee on the harp o God my God:
42:5 why art thou sorrowful, o my soul? And why dost thou trouble me? Hope in God, because yet will I confess to him: the salvation of my countenance, and my God.

text.NRSV:
43:1 Give judgment for me, O God, and defend my cause against an ungodly people; deliver me from the deceitful and the wicked.
43:2 For you are the God of my strength; why have you put me from you? and why do I go so heavily while the enemy oppresses me?
43:3 Send out your light and your truth, that they may lead me, and bring me to your holy hill and to your dwelling;
43:4 That I may go to the altar of God, to the God of my joy and gladness; and on the harp I will give thanks to you, O God my God.
43:5 Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? and why are you so disquieted within me?
43:6 Put your trust in God; for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.

text.KJV:
43:1 Iudge mee, O God, and plead my cause against an vngodly nation; O deliuer me from the deceitfull and vniust man.
43:2 For thou art the God of my strength, why doest thou cast me off? why goe I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
43:3 O send out thy light & thy trueth; let them leade mee, let them bring mee vnto thy holy hill, and to thy Tabernacles.
43:4 Then will I goe vnto the Altar of God, vnto God my exceeding ioy: yea vpon the harpe will I praise thee, O God, my God.
43:5 Why art thou cast downe, O my soule? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope in God, for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
text.JPS1985:
43:1 Vindicate me, O God, champion my cause against faithless people; rescue me from the treacherous, dishonest man.
43:2 For You are my God, my stronghold; why have You rejected me? Why must I walk in gloom, oppressed by the enemy?
43:3 Send forth Your light and Your truth; they will lead me; they will bring me to Your holy mountain, to Your dwelling-place,
43:4 that I may come to the altar of God, God, my delight, my joy; that I may praise You with the lyre, O God, my God.
43:5 Why so downcast, my soul, why disquieted within me? Have hope in God; I will yet praise Him, my ever-present help, my God.

text.Rotherham:
43:1 Vindicate me, O God, and plead my cause, Against a nation, without lovingkindness, From the man of deceit and perversity, wilt thou deliver me?
43:2 For, thou, art my defending God—Wherefore hast thou rejected me? Wherefore in gloom should I wander, because of the oppression of an enemy?
43:3 Send forth thy light and thy faithfulness, Let, them, lead me, Let them bring me into thy holy mountain, and into thy habitations:
43:4 That I may go in unto the altar of God, Unto GOD, mine exultant joy,—That I may praise thee with the lyre, O God—mine own God!
43:5 Why shouldst thou be cast down, O my soul? And why shouldst thou moan over me, Wait thou for God, for yet shall I praise him, As the triumph of my presence, and my God.
text.NABRE:
43:1 Grant me justice, O God; defend me from a faithless people; from the deceitful and unjust rescue me.
43:2 You, O God, are my strength. Why then do you spurn me? Why must I go about mourning, with the enemy oppressing me?
43:3 Send your light and your fidelity, that they may be my guide; Let them bring me to your holy mountain, to the place of your dwelling,
43:4 That I may come to the altar of God, to God, my joy, my delight. Then I will praise you with the harp, O God, my God.
43:5 Why are you downcast, my soul? Why do you groan within me? Wait for God, for I shall again praise him, my savior and my God.
text.DRB:
43:1 Unto the end, for the sons of Core to understanding.
43:2 O God we have heard with our ears: our fathers have declared to us. The work that thou hast wrought in their days: & in the days of old.
43:3 Thy hand destroyed the nations, and thou didst plant them: thou didst afflict the peoples, and expel them.
43:4 For not by their own sword did they possess the land, and their own arm did not save them: But thy right hand, and thine arm, and the illumination of thy countenance: because thou wast pleased in them.
43:5 Thou art the same my King and my God: which commandest the salvations of Jacob.
43:6 In thee we shall turn out our enemies with the horn, & in thy name we shall contemn them that rise up against us.
43:7 For I will not hope in my bow: and my sword will not save me.
43:8 For thou hast saved us from them that afflict us: and them that hate us thou hast confounded.
43:9 In God we shall be praised all the day: and in thy name we will confess for ever.
43:10 But now thou hast repelled and confounded us: and thou wilt not go forth, o God, in our hosts.
43:11 Thou hast turned us back behind our enemies: and they that hated us spoiled for themselves.
43:12 Thou hast given us as sheep that are to be eaten: and thou hast dispersed us among the nations.
43:13 Thou hast sold thy people without price: and there was no multitude in the exchanges of them.
43:14 Thou hast made us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and mocking stock to them that are round about us.
43:15 Thou hast made us for a parable to the Gentiles: a wagging of the head among the peoples.
43:16 All the day my shame is against me, and the confusion of my face hath covered me.
43:17 At the voice of the upbraider, and the reproacher: at the face of the enemy and persecutor.
43:18 All these things have come upon us, neither have we forgotten thee: and we have not done wickedly in thy testament.
43:19 And our heart hath not revolted backward: & thou hast declined our paths from thy way.
43:20 Because thou hast humbled us in the place of affliction, and the shadow of death hath covered us.
43:21 If we have forgotten the name of our God, and if we have spread forth our hands to a strange god,
43:22 Will not God inquire of these things? For he knoweth the secrets of the heart. Because for thee we are killed all the day: we are esteemed as sheep of slaughter.
43:23 Arise, why sleepest thou, o Lord? Arise, and expel us not to the end.
43:24 Why dost thou turn away thy face, forgettest our poverty and our tribulation?
43:25 Because our soul is humbled in the dust: our belly is glued in the earth.
43:26 Arise Lord, help us: and redeem us for thy name.

text.NRSV:
44:1 We have heard with our ears, O God, our forefathers have told us, all the things you did in their days,
44:2 How you drove out the nations with your hand and planted them; how you afflicted the peoples and made them flourish.
44:3 For they did not take the land by their sword, nor did their arm win the victory for them; but your right hand, your arm, and the light of your countenance, because you favored them.
44:4 You are my King and my God; you command victories for Jacob.
44:5 Through you we pushed back our adversaries; through your Name we trampled on those who rose up against us.
44:6 For I do not rely on my bow, and my sword does not give me the victory.
44:7 Surely you gave us victory over our adversaries and put those who hate us to shame.
44:8 Every day we gloried in God, and we will praise your Name for ever.
44:9 Nevertheless, you have rejected and humbled us and do not go forth with our armies.
44:10 You have made us fall back before our adversary, and our enemies have plundered us.
44:11 You have made us like sheep to be eaten and have scattered us among the nations.
44:12 You are selling your people for a trifle and are making no profit on the sale.
44:13 You have made us the scorn of our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
44:14 You have made us a byword among the nations, a laughingstock among the peoples.
44:15 All day long my disgrace is before me, and shame has covered my face;
44:16 At the sound of the taunter and reviler, and at the sight of the enemy and avenger.
44:17 All this has come upon us; yet we have not forgotten you, nor have we betrayed your covenant.
44:18 Our heart has not turned back, nor have our steps strayed from your path;
44:19 Though you would cast us out among wild beasts and cover us with the shadow of death.
44:20 If we have forgotten the Name of our God, or stretched out our hands to some strange god,
44:21 Will not God find it out? for he knows the secrets of the heart.
44:22 Indeed, for your sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
44:23 Awake, O Lord! why are you sleeping? Arise and do not reject us for ever.
44:24 Why do you hide your face and forget our affliction and oppression?
44:25 For we sink down into the dust; our body cleaves to the ground.
44:26 Rise up, help us, and save us, for the sake of your steadfast love.

text.KJV:
44:1 Wee haue heard with our eares, O God, our fathers haue told vs, what worke thou didst in their dayes, in the times of old.
44:2 How thou didst driue out the heathen with thy hand, & plantedst them; how thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.
44:3 For they got not the land in possession by their owne sword, neither did their owne arme saue them: but thy right hand, and thine arme, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a fauour vnto them.
44:4 Thou art my King, O God: command deliuerances for Iacob.
44:5 Through thee will wee push downe our enemies: through thy Name will wee tread them vnder that rise vp against vs.
44:6 For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword saue me.
44:7 But thou hast saued vs from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated vs.
44:8 In God we boast all the day long: and praise thy Name for euer. Selah.
44:9 But thou hast cast off and put vs to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.
44:10 Thou makest vs to turne backe from the enemie: and they which hate vs, spoile for themselues.
44:11 Thou hast giuen vs like sheepe appointed for meate: and hast scattered vs among the heathen.
44:12 Thou sellest thy people for nought, and doest not increase thy wealth by their price.
44:13 Thou makest vs a reproch to our neighbours, a scorne and a derision to them that are round about vs.
44:14 Thou makest vs a by-word among the heathen: a shaking of the head among the people.
44:15 My confusion is continually before me, and the shame of my face hath couered me.
44:16 For the voice of him that reproacheth, and blasphemeth: by reason of the enemie and auenger.
44:17 All this is come vpon vs; yet haue wee not forgotten thee, neither haue we dealt falsly in thy couenant.
44:18 Our heart is not turned backe: neither haue our steps declined from thy way,
44:19 Though thou hast sore broken vs in the place of dragons, and couered vs with the shadow of death.
44:20 If wee haue forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange God:
44:21 Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
44:22 Yea for thy sake are wee killed all the day long: wee are counted as sheepe for the slaughter.
44:23 Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast vs not off for euer.
44:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face? and forgettest our affliction, and our oppression?
44:25 For our soule is bowed downe to the dust; our belly cleaueth vnto the earth.
44:26 Arise for our helpe, and redeeme vs for thy mercies sake.
text.JPS1985:
44:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites. A maskil.
44:2 We have heard, O God, our fathers have told us the deeds You performed in their time, in days of old.
44:3 With Your hand You planted them, displacing nations; You brought misfortune on peoples, and drove them out.
44:4 It was not by their sword that they took the land, their arm did not give them victory, but Your right hand, Your arm, and Your goodwill, for You favored them.
44:5 You are my king, O God; decree victories for Jacob!
44:6 Through You we gore our foes; by Your name we trample our adversaries;
44:7 I do not trust in my bow; it is not my sword that gives me victory;
44:8 You give us victory over our foes; You thwart those who hate us.
44:9 In God we glory at all times, and praise Your name unceasingly. Selah.
44:10 Yet You have rejected and disgraced us; You do not go with our armies.
44:11 You make us retreat before our foe; our enemies plunder us at will.
44:12 You let them devour us like sheep; You disperse us among the nations.
44:13 You sell Your people for no fortune, You set no high price on them.
44:14 You make us the butt of our neighbors, the scorn and derision of those around us.
44:15 You make us a byword among the nations, a laughingstock among the peoples.
44:16 I am always aware of my disgrace; I am wholly covered with shame
44:17 at the sound of taunting revilers, in the presence of the vengeful foe.
44:18 All this has come upon us, yet we have not forgotten You, or been false to Your covenant.
44:19 Our hearts have not gone astray, nor have our feet swerved from Your path,
44:20 though You cast us, crushed, to where the sea monster is, and covered us over with deepest darkness.
44:21 If we forgot the name of our God and spread forth our hands to a foreign god,
44:22 God would surely search it out, for He knows the secrets of the heart.
44:23 It is for Your sake that we are slain all day long, that we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered.
44:24 Rouse Yourself; why do You sleep, O Lord? Awaken, do not reject us forever!
44:25 Why do You hide Your face, ignoring our affliction and distress?
44:26 We lie prostrate in the dust; our body clings to the ground.
44:27 Arise and help us, redeem us, as befits Your faithfulness.
text.Grail1963:
.2 We heard with our own ears, O God,
our fathers have told us the story
of the things you did in their days,
you yourself, in days long ago.
.3 To plant them you uprooted the nations;
to let them spread you laid peoples low.
.4 No sword of their own won the land;
no arm of their own brought them victory.
It was your right hand, your arm
and the light of your face; for you loved them.
.5 It is you, my king, my God,
who granted victories to Jacob.
.6 Through you we beat down our foes;
in your name we trampled down our aggressors.
.7 For it was not in my bow that I trusted
nor yet was I saved by my sword:
.8 it was you who saved us from our foes,
it was you who put our foes to shame.
.9 All day long our boast was in God
and we praised your name without ceasing.
.10 Yet now you have rejected us, disgraced us;
you no longer go forth with our armies.
.11 You make us retreat from the foe
and our enemies plunder us at will.
.12 You make us like sheep for the slaughter

and scatter us among the nations.
.13 You sell your own people for nothing
and make no profit by the sale.
.14 You make us the taunt of our neighbors,
the laughing stock of all who are near.
.15 Among the nations, you make us a byword,
among the peoples a thing of derision.
.16 All day long my disgrace is before me;
my face is covered with shame
.17 at the voice of the taunter, the scoffer,
at the sight of the foe and avenger.
* * *
.18 This befell us though we had not forgotten you,
though we had not been false to your covenant,
.19 though we had not withdrawn our hearts;
though our feet had not strayed from your path.
.20 Yet you have crushed us in a place of sorrows
and covered us with the shadow of death.
.21 Had we forgotten the name of our God,
or stretched out our hands to another god
.22 would not God have found this out,
he who knows the secrets of the heart?
.23 It is for you that we face death all day long
and are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
.24 Awake, O Lord, why do you sleep?
Arise, do not reject us for ever!
.25 Why do you hide your face
and forget our oppression and misery?
.26 For we are brought down low to the dust;
our body lies prostrate on the earth.
.27 Stand up and come to our help!
Redeem us because of your love!
text.Rotherham:
44:1 O God! with our own ears, have we heard, Our fathers, have recounted to us,—The work thou didst work, In their day, In the days of aforetime:
44:2 Thou thyself, with thine own hand—Didst dispossess, nations, and didst plant them, Didst break peoples in pieces, and didst spread them out:
44:3 For, not by their own sword, gat they possession of the land, Nor did, their own arm, win victory for them,—But thine own right hand, and thine own arm, and the light of thy face, Because thou hadst accepted them.
44:4 Thou thyself, art my king, O God, Command thou the victories of Jacob.
44:5 By thee, will we thrust at, our adversaries, In thy Name, will we tread down our assailants;
44:6 For, not in my bow, will I trust, Nor shall, my sword, give me victory;
44:7 For thou hast saved us from our adversaries, And, them who hated us, hast thou put to shame.
44:8 In God, have we boasted all the day, And, thy Name—unto times age-abiding, will we praise. [Selah.]
44:9 But nay thou hast rejected, and confounded us, And wilt not go forth with our hosts;
44:10 Thou sufferest us to turn back from the adversary, And, they who hate us, have plundered at will:
44:11 Thou dost give us up like sheep to be devoured, And, amongst the nations, hast thou scattered us.
44:12 Thou dost sell thy people for, no-value, And hast not made increase by their price.
44:13 Thou dost make us, A reproach to our neighbours, A mockery and a derision to them who are round about us:
44:14 Thou dost make us, A by-word among the nations,—A shaking of the head among the peoples.
44:15 All the day, is my confusion before me, And, the shame of my face, hath covered me:
44:16 At the voice of him who reproacheth and revileth, At the face of the foe and avenger.
44:17 All this, hath come upon us, Yet had we not forgotten thee, Neither had we dealt falsely with thy covenant;
44:18 Our heart had not drawn back, Nor had our goings swerved from thy path;
44:19 That thou shouldst have crushed us down in the place of wild dogs, And covered us over with a deadly shadow.
44:20 If we had forgotten the Name of our God, And had spread forth our hands unto the GOD of the foreigner,
44:21 Would not, God, have searched into this, Seeing that, he, knoweth the secrets of the heart?
44:22 Surely, for thy sake, have we been slain all the day, We have been accounted as sheep for slaughter.
44:23 Awake thou! wherefore shouldst thou sleep, O Lord? Bestir thee! do not reject us altogether!
44:24 Wherefore shouldst thou hide, thy face? Shouldst forget our humiliation and our oppression?
44:25 For our soul, sinketh down to the dust, Our body, cleaveth to the earth.
44:26 Arise to our help, And ransom us, because of thine own lovingkindness.
text.NABRE:
44:1 Psalm 44 God’s Past Favor and Israel’s Present Need - For the leader. A maskil of the Korahites.
44:2 I - O God, we have heard with our own ears; our ancestors have told us The deeds you did in their days, with your own hand in days of old:
44:3 You rooted out nations to plant them, crushed peoples and expelled them.
44:4 Not with their own swords did they conquer the land, nor did their own arms bring victory; It was your right hand, your own arm, the light of your face for you favored them.
44:5 You are my king and my God, who bestows victories on Jacob.
44:6 Through you we batter our foes; through your name we trample our adversaries.
44:7 Not in my bow do I trust, nor does my sword bring me victory.
44:8 You have brought us victory over our enemies, shamed those who hate us.
44:9 In God we have boasted all the day long; your name we will praise forever. Selah
44:10 II - But now you have rejected and disgraced us; you do not march out with our armies.
44:11 You make us retreat before the foe; those who hate us plunder us at will.
44:12 You hand us over like sheep to be slaughtered, scatter us among the nations.
44:13 You sell your people for nothing; you make no profit from their sale.
44:14 You make us the reproach of our neighbors, the mockery and scorn of those around us.
44:15 You make us a byword among the nations; the peoples shake their heads at us.
44:16 All day long my disgrace is before me; shame has covered my face
44:17 At the sound of those who taunt and revile, at the sight of the enemy and avenger.
44:18 III - All this has come upon us, though we have not forgotten you, nor been disloyal to your covenant.
44:19 Our hearts have not turned back, nor have our steps strayed from your path.
44:20 Yet you have left us crushed, desolate in a place of jackals; you have covered us with a shadow of death.
44:21 If we had forgotten the name of our God, stretched out our hands to another god,
44:22 Would not God have discovered this, God who knows the secrets of the heart?
44:23 For you we are slain all the day long, considered only as sheep to be slaughtered.
44:24 IV - Awake! Why do you sleep, O Lord? Rise up! Do not reject us forever!
44:25 Why do you hide your face; why forget our pain and misery?
44:26 For our soul has been humiliated in the dust; our belly is pressed to the earth.
44:27 Rise up, help us! Redeem us in your mercy.
text.DRB:
44:1 Unto the end, for them, that shall be changed, to the sons of Core for understanding, a Canticle for the beloved.
44:2 My heart hath uttered a good word: I tell my works to the king. My tongue is the pen of a scribe, that writeth swiftly.
44:3 Goodly of beauty above the sons of men, grace is poured abroad in thy lips: therefore hath God blessed thee for ever.
44:4 Be girded with thy sword upon thy thigh, o most mighty.
44:5 With thy beauty and fairness intend, proceed prosperously, and reign, Because of truth, and mildness, and justice: and thy right hand shall conduct thee marvelously.
44:6 Thy sharp arrows, the peoples underneath thee shall fall into the hearts of the king's enemies.
44:7 Thy seat o God for ever and ever: a rod of direction the rod of thy Kingdom.
44:8 Thou hast loved justice, and hast hated iniquity: therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
44:9 Myrrh, and Aloes, and Cassia from thy garments, from houses of ivory, out of the which
44:10 the daughters of kings have delighted thee in thy honour.
44:11 The Queen stood on thy right hand in golden raiment: compassed with variety. Hear daughter, and see, and incline thine ear: and forget thy people, and the house of thy father.
44:12 And the King will covet thy beauty: because he is the Lord thy God, and they shall adore him.
44:13 And the daughters of Tyre with gifts, all the rich of the people shall beseech thy countenance.
44:14 All the glory of that daughter of the King is within, in borders of gold,
44:15 clothed round about with varieties. Virgins shall be brought to the King after her: her neighbours shall be brought to thee.
44:16 They shall be brought in joy and exultation: they shall be brought into the temple of the King.
44:17 For thy fathers there are born sons to thee: thou shalt make them Princes over all the earth.
44:18 They shall be mindful of thy name in all generation and generation. Therefore shall peoples confess to thee for ever: and for ever and ever.

text.NRSV:
45:1 My heart is stirring with a noble song; let me recite what I have fashioned for the king; my tongue shall be the pen of a skilled writer.
45:2 You are the fairest of men; grace flows from your lips, because God has blessed you for ever.
45:3 Strap your sword upon your thigh, O mighty warrior, in your pride and in your majesty.
45:4 Ride out and conquer in the cause of truth and for the sake of justice; your right hand shall show you marvelous things.
45:5 Your arrows are very sharp, O mighty warrior; the peoples are falling at your feet, and the king's enemies are losing heart.
45:6 Your throne, O God, endures for ever and ever, a scepter of righteousness is the scepter of your kingdom; you love righteousness and hate iniquity.
45:7 Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.
45:8 All your garments are fragrant with myrrh, aloes, and cassia, and the music of strings from ivory palaces makes you glad.
45:9 Kings' daughters stand among the ladies of the court; on your right hand is the queen, adorned with the gold of Ophir.
45:10 'Hear, O daughter; consider and listen closely; forget your people and your father's house.
45:11 The king will have pleasure in your beauty; he is your master; therefore do him honor.
45:12 The people of Tyre are here with a gift; the rich among the people seek your favor.'
45:13 All glorious is the princess as she enters; her gown is cloth-of-gold.
45:14 In embroidered apparel she is brought to the king; after her the bridesmaids follow in procession.
45:15 With joy and gladness they are brought, and they enter into the palace of the king.
45:16 'In place of fathers, O king, you shall have sons; you shall make them princes over all the earth.
45:17 I will make your name to be remembered from one generation to another; therefore nations will praise you for ever and ever.'

text.KJV:
45:1 My heart is inditing a good matter: I speake of the things which I haue made, touching the King: my tongue is the penne of a ready writer.
45:2 Thou art fairer then the children of men: grace is powred into thy lips: therfore God hath blessed thee for euer.
45:3 Gird thy sword vpon thy thigh, O most mightie: with thy glory and thy maiestie.
45:4 And in thy maiestie ride prosperously, because of trueth and meekenes, and righteousnesse: and thy right hand shall teach thee terrible things.
45:5 Thine arrowes are sharpe in the heart of the Kings enemies; whereby the people fall vnder thee.
45:6 Thy throne (O God) is for euer and euer: the scepter of thy kingdome is a right scepter.
45:7 Thou louest righteousnesse, and hatest wickednesse: therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oyle of gladnesse aboue thy fellowes.
45:8 All thy garments smell of myrrhe, and aloes, and cassia: out of the Iuorie palaces, whereby they haue made thee glad.
45:9 Kings daughters were among thy honourable women: vpon thy right hand did stand the Queene in golde of Ophir.
45:10 Hearken (O daughter) and consider, and incline thine eare; forget also thine owne people, and thy fathers house.
45:11 So shall the king greatly desire thy beautie: for he is thy Lord, and worship thou him.
45:12 And the daughter of Tyre shall be there with a gift, euen the rich among the people shall intreate thy fauour.
45:13 The kings daughter is all glorious within; her clothing is of wrought gold.
45:14 She shall bee brought vnto the king in raiment of needle worke: the virgins her companions that followe her, shall be brought vnto thee.
45:15 With gladnesse and reioycing shall they be brought: they shall enter into the kings palace.
45:16 In stead of thy fathers shall bee thy children, whom thou mayest make princes in all the earth.
45:17 I will make thy name to bee remembred in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for euer and euer.
text.JPS1985:
45:1 For the leader; on shoshannim. Of the Korahites. A maskil. A love song.
45:2 My heart is astir with gracious words; I speak my poem to a king; my tongue is the pen of an expert scribe.
45:3 You are fairer than all men; your speech is endowed with grace; rightly has God given you an eternal blessing.
45:4 Gird your sword upon your thigh, O hero, in your splendor and glory;
45:5 in your glory, win success; ride on in the cause of truth and meekness and right; and let your right hand lead you to awesome deeds.
45:6 Your arrows, sharpened, [pierce] the breast of the king’s enemies; peoples fall at your feet.
45:7 Your divine throne is everlasting; your royal scepter is a scepter of equity.
45:8 You love righteousness and hate wickedness; rightly has God, your God, chosen to anoint you with oil of gladness over all your peers.
45:9 All your robes [are fragrant] with myrrh and aloes and cassia; from ivoried palaces lutes entertain you.
45:10 Royal princesses are your favorites; the consort stands at your right hand, decked in gold of Ophir.
45:11 Take heed, lass, and note, incline your ear: forget your people and your father’s house,
45:12 and let the king be aroused by your beauty; since he is your lord, bow to him.
45:13 O Tyrian lass, the wealthiest people will court your favor with gifts,
45:14 goods of all sorts. The royal princess, her dress embroidered with golden mountings,
45:15 is led inside to the king; maidens in her train, her companions, are presented to you.
45:16 They are led in with joy and gladness; they enter the palace of the king.
45:17 Your sons will succeed your ancestors; you will appoint them princes throughout the land.
45:18 I commemorate your fame for all generations, so peoples will praise you forever and ever.
text.Grail1963:
.2 My heart overflows with noble words.
To the king I must speak the song I have made,
my tongue as nimble as the pen of a scribe.
.3 You are the fairest of the children of men
and graciousness is poured upon your lips:
because God has blessed you for evermore.
.4 O mighty one, gird your sword upon your thigh;
/.5 in splendor and state, ride on in triumph
for the cause of truth and goodness and right.
Take aim with your bow in your dread right hand.

.6 Your arrows are sharp, peoples fall beneath you.
The foes of the king fall down and lose heart.
.7 Your throne, O God, shall endure for ever.
A scepter of justice is the scepter of your kingdom.
.8 Your love is for justice; your hatred for evil.
Therefore God, your God, has anointed you
with the oil of gladness above other kings:
.9 your robes are fragrant with aloes and myrrh.
From the ivory palace you are greeted with music.
.10 The daughters of kings are among your loved ones.
On your right stands the queen in gold of Ophir.
* * *
.11 Listen, O daughter, give ear to my words:
forget your own people and your father's house.
.12 So will the king desire your beauty:
He is your lord, pay homage to him.
.13 And the people of Tyre shall come with gifts,
the richest of the people shall seek your favor.
.14 The daughter of the king is clothed with splendor,
her robes embroidered with pearls set in gold.
.15 She is led to the king with her maiden companions.
.16 They are escorted amid gladness and joy;
they pass within the palace of the king.
* * *
.17 Sons shall be yours in place of your fathers:
you will make them rulers over all the earth.
.18 May this song make your name for ever remembered.
May the peoples praise you from age to age.
text.Rotherham:
45:1 Overflowed hath my heart, with an excellent theme, I will recite my poem concerning the king, Be, my tongue, [like] the pen of a scribe who is skilled.
45:2 Most beautiful thou art, beyond the sons of men, Graciousness hath been poured forth by thy lips, For this cause, hath God blessed thee, to times age-abiding.
45:3 Gird thy sword upon thy thigh, O mighty one, [’Tis] thine honour and thy majesty;
45:4 And, [in] thy majesty, be successful! ride forth! on behalf of faithfulness, and humility—righteousness, And let thine own right hand show thee wonderful things.
45:5 Thine arrows, are sharp—Peoples, under thee, fall! In the heart of the foes of the king.
45:6 Thy throne, O God, is to times age-abiding and beyond, A sceptre of equity, is the sceptre of thy kingdom.
45:7 Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated lawlessness,—For this cause, hath God, thine own God, anointed thee, With the oil of gladness, beyond thy partners.
45:8 Myrrh and aloes, cassias, all thy garments,—Out of the palaces of ivory, the tones of strings, have rejoiced thee.
45:9 Daughters of kings, are among thine honourable women, Stationed is the bride at thy right hand, in gold of Ophir.
45:10 Hearken, O daughter, and observe, Incline also thine ear, Forget, then, thine own people, And the house of thy father;
45:11 When the king shall desire thy beauty, Surely, he, is thy lord, then bow down to him.
45:12 Also, the daughter of Tyre, [cometh in] with a present! Thy face, shall the rich of the people appease.
45:13 All glorious—the daughter of a king [sitteth] within, Brocades, wrought with gold, [are] her clothing!
45:14 In embroidered raiment, shall she be brought unto the king—The virgins that follow her, her companions, are to be conducted unto thee:
45:15 They shall be brought, with rejoicings and exultation,—They shall enter into the palace of the king!
45:16 Instead of thy fathers, be thy sons, Thou shalt make them rulers in all the earth.
45:17 I will keep in remembrance thy Name, through each succeeding generation, For this cause, peoples, shall praise thee, to times age-abiding and beyond.
text.NABRE:
45:1 Psalm 45 Song for a Royal Wedding - For the leader; according to “Lilies.” A maskil of the Korahites. A love song.
45:2 I - My heart is stirred by a noble theme, as I sing my ode to the king. My tongue is the pen of a nimble scribe.
45:3 II - You are the most handsome of men; fair speech has graced your lips, for God has blessed you forever.
45:4 Gird your sword upon your hip, mighty warrior! In splendor and majesty ride on triumphant!
45:5 In the cause of truth, meekness, and justice may your right hand show your wondrous deeds.
45:6 Your arrows are sharp; peoples will cower at your feet; the king’s enemies will lose heart.
45:7 Your throne, O God, stands forever; your royal scepter is a scepter for justice.
45:8 You love justice and hate wrongdoing; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellow kings.
45:9 With myrrh, aloes, and cassia your robes are fragrant. From ivory-paneled palaces stringed instruments bring you joy.
45:10 Daughters of kings are your lovely wives; a princess arrayed in Ophir’s gold comes to stand at your right hand.
45:11 III - Listen, my daughter, and understand; pay me careful heed. Forget your people and your father’s house,
45:12 that the king might desire your beauty. He is your lord;
45:13 honor him, daughter of Tyre. Then the richest of the people will seek your favor with gifts.
45:14 All glorious is the king’s daughter as she enters, her raiment threaded with gold;
45:15 In embroidered apparel she is led to the king. The maids of her train are presented to the king.
45:16 They are led in with glad and joyous acclaim; they enter the palace of the king.
45:17 IV - The throne of your fathers your sons will have; you shall make them princes through all the land.
45:18 I will make your name renowned through all generations; thus nations shall praise you forever.
text.DRB:
45:1 Unto the end, to the sons of Core, for the secrets.
45:2 Our God is a refuge and strength: an helper in tribulations, which have found us exceedingly.
45:3 Therefore will we not fear when the earth shall be troubled: and mountains transported into the heart of the sea.
45:4 Their waters have sounded, and were troubled: the mountains were troubled in his strength.
45:5 The violence of the river maketh the city of God joyful: the Highest hath sanctified his tabernacle.
45:6 God is in the midst thereof, it shall not be moved: God will help it in the morning early.
45:7 Nations are troubled, and Kingdoms are inclined: he gave his voice, the earth was moved.
45:8 The Lord of hosts is with us: the God of Jacob is our defender.
45:9 Come ye, & see the works of our Lord, what wonders he hath put upon the earth:
45:10 taking away wars even unto the end of the earth. He shall destroy bow, & break weapons: and shields he shall burn with fire.
45:11 Be quiet, and see that I am God: I shall be exalted among the gentiles, and I shall be exalted in the earth.
45:12 The Lord of hosts is with us: the God of Jacob is our defender.

text.NRSV:
46:1 God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
46:2 Therefore we will not fear, though the earth be moved, and though the mountains be shaken into the depths of the sea;
46:3 Though its waters rage and foam, and though the mountains tremble at its tumult.
46:4 There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God, the holy habitation of the Most High.
46:5 God is in the midst of her; she shall not be overthrown; God shall help her at the break of day.
46:6 The nations make much ado, and the kingdoms are shaken; God has uttered his voice, and the earth shall melt away.
46:7 The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
46:8 Come now and look upon the works of the Lord, what awesome things he has done on earth.
46:9 It is he who makes war to cease in all the world; he breaks the bow, and shatters the spear, and burns the shields with fire.
46:10 'Be still, then, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations; I will be exalted in the earth.'
46:11 The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our stronghold.

      I will be exalted in the earth."
46:12 The LORD of hosts is with us; *
      the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
text.KJV:
46:1 God is our refuge and strength: a very present helpe in trouble.
46:2 Therfore will not we feare, though the earth be remoued: and though the mountaines be caried into the midst of the sea.
46:3 Though the waters thereof roare, and be troubled, though the mountaines shake with the swelling thereof. Selah.
46:4 There is a riuer, the streames wherof shall make glad the citie of God: the holy place of the Tabernacles of the most High.
46:5 God is in the midst of her: she shal not be moued; God shall helpe her, and that right early.
46:6 The heathen raged, the kingdomes were mooued: he vttered his voyce, the earth melted.
46:7 The Lord of hosts is with vs; the God of Iacob is our refuge. Selah.
46:8 Come, behold the workes of the Lord, what desolations hee hath made in the earth.
46:9 He maketh warres to cease vnto the end of the earth: hee breaketh the bow, and cutteth the speare in sunder, he burneth the chariot in the fire.
46:10 Be stil, and know that I am God: I will bee exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
46:11 The Lord of hosts is with vs; the God of Iacob is our refuge. Selah.
text.JPS1985:
46:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites; on alamoth. A song.
46:2 God is our refuge and stronghold, a help in trouble, very near.
46:3 Therefore we are not afraid though the earth reels, though mountains topple into the sea—
46:4 its waters rage and foam; in its swell mountains quake. Selah.
46:5 There is a river whose streams gladden God’s city, the holy dwelling-place of the Most High.
46:6 God is in its midst, it will not be toppled; by daybreak God will come to its aid.
46:7 Nations rage, kingdoms topple; at the sound of His thunder the earth dissolves.
46:8 The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our haven. Selah.
46:9 Come and see what the LORD has done, how He has wrought desolation on the earth.
46:10 He puts a stop to wars throughout the earth, breaking the bow, snapping the spear, consigning wagons to the flames.
46:11 “Desist! Realize that I am God! I dominate the nations; I dominate the earth.”
46:12 The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our haven. Selah.
text.Grail1963:
.2 God is for us a refuge and strength,
a helper close at hand, in time of distress,
.3 so we shall not fear though the earth should rock,
though the mountains fall into the depths of the sea;
.4 even though its waters rage and foam,
even though the mountains be shaken by its waves.
The Lord of hosts is with us:
the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
.5 The waters of a river give joy to God's city,
the holy place where the Most High dwells.
.6 God is within, it cannot be shaken;
God will help it at the dawning of the day.

.7 Nations are in tumult, kingdoms are shaken:
he lifts his voice, the earth shrinks away.
.8 The Lord of hosts is with us:
the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
.9 Come, consider the works of the Lord,
the redoubtable deeds he has done on the earth.
.10 He puts an end to wars over all the earth;
the bow he breaks, the spear he snaps.
(He burns the shields with fire.)
.11 "Be still and know that I am God,
supreme among the nations, supreme on the earth!"
.12 The Lord of hosts is with us:
the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
text.Rotherham:
46:1 God—for us, is a refuge and strength, A help in distresses, soon found.
46:2 For this cause, will we not fear, Though the earth showeth change, Or the mountains slip into the heart of the seas:
46:3 The waters thereof roar and foam, The mountains tremble with the swelling thereof [Selah.]
46:4 A river! whose channels, shall gladden the city of God, The Most High, hath hallowed his habitation.
46:5 God, is in the midst of her, she shall not be shaken, God will help her, by the turnings of the morning.
46:6 Nations have roared, Kingdoms have tottered, He hath uttered his voice, Earth melteth.
46:7 Yahweh of hosts, is with us, A high tower for us, is the God of Jacob. [Selah.]
46:8 Come! view the doings of Yahweh,—Who hath set desolations in the earth;
46:9 Causing wars to cease unto the end of the earth,—The bow, he shivereth, And breaketh in pieces the spear, war-chariots, burneth he up with fire.
46:10 Let be! and know that, I, am God, I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.
46:11 Yahweh of hosts, is with us, A high tower for us, is the God of Jacob. [Selah.]
text.NABRE:
46:1 Psalm 46 God, the Protector of Zion - For the leader. A song of the Korahites. According to alamoth.
46:2 I - God is our refuge and our strength, an ever-present help in distress.
46:3 Thus we do not fear, though earth be shaken and mountains quake to the depths of the sea,
46:4 Though its waters rage and foam and mountains totter at its surging. Selah
46:5 II - Streams of the river gladden the city of God, the holy dwelling of the Most High.
46:6 God is in its midst; it shall not be shaken; God will help it at break of day.
46:7 Though nations rage and kingdoms totter, he utters his voice and the earth melts.
46:8 The LORD of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah
46:9 III - Come and see the works of the LORD, who has done fearsome deeds on earth;
46:10 Who stops wars to the ends of the earth, breaks the bow, splinters the spear, and burns the shields with fire;
46:11 “Be still and know that I am God! I am exalted among the nations, exalted on the earth.”
46:12 The LORD of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah
text.DRB:
46:1 Unto the end, for the sons of Core.
46:2 All ye Nations clap hands: make jubilation to God in the voice of exultation.
46:3 Because our Lord is high, terrible: a great King over all the earth.
46:4 He hath made peoples subject to us: & gentiles under our feet.
46:5 He hath chosen his inheritance in us: the beauty of Jacob which he loved.
46:6 God is ascended in jubilation: and our Lord in the voice of trumpet.
46:7 Sing ye to our God, sing ye: Sing ye to our King, sing ye.
46:8 Because God is King of all the earth: sing ye wisely.
46:9 God shall reign over the gentiles: God sitteth upon his holy seat.
46:10 Princes of peoples are gathered together with the God of Abraham: because the strong gods of the earth are exceedingly advanced.

text.NRSV:
47:1 Clap your hands, all you peoples; shout to God with a cry of joy.
47:2 For the Lord Most High is to be feared; he is the great King over all the earth.
47:3 He subdues the peoples under us, and the nations under our feet.
47:4 He chooses our inheritance for us, the pride of Jacob whom he loves.
47:5 God has gone up with a shout, the Lord with the sound of the ram's-horn.
47:6 Sing praises to God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises.
47:7 For God is the King of all the earth; sing praises with all your skill.
47:8 God reigns over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.
47:9 The nobles of the peoples have gathered together with the people of the God of Abraham.
47:10 The shields of the earth belong to God; he is highly exalted.

text.KJV:
47:1 O Clap your hands (all ye people:) shoute vnto God with the voyce of triumph:
47:2 For the Lord most high is terrible; he is a great King ouer all the earth.
47:3 Hee shall subdue the people vnder vs, and the nations vnder our feet.
47:4 He shall chuse our inheritance for vs, the excellencie of Iacob whom hee loued. Selah.
47:5 God is gone vp with a shout, the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.
47:6 Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises vnto our King, sing praises.
47:7 For God is the King of all the earth, sing ye praises with vnderstanding.
47:8 God reigneth ouer the heathen: God sitteth vpon the throne of his holinesse.
47:9 The princes of the people are gathered together, euen the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth belong vnto God: hee is greatly exalted.
text.JPS1985:
47:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites. A psalm.
47:2 All you peoples, clap your hands, raise a joyous shout for God.
47:3 For the LORD Most High is awesome, great king over all the earth;
47:4 He subjects peoples to us, sets nations at our feet.
47:5 He chose our heritage for us, the pride of Jacob whom He loved. Selah.
47:6 God ascends midst acclamation; the LORD, to the blasts of the horn.
47:7 Sing, O sing to God; sing, O sing to our king;
47:8 for God is king over all the earth; sing a hymn.
47:9 God reigns over the nations; God is seated on His holy throne.
47:10 The great of the peoples are gathered together, the retinue of Abraham’s God; for the guardians of the earth belong to God; He is greatly exalted.

text.Rotherham:
47:1 All ye peoples, clap your hands, Shout unto God, with the voice of triumph;
47:2 For, Yahweh—as Most High, is to be revered, A great king, over all the earth,
47:3 He will subjugate, Peoples under us, and, Tribes of men beneath our feet.
47:4 He chooseth for us our inheritance, The excellence of Jacob, which he hath loved. [Selah.]
47:5 God hath ascended with a shout, Yahweh, with the sound of a horn.
47:6 Sing praises unto God, sing praises, Sing praises to our King, sing praises;
47:7 For God is king of all the earth, Sing praises with understanding.
47:8 God, hath become king, over the nations, God, hath taken his seat upon his holy throne.
47:9 The willing-hearted of the peoples, have gathered themselves together, The people of the God of Abraham; For, to God, belong the shields of the earth, Greatly is he exalted.
text.NABRE:
47:1 Psalm 47 The Ruler of All the Nations - For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites.
47:2 I - All you peoples, clap your hands; shout to God with joyful cries.
47:3 For the LORD, the Most High, is to be feared, the great king over all the earth,
47:4 Who made people subject to us, nations under our feet,
47:5 Who chose our heritage for us, the glory of Jacob, whom he loves. Selah
47:6 II - God has gone up with a shout; the LORD, amid trumpet blasts.
47:7 Sing praise to God, sing praise; sing praise to our king, sing praise.
47:8 III - For God is king over all the earth; sing hymns of praise.
47:9 God rules over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.
47:10 The princes of the peoples assemble with the people of the God of Abraham. For the shields of the earth belong to God, highly exalted.
text.DRB:
47:1 A Psalm of Canticle to the sons of Core, the second of the Sabbath.
47:2 Great is our Lord, and to be praised exceedingly, in the city of our God, in his holy mount.
47:3 Mount Sion is founded with the exultation of the whole earth, the sides of the North, the city of the great King.
47:4 God shall be known in the houses thereof, when he shall receive it.
47:5 For behold the kings of the earth were gathered together: they assembled in one.
47:6 They seeing it so were in admiration, were troubled, were moved:
47:7 Trembling took them. Their sorrows as a woman travailing.
47:8 In a vehement spirit thou shalt break the ships of Tharsis.
47:9 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God: God hath founded it for ever.
47:10 We have received thy mercy, o God, in the midst of thy temple.
47:11 According to thy name, o God, so also is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of justice.
47:12 Let mount Sion be glad, and the daughters of Juda rejoice, because of thy judgments, o Lord.
47:13 Compass Sion, and embrace ye her: tell ye in her towers.
47:14 Set your hearts on her strength: and distribute ye her houses, that you may declare it in another generation.
47:15 Because this is God, our God for ever, and for ever and ever: he shall rule us evermore.

text.NRSV:
48:1 Great is the Lord, and highly to be praised; in the city of our God is his holy hill.
48:2 Beautiful and lofty, the joy of all the earth, is the hill of Zion, the very center of the world and the city of the great King.
48:3 God is in her citadels; he is known to be her sure refuge.
48:4 Behold, the kings of the earth assembled and marched forward together.
48:5 They looked and were astounded; they retreated and fled in terror.
48:6 Trembling seized them there; they writhed like a woman in childbirth, like ships of the sea when the east wind shatters them.
48:7 As we have heard, so have we seen, in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God; God has established her for ever.
48:8 We have waited in silence on your loving-kindness, O God, in the midst of your temple.
48:9 As your Name, O God, so also your praise reaches to the ends of the earth.
48:10 Your right hand is full of justice; let Mount Zion be glad and the cities of Judah rejoice, because of your judgments.
48:11 Make the circuit of Zion; walk round about her; count the number of her towers.
48:12 Consider well her bulwarks; examine her strongholds; that you may tell those who come after.
48:13 This God is our God for ever and ever; he shall be our guide for evermore.

text.KJV:
48:1 Great is the Lord, and greatly to bee praised in the citie of our God, in the mountaine of his holinesse.
48:2 Beautifull for situation, the ioy of the whole earth is moūt Sion, on the sides of the North, the citie of the great King.
48:3 God is knowen in her palaces for a refuge.
48:4 For loe, the kings were assembled: they passed by together.
48:5 They sawe it, and so they marueiled, they were troubled and hasted away.
48:6 Feare tooke holde vpon them there, and paine, as of a woman in trauaile.
48:7 Thou breakest the ships of Tarshish with an East wind.
48:8 As we haue heard, so haue wee seene in the citie of the Lord of hosts, in the citie of our God, God will establish it for euer. Selah,
48:9 Wee haue thought of thy louing kindnesse, O God, in the middest of thy Temple.
48:10 According to thy Name, O God, so is thy praise vnto the endes of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousnesse.
48:11 Let mount Sion reioyce, let the daughters of Iudah be glad, because of thy iudgements.
48:12 Walke about Sion, and goe round about her: tell the towres thereof.
48:13 Marke yee well her bulwarkes, consider her palaces; that yee may tell it to the generation following.
48:14 For this God is our God for euer, and euer; he will be our guide euen vnto death.
text.JPS1985:
48:1 A song. A psalm of the Korahites.
48:2 The LORD is great and much acclaimed in the city of our God, His holy mountain—
48:3 fair-crested, joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, summit of Zaphon, city of the great king.
48:4 Through its citadels, God has made Himself known as a haven.
48:5 See, the kings joined forces; they advanced together.
48:6 At the mere sight of it they were stunned, they were terrified, they panicked;
48:7 they were seized there with a trembling, like a woman in the throes of labor,
48:8 as the Tarshish fleet was wrecked in an easterly gale.
48:9 The likes of what we heard we have now witnessed in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God— may God preserve it forever! Selah.
48:10 In Your temple, God, we meditate upon Your faithful care.
48:11 The praise of You, God, like Your name, reaches to the ends of the earth; Your right hand is filled with beneficence.
48:12 Let Mount Zion rejoice! Let the towns of Judah exult, because of Your judgments.
48:13 Walk around Zion, circle it; count its towers,
48:14 take note of its ramparts; go through its citadels, that you may recount it to a future age.
48:15 For God—He is our God forever; He will lead us evermore.
text.Grail1963:
.2 The Lord is great and worthy to be praised
in the city of our God.
/.3 His holy mountain rises in beauty,
the joy of all the earth.
Mount Zion, true pole of the earth,
the Great King's city!

.4 God, in the midst of its citadels,
has shown himself its stronghold.
.5 For the kings assembled together,
together they advanced.
.6 They saw; at once they were astounded;
dismayed, they fled in fear.
.7 A trembling seized them there,
like the pangs of birth.
.8 By the east wind you have destroyed
the ships of Tarshish.
.9 As we have heard, so we have seen
in the city of our God,
in the city of the Lord of hosts
which God upholds for ever.
.10 O God, we ponder your love
within your temple.
.11 Your praise, O God, like your name
reaches the ends of the earth.
With justice your right hand is filled.
.12 Mount Zion rejoices;
the people of Judah rejoice
at the sight of your judgments.
.13 Walk through Zion, walk all round it;
count the number of its towers.
.14 Review all its ramparts,
examine its castles,
that you may tell the next generation
.15 that such is our God,
our God for ever and always.
It is he who leads us.
text.Rotherham:
48:1 Great is Yahweh, and worthy to be mightily praised, In the city of our God, His holy mountain.
48:2 Beautiful in elevation, the joy of all the land, Is Mount Zion, in the recesses of the north,—The city of a great king.
48:3 God, in her palaces, is to be known as a high tower.
48:4 For lo! Kings—Met as appointed, Passed by together;
48:5 They themselves, saw, So, were they amazed, Dismayed—they hurried way!
48:6 Trembling, seized them, there, Pangs. like hers who is in travail.
48:7 With an east wind, wilt thou shatter the ships of Tarshish.
48:8 Just as we have heard, So, have we seen, In the city of Yahweh of hosts, In the city of our God, God himself, will establish her, unto times age-abiding. [Selah.]
48:9 We have thought, O God, upon thy lovingkindness, In the midst of thy temple:
48:10 According to thy Name, O God, So, be thy praise, unto the ends of the earth, With righteousness, is, thy right hand, filled.
48:11 Let Mount Zion rejoice, Let the daughters of Judah exult, Because of thy judgments.
48:12 Go round Zion, and compass her about, Reckon up her towers;
48:13 Apply your mind to her rampart, Pass between her palaces, That ye may recount them to an after generation;
48:14 For, this God, is our God, to times age-abiding and beyond, He himself, will conduct us till death.
text.NABRE:
48:1 Psalm 48 The Splendor of the Invincible City - A psalm of the Korahites. A song.
48:2 I - Great is the LORD and highly praised in the city of our God: His holy mountain,
48:3 fairest of heights, the joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, the heights of Zaphon, the city of the great king.
48:4 II - God is in its citadel, renowned as a stronghold.
48:5 See! The kings assembled, together they advanced.
48:6 When they looked they were astounded; terrified, they were put to flight!
48:7 Trembling seized them there, anguish, like a woman’s labor,
48:8 As when the east wind wrecks the ships of Tarshish!
48:9 III - What we had heard we have now seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, In the city of our God, which God establishes forever. Selah
48:10 We ponder, O God, your mercy within your temple
48:11 Like your name, O God, so is your praise to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is fully victorious.
48:12 Mount Zion is glad! The daughters of Judah rejoice because of your judgments!
48:13 IV - Go about Zion, walk all around it, note the number of its towers.
48:14 Consider the ramparts, examine its citadels, that you may tell future generations:
48:15 That this is God, our God for ever and ever. He will lead us until death.
text.DRB:
48:1 Unto the end, to the sons of Core a Psalm.
48:2 Hear these things all ye Gentiles: receive with your ears all ye that inhabit the earth.
48:3 All ye earthly persons, and children of men: together in one the rich and the poor.
48:4 My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart prudence.
48:5 I will incline mine ear unto a parable: I will open my proposition on a Psalter.
48:6 Why shall I fear in the evil day? The iniquity of my heel shall compass me.
48:7 They that trust in their strength: and glory in the multitude of their riches.
48:8 A brother doth not redeem, man shall redeem: he shall not give unto God his reconciliation.
48:9 And the price of the redemption of his own soul: and he shall labour for ever,
48:10 and shall live yet unto the end.
48:11 He shall not see death, when he shall see the wise dying, the unwise, and the fool shall perish together. And they shall leave their riches to strangers:
48:12 and their sepulchres their houses for ever. Their Tabernacles in generation and generation: they have renowned their names in their lands.
48:13 And man when he was in honour, did not understand: he was compared to beasts without understanding, and became like to them.
48:14 This their way is a scandal to them: and afterward in their mouth they shall take pleasure.
48:15 As sheep they are put in hell: death shall feed upon them. And the just shall rule over them in the morning: and their aid shall wax old in hell from their glory.
48:16 Nevertheless God will redeem my soul out of the hand of hell, when he shall take me,
48:17 Fear not when a man shall be made rich: and when the glory of his house shall be multiplied.
48:18 Because when he shall die, he shall not take all things: neither shall his glory go down with him.
48:19 Because his soul in his life shall be blessed: he will confess to thee when thou shalt do him good.
48:20 He shall enter in, even to the progenies of his fathers: and he shall not see light for ever.
48:21 Man, when he was in honour, did not understand: he was compared to beasts without understanding, and became like to them.

text.NRSV:
49:1 Hear this, all you peoples; hearken, all you who dwell in the world,
49:2 You of high degree and low, rich and poor together.
49:3 My mouth shall speak of wisdom, and my heart shall meditate on understanding.
49:4 I will incline my ear to a proverb and set forth my riddle upon the harp.
49:5 Why should I be afraid in evil days, when the wickedness of those at my heels surrounds me,
49:6 The wickedness of those who put their trust in their goods, and boast of their great riches?
49:7 We can never ransom ourselves, or deliver to God the price of our life;
49:8 For the ransom of our life is so great, that we should never have enough to pay it,
49:9 In order to live for ever and ever, and never see the grave.
49:10 For we see that the wise die also; like the dull and stupid they perish and leave their wealth to those who come after them.
49:11 Their graves shall be their homes for ever, their dwelling places from generation to generation, though they call the lands after their own names.
49:12 Even though honored, they cannot live for ever; they are like the beasts that perish.
49:13 Such is the way of those who foolishly trust in themselves, and the end of those who delight in their own words.
49:14 Like a flock of sheep they are destined to die; Death is their shepherd; they go down straightway to the grave.
49:15 Their form shall waste away, and the land of the dead shall be their home.
49:16 But God will ransom my life; he will snatch me from the grasp of death.
49:17 Do not be envious when some become rich, or when the grandeur of their house increases;
49:18 For they will carry nothing away at their death, nor will their grandeur follow them.
49:19 Though they thought themselves fortunate in their lifetime, and men praise you when you do well for yourself,
49:20 They will join the company of their forebears, who will never see the light again.
49:21 Those who are honored, but have no understanding, are like the beasts that perish.

49:10 Their graves shall be their homes for ever,
      their dwelling places from generation to generation, *
      though they call the lands after their own names.
49:11 Even though honored, they cannot live for ever; *
      they are like the beasts that perish.
49:12 Such is the way of those who foolishly trust in themselves, *
      and the end of those who delight in their own words.
49:13 Like a flock of sheep they are destined to die;
      Death is their shepherd; *
      they go down straightway to the grave.
49:14 Their form shall waste away, *
      and the land of the dead shall be their home.
49:15 But God will ransom my life; *
      he will snatch me from the grasp of death.
49:16 Do not be envious when some become rich, *
      or when the grandeur of their house increases;
49:17 For they will carry nothing away at their death, *
      nor will their grandeur follow them.
49:18 Though they thought highly of themselves while they lived, *
      and were praised for their success,
49:19 They shall join the company of their forebears, *
      who will never see the light again.
49:20 Those who are honored, but have no understanding, *
      are like the beasts that perish.
text.KJV:
49:1 Heare this, all yee people, giue eare all yee inhabitants of the world:
49:2 Both low, and high, rich and poore together.
49:3 My mouth shall speake of wisedome: and the meditation of my heart shalbe of vnderstanding.
49:4 I will incline mine eare to a parable; I will open my darke saying vpon the harpe.
49:5 Wherefore should I feare in the daies of euill, when the iniquitie of my heeles shall compasse me about?
49:6 They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselues in the multitude of their riches:
49:7 None of them can by any meanes redeeme his brother, nor giue to God a ransome for him:
49:8 (For the redemption of their soule is precious, and it ceaseth for euer.)
49:9 That he should still liue for euer, and not see corruption.
49:10 For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the foole, and the brutish person perish, and leaue their wealth to others.
49:11 Their inward thought is, that their houses shall continue for euer, and their dwelling places to all generations; they call their lands after their owne names.
49:12 Neuerthelesse man being in honour abideth not: he is like the beastes that perish.
49:13 This their way is their follie; yet their posteritie approue their sayings. Selah.
49:14 Like sheepe they are layd in the graue, death shall feede on them; and the vpright shall haue dominion ouer them in the morning, and their beauty shall consume in the graue, from their dwelling.
49:15 But God will redeeme my soule from the power of the graue; for he shall receiue me. Selah.
49:16 Be not thou afraid when one is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased.
49:17 For when he dieth, he shall carry nothing away: his glory shall not descend after him.
49:18 Though whiles he liued, he blessed his soule: and men will praise thee, when thou doest well to thy selfe.
49:19 Hee shall goe to the generation of his fathers, they shall neuer see light.
49:20 Man that is in honour and vnderstandeth not, is like the beasts that perish.
text.JPS1985:
49:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites. A psalm.
49:2 Hear this, all you peoples; give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
49:3 men of all estates, rich and poor alike.
49:4 My mouth utters wisdom, my speech is full of insight.
49:5 I will turn my attention to a theme, set forth my lesson to the music of a lyre.
49:6 In time of trouble, why should I fear the encompassing evil of those who would supplant me—
49:7 men who trust in their riches, who glory in their great wealth?
49:8 Ah, it cannot redeem a man, or pay his ransom to God;
49:9 the price of life is too high; and so one ceases to be, forever.
49:10 Shall he live eternally, and never see the grave?
49:11 For one sees that the wise die, that the foolish and ignorant both perish, leaving their wealth to others.
49:12 Their grave is their eternal home, the dwelling-place for all generations of those once famous on earth.
49:13 Man does not abide in honor; he is like the beasts that perish.
49:14 Such is the fate of those who are self-confident, the end of those pleased with their own talk. Selah.
49:15 Sheeplike they head for Sheol, with Death as their shepherd. The upright shall rule over them at daybreak, and their form shall waste away in Sheol till its nobility be gone.
49:16 But God will redeem my life from the clutches of Sheol, for He will take me. Selah.
49:17 Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich, when his household goods increase;
49:18 for when he dies he can take none of it along; his goods cannot follow him down.
49:19 Though he congratulates himself in his lifetime — “They must admit that you did well by yourself” —
49:20 yet he must join the company of his ancestors, who will never see daylight again.
49:21 Man does not understand honor; he is like the beasts that perish.
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
49:1 Hear ye, this all ye peoples, Give ear, all ye inhabitants of this passing world;
49:2 Both sons of the low, And sons of the high,—Together both rich and needy:—
49:3 My mouth, shall speak forth Wisdom, And the soft utterance of my heart be Understanding:
49:4 I will bend, to a by-word, mine ear, I will open, on the lyre, mine enigma.
49:5 Wherefore should I fear in the days of calamity, Though the iniquity of them who lie in wait for me should enclose me?
49:6 As for them who are trusting in their wealth,—And, in the abundance of their riches, do boast themselves,
49:7 A brother, can none of them, redeem, he cannot give unto God a ransom for himself:
49:8 So costly, is the redemption of their soul, That it faileth unto times age-abiding;
49:9 That he should, yet, live on, continually, Should not see corruption.
49:10 For it is seen that, the wise, die, Together with the dullard, and the brutish, do they perish, And leave, to others, their wealth:
49:11 Their, inward thought, is that their houses are for times age-abiding, Their habitations, for generation after generation,—They give their own names unto lands!
49:12 But, a son of earth, though wealthy, cannot tarry, He hath made himself a by-word—Beasts, they resemble:
49:13 This, their way, is a folly to them, And yet, their followers, with their mouth, approve. [Selah.]
49:14 Like sheep—into hades, are they driven, Death shall shepherd them,—And the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning, Even their form, is to decay, Hades, is all that remaineth of a habitation for him.
49:15 But, God, will redeem my soul, out of the hand of hades, For he will take me. [Selah.]
49:16 Do not fear, When a man becometh rich, When the glory of his house increaseth;
49:17 For, when he dieth, he shall take, nothing, his glory shall not descend after him;
49:18 For, though, his own self—while he lived, he used to bless, And they will praise thee, when thou doest well to thyself,
49:19 He shall enter as far as the circle of his fathers, Nevermore, shall they see the light.
49:20 A son of earth though wealthy, who discerneth not, Hath made himself a by-word, Beasts, they resemble.
text.NABRE:
49:1 Psalm 49 Confidence in God Rather than in Riches - For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites.
49:2 Hear this, all you peoples! Give ear, all who inhabit the world,
49:3 You of lowly birth or high estate, rich and poor together.
49:4 My mouth shall speak words of wisdom, my heart shall offer insights.
49:5 I will turn my ear to a riddle, expound my question on a lyre.
49:6 I - Why should I fear in evil days, with the iniquity of my assailants surrounding me,
49:7 Of those who trust in their wealth and boast of their abundant riches?
49:8 No man can ransom even a brother, or pay to God his own ransom.
49:9 The redemption of his soul is costly; and he will pass away forever.
49:10 Will he live on forever, then, and never see the Pit of Corruption?
49:11 Indeed, he will see that the wise die, and the fool will perish together with the senseless, and they leave their wealth to others.
49:12 Their tombs are their homes forever, their dwellings through all generations, “They named countries after themselves”
49:13 —but man does not abide in splendor. He is like the beasts—they perish.
49:14 II - This is the way of those who trust in themselves, and the end of those who take pleasure in their own mouth. Selah
49:15 Like a herd of sheep they will be put into Sheol, and Death will shepherd them. Straight to the grave they descend, where their form will waste away, Sheol will be their palace.
49:16 But God will redeem my life, will take me from the hand of Sheol. Selah
49:17 Do not fear when a man becomes rich, when the wealth of his house grows great.
49:18 At his death he will not take along anything, his glory will not go down after him.
49:19 During his life his soul uttered blessings; “They will praise you, for you do well for yourself.”
49:20 But he will join the company of his fathers, never again to see the light.
49:21 In his prime, man does not understand. He is like the beasts—they perish.
text.DRB:
49:1 A Psalm to Asaph. The God of gods our Lord hath spoken: and he hath called the earth, from the rising of the sun even to the going down.
49:2 Out of Sion the beauty of his comeliness.
49:3 God will come manifestly: our God and he will not keep silence. Fire shall burn forth in his sight: and round about him a mighty tempest.
49:4 He shall call the heaven from above: and the earth to discern his people.
49:5 Gather ye together his Saints unto him: which ordain his testament above sacrifices.
49:6 And the heavens shall shew forth his justice: because God is Judge.
49:7 Hear, o my people, and I will speak: Israel, and I will testify to thee: God thy God am I.
49:8 I will not rebuke thee in thy sacrifices: and thy holocausts are in my sight always.
49:9 I will not take calves out of thy house: nor buck-goats out of thy flocks.
49:10 Because all the wild beasts of the woods be mine, the cattle in the mountains and oxen.
49:11 I have known all the fowls of the air: and the beauty of the field is with me.
49:12 If I shall be hungry, I will not tell thee: for the round earth is mine, and the fullness thereof.
49:13 Will I eat the flesh of oxen? Or will I drink the blood of buck-goats?
49:14 Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise, and pay thy vows to the Highest.
49:15 And invocate me in the day of tribulation: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
49:16 But to the sinner God hath said: Why doest thou declare my justices, and takest my testament by thy mouth?
49:17 But thou hast hated discipline & cast my words behind thee.
49:18 If thou didst see a thief, thou didst run with him: and with adulterers thou didst put thy portion.
49:19 Thy mouth hath abounded with malice: and thy tongue forged guiles.
49:20 Sitting thou spakest against thy brother, and against thy mother's son thou didst put a scandal:
49:21 these things hast thou done, and I have held my peace. Thou hast thought unjustly that I will be like thee: I will reprove thee, and set it against thy face.
49:22 Understand these things you that forget God: lest sometime he take you violently and there be none to deliver you.
49:23 The sacrifice of praise shall glorify me: and there is the way, by which I will shew him the salvation of God.

text.NRSV:
50:1 The Lord, the God of gods, has spoken; he has called the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50:2 Out of Zion, perfect in its beauty, God reveals himself in glory.
50:3 Our God will come and will not keep silence; before him there is a consuming flame, and round about him a raging storm.
50:4 He calls the heavens and the earth from above to witness the judgment of his people.
50:5 'Gather before me my loyal followers, those who have made a covenant with me and sealed it with sacrifice.'
50:6 Let the heavens declare the rightness of his cause; for God himself is judge.
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak: 'O Israel, I will bear witness against you; for I am God, your God.
50:8 I do not accuse you because of your sacrifices; your offerings are always before me.
50:9 I will take no bull-calf from your stalls, nor he-goats out of your pens;
50:10 For all the beasts of the forest are mine, the herds in their thousands upon the hills.
50:11 I know every bird in the sky, and the creatures of the fields are in my sight.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the whole world is mine and all that is in it.
50:13 Do you think I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving and make good your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall honor me.'
50:16 But to the wicked God says: 'Why do you recite my statutes, and take my covenant upon your lips;
50:17 Since you refuse discipline and toss my words behind your back?
50:18 When you see a thief, you make him your friend, and you cast in your lot with adulterers.
50:19 You have loosed your lips for evil and harnessed your tongue to a lie.
50:20 You are always speaking evil of your brother and slandering your own mother's son.
50:21 These things you have done, and I kept silent, and you thought that I am like you. I have made my accusation; I have put my case in order before your eyes.
50:22 Consider this well, you who forget God, lest I rend you and there be none to deliver you.
50:23 Whoever offers me the sacrifice of thanksgiving honors me; but to those who keep in my way will I show the salvation of God.'
