text.KJV:
50:1 The mightie God, euen the Lord hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sunne, vnto the going downe thereof.
50:2 Out of Sion the perfection of beautie, God hath shined.
50:3 Our God shall come, and shall not keepe silence: a fire shall deuoure before him, and it shalbe very tempestuous round about him.
50:4 He shall call to the heauens from aboue, and to the earth, that hee may iudge his people.
50:5 Gather my Saints together vnto mee: those that haue made a couenant with me, by sacrifice.
50:6 And the heauens shall declare his righteousnes; for God is iudge himselfe. Selah.
50:7 Heare, O my people, and I will speake, O Israel, and I will testifie against thee; I am God, euen thy God.
50:8 I will not reproue thee for thy sacrifices, or thy burnt offerings, to haue bene continually before me.
50:9 I will take no bullocke out of thy house, nor hee goates out of thy folds.
50:10 For euery beast of the forrest is mine, and the cattell vpon a thousand hilles.
50:11 I know all the foules of the mountaines: and the wild beasts of the field are mine.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell thee, for the world is mine, and the fulnesse thereof.
50:13 Will I eate the flesh of bulles, or drinke the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer vnto God thankesgiuing, and pay thy vowes vnto the most high.
50:15 And call vpon mee in the day of trouble; I will deliuer thee, and thou shalt glorifie me.
50:16 But vnto the wicked God saith, What hast thou to doe, to declare my Statutes, or that thou shouldest take my Couenant in thy mouth?
50:17 Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behinde thee.
50:18 When thou sawest a thiefe, then thou consentedst with him, and hast bene partaker with adulterers.
50:19 Thou giuest thy mouth to euill, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
50:20 Thou sittest and speakest against thy brother; thou slanderest thine owne mothers sonne.
50:21 These things hast thou done, and I kept silence: thou thoughtest that I was altogether such a one as thy selfe: but I will reproue thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
50:22 Now consider this, ye that forget God, lest I teare you in pieces, and there be none to deliuer.
50:23 Who so offereth praise, glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conuersation aright, will I shew the saluation of God.
text.JPS1985:
50:1 A psalm of Asaph. God, the LORD God spoke and summoned the world from east to west.
50:2 From Zion, perfect in beauty, God appeared
50:3 —let our God come and not fail to act! Devouring fire preceded Him; it stormed around Him fiercely.
50:4 He summoned the heavens above, and the earth, for the trial of His people.
50:5 “Bring in My devotees, who made a covenant with Me over sacrifice! ”
50:6 Then the heavens proclaimed His righteousness, for He is a God who judges. Selah.
50:7 “Pay heed, My people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will arraign you. I am God, your God.
50:8 I censure you not for your sacrifices, and your burnt offerings, made to Me daily;
50:9 I claim no bull from your estate, no he-goats from your pens.
50:10 For Mine is every animal of the forest, the beasts on a thousand mountains.
50:11 I know every bird of the mountains, the creatures of the field are subject to Me.
50:12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you, for Mine is the world and all it holds.
50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of he-goats?
50:14 Sacrifice a thank offering to God, and pay your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Call upon Me in time of trouble; I will rescue you, and you shall honor Me.”
50:16 And to the wicked, God said: “Who are you to recite My laws, and mouth the terms of My covenant,
50:17 seeing that you spurn My discipline, and brush My words aside?
50:18 When you see a thief, you fall in with him, and throw in your lot with adulterers;
50:19 you devote your mouth to evil, and yoke your tongue to deceit;
50:20 you are busy maligning your brother, defaming the son of your mother.
50:21 If I failed to act when you did these things, you would fancy that I was like you; so I censure you and confront you with charges.
50:22 Mark this, you who are unmindful of God, lest I tear you apart and no one save you.
50:23 He who sacrifices a thank offering honors Me, and to him who improves his way I will show the salvation of God.”
text.Grail1963:
1 The God of gods, the Lord,
has spoken and summoned the earth,
from the rising of the sun to its setting.
2 Out of Zion's perfect beauty he shines.
3 (Our God comes, he keeps silence no longer.)
Before him fire devours,
around him tempest rages.
4 He calls on the heavens and the earth

to witness his judgment of his people.
5 "Summon before me my people
who made covenant with me by sacrifice."
6 The heavens proclaim his justice,
for he, God, is the judge.
7 "Listen, my people, I will speak;
Israel, I will testify against you,
for I am God, your God.
21c I accuse you, lay the charge before you.
8 I find no fault with your sacrifices,
your offerings are always before me.
9 I do not ask more bullocks from your farms,
nor goats from among your herds.
10 For I own all the beasts of the forest,
beasts in their thousands on my hills.
11 I know all the birds in the sky,
all that moves in the field belongs to me.
12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you,
for I own the world and all it holds.
13 Do you think I eat the flesh of bulls,
or drink the blood of goats?
14 Pay your sacrifice of thanksgiving to God
and render him your votive offerings.
15 Call on me in the day of distress.
I will free and you shall honor me."
16 (But God says to the wicked:)
"But how can you recite my commandments
and take my covenant on your lips,
17 you who despise my law
and throw my words to the winds,
18 you who see a thief and go with him;
who throw in your lot with adulterers,
19 who unbridle your mouth for evil
and whose tongue is plotting crime,
20 you who sit and malign your brother
and slander your own mother's son.
21 You do this, and should I keep silence?
Do you think that I am like you?
22 Mark this, you who never think of God,
lest I seize you and you cannot escape;
23 a sacrifice of thanksgiving honors me
and I will show God's salvation to the upright."
text.Rotherham:
50:1 El, Elohim, Yahweh, hath spoken, and culled the earth, From the rising of the sun, unto the going in thereof:
50:2 Out of Zion the perfection of beauty, God, hath shone forth.
50:3 Let our God come, and let him not keep silence! A fire—before him, shall devour, And, around him, hath it become exceeding tempestuous:
50:4 He calleth, Unto the heavens above, And unto the earth, That he may judge his people.
50:5 Gather yourselves unto me—ye my men of lovingkindness, Who have solemnised my covenant over sacrifice.
50:6 Now have the heavens declared his righteousness, Because, God, is, about to judge. [Selah.]
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will adjure thee, God, thine own God, I am:—
50:8 Not, for thy sacrifices, will I reprove thee, Nor for thine ascending-offerings, before me continually:
50:9 I will not take out of thy house—a bullock, Nor out of thy folds—he-goats;
50:10 For, mine, is every wild-beast of the forest, The cattle on the mountains, in their thousands;
50:11 I know every bird of the mountains, And, the moving things of the plain, are with me:
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell thee, For, mine, is the world, and the fulness thereof.
50:13 Will I eat the flesh of mighty oxen? Or, the blood of he-goats, will I drink?
50:14 Sacrifice to God a thankoffering, And pay to the Most High thy vows;
50:15 Call upon me, then, in the day of distress, I will deliver thee, that thou mayest glorify me.
50:16 But, to the lawless one, God saith, What hast, thou, to do, to recount my statutes? Or that thou hast taken up my covenant upon thy mouth?
50:17 Seeing that, thou, hast hated correction, And hast cast my words behind thee;
50:18 If thou sawest a thief, then didst thou run with him,—And, with adulterers, hath been thy chosen life;
50:19 Thy mouth, hast thou thrust into wickedness, And, thy tongue, kept weaving deceit;
50:20 Thou wouldst sit down—Against thine own brother, wouldst thou speak, Against thine own mother’s son, wouldst thou expose a fault:—
50:21 These things, hast thou done, and I have kept silence, Thou thoughtest that I should really be like thyself, I will convict thee, yea I will set [thine offences] in order before thine eyes.
50:22 Understand this, I pray you, ye forgetters of GOD, Lest I tear in pieces, and there be none to deliver:—
50:23 He that sacrificeth a thankoffering, will glorify me,—And will prepare a way by which I may show him the salvation of God.
text.NABRE:
50:1 Psalm 50 The Acceptable Sacrifice - A psalm of Asaph. I - The God of gods, the LORD, has spoken and summoned the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50:2 From Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines forth.
50:3 Our God comes and will not be silent! Devouring fire precedes him, it rages strongly around him.
50:4 He calls to the heavens above and to the earth to judge his people:
50:5 “Gather my loyal ones to me, those who made a covenant with me by sacrifice.”
50:6 The heavens proclaim his righteousness, for God himself is the judge. Selah
50:7 II - “Listen, my people, I will speak; Israel, I will testify against you; God, your God, am I.
50:8 Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you, your burnt offerings are always before me.
50:9 I will not take a bullock from your house, or he-goats from your folds.
50:10 For every animal of the forest is mine, beasts by the thousands on my mountains.
50:11 I know every bird in the heights; whatever moves in the wild is mine.
50:12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you, for mine is the world and all that fills it.
50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of he-goats?
50:14 Offer praise as your sacrifice to God; fulfill your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Then call on me on the day of distress; I will rescue you, and you shall honor me.”
50:16 III - But to the wicked God says: “Why do you recite my commandments and profess my covenant with your mouth?
50:17 You hate discipline; you cast my words behind you!
50:18 If you see a thief, you run with him; with adulterers you throw in your lot.
50:19 You give your mouth free rein for evil; you yoke your tongue to deceit.
50:20 You sit and speak against your brother, slandering your mother’s son.
50:21 When you do these things should I be silent? Do you think that I am like you? I accuse you, I lay out the matter before your eyes.
50:22 IV - “Now understand this, you who forget God, lest I start ripping apart and there be no rescuer.
50:23 Those who offer praise as a sacrifice honor me; I will let him whose way is steadfast look upon the salvation of God.”
text.DRB:
50:1 Unto the end, a Psalm of David,
50:2 when Nathan the Prophet came to him, after that he had sinned with Bethsabee.
50:3 Have mercy on me, o God, according to thy great mercy. And according to the multitude of thy commiserations, take away mine iniquity.
50:4 Wash me more amply from mine iniquity: & cleanse me from my sin.
50:5 Because I do know mine iniquity: and my sin is before me always.
50:6 To thee only have I sinned, and have done evil before thee: that thou mayst be justified in thy words, and mayst overcome when thou art judged.
50:7 For behold I was conceived in iniquities: & my mother conceived me in sins.
50:8 For behold thou hast loved truth: the uncertain and hidden things of thy wisdom thou hast made manifest to me.
50:9 Thou shalt sprinkle me with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed: thou shalt wash me, and I shall be made whiter than snow.
50:10 To my hearing thou shalt give joy and gladness, and the bones humbled shall rejoice.
50:11 Turn away thy face from my sins: and wipe away all mine iniquities.
50:12 Create a clean heart in me, o God: and renew a right spirit in my bowels.
50:13 Cast me not away from thy face: and thy Holy spirit take not from me.
50:14 Render unto me the joy of thy salvation? And confirm me with the principal spirit.
50:15 I will teach the unjust thy ways: and the impious shall be converted to thee.
50:16 Deliver me from bloods, o God, the God of my salvation: and my tongue shall exult thy justice.
50:17 Lord thou wilt open my lips: & my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
50:18 Because if thou wouldest have had sacrifice, I had verily given it: with holocausts thou wilt not be delighted.
50:19 Asacrifice to God is an afflicted spirit: a contrite and humbled heart, o God, thou wilt not despise.
50:20 Deal favourably, o Lord, in thy good will with Sion: that the walls of Jerusalem may be built up.
50:21 Then shall thou accept sacrifice of justice, oblations, & holocausts: then shall they lay calves upon thine altar.

text.NRSV:
51:1 Have mercy on me, O God, according to your loving-kindness; in your great compassion blot out my offenses.
51:2 Wash me through and through from my wickedness and cleanse me from my sin.
51:3 For I know my transgressions, and my sin is ever before me.
51:4 Against you only have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight.
51:5 And so you are justified when you speak and upright in your judgment.
51:6 Indeed, I have been wicked from my birth, a sinner from my mother's womb.
51:7 For behold, you look for truth deep within me and will make me understand wisdom secretly.
51:8 Purge me from my sin, and I shall be pure; wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51:9 Make me hear of joy and gladness, that the body you have broken may rejoice.
51:10 Hide your face from my sins and blot out all my iniquities.
51:11 Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me.
51:12 Cast me not away from your presence and take not your holy Spirit from me.
51:13 Give me the joy of your help again and sustain me with your willing Spirit.
51:14 I shall teach your ways to the wicked, and sinners shall return to you.
51:15 Deliver me from death, O God, and my tongue shall sing of your righteousness, O God of my salvation.
51:16 Open my lips, O Lord, and my mouth shall proclaim your praise.
51:17 Had you desired it, I would have offered sacrifice, but you take no delight in burnt-offerings.
51:18 The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
51:19 Be favorable and gracious to Zion, and rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:20 Then you will be pleased with the appointed sacrifices, with burnt-offerings and oblations; then shall they offer young bullocks upon your altar.

text.KJV:
51:1 Haue mercie vpon mee, O God, according to thy louing kindnesse: according vnto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
51:2 Wash mee throughly from mine iniquitie, and clense me from my sinne.
51:3 For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sinne is euer before mee.
51:4 Against thee, thee onely haue I sinned, and done this euill in thy sight: that thou mightest bee iustified when thou speakest, and be cleare when thou iudgest.
51:5 Behold, I was shapen in iniquitie: and in sinne did my mother conceiue me.
51:6 Behold, thou desirest trueth in the inward parts: and in the hidden part thou shalt make me to know wisedome.
51:7 Purge me with hyssope, and I shalbe cleane: wash me, and I shall be whiter then snow.
51:8 Make mee to heare ioy and gladnesse: that the bones which thou hast broken, may reioyce.
51:9 Hide thy face from my sinnes; and blot out all mine iniquities.
51:10 Create in mee a cleane heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within mee.
51:11 Cast mee not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy Spirit from me.
51:12 Restore vnto me the ioy of thy saluation: and vphold mee with thy free Spirit.
51:13 Then will I teach transgressours thy wayes, and sinners shalbe conuerted vnto thee.
51:14 Deliuer mee from blood-guiltinesse, O God, thou God of my saluation: and my tongue shall sing alowd of thy righteousnesse.
51:15 O Lord open thou my lips, and my mouth shall shew foorth thy praise.
51:16 For thou desirest not sacrifice: else would I giue it: thou delightest not in burnt offering.
51:17 The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.
51:18 Doe good in thy good pleasure vnto Sion: build thou the walles of Ierusalem.
51:19 Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifices of righteousnesse, with burnt offering and whole burnt offering: then shall they offer bullockes vpon thine altar.
text.JPS1985:
51:1 For the leader. A psalm of David,
51:2 when Nathan the prophet came to him after he had come to Bathsheba.
51:3 Have mercy upon me, O God, as befits Your faithfulness; in keeping with Your abundant compassion, blot out my transgressions.
51:4 Wash me thoroughly of my iniquity, and purify me of my sin;
51:5 for I recognize my transgressions, and am ever conscious of my sin.
51:6 Against You alone have I sinned, and done what is evil in Your sight; so You are just in Your sentence, and right in Your judgment.
51:7 Indeed I was born with iniquity; with sin my mother conceived me.
51:8 Indeed You desire truth about that which is hidden; teach me wisdom about secret things.
51:9 Purge me with hyssop till I am pure; wash me till I am whiter than snow.
51:10 Let me hear tidings of joy and gladness; let the bones You have crushed exult.
51:11 Hide Your face from my sins; blot out all my iniquities.
51:12 Fashion a pure heart for me, O God; create in me a steadfast spirit.
51:13 Do not cast me out of Your presence, or take Your holy spirit away from me.
51:14 Let me again rejoice in Your help; let a vigorous spirit sustain me.
51:15 I will teach transgressors Your ways, that sinners may return to You.
51:16 Save me from bloodguilt, O God, God, my deliverer, that I may sing forth Your beneficence.
51:17 O Lord, open my lips, and let my mouth declare Your praise.
51:18 You do not want me to bring sacrifices; You do not desire burnt offerings;
51:19 True sacrifice to God is a contrite spirit; God, You will not despise a contrite and crushed heart.
51:20 May it please You to make Zion prosper; rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:21 Then You will want sacrifices offered in righteousness, burnt and whole offerings; then bulls will be offered on Your altar.

text.Rotherham:
51:1 Be favorable unto me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness, And, in the multitude of thy compassions, blot out my transgressions;
51:2 Thoroughly wash me from mine iniquity, And, from my sin, make me pure;
51:3 For, my transgressions, do, I, acknowledge, And, my sin, is before me continually:
51:4 Against thee—against thee alone, have I sinned, And, wickedness—in thine eyes, have I done,—That thou mayest, Be justified when thou speakest,—Be clear when thou judgest.
51:5 Lo! in iniquity, was I brought forth, And, in sin, did my mother conceive me.
51:6 Lo! faithfulness, hast thou desired in the inward parts, Yea, in the hidden part, wilt thou cause me to know, wisdom.
51:7 Wilt thou cleanse me from sin with hyssop, That I may be pure? Wilt thou wash me, That I may be whiter, than snow?
51:8 Wilt thou cause me to hear joy and gladness? The bones thou hast crushed would exult.
51:9 Hide thy face from my sins,—And, all mine iniquities, blot out;
51:10 A pure heart, create for me, O God, And, a steadfast spirit, renew within me.
51:11 Do not cast me away from thy presence, And, thy Holy Spirit, do not take from me:
51:12 Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation, And, with a willing spirit, wilt thou uphold me:—
51:13 I would teach transgressors thy ways, And, sinners, unto thee, should return.
51:14 Rescue me from shed-blood, O God, the God of my salvation, My tongue should shout thy righteousness;
51:15 O My Lord! my lips, wilt thou open, And, my mouth, shall declare thy praise.
51:16 For thou wilt not desire sacrifice—that I should give it, Ascending-offering, will not please:
51:17 The sacrifices of God, are a spirit that is broken,—A heart—broken and crushed, O God, thou wilt not despise.
51:18 Do good, in thy good-pleasure, unto Zion, Wilt thou build the walls of Jerusalem!
51:19 Then, shalt thou desire the sacrifices of righteousness, Ascending-sacrifice and whole burnt-offering, Then, shall ascend upon thine altar, young bulls.
text.NABRE:
51:1 Psalm 51 The Miserere: Prayer of Repentance - For the leader. A psalm of David,
51:2 when Nathan the prophet came to him after he had gone in to Bathsheba.
51:3 I - Have mercy on me, God, in accord with your merciful love; in your abundant compassion blot out my transgressions.
51:4 Thoroughly wash away my guilt; and from my sin cleanse me.
51:5 For I know my transgressions; my sin is always before me.
51:6 Against you, you alone have I sinned; I have done what is evil in your eyes So that you are just in your word, and without reproach in your judgment.
51:7 Behold, I was born in guilt, in sin my mother conceived me.
51:8 Behold, you desire true sincerity; and secretly you teach me wisdom.
51:9 Cleanse me with hyssop, that I may be pure; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
51:10 You will let me hear gladness and joy; the bones you have crushed will rejoice.
51:11 II - Turn away your face from my sins; blot out all my iniquities.
51:12 A clean heart create for me, God; renew within me a steadfast spirit.
51:13 Do not drive me from before your face, nor take from me your holy spirit.
51:14 Restore to me the gladness of your salvation; uphold me with a willing spirit.
51:15 I will teach the wicked your ways, that sinners may return to you.
51:16 Rescue me from violent bloodshed, God, my saving God, and my tongue will sing joyfully of your justice.
51:17 Lord, you will open my lips; and my mouth will proclaim your praise.
51:18 For you do not desire sacrifice or I would give it; a burnt offering you would not accept.
51:19 My sacrifice, O God, is a contrite spirit; a contrite, humbled heart, O God, you will not scorn.
51:20 III - Treat Zion kindly according to your good will; build up the walls of Jerusalem.
51:21 Then you will desire the sacrifices of the just, burnt offering and whole offerings; then they will offer up young bulls on your altar.
text.DRB:
51:1 Unto the end, understanding to David,
51:2 When Doeg the Idumeite came and told Saul: David is come into the house of Achimelech.
51:3 Why doest thou glory in malice, which are mighty in iniquity?
51:4 All the day hath thy tongue thought injustice: as a sharp razor thou hast done guile.
51:5 Thou hast loved malice more than benignity: iniquity rather than to speak equity.
51:6 Thou hast loved all words of precipitation, a deceitful tongue.
51:7 Therefore will God destroy thee for ever, he will pluck thee out, & remove thee out of thy tabernacle: & thy root out of the land of the living.
51:8 The just shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him, and they shall say:
51:9 Behold the man, that hath not put God for his helper. But hath hoped in the multitude of his riches, and hath prevailed in his vanity.
51:10 But I as a fruitful olive tree in the house of God, have hoped in the mercy of God for ever: and for ever and ever.
51:11 I will confess to thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will expect thy name, because it is good in the sight of thy Saints.

text.NRSV:
52:1 You tyrant, why do you boast of wickedness against the godly all day long?
52:2 You plot ruin; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, O worker of deception.
52:3 You love evil more than good and lying more than speaking the truth.
52:4 You love all words that hurt, O you deceitful tongue.
52:5 Oh, that God would demolish you utterly, topple you, and snatch you from your dwelling, and root you out of the land of the living!
52:6 The righteous shall see and tremble, and they shall laugh at him, saying,
52:7 'See the man who did not make God his stronghold, but trusted in the height of his wealth and relied upon his wickedness.'
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
52:9 I will give you thanks for ever for what you have done; in the presence of the faithful I will wait upon your Name, for it is good.
text.Coverdale:

text.KJV:
52:1 Why boastest thou thy selfe in mischiefe, O mightie man? the goodnesse of God indureth continually.
52:2 Thy tongue deuiseth mischiefes: like a sharpe rasor, working deceitfully.
52:3 Thou louest euill more then good; and lying rather then to speake righteousnesse. Selah.
52:4 Thou louest all deuouring words, O thou deceitfull tongue.
52:5 God shall likewise destroy thee for euer, hee shall take thee away and plucke thee out of thy dwelling place, and roote thee out of the land of the liuing. Selah.
52:6 The righteous also shall see, and feare, and shall laugh at him.
52:7 Loe, this is the man that made not God his strength: but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himselfe in his wickednesse.
52:8 But I am like a greene oliue tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for euer and euer.
52:9 I will prayse thee for euer, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name, for it is good before thy Saints.
text.JPS1985:
52:1 For the leader. A maskil of David,
52:2 when Doeg the Edomite came and informed Saul, telling him, “David came to Ahimelech’s house.”
52:3 Why do you boast of your evil, brave fellow? God’s faithfulness never ceases.
52:4 Your tongue devises mischief, like a sharpened razor that works treacherously.
52:5 You prefer evil to good, the lie, to speaking truthfully. Selah.
52:6 You love all pernicious words, treacherous speech.
52:7 So God will tear you down for good, will break you and pluck you from your tent, and root you out of the land of the living. Selah.
52:8 The righteous, seeing it, will be awestruck; they will jibe at him, saying,
52:9 “Here was a fellow who did not make God his refuge, but trusted in his great wealth, relied upon his mischief.”
52:10 But I am like a thriving olive tree in God’s house; I trust in the faithfulness of God forever and ever.
52:11 I praise You forever, for You have acted; I declare that Your name is good in the presence of Your faithful ones.

text.Rotherham:
52:1 Why dost thou boast thyself of wickedness, O mighty man? The lovingkindness of GOD, [lasteth] all the day.
52:2 Engulfing ruin, doth thy tongue devise, Like a whetted razor, working deceit;
52:3 Thou lovest Evil more than good, Falsehood, more than speaking righteousness. [Selah.]
52:4 Thou lovest all devouring words, O deceitful tongue!
52:5 GOD also, will break thee down utterly, He will snatch thee up and tear thee away tentless, And uproot thee, out of the land of the living. [Selah.]
52:6 The righteous shall both see and fear, And, over him, shall laugh:
52:7 Lo! the man who made not God his refuge,—But trusted in the abundance of his riches, Emboldened himself in his wealth!
52:8 But, I, am like a flourishing olive-tree, in the house of God, I have put confidence in the lovingkindness of God, for times age-abiding and beyond.
52:9 I will praise thee to times age-abiding, Because thou didst effectually work,—And I will wait on thy Name, Because it is good, In the presence of thy men of lovingkindness.
text.NABRE:
52:1 Psalm 52 The Deceitful Tongue - For the leader. A maskil of David,
52:2 when Doeg the Edomite entered and reported to Saul, saying to him: “David has entered the house of Ahimelech.”
52:3 I - Why do you glory in what is evil, you who are mighty by the mercy of God? All day long
52:4 you are thinking up intrigues; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, you worker of deceit.
52:5 You love evil more than good, lying rather than saying what is right. Selah
52:6 You love all the words that create confusion, you deceitful tongue.
52:7 II - God too will strike you down forever, he will lay hold of you and pluck you from your tent, uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
52:8 The righteous will see and they will fear; but they will laugh at him:
52:9 “Behold the man! He did not take God as his refuge, but he trusted in the abundance of his wealth, and grew powerful through his wickedness.”
52:10 III - But I, like an olive tree flourishing in the house of God, I trust in God’s mercy forever and ever.
52:11 I will thank you forever for what you have done. I will put my hope in your name—for it is good, —in the presence of those devoted to you.
text.DRB:
52:1 Unto the end, for Mæleth, understandings of David. The fool hath said in his heart: There is no God.
52:2 They are corrupt, and become abominable in iniquities: there is not that doth good.
52:3 God hath looked forth from heaven, upon the children of men: to see if there be that understandeth, or seeketh after God.
52:4 All have declined, they are become unprofitable together: there is not that doth good, no there is not one.
52:5 Shall they not all know that work iniquity, that devour my people as food of bread?
52:6 God they have not invocated: there have they trembled for fear, where no fear was. Because God hath dissipated the bones of them that please men: they are confounded, because God hath despised them.
52:7 Who will give out of Sion the salvation of Israel? When God shall convert the captivity of his people: Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

text.NRSV:
53:1 The fool has said in his heart, 'There is no God.' They are corrupt, and have done abominable iniquity; there is none who does good.
53:2 God looks down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there is any who is wise, who seeks after God.
53:3 Every one has gone astray; they have all become corrupt; there is none who does good, no, not one.
53:4 Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and do not call upon God?
53:5 There they are, in great fear, where no fear was! For God has scattered the bones of those who encamp against you; you put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:6 Oh, that salvation for Israel would come out of Zion! When God restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice; Israel will be glad.
text.Coverdale:
53:1 The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." *
      All are corrupt and commit abominable acts;
      there is none who does any good.
53:2 God looks down from heaven upon us all, *
      to see if there is any who is wise,
      if there is one who seeks after God.
53:3 Every one has proved faithless;
      all alike have turned bad; *
      there is none who does good; no, not one.
53:4 Have they no knowledge, those evildoers *
      who eat up my people like bread
      and do not call upon God?
53:5 See how greatly they tremble,
      such trembling as never was; *
      for God has scattered the bones of the enemy;
      they are put to shame, because God has rejected them.
53:6 Oh, that Israel's deliverance would come out of Zion! *
      when God restores the fortunes of his people,
      Jacob will rejoice and Israel be glad.
text.KJV:
53:1 The foole hath sayde in his heart, There is no god; Corrupt are they, and haue done abhominable iniquitie; there is none that doth good.
53:2 God looked downe from heauen vpon the children of men, to see if there were any that did vnderstand, that did seeke God.
53:3 Euery one of them is gone backe, they are altogether become filthy: there is none that doth good, no not one.
53:4 Haue the workers of iniquitie no knowledge? who eate vp my people, as they eate bread; they haue not called vpon God.
53:5 There were they in great feare, where no feare was: for God hath scattered the bones of him that incampeth against thee, thou hast put them to shame, because God hath despised them.
53:6 O that the saluation of Israel were come out of Sion! when God bringeth backe the captiuitie of his people, Iaakob shall reioyce, and Israel shall be glad.
text.JPS1985:
53:1 For the leader; on mahalath. A maskil of David.
53:2 The benighted man thinks, “God does not care.” Man’s wrongdoing is corrupt and loathsome; no one does good.
53:3 God looks down from heaven on mankind to find a man of understanding, a man mindful of God.
53:4 Everyone is dross, altogether foul; there is none who does good, not even one.
53:5 Are they so witless, those evildoers, who devour my people as they devour food, and do not invoke God?
53:6 There they will be seized with fright — never was there such a fright— for God has scattered the bones of your besiegers; you have put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:7 O that the deliverance of Israel might come from Zion! When God restores the fortunes of His people, Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.
text.Grail1963:
.2 The fool has said in his heart:
"There is no God above."
Their deeds are corrupt, depraved;
not a good man is left.
.3 God looks down from heaven
on the sons of men
to see if any are wise,
if any seek God.
.4 All have left the right path;
depraved, every one;
There is not a good man left,
no, not even one.
.5 Will the evildoers not understand?
They eat up my people
as though they were eating bread;
they never pray to God.
.6 See how they tremble with fear
without cause for fear,

for God scatters the bones of the wicked.
They are shamed, God rejects them.
.7 O that Israel's salvation might come from Zion!
When God delivers his people from bondage,
then Jacob will be glad and Israel rejoice.
text.Rotherham:
53:1 The impious hath said in his heart, There is no God! They have acted corruptly, They have wrought abominable perversity, There is none that doeth good:—
53:2 God, out of the heavens, looked down upon the sons of men,—To see whether there was one that showed wisdom, Enquiring after God.
53:3 They all, have turned back, together have they become tainted,—There is none that doeth good, Not so much as, one!
53:4 Are the workers of iniquity, without knowledge? Devouring my people, [as] they devour food? Upon God, have they not called.
53:5 There have they been in great dread where no dread was, Because, God, hath scattered the bones of thy besieger,—Thou hast put [him] to shame, Because, God, had, rejected, them.
53:6 Oh that, out of Zion, were granted the salvations of Israel! When God bringeth back the captives of his people, Jacob, shall exult, Israel, shall be glad.
text.NABRE:
53:1 Psalm 53 A Lament over Widespread Corruption - For the leader; according to Mahalath. A maskil of David.
53:2 I - The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” They act corruptly and practice injustice; there is none that does good.
53:3 God looks out from the heavens upon the children of Adam, To see if there is a discerning person who is seeking God.
53:4 All have gone astray; each one is altogether perverse. There is not one who does what is good, not even one.
53:5 II - Do they not know better, those who do evil, who feed upon my people as they feed upon bread? Have they not called upon God?
53:6 They are going to fear his name with great fear, though they had not feared it before. For God will scatter the bones of those encamped against you. They will surely be put to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:7 III - Who will bring forth from Zion the salvation of Israel? When God reverses the captivity of his people Jacob will rejoice and Israel will be glad.
text.DRB:
53:1 Unto the end, in songs understanding for David
53:2 when the Zipheites were come, and said to Saul: Is not David hid with us?
53:3 O God save me in thy name: and in thy strength judge me.
53:4 O God hear my prayer with thine ears: receive the words of my mouth.
53:5 Because strangers have risen up against me, & the strong have sought my soul: and they have not set God before their eyes.
53:6 For behold God helpeth me: and our Lord is the receiver of my soul.
53:7 Turn away the evils of mine enemies: and in thy truth destroy them.
53:8 I will voluntarily sacrifice to thee, and will confess to thy name o Lord, because it is good:
53:9 Because thou hast delivered me out of all tribulation: and mine eye hath looked down upon mine enemies.

text.NRSV:
54:1 Save me, O God, by your name, and vindicate me by your might.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For strangers have risen against me, and ruthless ones seek my life; they do not set God before their eyes. Selah
54:4 Behold, God is my helper; the Lord is the upholder of my life.
54:5 He will repay my enemies for their evil; in your faithfulness, put an end to them.
54:6 With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, O Lord, for it is good.
54:7 For he has delivered me from every trouble, and my eye has looked in triumph on my enemies.
text.Coverdale:
54:1 Save me, O God, by your Name; *
      in your might, defend my cause.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; *
      give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For the arrogant have risen up against me,
      and the ruthless have sought my life, *
      those who have no regard for God.
54:4 Behold, God is my helper; *
      it is the Lord who sustains my life.
54:5 Render evil to those who spy on me; *
      in your faithfulness, destroy them.
54:6 I will offer you a freewill sacrifice *
      and praise your Name, O LORD, for it is good.
54:7 For you have rescued me from every trouble, *
      and my eye has seen the ruin of my foes.
text.KJV:
54:1 Saue me, O God, by thy name, and iudge me by thy strength.
54:2 Heare my prayer, O God; giue eare to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For strangers are risen vp against me, and oppressors seeke after my soule; they haue not set God before them. Selah.
54:4 Behold, God is mine helper: the Lord is with them that vphold my soule.
54:5 He shall reward euill vnto mine enemies: cut them off in thy trueth.
54:6 I will freely sacrifice vnto thee; I will praise thy name (O Lord:) for it is good.
54:7 For hee hath deliuered me out of all trouble: and mine eye hath seene his desire vpon mine enemies.
text.JPS1985:
54:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A maskil of David,
54:2 when the Ziphites came and told Saul, “Know, David is in hiding among us.”
54:3 O God, deliver me by Your name; by Your power vindicate me.
54:4 O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:5 For strangers have risen against me, and ruthless men seek my life; they are unmindful of God. Selah.
54:6 See, God is my helper; the Lord is my support.
54:7 He will repay the evil of my watchful foes; by Your faithfulness, destroy them!
54:8 Then I will offer You a freewill sacrifice; I will praise Your name, LORD, for it is good,
54:9 for it has saved me from my foes, and let me gaze triumphant upon my enemies.
text.Grail1963:
.3 O God, save me by your name;
by your power, uphold my cause.
.4 O God, hear my prayer;
listen to the words of my mouth.
.5 For proud men have risen against me,
ruthless men seek my life.
They have no regard for God.

text.NABRE:
54:1 Psalm 54 Confident Prayer in Great Peril - For the leader. On stringed instruments. A maskil of David,
54:2 when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, “David is hiding among us.”
54:3 I - O God, by your name save me. By your strength defend my cause.
54:4 O God, hear my prayer. Listen to the words of my mouth.
54:5 Strangers have risen against me; the ruthless seek my life; they do not keep God before them. Selah
54:6 II - God is present as my helper; the Lord sustains my life.
54:7 Turn back the evil upon my foes; in your faithfulness, destroy them.
54:8 Then I will offer you generous sacrifice and give thanks to your name, LORD, for it is good.
54:9 Because it has rescued me from every trouble, and my eyes look down on my foes.
text.DRB:
54:1 Unto the end in songs, understanding to David.
54:2 Hear my prayer, o God, despise not my petition.
54:3 Attend to me and hear me.
54:4 I am made sorrowful in my exercise: and am troubled at the voice of the enemy: and at the tribulation of the sinner. Because they have wrested iniquities upon me: & in anger they were troublesome to me.
54:5 My heart is troubled in me: & the fear of death is fallen upon me.
54:6 Fear and trembling are come upon me: and darkness hath covered me.
54:7 And I said: Who will give me wings as of a dove, and I will fly: and rest?
54:8 Lo I have gone far flying away: and I abode in the wilderness.
54:9 I expected him, that saved me from pusillanimity of spirit, and tempest.
54:10 Precipitate, o Lord, and divide their tongues: because I have seen iniquity, and contradiction in the city.
54:11 Day and night shall iniquity compass it upon the walls thereof, and labour in the midst thereof, and injustice.
54:12 And there hath not ceased out of the streets thereof usury and guile.
54:13 For if mine enemy had spoken evil to me, I would verily have born it. And if he that hated me had spoken great things upon me: I would perhaps have hid myself from him.
54:14 But thou a man of the same mind: my guide, and my familiar.
54:15 Which didest take sweet meats together with me: in the house of God we walked with consent.
54:16 Let death come upon them: and let them go down quick into hell. Because there is wickedness in their habitations, in the midst of them.
54:17 But I have cried to God, and our Lord will save me.
54:18 In the evening, and morning, & at midday, I will speak, and declare, and he will hear my voice.
54:19 He will redeem my soul in peace from them, that approach to me: because among many they were with me.
54:20 God will hear, and he which is before the worlds will humble them.
54:21 For there is no change with them, & they feared not God: he hath stretched forth his hand in repaying.
54:22 They have contaminated his testament, they are divided by the wrath of his countenance; and his heart hath approached. His words are made softer than oil: and the same are darts.
54:23 Cast thy care upon our Lord: and he will nourish thee: he will not give fluctuation to the just for ever.
54:24 But thou, o God, wilt bring them down into the pit of destruction. Bloody and deceitful men shall not live half their days, but I will hope in thee, o Lord.

text.NRSV:
55:1 Give ear to my prayer, O God, and do not hide yourself from my supplication.
55:2 Attend to me, and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and I moan,
55:3 because of the noise of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
55:4 My heart is in anguish within me, the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55:5 Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.
55:6 And I say, 'O that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest;'
55:7 truly, I would flee far away; I would lodge in the wilderness;
55:8 I would hurry to find a shelter for myself from the raging wind and tempest.'
55:9 Confuse, O Lord, confound their speech; for I see violence and strife in the city.
55:10 Day and night they go around it on its walls, and iniquity and trouble are within it;
55:11 ruin is in its midst; oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
55:12 It is not enemies who taunt me—I could bear that; it is not adversaries who deal insolently with me—I could hide from them.
55:13 But it is you, my equal, my companion, my familiar friend,
55:14 with whom I kept pleasant company; we walked in the house of God with the throng.
55:15 Let death come upon them; let them go down alive to Sheol; for evil is in their homes and in their hearts.
text.Coverdale:
55:1 Hear my prayer, O God; *
      do not hide yourself from my petition.
55:2 Listen to me and answer me; *
      I have no peace, because of my cares.
55:3 I am shaken by the noise of the enemy *
      and by the pressure of the wicked;
55:4 For they have cast trouble upon me, *
      and in their anger they bear a grudge against me.
55:5 My heart quakes within me, *
      and the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55:6 Fear and trembling have come over me, *
      and horror overwhelms me.
55:7 And I said, "Oh, that I had wings like a dove! *
      I would fly away and be at rest.
55:8 I would flee to a far-off place *
      and make my lodging in the wilderness.
55:9 I would hasten to escape *
      from the stormy wind and tempest."
55:10 Swallow them up, O Lord;
      confound their speech; *
      for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55:11 Day and night the watchmen make their rounds upon her walls, *
      but trouble and misery are in the midst of her.
55:12 There is corruption at her heart; *
      her streets are never free of oppression and deceit.
55:13 For had it been an adversary who taunted me,
      then I could have borne it; *
      or had it been an enemy who vaunted himself against me,
      then I could have hidden from him.
55:14 But it was you, a man after my own heart, *
      my companion, my own familiar friend.
55:15 We took sweet counsel together, *
      and walked with the throng in the house of God.
55:16 Let death come upon them suddenly;
      let them go down alive to the grave; *
      for wickedness is in their dwellings, in their very midst.
55:17 But I will call upon God, *
      and the LORD will deliver me.
55:18 In the evening, in the morning, and at the noonday,
      I will complain and lament, *
      and he will hear my voice.
55:19 He will bring me safely back from the battle waged against me; *
      for there are many who fight me.
55:20 God, who is enthroned of old, will hear me and bring them down; *
      they never change; they do not fear God.
55:21 My companion stretched forth his hand against his comrade; *
      he has broken his covenant.
55:22 His speech is softer than butter, *
      but war is in his heart.
55:23 His words are smoother than oil, *
      but they are drawn swords.
55:24 Cast your burden upon the LORD,
      and he will sustain you; *
      he will never let the righteous stumble.
55:25 For you will bring the bloodthirsty and deceitful *
      down to the pit of destruction, O God.
55:26 They shall not live out half their days, *
      but I will put my trust in you.
text.KJV:
55:1 Giue eare to my prayer, O God: and hide not thy selfe from my supplication.
55:2 Attend vnto me, and heare me: I mourne in my complaint, and make a noise.
55:3 Because of the voyce of the enemie, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquitie vpon me, and in wrath they hate me.
55:4 My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrours of death are fallen vpon me.
55:5 Fearefulnesse and trembling are come vpon me, and horrour hath ouerwhelmed me.
55:6 And I said, O that I had wings like a doue; for then would I flee away and be at rest.
55:7 Loe, then would I wander farre off, and remaine in the wildernesse. Selah.
55:8 I would hasten my escape from the windie storme, and tempest.
55:9 Destroy, O Lord, and diuide their tongues: for I haue seene violence and strife in the citie.
55:10 Day and night they goe about it vpon the walles thereof: mischiefe also and sorrow are in the midst of it.
55:11 Wickednesse is in the midst thereof: deceite and guile depart not from her streets.
55:12 For it was not an enemie that reproached me, then I could haue borne it, neither was it hee that hated me, that did magnifie himselfe against me, then I would haue hid my selfe from him.
55:13 But it was thou, a man, mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.
55:14 Wee tooke sweet counsell together, and walked vnto the house of God in companie.
55:15 Let death seaze vpon them, and let them goe downe quicke into hell: for wickednes is in their dwellings, and among them.
55:16 As for me, I will call vpon God: and the Lord shall saue me.
55:17 Euening and morning, and at noone will I pray, and crie aloud: and he shall heare my voyce.
55:18 He hath deliuered my soule in peace from the battell that was against me: for there were many with me.
55:19 God shall heare and afflict them, euen he that abideth of old, Selah: because they haue no changes, therefore they feare not God.
55:20 He hath put foorth his handes against such as be at peace with him: he hath broken his couenant.
55:21 The words of his mouth were smoother then butter, but warre was in his heart: his words were softer then oyle, yet were they drawen swords.
55:22 Cast thy burden vpon the Lord, and he shall sustaine thee: hee shall neuer suffer the righteous to bee moued.
55:23 But thou, O God, shalt bring them downe into the pit of destruction: Bloody and deceitfull men shall not liue out halfe their dayes, but I will trust in thee.
text.JPS1985:
55:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A maskil of David.
55:2 Give ear, O God, to my prayer; do not ignore my plea;
55:3 pay heed to me and answer me. I am tossed about, complaining and moaning
55:4 at the clamor of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked; for they bring evil upon me and furiously harass me.
55:5 My heart is convulsed within me; terrors of death assail me.
55:6 Fear and trembling invade me; I am clothed with horror.
55:7 I said, “O that I had the wings of a dove! I would fly away and find rest;
55:8 surely, I would flee far off; I would lodge in the wilderness; selah
55:9 I would soon find me a refuge from the sweeping wind, from the tempest.”
55:10 O Lord, confound their speech, confuse it! For I see lawlessness and strife in the city;
55:11 day and night they make their rounds on its walls; evil and mischief are inside it.
55:12 Malice is within it; fraud and deceit never leave its square.
55:13 It is not an enemy who reviles me —I could bear that; it is not my foe who vaunts himself against me —I could hide from him;
55:14 but it is you, my equal, my companion, my friend;
55:15 sweet was our fellowship; we walked together in God’s house.
55:16 Let Him incite death against them; may they go down alive into Sheol! For where they dwell, there evil is.
55:17 As for me, I call to God; the LORD will deliver me.
55:18 Evening, morning, and noon, I complain and moan, and He hears my voice.
55:19 He redeems me unharmed from the battle against me; it is as though many are on my side.
55:20 God who has reigned from the first, who will have no successor, hears and humbles those who have no fear of God. Selah.
55:21 He harmed his ally, he broke his pact;
55:22 his talk was smoother than butter, yet his mind was on war; his words were more soothing than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
55:23 Cast your burden on the LORD and He will sustain you; He will never let the righteous man collapse.
55:24 For You, O God, will bring them down to the nethermost Pit— those murderous, treacherous men; they shall not live out half their days; but I trust in You.

text.Rotherham:
55:1 Give ear, O God, to my prayer, And do not hide thyself from my supplication:
55:2 Attend unto me, and answer me, I may ramble in my complaining, And be driven to and fro—
55:3 Because of the noise of the enemy, By reason of the oppression of the lawless one, For they would let trouble drop upon me, And, in anger, would they entrap me.
55:4 My heart, would writhe within me, And, the terrors of Death, have fallen upon me:
55:5 Fear and trembling, would enter me, And there would have overwhelmed me—a horror!
55:6 Then I said—Oh that there were given me a pinion, like a dove, I would fly away and be at rest:
55:7 Lo! I would take a distant flight, I would tarry in the wilderness. [Selah.]
55:8 I would hasten mine escape, From rushing wind, from storm.
55:9 Confuse, O My Lord, divide their speech, For I have seen violence and contention in the city;
55:10 Day and night, they go round her, upon her walls, And, trouble and misery, are in her midst;
55:11 Engulfing ruin, is in her midst, And there depart not from her broadway, oppression and deceit.
55:12 For it is, not an enemy, that reproacheth me, Or I could bear it,—Not one that hath hated me, who, against me, hath magnified himself, Or I might hide myself from him;
55:13 But it is, thou, a man esteemed as mine equal, mine associate, and mine acquaintance;
55:14 So that together have we been wont to find sweetness in counsel, In the house of God, used we to walk in the throng.
55:15 Desolations on them! Let them go down into hades alive, For, wicked doings, are at home within them.
55:16 I, unto God, will cry,—And, Yahweh, will save me.
55:17 At evening and morning and high noon, have I been wont to lament and complain, And he hath heard my voice!
55:18 He hath completely redeemed my soul, out of the attack upon me, For, in multitudes, were they [in conflict] with me.
55:19 GOD will hear, Yea He will humble them who aforetime sat [enthroned] . [Selah.] With whom are no changings, Neither have they revered God.
55:20 He hath thrust forth his hands, against them he was wont to salute, he hath violated his covenant;
55:21 Smoother than curds, were [the words of] his mouth, But, war, was [in] his heart,—Softer his words than oil, Yet, they, were drawn swords!
55:22 Cast upon Yahweh thy lot, and, he, will sustain thee: He will not suffer, to times age-abiding, the righteous one to be shaken.
55:23 But, thou, O God, wilt bring them down to the pit of destruction, Men of bloodshed and deceit, shall not live out half their days; But, I, will trust in thee.
text.NABRE:
55:1 Psalm 55 A Lament over Betrayal - For the leader. On stringed instruments. A maskil of David.
55:2 I - Listen, God, to my prayer; do not hide from my pleading;
55:3 hear me and give answer. I rock with grief; I groan
55:4 at the uproar of the enemy, the clamor of the wicked. They heap trouble upon me, savagely accuse me.
55:5 My heart pounds within me; death’s terrors fall upon me.
55:6 Fear and trembling overwhelm me; shuddering sweeps over me.
55:7 I say, “If only I had wings like a dove that I might fly away and find rest.
55:8 Far away I would flee; I would stay in the desert. Selah
55:9 “I would soon find a shelter from the raging wind and storm.”
55:10 II - Lord, check and confuse their tongues. For I see violence and strife in the city
55:11 making rounds on its walls day and night. Within are mischief and trouble;
55:12 treachery is in its midst; oppression and fraud never leave its streets.
55:13 For it is not an enemy that reviled me— that I could bear— Not a foe who viewed me with contempt, from that I could hide.
55:14 But it was you, my other self, my comrade and friend,
55:15 You, whose company I enjoyed, at whose side I walked in the house of God.
55:16 III - Let death take them; let them go down alive to Sheol, for evil is in their homes and bellies.
55:17 But I will call upon God, and the LORD will save me.
55:18 At dusk, dawn, and noon I will grieve and complain, and my prayer will be heard.
55:19 He will redeem my soul in peace from those who war against me, though there are many who oppose me.
55:20 God, who sits enthroned forever, will hear me and afflict them. Selah For they will not mend their ways; they have no fear of God.
55:21 He stretched out his hand at his friends and broke his covenant.
55:22 Softer than butter is his speech, but war is in his heart. Smoother than oil are his words, but they are unsheathed swords.
55:23 Cast your care upon the LORD, who will give you support. He will never allow the righteous to stumble.
55:24 But you, God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction. These bloodthirsty liars will not live half their days, but I put my trust in you.
text.DRB:
55:1 Unto the end, for a people, that is made far from the Saints, David in the inscription of the title, when the foreigners held him in Geth.
55:2 Have mercy on me, o God, because man hath trodden upon me: all the day impugning he hath afflicted me.
55:3 Mine enemies have trodden upon me all the day: because they are many that war against me.
55:4 From the height of the day I shall fear: but I will trust in thee.
55:5 In God I will praise my words, in God have I hoped: I will not fear what flesh may do to me.
55:6 All the day did they detest my words; against me all their cogitations are unto evil.
55:7 They will inhabit and keep secret: they will observe my heel. As they have expected my soul,
55:8 for nothing shalt thou save them: in wrath thou wilt break peoples. O God,
55:9 I have shewed my life to thee: thou hast set my tears in thy sight. As also in thy promise:
55:10 then shall mine enemies be turned backward. In what day soever I shall invocate thee: lo I have known that thou art my God.
55:11 In God I will praise the word, in our Lord will I praise thee saying: I have hoped in God, I will not fear what man can do to me.
55:12 In me, o God are thy vows, which I will render, praises to thee.
55:13 Because thou hast delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling: that I may please before God, in the light of the living.

text.NRSV:
56:1 Have mercy on me, O God, for my enemies are hounding me; all day long they assault and oppress me.
56:2 My adversaries hound me all day long, and many are they who fight against me, O Most High.
56:3 Whenever I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.
56:4 In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, for what can flesh do to me?
56:5 All day long they damage my cause; their only thought is to do me evil.
56:6 They band together and lie in wait; they spy upon my footsteps; because they seek my life.
56:7 Shall they escape despite their wickedness? O God, in your anger, cast down the peoples.
56:8 You have noted my lamentation; put my tears into your bottle; are they not recorded in your book?
56:9 Whenever I call upon you, my enemies will be put to flight; this I know, for God is on my side.
56:10 In God the Lord, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, for what can mortals do to me?
56:11 I am bound by the vows I made to you, O God; I will present to you thank-offerings.
56:12 For you have rescued my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.Coverdale:
56:1 Have mercy on me, O God,
      for my enemies are hounding me; *
      all day long they assault and oppress me.
56:2 My foes are hounding me all day long; *
      truly there are many who fight against me, O Most High.
56:3 When I am afraid, *
      I put my trust in you.
56:4 In God, whose word I praise,
      in God I trust and will not be afraid, *
      for what can flesh do to me?
56:5 All day long they damage my cause; *
      their only thought is to do me evil.
56:6 They band together; they lie in wait; *
      they spy upon my footsteps;
      because they seek my life.
56:7 Shall they escape despite their wickedness? *
      O God, in your anger, cast down the peoples.
56:8 You have noted my lamentation;
      put my tears in your bottle; *
      are they not recorded in your book?
56:9 Whenever I call upon you, my enemies will be put to flight; *
      this I know, for God is on my side.
56:10 In God the LORD, whose word I praise,
      in God I trust and will not be afraid, *
      for what can mortals do to me?
56:11 I am bound by the vow I made to you, O God; *
      I will present my thank-offerings to you.
56:12 For you have rescued my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, *
      that I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.KJV:
56:1 Be mercifull vnto mee, O God, for man would swallow me vp: he fighting daily, oppresseth me.
56:2 Mine enemies would dayly swallow me vp: for they bee many that fight against me, O thou most high.
56:3 What time I am afraide, I will trust in thee.
56:4 In God I will praise his worde, In God I haue put my trust, I will not feare what flesh can doe vnto me.
56:5 Euery day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against mee for euill.
56:6 They gather themselues together; they hide themselues, they marke my steps when they wait for my soule.
56:7 Shall they escape by iniquitie? in thine anger cast downe the people, O God.
56:8 Thou tellest my wanderings, put thou my teares into thy bottle: are they not in thy booke?
56:9 When I crie vnto thee, then shall mine enemies turne backe: this I know, for God is for me.
56:10 In God will I praise his word: in the Lord will I praise his word.
56:11 In God haue I put my trust: I will not bee afraid what man can doe vnto me.
56:12 Thy vowes are vpon me, O God: I will render praises vnto thee.
56:13 For thou hast deliuered my soule from death: wilt not thou deliuer my feet from falling? that I may walke before God in the light of the liuing.
text.JPS1985:
56:1 For the leader; on yonath elem reḥokim. Of David. A michtam; when the Philistines seized him in Gath.
56:2 Have mercy on me, O God, for men persecute me; all day long my adversary oppresses me.
56:3 My watchful foes persecute me all day long; many are my adversaries, O Exalted One.
56:4 When I am afraid, I trust in You,
56:5 in God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can mortals do to me?
56:6 All day long they cause me grief in my affairs, they plan only evil against me.
56:7 They plot, they lie in ambush; they watch my every move, hoping for my death.
56:8 Cast them out for their evil; subdue peoples in Your anger, O God.
56:9 You keep count of my wanderings; put my tears into Your flask, into Your record.
56:10 Then my enemies will retreat when I call on You; this I know, that God is for me.
56:11 In God, whose word I praise, in the LORD, whose word I praise,
56:12 in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can man do to me?
56:13 I must pay my vows to You, O God; I will render thank offerings to You.
56:14 For You have saved me from death, my foot from stumbling, that I may walk before God in the light of life.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Have mercy on me, God, men crush me;
they fight me all day long and oppress me.
.3 My foes crush me all day long,
for many fight proudly against me.
.4 When I fear, I will trust in you,
.5 in God, who word I praise.
In God I trust, I shall not fear;
what can mortal man do to me?
.6 All day long they distort my words,
all their thought is to harm me.
.7 They band together in ambush,
track me down and seek my life.

in your anger, cast down the peoples.

This I know, that God is on my side.
.11 In God, whose word I praise,

what can mortal man do to me?

text.Rotherham:
56:1 Show me favour, O God, For weak man hath panted for me, All the day, a fighter oppresseth me:
56:2 Mine adversaries have panted all the day, For, many, are fighting with me, loftily.
56:3 What day I am afraid, I, unto thee will direct my confidence.
56:4 In God, I will praise his cause,—In God, have I trusted, I will not fear, What can flesh do unto me?
56:5 All the day, they wrest, my words, Against me, all their devices are for mischief;
56:6 They stir up strife—they lie hid, They, mark my steps,—Seeing they have waited for my life.
56:7 Because of iniquity, recompense thou them,—In anger, bring thou down, the peoples, O God.
56:8 My wandering, hast, thou, recorded,—Put thou my tears in thy bottle, Are they not in thy record?
56:9 Then, shall my foes turn back, in the day I cry, This, I know, for God is mine!
56:10 In God, will I praise with good cause: In Yahweh, will I praise with good cause;
56:11 In God, have I trusted, I will not fear, What can a son of earth do unto me!
56:12 Upon me, O God, are thy vows, I will pay back praises unto thee.
56:13 For thou hast rescued my soul from death, Wilt thou not [rescue] my feet from stumbling? That I may walk to and fro, before God, In the light of life.
text.NABRE:
56:1 Psalm 56 Trust in God - For the director. According to Yonath elem rehoqim. A miktam of David, when the Philistines seized him at Gath.
56:2 I - Have mercy on me, God, for I am treated harshly; attackers press me all the day.
56:3 My foes treat me harshly all the day; yes, many are my attackers. O Most High,
56:4 when I am afraid, in you I place my trust.
56:5 I praise the word of God; I trust in God, I do not fear. What can mere flesh do to me?
56:6 II - All the day they foil my plans; their every thought is of evil against me.
56:7 They hide together in ambush; they watch my every step; they lie in wait for my life.
56:8 They are evil; watch them, God! Cast the nations down in your anger!
56:9 My wanderings you have noted; are my tears not stored in your flask, recorded in your book?
56:10 My foes turn back when I call on you. This I know: God is on my side.
56:11 I praise the word of God, I praise the word of the LORD.
56:12 In God I trust, I do not fear. What can man do to me?
56:13 III - I have made vows to you, God; with offerings I will fulfill them,
56:14 For you have snatched me from death, kept my feet from stumbling, That I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.DRB:
56:1 Unto the end, destroy not, to David in the inscription of the title, when he fled from the face of Saul into the cave.
56:2 Have mercy on me, o God, have mercy on me: because my soul hath trusted in thee. And I will hope in the shadow of thy wings, until iniquity pass.
56:3 I will cry to God the highest: God that hath done me good.
56:4 He sent from heaven, and delivered me: he hath given into reproach them that trod upon me. God hath sent his mercy and his truth,
56:5 and hath delivered my soul out of the midst of lions' whelps: I slept troubled. The sons of men, their teeth are weapons and arrows: and their tongue a sharp sword.
56:6 Be exalted above the heavens, o God: and thy glory upon all the earth.
56:7 They prepared a snare for my feet: and bowed down my soul. They digged a pit before my face: and they are fallen into it.
56:8 My heart is ready, o God, my heart is ready: I will sing, and say a Psalm.
56:9 Arise my glory, arise psalter and harp: I will arise early.
56:10 I will confess to thee among peoples, o Lord: and I will say a Psalm to thee among the Gentiles.
56:11 Because thy mercy is magnified even to the heavens, and thy truth even to the clouds.
56:12 Be exalted above the heavens o God: and thy glory upon all the earth.

text.NRSV:
57:1 Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful, for I have taken refuge in you; in the shadow of your wings will I take refuge until this time of trouble has passed.
57:2 I will call upon the Most High God, the God who maintains my cause.
57:3 He will send from heaven and save me; he will confound those who trample upon me; God will send forth his love and his faithfulness.
57:4 I lie in the midst of lions that devour the people; their teeth are spears and arrows, their tongue a sharp sword.
57:5 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let your glory be over all the earth.
57:6 They have laid a net for my feet, and I am bowed low; they have dug a pit before me, but have fallen into it themselves.
57:7 My heart is firmly fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and make melody.
57:8 Wake up, my spirit; awake, lute and harp; I myself will waken the dawn.
57:9 I will confess you among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing praise to you among the nations.
57:10 For your loving-kindness is greater than the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
57:11 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let your glory be over all the earth.

text.KJV:
57:1 Be mercifull vnto mee, O God, be merciful vnto me, for my soule trusteth in thee: yea in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, vntill these calamities bee ouerpast.
57:2 I will crie vnto God most high: vnto God that perfourmeth all things for mee.
57:3 Hee shall send from heauen, and saue me from the reproch of him, that would swallow me vp; Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his trueth.
57:4 My soule is among lyons, and I lie euen among them that are set on fire: euen the sonnes of men, whose teeth are speares and arrowes, and their tongue a sharpe sword.
57:5 Be thou exalted, O God, aboue the heauens: let thy glory be aboue all the earth.
57:6 They haue prepared a net for my steppes, my soule is bowed downe: they haue digged a pit before me, into the midst whereof they are fallen themselues. Selah.
57:7 My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing, and giue praise.
57:8 Awake vp my glory, awake psalterie and harpe; I my selfe will awake early.
57:9 I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people; I will sing vnto thee among the nations.
57:10 For thy mercy is great vnto the heauens, and thy trueth vnto the clouds.
57:11 Be thou exalted, O God, aboue the heauens: let thy glory be aboue all the earth.
text.JPS1985:
57:1 For the leader; al tashḥeth. Of David. A michtam; when he fled from Saul into a cave.
57:2 Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me, for I seek refuge in You, I seek refuge in the shadow of Your wings, until danger passes.
57:3 I call to God Most High, to God who is good to me.
57:4 He will reach down from heaven and deliver me: God will send down His steadfast love; my persecutor reviles. Selah.
57:5 As for me, I lie down among man-eating lions whose teeth are spears and arrows, whose tongue is a sharp sword.
57:6 Exalt Yourself over the heavens, O God, let Your glory be over all the earth!
57:7 They prepared a net for my feet to ensnare me; they dug a pit for me, but they fell into it. Selah.
57:8 My heart is firm, O God; my heart is firm; I will sing, I will chant a hymn.
57:9 Awake, O my soul! Awake, O harp and lyre! I will wake the dawn.
57:10 I will praise You among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing a hymn to You among the nations;
57:11 for Your faithfulness is as high as heaven; Your steadfastness reaches to the sky.
57:12 Exalt Yourself over the heavens, O God, let Your glory be over all the earth!
text.Grail1963:

for in you my soul has taken refuge.
