text.Rotherham:
57:1 Show me favour, O God, Show me favour, For, in thee, hath my soul sought refuge,—And, in the shadow of thy wings, will I seek refuge, Until the storm of ruin pass by.
57:2 I will cry unto God Most High, Unto GOD, who accomplisheth on my behalf:
57:3 He will send out of heaven, that he may save me, He who is panting for me, hath reproached. [Selah.] God will send out his lovingkindness and his faithfulness.
57:4 My soul, is in the midst of lions, I lie down amidst flames, As for the sons of men, Their teeth, are spear and arrows, And, their tongue, is a sharp sword.
57:5 Be exalted Above the Heavens, O God, Above all the earth, thy glory!
57:6 A net, did they fix for my steps, My soul was bowed down,—They digged before me a pit, They fell into the midst thereof! [Selah.]
57:7 Fixed is my heart, O God, Fixed is my heart, I will sing, and touch the strings.
57:8 Awake, mine honour, Awake, harp and lyre, I will awaken the dawn;
57:9 I will thank thee, among the peoples, O My Lord, I will praise thee in song, among the races of men.
57:10 For, great, unto the heavens, is thy lovingkindness, And, unto the skies, thy faithfulness.
57:11 Be exalted Above the heavens, O God, Above all the earth, thy glory!
text.NABRE:
57:1 Psalm 57 Confident Prayer for Deliverance - For the director. Do not destroy. A miktam of David, when he fled from Saul into a cave.
57:2 I - Have mercy on me, God, have mercy on me. In you I seek refuge. In the shadow of your wings I seek refuge till harm pass by.
57:3 I call to God Most High, to God who provides for me.
57:4 May God send help from heaven to save me, shame those who trample upon me. May God send fidelity and mercy. Selah
57:5 I must lie down in the midst of lions hungry for human prey. Their teeth are spears and arrows; their tongue, a sharpened sword.
57:6 Be exalted over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth.
57:7 II - They have set a trap for my feet; my soul is bowed down; They have dug a pit before me. May they fall into it themselves! Selah
57:8 My heart is steadfast, God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing and chant praise.
57:9 Awake, my soul; awake, lyre and harp! I will wake the dawn.
57:10 I will praise you among the peoples, Lord; I will chant your praise among the nations.
57:11 For your mercy towers to the heavens; your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
57:12 Exalt yourself over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth.
text.DRB:
57:1 Unto the end, destroy not, to David, in the inscription of the title.
57:2 If in very deed you speak justice: judge right things ye sons of men.
57:3 For in the heart you work iniquities: in the earth your hands forge injustice.
57:4 Sinners are alienated from the matrice, they have erred from the womb: they have spoken false things.
57:5 They have fury according to the similitude of a serpent: as of the asp that is deaf, and stoppeth his ears.
57:6 Which will not hear the voice of the enchanters, and of the sorcerer enchanting wisely.
57:7 God shall break their teeth in their mouth, the cheek-tooth of the lions, our Lord will break in pieces.
57:8 They shall come to nothing as water running down: he hath bent his bow till they be weakened.
57:9 As wax that melteth, shall they be taken away: fire hath fallen on them, and they have not seen the sun.
57:10 Before your thorns did understand the old briar: as living so in wrath he swalloweth them.
57:11 The just shall rejoice when he shall see revenge: he shall wash his hands in the blood of a sinner.
57:12 And man shall say: If certes there be fruit to the just: there is a God certes judging them on the earth.

text.NRSV:
58:1 Do you indeed speak justly in council? do you judge with equity, you mortals?
58:2 No, in your mind you devise wickedness, and your hands deal out violence in the land.
58:3 The wicked are estranged from the womb; they have gone astray from their birth and speak lies.
58:4 Their poison is like the poison of a serpent; they are like the deaf adder that stops its ears,
58:5 Which does not heed the voice of the charmer, no matter how skillful his charming.
58:6 O God, break their teeth in their mouths; pull the fangs of the young lions, O Lord.
58:7 Let them vanish like water that runs off; let them wither like trodden grass.
58:8 Let them be like the snail that melts away, like a dead-born child that never sees the sun.
58:9 Before they bear fruit, let them be cut down like a brier; let them be swept away like the thorns of a desert.
58:10 The righteous shall rejoice when they see the vengeance; they shall wash their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 And people shall say, 'Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who rules in the earth.'
text.Coverdale:
58:1 Do you indeed decree righteousness, you rulers? *
      do you judge the peoples with equity?
58:2 No; you devise evil in your hearts, *
      and your hands deal out violence in the land.
58:3 The wicked are perverse from the womb; *
      liars go astray from their birth.
58:4 They are as venomous as a serpent, *
      they are like the deaf adder which stops its ears,
58:5 Which does not heed the voice of the charmer, *
      no matter how skillful his charming.
58:6 O God, break their teeth in their mouths; *
      pull the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
58:7 Let them vanish like water that runs off; *
      let them wither like trodden grass.
58:8 Let them be like the snail that melts away, *
      like a stillborn child that never sees the sun.
58:9 Before they bear fruit, let them be cut down like a brier; *
      like thorns and thistles let them be swept away.
58:10 The righteous will be glad when they see the vengeance; *
      they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 And people will say,
      "Surely, there is a reward for the righteous; *
      surely, there is a God who rules in the earth."
text.KJV:
58:1 Doe yee indeed speake righteousnesse, O congregation? doe ye iudge vprightly, O ye sonnes of men?
58:2 Yea, in heart you worke wickednesse; you waigh the violence of your hands in the earth.
58:3 The wicked are estranged from the wombe, they goe astray as soone as they be borne, speaking lies.
58:4 Their poison is like the poyson of a serpent; they are like the deafe adder that stoppeth her eare:
58:5 Which will not hearken to the voyce of charmers, charming neuer so wisely.
58:6 Breake their teeth, O God, in their mouth: breake out the great teeth of the young lyons, O Lord.
58:7 Let them melt away as waters, which runne continually: When he bendeth his bow to shoote his arrowes, let them be as cut in pieces.
58:8 As a snaile which melteth, let euery one of them passe away: like the vntimely birth of a woman, that they may not see the sunne.
58:9 Before your pots can feele the thornes, he shall take them away as with a whirlewind, both liuing, and in his wrath.
58:10 The righteous shall reioyce when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his feete in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 So that a man shall say, Uerily there is a reward for the righteous: verily hee is a God that iudgeth in the earth.
text.JPS1985:
58:1 For the leader; al tashḥeth. Of David. A michtam.
58:2 O mighty ones, do you really decree what is just? Do you judge mankind with equity?
58:3 In your minds you devise wrongdoing in the land; with your hands you deal out lawlessness.
58:4 The wicked are defiant from birth; the liars go astray from the womb.
58:5 Their venom is like that of a snake, a deaf viper that stops its ears
58:6 so as not to hear the voice of charmers or the expert mutterer of spells.
58:7 O God, smash their teeth in their mouth; shatter the fangs of lions, O LORD;
58:8 let them melt, let them vanish like water; let Him aim His arrows that they be cut down;
58:9 like a snail that melts away as it moves; like a woman’s stillbirth, may they never see the sun!
58:10 Before the thorns grow into a bramble, may He whirl them away alive in fury.
58:11 The righteous man will rejoice when he sees revenge; he will bathe his feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:12 Men will say, “There is, then, a reward for the righteous; there is, indeed, divine justice on earth.”
text.Rotherham:
58:1 Are ye, indeed, silent [when] righteousness, ye should speak? When, with equity, ye should judge, O ye sons of men?
58:2 Aye! ye all do work, perversity,—Throughout the land, your hands, weigh out, violence!
58:3 Lawless men have been estranged from birth, They have gone astray from their nativity, speaking falsehood;
58:4 Their poison, is like unto the poison of a serpent, Like the deaf adder, that stoppeth his ear;
58:5 That will not hearken to the voice of whisperers, Though the wise one try to bind him with spells.
58:6 O God! break away their teeth in their mouth, The biters of the young lions, knock thou out, O Yahweh!
58:7 Let such men flow away like waters that disperse themselves: He prepareth his arrow, Like [grass] let them be cut down:
58:8 Like a snail, which melteth away as it goeth, An untimely birth of a woman, which hath not seen the sun:
58:9 Before your kettles can perceive the [kindled] bramble, Be he green or be he withered, he shall be swept away.
58:10 The righteous man will rejoice when he hath seen an avenging, His feet, will he bathe in the blood of the lawless one:—
58:11 So that a son of earth may say—Surely there is fruit for the righteous man! Surely there are gods who judge in the earth!
text.NABRE:
58:1 Psalm 58 The Dethroning of Unjust Rulers - For the leader. Do not destroy. A miktam of David.
58:2 I - Do you indeed pronounce justice, O gods; do you judge fairly you children of Adam?
58:3 No, you freely engage in crime; your hands dispense violence to the earth.
58:4 II - The wicked have been corrupt since birth; liars from the womb, they have gone astray.
58:5 Their venom is like the venom of a snake, like that of a serpent stopping its ears,
58:6 So as not to hear the voice of the charmer or the enchanter with cunning spells.
58:7 III - O God, smash the teeth in their mouths; break the fangs of these lions, LORD!
58:8 Make them vanish like water flowing away; trodden down, let them wither like grass.
58:9 Let them dissolve like a snail that oozes away, like an untimely birth that never sees the sun.
58:10 Suddenly, like brambles or thistles, have the whirlwind snatch them away.
58:11 Then the just shall rejoice to see the vengeance and bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:12 Then people will say: “Truly there is a reward for the just; there is a God who is judge on earth!”
text.DRB:
58:1 Unto the end, destroy not, to David in the inscription of the title, when Saul sent, and watched his house to kill him.
58:2 Deliver me from mine enemies, o my God: and from them that rise up against me defend me.
58:3 Deliver me from them that work iniquity: and from bloody men save me.
58:4 Because lo they have taken my soul: the strong have fallen violently upon me.
58:5 Neither is it mine iniquity, nor my sin, o Lord: without iniquity have I run, and gone directly.
58:6 Rise up to meet me, and see: and thou, o Lord, the God of powers, God of Israel, attend to visit all nations: have no mercy on all that work iniquity.
58:7 They will return at evening: and they shall suffer famine as dogs, and shall compass the city.
58:8 Behold they will speak in their mouth, and a sword in their lips: because who hath heard?
58:9 And thou, o Lord wilt scorn them: thou wilt bring to nought all the nations.
58:10 I will keep my strength to thee: because thou art my receiver:
58:11 my God, thy mercy shall prevent me.
58:12 God will shew unto me concerning mine enemies, kill them not lest sometime my peoples forget. Disperse them in thy strength: and depose them my protector, o Lord.
58:13 The sin of their mouth, the word of their lips: and let them be taken in their pride. And for cursing and lying they shall be talked of
58:14 in consummation: in wrath of consummation and they shall not be. And they shall know that God will rule over Jacob: and over the ends of the earth.
58:15 They shall be turned at evening, and shall suffer famine as dogs: & shall compass the city.
58:16 They shall be dispersed to eat: and if they be not filled, they will murmur also.
58:17 But I will sing thy strength: and will exalt thy mercy in the morning. Because thou art become my receiver, and my refuge in the day of my tribulation.
58:18 My helper, I will sing to thee, because thou art God my receiver: my God, my mercy.

text.NRSV:
59:1 Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; protect me from those who rise up against me.
59:2 Deliver me from those who work evil; from the bloodthirsty save me.
59:3 Even now they lie in wait for my life; the mighty stir up strife against me. For no transgression or sin of mine, O Lord,
59:4 for no fault of mine, they run and make ready. Rouse yourself, come to my help and see!
59:5 You, Lord God of hosts, are God of Israel. Awake to punish all the nations; spare none of those who treacherously plot evil. Selah
59:6 They come each evening howling like dogs and prowl about the city.
59:7 See, they spew with their mouths, with swords in their lips—for 'Who,' they think, 'will hear us?'
59:8 But you, O Lord, laugh at them; you hold all the nations in derision.
59:9 O my strength, I will watch for you; for you, O God, are my fortress.
59:10 My God in his steadfast love will meet me; my God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.
59:11 Do not kill them, lest my people forget; scatter them by your power, and bring them down, O Lord, our shield.
59:12 For the sin of their mouths, the words of their lips, let them be trapped in their pride. For the cursing and lies that they utter,
59:13 consume them in wrath; consume them until they are no more, so that they may know that God rules over Jacob to the ends of the earth. Selah
59:14 Each evening they come howling like dogs and prowl about the city.
59:15 They roam about for food, and growl if they do not get their fill.
59:16 But I will sing of your might; I will sing aloud of your steadfast love in the morning. For you have been a fortress for me and a refuge in the day of my distress.
59:17 O my strength, I will sing praises to you, for you, O God, are my fortress, the God who shows me steadfast love.

text.KJV:
59:1 Deliuer me from mine enemies, O my God: defend mee from them that rise vp against me.
59:2 Deliuer mee from the workers of iniquitie, and saue me from bloodie men.
59:3 For loe, they lye in waite for my soule; the mighty are gathered against me, not for my transgression, nor for my sinne, O Lord.
59:4 They runne and prepare themselues without my fault: awake to helpe me, and behold.
59:5 Thou therefore, O Lord God of hostes, the God of Israel, awake to visite all the heathen: be not mercifull to any wicked transgressours. Selah.
59:6 They returne at euening: they make a noise like a dogge, and go round about the citie.
59:7 Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords are in their lippes; for who, say they, doeth heare?
59:8 But thou, O Lord, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt haue all the heathen in derision.
59:9 Because of his strength will I wait vpon thee: for God is my defence.
59:10 The God of my mercy shall preuent me; God shall let mee see my desire vpon mine enemies.
59:11 Slay them not, lest my people forget: scatter them by thy power; and bring them downe, O Lord our shield.
59:12 For the sinne of their mouth, and the words of their lips, let them euen be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying which they speake.
59:13 Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Iacob, vnto the ends of the earth. Selah.
59:14 And at euening let them returne, and let them make a noise like a dogge, and goe round about the citie.
59:15 Let them wander vp and downe for meate, and grudge if they be not satisfied.
59:16 But I will sing of thy power; yea I will sing alowd of thy mercy in the morning: for thou hast bene my defence and refuge, in the day of my trouble.
59:17 Vnto thee, O my strength, wil I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
text.JPS1985:
59:1 For the leader; al tashḥeth. Of David. A michtam; when Saul sent men to watch his house in order to put him to death.
59:2 Save me from my enemies, O my God; secure me against my assailants.
59:3 Save me from evildoers; deliver me from murderers.
59:4 For see, they lie in wait for me; fierce men plot against me for no offense of mine, for no transgression, O LORD;
59:5 for no guilt of mine do they rush to array themselves against me. Look, rouse Yourself on my behalf!
59:6 You, O LORD God of hosts, God of Israel, bestir Yourself to bring all nations to account; have no mercy on any treacherous villain. Selah.
59:7 They come each evening growling like dogs, roaming the city.
59:8 They rave with their mouths, sharp words are on their lips; [they think, ] “Who hears? ”
59:9 But You, O LORD, laugh at them; You mock all the nations.
59:10 O my strength, I wait for You; for God is my haven.
59:11 My faithful God will come to aid me; God will let me gloat over my watchful foes.
59:12 Do not kill them lest my people be unmindful; with Your power make wanderers of them; bring them low, O our shield, the Lord,
59:13 because of their sinful mouths, the words on their lips. Let them be trapped by their pride, and by the imprecations and lies they utter.
59:14 In Your fury put an end to them; put an end to them that they be no more; that it may be known to the ends of the earth that God does rule over Jacob. Selah.
59:15 They come each evening growling like dogs, roaming the city.
59:16 They wander in search of food; and whine if they are not satisfied.
59:17 But I will sing of Your strength, extol each morning Your faithfulness; for You have been my haven, a refuge in time of trouble.
59:18 O my strength, to You I sing hymns; for God is my haven, my faithful God.

text.Rotherham:
59:1 Rescue me from my foes, O my God, From them who lift themselves up against me, wilt thou set me on high:
59:2 Rescue me, from the workers of iniquity, And, from the men of bloodshed, save me.
59:3 For lo! they have lain in wait for my life, Mighty ones stir up strife against me, Without transgression of mine, and without sin of mine, O Yahweh;
59:4 For no iniquity, do they run that they may take their stand, Rouse thyself to meet me, and see.
59:5 Thou, therefore, O Yahweh, God of hosts, God of Israel, Awake, to visit all the nations, Do not show favour to any iniquitous traitors. [Selah.]
59:6 They return at evening, They grow like a dog, And go round the city.
59:7 Lo! they belch forth with their mouth, Swords, are in their lips, For [say they], Who doth hear?
59:8 Thou, therefore, O Yahweh, wilt laugh at them, Thou wilt mock at all nations.
59:9 O my Strength, unto thee, will I make melody, For, God, is my high tower.
59:10 My God of lovingkindness, will come to meet me, God, will let me look on mine adversaries.
59:11 Do not slay them, lest my people forget, Cause them to wander by thy strength, and prostrate them, [Thou] our shield, O Lord!
59:12 The sin of their mouth, The word of their lips, Let them then be captured in their pride, Both for the oath and for the deception they record.
59:13 Bring to a full end in wrath, Bring to a full end, that they be no more,—That men may know that, God, is ruling in Jacob, Unto the ends of the earth. [Selah.]
59:14 Let them return, then, at evening, Let them growl like a dog, And go round the city.
59:15 They, may prowl about for food,—And, if they are not satisfied, then let them whine!
59:16 But, I, will sing thy power, And will shout aloud, in the morning, thy lovingkindness,—For thou has become a refuge for me, And a place to flee to in the day of my distress.
59:17 O my Strength, unto thee, will I make melody, For, God, is my high tower, my God of lovingkindness.
text.NABRE:
59:1 Psalm 59 Complaint Against Bloodthirsty Enemies - For the director. Do not destroy. A miktam of David, when Saul sent people to watch his house and kill him.
59:2 I - Rescue me from my enemies, my God; lift me out of reach of my foes.
59:3 Deliver me from evildoers; from the bloodthirsty save me.
59:4 They have set an ambush for my life; the powerful conspire against me. For no offense or misdeed of mine, LORD,
59:5 for no fault they hurry to take up arms. Come near and see my plight!
59:6 You, LORD God of hosts, are the God of Israel! Awake! Punish all the nations. Have no mercy on these worthless traitors. Selah
59:7 Each evening they return, growling like dogs, prowling the city.
59:8 Their mouths pour out insult; sharp words are on their lips. They say: “Who is there to hear?”
59:9 But you, LORD, laugh at them; you deride all the nations.
59:10 My strength, for you I watch; you, God, are my fortress,
59:11 my loving God. II - May God go before me, and show me my fallen foes.
59:12 Slay them, God, lest they deceive my people. Shake them by your power; Lord, our shield, bring them down.
59:13 For the sinful words of their mouths and lips let them be caught in their pride. For the lies they have told under oath
59:14 destroy them in anger, destroy till they are no more. Then people will know God rules over Jacob, yes, even to the ends of the earth. Selah
59:15 Each evening they return, growling like dogs, prowling the city.
59:16 They roam about as scavengers; if they are not filled, they howl.
59:17 III - But I shall sing of your strength, extol your mercy at dawn, For you are my fortress, my refuge in time of trouble.
59:18 My strength, your praise I will sing; you, God, are my fortress, my loving God.
text.DRB:
59:1 Unto the end, for them that shall be changed, in the inscription of the title to David himself, for doctrine,
59:2 when he set fire on Mesopotamia of Syria, and in Sobal, and Joab returned, and struck Idumæa in the valley of salt-pits twelve thousand.
59:3 O God thou hast repelled us, & hast destroyed us: thou wast angry and hast had mercy on us.
59:4 Thou hast moved the earth, and hast troubled it: heal the breaches thereof, because it is moved.
59:5 Thou hast shewed unto thy people hard things: thou hast made us drink the wine of compunction.
59:6 Thou hast given a signification to them that fear thee: that they fly from the face of the bow.
59:7 That thy beloved may be delivered, save me with thy right hand, and hear me.
59:8 God hath spoken in his holy: I shall rejoice, and shall divide Sichem: and shall measure the valley of tabernacles.
59:9 Galaad is mine, and Manasses is mine: and Ephraim the strength of my head.
59:10 Juda my king: Moab the pot of my hope. Into Edom I will stretch out my shoe: the foreigners are subject to me.
59:11 Who shall conduct me into a fenced city? Who shall conduct me even into Idumea?
59:12 Shall not thou, o God, that hast expelled us: and wilt not thou o God, go forth in our hosts?
59:13 Give us aid from tribulation: because man's salvation is vain.
59:14 In God we shall do strength: and he shall bring to nothing them that afflict us.

text.NRSV:
60:1 O God, you have rejected us, broken our defenses; you have been angry; now restore us!
60:2 You have caused the land to quake; you have torn it open; repair the cracks in it, for it is tottering.
60:3 You have made your people suffer hard things; you have given us wine to drink that makes us reel.
60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, to rally to it from the bow. Selah
60:5 Give victory with your right hand, and answer us, so that those whom you love may be rescued.
60:6 God has spoken in his sanctuary: 'With exultation I will divide up Shechem, and portion out the Vale of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet; Judah is my scepter.
60:8 Moab is my washbasin; on Edom I hurl my shoe; over Philistia I shout in triumph.'
60:9 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
60:10 Have you not rejected us, O God? You do not go out, O God, with our armies.
60:11 O grant us help against the foe, for human help is worthless.
60:12 With God we shall do valiantly; it is he who will tread down our foes.
text.Coverdale:
60:1 O God, you have cast us off and broken us; *
      you have been angry;
      oh, restore us to yourself again.
60:2 You have shaken the earth and split it open; *
      repair the cracks in it, for it totters.
60:3 You have made your people know hardship; *
      you have given us wine that makes us stagger.
60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, *
      to be a refuge from the power of the bow.
60:5 Save us by your right hand and answer us, *
      that those whom you love may be delivered.
60:6 God spoke from his holy place and said: *
      "I will exult and parcel out Shechem;
      I will divide the valley of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; *
      Ephraim is my helmet and Judah my scepter.
60:8 Moab is my wash-basin,
      on Edom I throw down my sandal to claim it, *
      and over Philistia will I shout in triumph."
60:9 Who will lead me into the strong city? *
      who will bring me into Edom?
60:10 Have you not cast us off, O God? *
      you no longer go out, O God, with our armies.
60:11 Grant us your help against the enemy, *
      for vain is the help of man.
60:12 With God we will do valiant deeds, *
      and he shall tread our enemies under foot.
text.KJV:
60:1 O God, thou hast cast vs off; thou hast scattered vs, thou hast bene displeased, O turne thy selfe to vs againe.
60:2 Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it: heale the breaches thereof, for it shaketh.
60:3 Thou hast shewed thy people hard things: thou hast made vs to drinke the wine of astonishment.
60:4 Thou hast giuen a banner to them that feare thee: that it may be displayed because of the trueth. Selah.
60:5 That thy beloued may be deliuered; saue with thy right hand, and heare mee.
60:6 God hath spoken in his holinesse, I wil reioyce: I will diuide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim also is the strength of mine head; Iudah is my Lawgiuer.
60:8 Moab is my wash-pot, ouer Edom wil I cast out my shooe: Philistia, triumph thou because of me.
60:9 Who wil bring me into the strong citie? who will lead me into Edom?
60:10 Wilt not thou, O God, which hadst cast vs off? and thou, O God, which didst not goe out with our armies.
60:11 Giue vs helpe from trouble: for vaine is the helpe of man.
60:12 Through God wee shall doe valiantly: for he it is that shall tread downe our enemies.
text.JPS1985:
60:1 For the leader; on shushan eduth. A michtam of David (to be taught),
60:2 when he fought with Aram-Naharaim and Aram-Zobah, and Joab returned and defeated Edom—[an army] of twelve thousand men—in the Valley of Salt.
60:3 O God, You have rejected us, You have made a breach in us; You have been angry; restore us!
60:4 You have made the land quake; You have torn it open. Mend its fissures, for it is collapsing.
60:5 You have made Your people suffer hardship; You have given us wine that makes us reel.
60:6 Give those who fear You because of Your truth a banner for rallying. Selah.
60:7 That those whom You love might be rescued, deliver with Your right hand and answer me.
60:8 God promised in His sanctuary that I would exultingly divide up Shechem, and measure the Valley of Sukkoth;
60:9 Gilead and Manasseh would be mine, Ephraim my chief stronghold, Judah my scepter;
60:10 Moab would be my washbasin; on Edom I would cast my shoe; acclaim me, O Philistia!
60:11 Would that I were brought to the bastion! Would that I were led to Edom!
60:12 But You have rejected us, O God; God, You do not march with our armies.
60:13 Grant us Your aid against the foe, for the help of man is worthless.
60:14 With God we shall triumph; He will trample our foes.
text.Grail1963:
.3 O God, you have rejected us and broken us.
You have been angry; come back to us.
.4 You have made the earth quake, torn it open.
Repair what is shattered for it sways.
.5 You have inflicted hardships on your people
and made us drink a wine that dazed us.
.6 You have given those who fear you a signal
to flee from the enemy's bow.
.7 O come, and deliver your friends,
help with your right hand and reply.
* * *
.8 From his holy place God has made this promise;
"I will triumph and divide the land of Shechem,
I will measure out the valley of Succoth.
.9 Gilead is mine and Manasseh,
Ephraim I take for my helmet,
Judah for my commander's staff.
.10 Moab I will use for my washbowl;
on Edom I will plant my shoe.
Over the Philistines I will shout in triumph."
.11 But who will lead me to conquer the fortress?
Who will bring me face to face with Edom?
.12 Will you utterly reject us, O God,
and no longer march with our armies?
.13 Give us help against the foe:
for the help of man is vain.
.14 With God we shall do bravely
and he will trample down our foes.
text.Rotherham:
60:1 O God, thou hast rejected us—hast scattered us, Thou hast been angry, Wilt thou restore us?
60:2 Thou hast shaken the land—hast rent it, Heal thou the fractures thereof—for it hath tottered:
60:3 Thou hast suffered thy people to see hardship, Thou hast let them drink the wine of confusion.
60:4 Thou hadst given—to them that revere thee—a banner, to float aloft, Because of [thy] faithfulness. [Selah.]
60:5 That thy beloved ones may be delivered, Save thou with thine own right hand—and answer us.
60:6 God, hath spoken in his holiness—I will exult! I will apportion Shechem, And, the Vale of Succoth, will I measure out;
60:7 Mine, is Gilead—and mine, Manasseh, But, Ephraim, is the defence, of my head, Judah, is my commander’s staff;
60:8 Moab, is my wash-bowl, Upon Edom, will I throw my shoe, Over Philistia! raise shout of triumph.
60:9 Who will conduct me to a fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom!
60:10 Is it not, thou, O God?—thou hast rejected us! And wilt thou not go forth, O God, with our hosts?
60:11 Grant us help out of distress, For, vain, is the deliverance of man:
60:12 In God, we shall do valiantly, He himself, therefore, will tread down our adversaries.
text.NABRE:
60:1 Psalm 60 Lament After Defeat in Battle - For the leader; according to “The Lily of.…” A miktam of David (for teaching),
60:2 when he fought against Aram-Naharaim and Aram-Zobah; and Joab, coming back, killed twelve thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
60:3 I - O God, you rejected us, broke our defenses; you were angry but now revive us.
60:4 You rocked the earth, split it open; repair the cracks for it totters.
60:5 You made your people go through hardship, made us stagger from the wine you gave us.
60:6 Raise up a banner for those who revere you, a refuge for them out of bow shot. Selah
60:7 Help with your right hand and answer us that your loved ones may escape.
60:8 II - In the sanctuary God promised: “I will exult, will apportion Shechem; the valley of Succoth I will measure out.
60:9 Gilead is mine, mine is Manasseh; Ephraim is the helmet for my head, Judah, my own scepter.
60:10 Moab is my washbowl; upon Edom I cast my sandal. I will triumph over Philistia.”
60:11 III - Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me into Edom?
60:12 Was it not you who rejected us, God? Do you no longer march with our armies?
60:13 Give us aid against the foe; worthless is human help.
60:14 We will triumph with the help of God, who will trample down our foes.
text.DRB:
60:1 Unto the end, in hymns to David.
60:2 Hear, o God, my petition: attend to my prayer.
60:3 From the ends of the earth I have cried to thee: whilst my heart was in anguish, thou didst exalt me on a rock.
60:4 Thou hast conducted me, because thou art made my hope: a tower of strength from the face of the enemy.
60:5 Ishall inhabit in thy tabernacle for ever: I shall be protected in the covert of thy wings.
60:6 Because thou my God hast heard my prayer: thou hast given inheritance to those that fear thy name.
60:7 Thou wilt add days upon the days of the king: his years even unto the day of generation and generation.
60:8 He is permanent for ever in the sight of God: his mercy and truth who shall require?
60:9 So will I say a Psalm to thy name for ever and ever: that I may render my vows from day to day.

text.NRSV:
61:1 Hear my cry, O God; listen to my prayer.
61:2 From the end of the earth I call to you, when my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I;
61:3 for you are my refuge, a strong tower against the enemy.
61:4 Let me abide in your tent forever, find refuge under the shelter of your wings. Selah
61:5 For you, O God, have heard my vows; you have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
61:6 Prolong the life of the king; may his years endure to all generations!
61:7 May he be enthroned in the presence of God forever; appoint steadfast love and faithfulness to watch over him!
61:8 So I will always sing praises to your name, as I pay my vows day after day.
text.Coverdale:

      and listen to my prayer.
61:2 I call upon you from the ends of the earth
      with heaviness in my heart; *
      set me upon the rock that is higher than I.
61:3 For you have been my refuge, *
      a strong tower against the enemy.
61:4 I will dwell in your house for ever; *
      I will take refuge under the cover of your wings.
61:5 For you, O God, have heard my vows; *
      you have granted me the heritage of those who fear your Name.
61:6 Add length of days to the king's life; *

61:7 Let him sit enthroned before God for ever; *

61:8 So will I always sing the praise of your Name, *

text.KJV:
61:1 Heare my cry, O God, attend vnto my prayer.
61:2 From the end of the earth wil I cry vnto thee, when my heart is ouerwhelmed: leade me to the rocke, that is higher then I.
61:3 For thou hast bene a shelter for me, and a strong tower from the enemy.
61:4 I will abide in thy Tabernacle for euer: I will trust in the couert of thy wings. Selah.
61:5 For thou, O God, hast heard my vowes: thou hast giuen me the heritage of those that feare thy name.
61:6 Thou wilt prolong the kings life: and his yeeres as many generations.
61:7 He shall abide before God for euer: O prepare mercy and trueth which may preserue him.
61:8 So will I sing praise vnto thy name for euer, that I may daily performe my vowes.
text.JPS1985:
61:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. Of David.
61:2 Hear my cry, O God, heed my prayer.
61:3 From the end of the earth I call to You; when my heart is faint, You lead me to a rock that is high above me.
61:4 For You have been my refuge, a tower of strength against the enemy.
61:5 O that I might dwell in Your tent forever, take refuge under Your protecting wings. Selah.
61:6 O God, You have heard my vows; grant the request of those who fear Your name.
61:7 Add days to the days of the king; may his years extend through generations;
61:8 may he dwell in God’s presence forever; appoint steadfast love to guard him.
61:9 So I will sing hymns to Your name forever, as I fulfill my vows day after day.
text.Grail1963:
.2 O God, hear my cry!

Listen to my prayer!
.3 From the end of the earth I call;
my heart is faint.
On a rock too high for me to reach
set me on high,
.4 O you who have been my refuge,
my tower against the foe.
.5 Let me dwell in your tent for ever
and hide in the shelter of your wings.
.6 For you, O God, hear my prayer,
grant me the heritage of those who fear you.
.7 May you lengthen the life of the king;
may his years cover many generations.
.8 May he ever sit enthroned before God:
bid love and truth be his protection.
.9 So I will always praise your name
and day after day fulfill my vows.
text.Rotherham:
61:1 Hear, O God, my loud cry, Attend unto my prayer:
61:2 From the end of the earth, unto thee do I cry, When my heart fainteth away, Unto a rock that is higher than I, wilt thou lead me.
61:3 For thou hast been, A Refuge to me, A Tower of Strength, from the face of the foe.
61:4 I would be a guest in thy tent to the ages, I would seek refuge in the concealment of thy wings. [Selah.]
61:5 For, thou, O God, hast hearkened to my vows, Thou hast granted a possession, unto them who revere thy Name.
61:6 Days—unto the days of the king, wilt thou add. His years, as of generation after generation:
61:7 Let him retain his seat age-abidingly before God, Appoint that, lovingkindness and faithfulness, my watch over him!
61:8 So, will I sing thy Name unto futurity, Paying my vows, day by day.
text.NABRE:
61:1 Psalm 61 Prayer of the King in Time of Danger - For the leader; with stringed instruments. Of David.
61:2 I - Hear my cry, O God, listen to my prayer!
61:3 From the ends of the earth I call; my heart grows faint. Raise me up, set me on a rock,
61:4 for you are my refuge, a tower of strength against the foe.
61:5 Let me dwell in your tent forever, take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah
61:6 II - For you, O God, have heard my vows, you have granted me the heritage of those who revere your name.
61:7 Add days to the life of the king; may his years be as from generation to generation;
61:8 May he reign before God forever; send your love and fidelity to preserve him—
61:9 I will duly sing to your name forever, fulfill my vows day after day.
text.DRB:
61:1 Unto the end, for Idithun a Psalm of David.
61:2 Shall not my soul be subject to God? For of him is my salvation.
61:3 For he is my God, and my saviour: my receiver, I shall be moved no more.
61:4 How long set you violently upon a man: you all do kill, as it were upon a wall that is leaning, and a wall shaken.
61:5 But yet they thought to repel my price. I ran in thirst: they blessed with their mouth, and cursed with their heart.
61:6 But yet my soul be thou subject to God: because my patience is from him.
61:7 Because he is my God, and my saviour: my helper I shall not remove.
61:8 In God is my salvation, and my glory: the God of my help, and my hope is in God.
61:9 Hope in him all ye the congregation of people: pour out your hearts before him: God is our helper for ever.
61:10 But yet the children of men are vain, the children of men are liars in balances: that they may deceive by vanity together.
61:11 Hope not in iniquity, and covet not robberies: if riches abound set not your heart upon them.
61:12 Once hath God spoken, these two things have I heard:
61:13 That power is God's, and mercy, o Lord, is to thee: because thou wilt render to every one according to his works.

text.NRSV:
62:1 For God alone my soul in silence waits; from him comes my salvation.
62:2 He alone is my rock and my salvation, my stronghold, so that I shall not be greatly shaken.
62:3 How long will you assail me to crush me, all of you together, as if you were a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
62:4 Their only plan is to bring me down from my height; they delight in lies; they bless with their mouth, but inwardly they curse.
62:5 For God alone my soul in silence waits; truly, my hope is in him.
62:6 He alone is my rock and my salvation, my stronghold, so that I shall not be shaken.
62:7 In God is my safety and my honor; God is my strong rock and my refuge.
62:8 Put your trust in him always, O people, pour out your hearts before him, for God is our refuge.
62:9 Those of high degree are but a fleeting breath, even those of low estate cannot be trusted.
62:10 On the scales they are lighter than a puff of wind, all of them together.
62:11 Put no trust in extortion; in robbery take no empty pride; though wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.
62:12 God has spoken once, twice have I heard it, that power belongs to God.
62:13 Steadfast love is yours, O Lord, for you repay everyone according to his deeds.
text.Coverdale:
62:1 For God alone my soul in silence waits; *
      from him comes my salvation.
62:2 He alone is my rock and my salvation, *
      my stronghold, so that I shall not be greatly shaken.
62:3 How long will you assail me to crush me,
      all of you together, *
      as if you were a leaning fence, a toppling wall?
62:4 They seek only to bring me down from my place of honor; *
      lies are their chief delight.
62:5 They bless with their lips, *
      but in their hearts they curse.
62:6 For God alone my soul in silence waits; *
      truly, my hope is in him.
62:7 He alone is my rock and my salvation, *
      my stronghold, so that I shall not be shaken.
62:8 In God is my safety and my honor; *
      God is my strong rock and my refuge.
62:9 Put your trust in him always, O people, *
      pour out your hearts before him, for God is our refuge.
62:10 Those of high degree are but a fleeting breath, *
      even those of low estate cannot be trusted.
62:11 On the scales they are lighter than a breath, *
      all of them together.
62:12 Put no trust in extortion;
      in robbery take no empty pride; *
      though wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.
62:13 God has spoken once, twice have I heard it, *
      that power belongs to God.
62:14 Steadfast love is yours, O Lord, *
      for you repay everyone according to his deeds.
text.KJV:
62:1 Truely my soule waiteth vpon God: from him commeth my saluation.
62:2 He onely is my rocke and my saluation: he is my defence, I shall not be greatly moued.
62:3 How long wil ye imagine mischiefe against a man? ye shall be slaine all of you: as a bowing wall shall ye be, and as a tottering fence.
62:4 They onely consult to cast him downe from his excellency, they delight in lies: they blesse with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.
62:5 My soule, wait thou onely vpon God: for my expectation is from him.
62:6 He onely is my rocke and my saluation; he is my defence; I shall not bee moued.
62:7 In God is my saluation, and my glorie: the rocke of my strength, and my refuge is in God.
62:8 Trust in him at all times; ye people, powre out your heart before him: God is a refuge for vs. Selah.
62:9 Surely men of low degree are vanitie, and men of high degree are a lie: to be laid in the ballance, they are altogether lighter then vanitie.
62:10 Trust not in oppression, become not vaine in robberie: if riches increase, set not your heart vpon them.
62:11 God hath spoken once; twice haue I heard this, that power belongeth vnto God.
62:12 Also vnto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercie: for thou renderest to euery man according to his worke.
text.JPS1985:
62:1 For the leader; on Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
62:2 Truly my soul waits quietly for God; my deliverance comes from Him.
62:3 Truly He is my rock and deliverance, my haven; I shall never be shaken.
62:4 How long will all of you attack a man, to crush him, as though he were a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
62:5 They lay plans to topple him from his rank; they delight in falsehood; they bless with their mouths, while inwardly they curse. Selah.
62:6 Truly, wait quietly for God, O my soul, for my hope comes from Him.
62:7 He is my rock and deliverance, my haven; I shall not be shaken.
62:8 I rely on God, my deliverance and glory, my rock of strength; in God is my refuge.
62:9 Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before Him; God is our refuge. Selah.
62:10 Men are mere breath; mortals, illusion; placed on a scale all together, they weigh even less than a breath.
62:11 Do not trust in violence, or put false hopes in robbery; if force bears fruit pay it no mind.
62:12 One thing God has spoken; two things have I heard: that might belongs to God,
62:13 and faithfulness is Yours, O Lord, to reward each man according to his deeds.
text.Grail1963:
.2 In God alone is my soul at rest;
my help comes from him.
.3 He alone is my rock, my stronghold,
my fortress; I stand firm.
.4 How long will you all attack one man
to break him down,
as though he were a tottering wall,
or a tumbling fence?
.5 Their plan is only to destroy;

With their mouth they utter blessing
but in their heart they curse.
.6 In God alone be at rest, my soul;

my fortress; I stand firm.

the rock of my strength.
/.9 Take refuge in God, all you people.
Trust him at all times.
Pour out your hearts before him
for God is our refuge.
.10 Common folk are only a breath,
great men an illusion.
Placed in the scales, they rise;
they weigh less than a breath.

.11 Do not put your trust in oppression
nor vain hopes on plunder.
Do not set your heart on riches

text.Rotherham:
62:1 Surely, towards God, silence [becometh] my soul, From him, is my salvation:
62:2 Surely, he, is my rock and my salvation, My high tower—I shall not be greatly shaken!
62:3 How long will ye shout at a man? Ye shall be crushed all of you,—Like a wall that bulgeth,—a fence pushed in!
62:4 Surely, from his elevation, they have taken counsel to thrust him down, They accept falsehood,—With his mouth, they each of them bless, But, inwardly, they revile. [Selah.]
62:5 Surely, towards God, be thou silent, my soul, For, from him, is mine expectation:
62:6 Surely, he, is my rock, and my salvation, My high tower—I shall not be shaken!
62:7 Upon God, [depend] my salvation and mine honour, My rock of strength, my refuge, are in God.
62:8 Trust ye in him all ye assembly of the people, Pour out, before him, your heart, God, is a refuge for us. [Selah.]
62:9 Surely, vanity, are men of low degree, Deception, men of high degree,—In the balances, they go up, They, are [made] of vanity, altogether.
62:10 Do not trust in extortion, Nor, with robbery, become vain, As for wealth, when it beareth fruit, Do not set [thereon your] heart.
62:11 One thing, hath God spoken, Two things, [there are] which I have heard, That, power, belongeth unto God;
62:12 And, thine, O My Lord, is lovingkindness,—For, thou, wilt pay back unto every man—according to his deed.
text.NABRE:
62:1 Psalm 62 Trust in God Alone - For the leader; ‘al Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
62:2 I - My soul rests in God alone, from whom comes my salvation.
62:3 God alone is my rock and salvation, my fortress; I shall never fall.
62:4 How long will you set yourself against a man? You shall all be destroyed, Like a sagging wall or a tumbled down fence!
62:5 Even highly placed people plot to overthrow him. They delight in lies; they bless with their mouths, but inwardly they curse. Selah
62:6 II - My soul, be at rest in God alone, from whom comes my hope.
62:7 God alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not fall.
62:8 My deliverance and honor are with God, my strong rock; my refuge is with God.
62:9 Trust God at all times, my people! Pour out your hearts to God our refuge! Selah
62:10 III - Mortals are a mere breath, the sons of man but an illusion; On a balance they rise; together they weigh nothing.
62:11 Do not trust in extortion; in plunder put no empty hope. On wealth that increases, do not set your heart.
62:12 One thing God has said; two things I have heard: Strength belongs to God;
62:13 so too, my Lord, does mercy, For you repay each man according to his deeds.
text.DRB:
62:1 A Psalm of David when he was in the desert of Juda.
62:2 O God my God to thee I watch, from the morning light. My soul hath thirsted to thee, my flesh to thee very many ways.
62:3 In a desert land, and inaccessible, and without water, so in the holy have I appeared to thee, that I might see thy strength, and thy glory.
62:4 Because thy mercy is better than lives, my lips shall praise thee.
62:5 So will I bless thee in my life: and in thy name I will lift up my hands.
62:6 As with marrow and fatness let my soul be filled: and my mouth shall praise with lips of exultation.
62:7 If I have been mindful of thee upon my bed, in the morning I will meditate on thee:
62:8 because thou hast been my helper.
62:9 And in the covert of thy wings I will rejoice, my soul hath cleaved after thee: thy right hand hath received me.
62:10 But they in vain have sought my soul, they shall enter into the inferior parts of the earth.
62:11 They shall be delivered into the hands of the sword, they shall be the portions of foxes.
62:12 But the king shall rejoice in God, all shall be praised that swear by him: because the mouth is stopped of those that speak wicked things.

text.NRSV:
63:1 O God, you are my God; eagerly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you, my flesh faints for you, as in a barren and dry land where there is no water.
63:2 Therefore I have gazed upon you in your holy place, that I might behold your power and your glory.
63:3 For your loving-kindness is better than life itself; my lips shall give you praise.
63:4 So will I bless you as long as I live and lift up my hands in your Name.
63:5 My soul is content, as with marrow and fatness, and my mouth praises you with joyful lips,
63:6 When I remember you upon my bed and meditate on you in the night watches.
63:7 For you have been my helper, and under the shadow of your wings I will rejoice.
63:8 My soul clings to you; your right hand holds me fast.
63:9 May those who seek my life to destroy it go down into the depths of the earth;
63:10 Let them fall by the edge of the sword, and let them be food for jackals.
63:11 But the king shall rejoice in God; all those who swear by him shall be glad, for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be stopped.

text.KJV:
63:1 O God, thou art my God, earely will I seeke thee: my soule thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee, in a drie and thirstie lande, where no water is:
63:2 To see thy power and thy glory, so as I haue seen thee in the Sanctuary.
63:3 Because thy louing kindnes is better then life: my lips shal praise thee.
63:4 Thus will I blesse thee, while I liue: I will lift vp my handes in thy Name.
63:5 My soule shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatnesse: and my mouth shall praise thee with ioyfull lips:
63:6 When I remember thee vpon my bed, and meditate on thee in the night watches.
63:7 Because thou hast bene my helpe; therefore in the shadow of thy wings will I reioyce.
63:8 My soule followeth hard after thee: thy right hand vpholdeth me.
63:9 But those that seeke my soule to destroy it, shall goe into the lower parts of the earth.
63:10 They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.
63:11 But the King shal reioyce in God; euery one that sweareth by him shall glorie: but the mouth of them that speake lies, shall be stopped.
text.JPS1985:
63:1 A psalm of David, when he was in the Wilderness of Judah.
63:2 God, You are my God; I search for You, my soul thirsts for You, my body yearns for You, as a parched and thirsty land that has no water.
63:3 I shall behold You in the sanctuary, and see Your might and glory,
63:4 Truly Your faithfulness is better than life; my lips declare Your praise.
63:5 I bless You all my life; I lift up my hands, invoking Your name.
63:6 I am sated as with a rich feast, I sing praises with joyful lips
63:7 when I call You to mind upon my bed, when I think of You in the watches of the night;
63:8 for You are my help, and in the shadow of Your wings I shout for joy.
63:9 My soul is attached to You; Your right hand supports me.
63:10 May those who seek to destroy my life enter the depths of the earth.
63:11 May they be gutted by the sword; may they be prey to jackals.
63:12 But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by Him shall exult, when the mouth of liars is stopped.
text.Grail1963:
.2 O God, you are my God, for you I long;
for you my soul is thirsting.
My body pines for you
like a dry, weary land without water.
.3 So I gaze on you in the sanctuary
to see your strength and your glory.
.4 For your love is better than life,
my lips will speak your praise.
.5 So I will bless you all my life,
in your name I will lift up my hands.
.6 My soul shall be filled as with a banquet,
my mouth shall praise you with joy.
.7 On my bed I remember you.
On you I muse through the night
.8 for your have been my help;
in the shadow of your wings I rejoice.
.9 My soul clings to you;
your right hand holds me fast.
.10 Those who seek to destroy my life
shall go down to the depths of the earth.
.11 They shall be put into the power of the sword
and left as the prey of the jackals.
.12 But the king shall rejoice in God;
(all that swear by him shall be blessed,)

text.Rotherham:
63:1 O God, my GOD, thou art, Earnestly do I desire thee,—My soul thirsteth for thee, My flesh fainteth for thee, In a land—dry, and weary for want of water,—
63:2 In like manner as, in the sanctuary, I have had vision of thee, To behold thy power and thy glory.
63:3 Because better is thy lovingkindness than life, My lips, aloud shall praise thee!
63:4 Thus, will I bless thee while I live, In thy Name, will I lift up mine outspread hands:
63:5 As with fatness and richness, shall my soul be satisfied, And, with joyfully shouting lips, shall my mouth utter praise.
63:6 Should I call thee to mind upon my couch, In the watches of the night, would I breathe soft speech of thee.
63:7 For thou hast become a help unto me,—And, in the shadow of thy wings, will I shout for joy.
63:8 My soul hath run clinging to thee, On me, hath thy right hand laid hold.
63:9 When, they, to crush it, would seek my life, They shall go into the lower parts of the earth:
63:10 Every one shall be given up into the power of the sword, The portion of jackals, shall they become.
63:11 But, the king, shall rejoice in God,—Every one shall glory who sweareth by him, For the mouth of them who speak falsehood, shall be stopped.
text.NABRE:
63:1 Psalm 63 Ardent Longing for God - A psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah.
63:2 I - O God, you are my God— it is you I seek! For you my body yearns; for you my soul thirsts, In a land parched, lifeless, and without water.
63:3 I look to you in the sanctuary to see your power and glory.
63:4 For your love is better than life; my lips shall ever praise you!
63:5 II - I will bless you as long as I live; I will lift up my hands, calling on your name.
63:6 My soul shall be sated as with choice food, with joyous lips my mouth shall praise you!
63:7 I think of you upon my bed, I remember you through the watches of the night
63:8 You indeed are my savior, and in the shadow of your wings I shout for joy.
63:9 My soul clings fast to you; your right hand upholds me.
63:10 III - But those who seek my life will come to ruin; they shall go down to the depths of the netherworld!
63:11 Those who would hand over my life to the sword shall become the prey of jackals!
63:12 But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by the Lord shall exult, but the mouths of liars will be shut!
text.DRB:
63:1 Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
63:2 Hear o God my prayer when I make petition: from the fear of my enemy deliver my soul.
63:3 Thou hast protected me from the assembly of the malignant: from the multitude of them that work iniquity.
63:4 Because they have sharpened their tongues as a sword: they have bent the bow a bitter thing,
63:5 that they may shoot in secrets at the immaculate.
63:6 Suddenly they will shoot at him, and will not fear: they have confirmed to themselves a wicked word. They have talked to hide snares: they have said, Who shall see them?
63:7 They have searched iniquities: they have failed searching with scrutiny.
63:8 Man shall come to a deep heart: and God shall be exalted. Children's arrows are made their wounds:
63:9 and their tongues are weakened against them. All that saw them were troubled:
63:10 and every man feared. And they shewed forth the works of God: and they understood his doings.
63:11 The just shall rejoice in our Lord, and shall hope in him, & all the right of heart shall be praised.

text.NRSV:
64:1 Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint; guard my life from fear of the enemy.
64:2 Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, from the gathering of evildoers;
64:3 They sharpen their tongue like a sword and aim their bitter words like arrows;
64:4 That they may shoot at the innocent from ambush; they shoot suddenly and do not fear.
64:5 They hold fast to their evil course; they plan how they may hide their snares; they say, 'Who will see us?'
64:6 They search out wickedness and lay a cunning trap; for deep are the inward thoughts and hearts of them all.
64:7 But God will shoot at them with his swift arrow, and suddenly they shall be wounded.
64:8 Their own tongues shall make them fall, and all who see them shall shake their heads.
64:9 Everyone shall stand in awe and declare God's deeds; they shall consider his handiwork.
64:10 The righteous shall rejoice in the Lord and put their trust in him, and all who are true of heart shall glory.
text.Coverdale:
64:1 Hear my voice, O God, when I complain; *
      protect my life from fear of the enemy.
64:2 Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, *
      from the mob of evildoers.
64:3 They sharpen their tongue like a sword, *
      and aim their bitter words like arrows,
64:4 That they may shoot down the blameless from ambush; *
      they shoot without warning and are not afraid.
64:5 They hold fast to their evil course;
      they plan to hide their snares, *
      and they say, "Who will see us?"
64:6 They devise the most wicked schemes
      and say, "We have thought out a perfect plot." *
      How fathomless is the human heart and mind!
64:7 But God will loose an arrow at them, *
      and suddenly they will be wounded.
64:8 He will make them stumble over their own tongue; *
      all who see them will shake their heads.
64:9 Everyone will stand in awe and declare God's deeds; *
      they will recognize his works.
64:10 The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and put their trust in him, *
      and all who are true of heart will glory.
text.KJV:
64:1 Heare my voice, O God, in my praier; preserue my life from feare of the enemie.
64:2 Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked: from the insurrection of the workers of iniquitie:
64:3 Who whet their tongue like a sword, and bend their bowes to shoote their arrowes, euen bitter words:
64:4 That they may shoote in secret at the perfect: suddenly doe they shoote at him, and feare not.
64:5 They incourage themselues in an euill matter: they commune of laying snares priuily; they say, Who shall see them?
64:6 They search out iniquities, they accomplish a diligent search: both the inward thought of euery one of them, and the heart, is deepe.
64:7 But God shall shoote at them: with an arrowe, sodenly shall they be wounded.
64:8 So they shall make their owne tongue to fall vpon themselues: all that see them, shall flee away.
64:9 And all men shall feare, and shall declare the worke of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.
64:10 The righteous shalbe glad in the Lord, and shall trust in him; and all the vpright in heart shall glory.
text.JPS1985:
64:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
64:2 Hear my voice, O God, when I plead; guard my life from the enemy’s terror.
64:3 Hide me from a band of evil men, from a crowd of evildoers,
64:4 who whet their tongues like swords; they aim their arrows—cruel words—
64:5 to shoot from hiding at the blameless man; they shoot him suddenly and without fear.
64:6 They arm themselves with an evil word; when they speak, it is to conceal traps; they think, “Who will see them? ”
64:7 Let the wrongdoings they have concealed, each one inside him, his secret thoughts, be wholly exposed.
64:8 God shall shoot them with arrows; they shall be struck down suddenly.
64:9 Their tongue shall be their downfall; all who see them shall recoil in horror;
64:10 all men shall stand in awe; they shall proclaim the work of God and His deed which they perceived.
64:11 The righteous shall rejoice in the LORD, and take refuge in Him; all the upright shall exult.
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
64:1 Hear, O God, my voice when I complain, From dread peril by the foe, wilt thou guard my life.
64:2 Wilt thou hide me, From the conclave of evil-doers, From the crowd of workers of iniquity.
64:3 Who have sharpened, like a sword, their tongue, Have made ready their arrow—a bitter word;
64:4 To shoot, in secret places, at the blameless one, Suddenly they shoot at him, and fear not.
64:5 They strengthen for them a wicked word, They talk of hiding snares, They have said, Who can see them?
64:6 They devise perverse things, They have completed the device well devised, Both the intent of each one, and the mind, are unsearchable.
64:7 Once let God have shot at them an arrow, Suddenly have appeared their own wounds!
64:8 When they were to have ruined another, their tongue smote themselves, All who observe them take flight.
64:9 Therefore have all men feared,—And have told the doing of God, And, his work, have considered.
64:10 The righteous man shall rejoice in Yahweh, and seek refuge in him, Then shall glory—all who are upright in heart.
text.NABRE:
64:1 Psalm 64 Treacherous Conspirators Punished by God - For the leader. A psalm of David.
64:2 I - O God, hear my anguished voice; from a dreadful foe protect my life.
64:3 Hide me from the malicious crowd, the mob of evildoers.
64:4 They sharpen their tongues like swords, bend their bows of poison words.
64:5 They shoot at the innocent from ambush, they shoot him in a moment and do not fear.
64:6 They resolve on their wicked plan; they conspire to set snares; they say: “Who will see us?”
64:7 They devise wicked schemes, conceal the schemes they devise; the designs of their hearts are hidden.
64:8 II - God shoots an arrow at them; in a moment they are struck down.
64:9 They are brought down by their own tongues; all who see them flee.
64:10 Every person fears and proclaims God’s actions, they ponder his deeds.
64:11 The righteous rejoices and takes refuge in the LORD; all the upright give praise.
text.DRB:
64:1 To the end, a Psalm of David, the Canticle of Jeremy, and Ezechiel, to the people of the transmigration, when they began to go forth.
64:2 An hymn, o God, becometh thee in Sion: & a vow shall be rendered to thee in Jerusalem.
64:3 Hear my prayer: all flesh shall come to thee.
64:4 The words of the wicked have prevailed upon us: and thou wilt be propitious to our impieties.
64:5 Blessed is he whom thou hast chosen and taken: he shall dwell in thy courts.
64:6 We shall be replenished in the goods of thy house: holy is thy temple, marvelous in equity. Hear us, o God our saviour, the hope of all the ends of the earth, and in the sea far.
64:7 Preparing mountains in thy strength, girded with might:
64:8 which troublest the depths of the sea, the sound of the waves thereof.
64:9 The Gentiles shall be troubled, and they that inhabit the borders shall be afraid of thy signs: the out-goings of the morning and evening thou shalt delight.
64:10 Thou hast visited the earth, and hast inebriated it: thou hast multiplied to enrich it. The river of God is replenished with waters, thou hast prepared their meat: because so is the preparation thereof.
64:11 Inebriate her rivers, multiply her fruits: in her drops she shall rejoice springing.
64:12 Thou wilt bless the crown of the year of thy goodness: and thy fields shall be replenished with plenty.
64:13 The beautiful places of the desert shall be fat: and the little hills shall be girded about with exultation.
64:14 The rams of the sheep are clothed, and the valleys shall abound with corn: they will cry, yea they will say an hymn.

text.NRSV:
65:1 Praise is due to you, O God, in Zion; and to you shall vows be performed.
65:2 O you who answer prayer! To you all flesh shall come.
65:3 When deeds of iniquity overwhelm us, you forgive our transgressions.
65:4 Happy are those whom you choose and bring near to live in your courts. We shall be satisfied with the goodness of your house, your holy temple.
65:5 By awesome deeds you answer us with deliverance, O God of our salvation; you are the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the farthest seas.
65:6 By your strength you established the mountains; you are girded with power.
65:7 You silence the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, the tumult of the peoples.
65:8 Those who live at earth's farthest bounds are awed by your signs; you make the morning and the evening shout for joy.
65:9 You visit the earth and water it, you greatly enrich it; the river of God is full of water; you provide the people with grain, for so you have prepared it.
65:10 You water its furrows abundantly, settling its ridges, softening it with showers, and blessing its growth.
65:11 You crown the year with your bounty; your wagon tracks overflow with richness.
65:12 The pastures of the wilderness overflow, the hills gird themselves with joy,
65:13 the meadows clothe themselves with flocks, the valleys deck themselves with grain, they shout and sing together for joy.
text.Coverdale:
65:1 You are to be praised, O God, in Zion; *
      to you shall vows be performed in Jerusalem.
65:2 To you that hear prayer shall all flesh come, *
      because of their transgressions.
65:3 Our sins are stronger than we are, *
      but you will blot them out.
65:4 Happy are they whom you choose
      and draw to your courts to dwell there! *
      they will be satisfied by the beauty of your house,
      by the holiness of your temple.
65:5 Awesome things will you show us in your righteousness,
      O God of our salvation, *
      O Hope of all the ends of the earth
      and of the seas that are far away.
65:6 You make fast the mountains by your power; *
      they are girded about with might.
65:7 You still the roaring of the seas, *
      the roaring of their waves,
      and the clamor of the peoples.
65:8 Those who dwell at the ends of the earth will tremble at your marvelous signs; *
      you make the dawn and the dusk to sing for joy.
65:9 You visit the earth and water it abundantly;
      you make it very plenteous; *
      the river of God is full of water.
65:10 You prepare the grain, *
      for so you provide for the earth.
65:11 You drench the furrows and smooth out the ridges; *
      with heavy rain you soften the ground and bless its increase.
65:12 You crown the year with your goodness, *
      and your paths overflow with plenty.
65:13 May the fields of the wilderness be rich for grazing, *
      and the hills be clothed with joy.
65:14 May the meadows cover themselves with flocks,
      and the valleys cloak themselves with grain; *
      let them shout for joy and sing.
text.KJV:
65:1 Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and vnto thee shall the vowe be performed.
65:2 O thou that hearest prayer, vnto thee shall all flesh come.
65:3 Iniquities preuaile against me: as for our transgressions, thou shalt purge them away.
65:4 Blessed is the man whom thou choosest and causest to approach vnto thee, that hee may dwell in thy Courts: we shalbe satisfied with the goodnesse of thy house, euen of thy holy temple.
65:5 By terrible things in righteousnesse, wilt thou answere vs, O God of our saluation: who art the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of them that are a farre off vpon the sea.
65:6 Which by his strength setteth fast the mountaines; being girded with power.
65:7 Which stilleth the noise of the seas; the noise of their waues, and the tumult of the people.
65:8 They also that dwell in the vttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens: thou makest the outgoings of the morning, and euening to reioyce.
65:9 Thou visitest the earth and waterest it: thou greatly inrichest it with the riuer of God which is full of water; thou preparest them corne, when thou hast so prouided for it.
65:10 Thou waterest the ridges thereof abundantly: thou settlest the furrowes thereof: thou makest it soft with showres, thou blessest the springing thereof.
65:11 Thou crownest the yeere with thy goodnesse; and thy paths drop fatnesse.
65:12 They drop vpon the pastures of the wildernesse; and the little hilles reioyce on euery side.
65:13 The pastures are cloathed with flockes; the valleis also are couered ouer with corne; they shout for ioy, they also sing.
text.JPS1985:
65:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. A song.
65:2 Praise befits You in Zion, O God; vows are paid to You;
65:3 all mankind comes to You, You who hear prayer.
65:4 When all manner of sins overwhelm me, it is You who forgive our iniquities.
65:5 Happy is the man You choose and bring near to dwell in Your courts; may we be sated with the blessings of Your house, Your holy temple.
65:6 Answer us with victory through awesome deeds, O God, our deliverer, in whom all the ends of the earth and the distant seas put their trust;
65:7 who by His power fixed the mountains firmly, who is girded with might,
65:8 who stills the raging seas, the raging waves, and tumultuous peoples.
65:9 Those who live at the ends of the earth are awed by Your signs; You make the lands of sunrise and sunset shout for joy.
65:10 You take care of the earth and irrigate it; You enrich it greatly, with the channel of God full of water; You provide grain for men; for so do You prepare it.
65:11 Saturating its furrows, leveling its ridges, You soften it with showers, You bless its growth.
65:12 You crown the year with Your bounty; fatness is distilled in Your paths;
65:13 the pasturelands distill it; the hills are girded with joy.
65:14 The meadows are clothed with flocks, the valleys mantled with grain; they raise a shout, they break into song.
text.Grail1963:
.2 To you our praise is due

Too heavy for us, our offenses,
but you wipe them away.

We are filled with the blessings of your house,
of your holy temple.

O God our savior,
the hope of all the earth
and of far distant isles.

you are girded with power.
.8 You still the roaring of the seas,
(the roaring of their waves,)
and the tumult of the peoples.

at the sight of your wonders.
The lands of sunrise and sunset

you fill with your joy.

you fill it with riches.
Your river in heaven brims over
to provide its grain.
And thus you provide for the earth;
