your scepter of power:
rule in the midst of all your foes.
3 A prince from the day of your birth
on the holy mountains;
from the womb before the dawn I begot you.
4 The Lord has sworn an oath he will not change.
"You are a priest for ever,
a priest like Melchizedek of old."

* * *
5 The Master standing at your right hand
will shatter kings in the day of his wrath.
6 He, the judge of the nations
will heap high the bodies;
heads shall be scattered far and wide.
7 He shall drink from the stream by the wayside
and therefore he shall lift up his head.
text.Rotherham:
110:1 The declaration of Yahweh to my Lord—Sit thou at my right hand, Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
110:2 Thy sceptre of strength, will Yahweh extend out of Zion, Tread thou down, in the midst of thy foes.
110:3 Thy people, will freely offer themselves, in the day of thine army,—in the splendours of holiness, out of the womb of the dawn, To thee, [shall spring forth] the dew of thy youth.
110:4 Yahweh, hath sworn—and will not repent, Thou, [shalt be] a priest unto times age-abiding, after the manner of Melchizedek.
110:5 My Lord, on thy right hand,—hath shattered—in the day of his anger—kings;
110:6 He will judge among the nations—full of dead bodies! He hath shattered the head over a land far extended:
110:7 Of the torrent in the way, will he drink;—For this cause, will he lift up [his] head.
text.NABRE:
110:1 Psalm 110 God Appoints the King both King and Priest - A psalm of David. The LORD says to my lord: “Sit at my right hand, while I make your enemies your footstool.”
110:2 The scepter of your might: the LORD extends your strong scepter from Zion. Have dominion over your enemies!
110:3 Yours is princely power from the day of your birth. In holy splendor before the daystar, like dew I begot you.
110:4 The LORD has sworn and will not waver: “You are a priest forever in the manner of Melchizedek.”
110:5 At your right hand is the Lord, who crushes kings on the day of his wrath,
110:6 Who judges nations, heaps up corpses, crushes heads across the wide earth,
110:7 Who drinks from the brook by the wayside and thus holds high his head.
text.DRB:
110:1 Alleluia. I will confess to thee, o Lord, with all my heart: in the counsel of the just, and the congregation.
110:2 The works of our Lord are great: exquisite according to all his wills.
110:3 Confession and magnificence his work: and his justice continueth for ever and ever.
110:4 He hath made a memory of his marvelous works; a merciful & pitiful Lord:
110:5 he hath given meat to them that fear him. He will be mindful for ever of his testament:
110:6 the force of his works he will shew forth to his people:
110:7 To give them the inheritance of the gentiles: the works of his hands truth and judgment.
110:8 All his commandments are faithful: confirmed for ever and ever, made in truth and equity.
110:9 He sent redemption to his people: he commanded his testament for ever. Holy and terrible is his name:
110:10 the fear of our Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Understanding is good to all that do it: his praise remaineth for ever and ever.

text.NRSV:
111:1 Praise the Lord! I will give thanks to the Lord with my whole heart, in the company of the upright, in the congregation.
111:2 Great are the works of the Lord, studied by all who delight in them.
111:3 Full of honor and majesty is his work, and his righteousness endures forever.
111:4 He has gained renown by his wonderful deeds; the Lord is gracious and merciful.
111:5 He provides food for those who fear him; he is ever mindful of his covenant.
111:6 He has shown his people the power of his works, in giving them the heritage of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands are faithful and just; all his precepts are trustworthy.
111:8 They are established forever and ever, to be performed with faithfulness and uprightness.
111:9 He sent redemption to his people; he has commanded his covenant forever. Holy and awesome is his name.
111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; all those who practice it have a good understanding. His praise endures forever.
text.Coverdale:
111:1 I will give thanks unto the LORD with my whole heart, *
      secretly among the faithful, and in the congregation.
111:2 The works of the LORD are great, *
      sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.
111:3 His work is worthy to be praised and had in honor, *
      and his righteousness endures for ever.
111:4 The merciful and gracious LORD has so done his marvelous works, *
      that they ought to be had in remembrance.
111:5 He has given meat unto them that fear him; *
      he shall ever be mindful of his covenant.
111:6 He has showed his people the power of his works, *
      that he may give them the heritage of the heathen.
111:7 The works of his hands are verity and judgment; *
      all his commandments are true.
111:8 They stand fast for ever and ever, *
      and are done in truth and equity.
111:9 He sent redemption unto his people; *
      he has commanded his covenant for ever; holy and reverend is his Name.
111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; *
      a good understanding have all they that do thereafter; the praise of it endures for ever.
text.KJV:
111:1 Praise yee the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart: in the assembly of the vpright, and in the Congregation.
111:2 The workes of the Lord are great: sought out of all them that haue pleasure therein.
111:3 His worke is honourable and glorious: and his righteousnesse endureth for euer.
111:4 Hee hath made his wonderfull works to be remembred: the Lord is gracious, and full of compassion.
111:5 He hath giuen meate vnto them that feare him: he will euer be mindfull of his couenant.
111:6 He hath shewed his people the power of his workes: that he may giue them the heritage of the heathen.
111:7 The works of his hands are veritie and iudgment: all his commandements are sure.
111:8 They stand fast for euer and euer: and are done in trueth and vprightnes.
111:9 He sent redemption vnto his people, hee hath commanded his couenant for euer: holy and reuerend is his Name.
111:10 The feare of the Lord is the beginning of wisedome, a good vnderstanding haue all they that doe his commandements: his praise endureth for euer.
text.JPS1985:
111:1 Hallelujah. I praise the LORD with all my heart in the assembled congregation of the upright.
111:2 The works of the LORD are great, within reach of all who desire them.
111:3 His deeds are splendid and glorious; His beneficence is everlasting;
111:4 He has won renown for His wonders. The LORD is gracious and compassionate;
111:5 He gives food to those who fear Him; He is ever mindful of His covenant.
111:6 He revealed to His people His powerful works, in giving them the heritage of nations.
111:7 His handiwork is truth and justice; all His precepts are enduring,
111:8 well-founded for all eternity, wrought of truth and equity.
111:9 He sent redemption to His people; He ordained His covenant for all time; His name is holy and awesome.
111:10 The beginning of wisdom is the fear of the LORD; all who practice it gain sound understanding. Praise of Him is everlasting.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
I will thank the Lord with all my heart
in the meeting of the just and their assembly.
2 Great are the works of the Lord,
to be pondered by all who love them.
3 Majestic and glorious his work,
his justice stands firm for ever.
4 He makes us remember his wonders.
The Lord is compassion and love.
5 He gives food to those who fear him;
keeps his covenant ever in mind.
6 He has shown his might to his people
by giving them the lands of the nations.
7 His works are justice and truth,
his precepts are all of them sure,
8 standing firm for ever and ever;
they are made in uprightness and truth.
9 He has sent deliverance to his people
and established his covenant for ever.
Holy his name, to be feared.
10 To fear the Lord is the first stage of wisdom;
all who do so prove themselves wise.
His praise shall last for ever!
text.Rotherham:
111:1 Praise ye Yah! I will give thanks unto Yahweh, with a whole heart, in the circle of the upright and the assembly.
111:2 Great are the works of Yahweh, sought out, by all who find pleasure therein.
111:3 Honourable and majestic, is his doing, and, his righteousness, standeth for aye.
111:4 A memorial, hath he made by his wonders, Gracious and compassionate, is Yahweh.
111:5 Food, hath he given to them who revere him, He will remember, age-abidingly, his covenant.
111:6 The might of his works, hath he declared to his people, that he may give them the inheritance of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands, are faithful and just, Firm are all his precepts;
111:8 Upheld to futurity, to times age-abiding, done in faithfulness and equity.
111:9 Ransom, hath he sent to his people, He hath commanded, to times age-abiding, his covenant, Holy and reverend, is his Name.
111:10 The beginning of wisdom, is the reverence of Yahweh, Good discretion, have all that do them, His praise, endureth for aye.
text.NABRE:
111:1 Psalm 111 Praise of God for Goodness to Israel - Hallelujah! I will praise the LORD with all my heart in the assembled congregation of the upright.
111:2 Great are the works of the LORD, studied by all who delight in them.
111:3 Majestic and glorious is his work, his righteousness endures forever.
111:4 He won renown for his wondrous deeds; gracious and merciful is the LORD.
111:5 He gives food to those who fear him, he remembers his covenant forever.
111:6 He showed his powerful deeds to his people, giving them the inheritance of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands are true and just, reliable all his decrees,
111:8 Established forever and ever, to be observed with truth and equity.
111:9 He sent release to his people, decreed his covenant forever; holy and fearsome is his name.
111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; prudent are all who practice it. His praise endures forever.
text.DRB:
111:1 Alleluia. Of the return of Aggeus and Zachary. Blessed is the man that feareth our Lord: he shall have great delight in his commandments.
111:2 His seed shall be mighty in the earth: the generation of the righteous shall be blessed.
111:3 Glory and riches in his house: and his justice abideth for ever and ever.
111:4 Light is risen up in darkness to the righteous: he is merciful, and pitiful, and just.
111:5 Acceptable is the man, that is merciful and lendeth, that shall dispose his words in judgment:
111:6 Because he shall not be moved for ever.
111:7 The just shall be in eternal memory: he shall not fear at the hearing of evil. His heart is ready to hope in our Lord,
111:8 his heart is confirmed: he shall not be moved till he look over his enemies.
111:9 He distributed, he gave to the poor: his justice remaineth for ever and ever, his horn shall be exalted in glory.
111:10 The sinner shall see, and will be angry, he shall gnash his teeth and pine away: the desire of sinners shall perish.

text.NRSV:
112:1 Praise the Lord! Happy are those who fear the Lord, who greatly delight in his commandments.
112:2 Their descendants will be mighty in the land; the generation of the upright will be blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches are in their houses, and their righteousness endures forever.
112:4 They rise in the darkness as a light for the upright; they are gracious, merciful, and righteous.
112:5 It is well with those who deal generously and lend, who conduct their affairs with justice.
112:6 For the righteous will never be moved; they will be remembered forever.
112:7 They are not afraid of evil tidings; their hearts are firm, secure in the Lord.
112:8 Their hearts are steady, they will not be afraid; in the end they will look in triumph on their foes.
112:9 They have distributed freely, they have given to the poor; their righteousness endures forever; their horn is exalted in honor.
112:10 The wicked see it and are angry; they gnash their teeth and melt away; the desire of the wicked comes to nothing.
text.Coverdale:
112:1 Blessed is the man that fears the LORD; *
      he has great delight in his commandments.
112:2 His seed shall be mighty upon earth; *
      the generation of the faithful shall be blessed.
112:3 Riches and plenteousness shall be in his house; *
      and his righteousness endures for ever.
112:4 Unto the godly there arises up light in the darkness; *
      he is merciful, loving, and righteous.
112:5 A good man is merciful, and lends; *
      and will guide his words with discretion.
112:6 For he shall never be moved; *
      and the righteous shall be had in everlasting remembrance.
112:7 He will not be afraid of any evil tidings; *
      for his heart stands fast, and believes in the LORD.
112:8 His heart is stablished, and will not shrink, *
      until he see his desire upon his enemies.
112:9 He has dispersed abroad, and given to the poor, *
      and his righteousness remains for ever; his horn shall be exalted with honor.
112:10 The ungodly shall see it, and it shall grieve him; *
      he shall gnash with his teeth, and consume away; the desire of the ungodly shall perish.
text.KJV:
112:1 Praise ye the Lord. Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord, that delighteth greatly in his Commaundements.
112:2 His seed shall bee mightie vpon earth: the generation of the vpright shalbe blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches shalbe in his house: and his righteousnesse endureth for euer.
112:4 Unto the vpright there ariseth light in the darknesse: hee is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.
112:5 A good man sheweth fauour and lendeth: he will guide his affaires with discretion.
112:6 Surely he shall not be moued for euer: the righteous shalbe in euerlasting remembrance.
112:7 He shall not be afraid of euill tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord.
112:8 His heart is established, hee shall not be afraid, vntill he see his desire vpon his enemies.
112:9 He hath dispersed, he hath giuen to the poore: his righteousnesse endureth for euer; his horne shalbe exalted with honour.
112:10 The wicked shall see it, and be grieued; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.
text.JPS1985:
112:1 Hallelujah. Happy is the man who fears the LORD, who is ardently devoted to His commandments.
112:2 His descendants will be mighty in the land, a blessed generation of upright men.
112:3 Wealth and riches are in his house, and his beneficence lasts forever.
112:4 A light shines for the upright in the darkness; he is gracious, compassionate, and beneficent.
112:5 All goes well with the man who lends generously, who conducts his affairs with equity.
112:6 He shall never be shaken; the beneficent man will be remembered forever.
112:7 He is not afraid of evil tidings; his heart is firm, he trusts in the LORD.
112:8 His heart is resolute, he is unafraid; in the end he will see the fall of his foes.
112:9 He gives freely to the poor; his beneficence lasts forever; his horn is exalted in honor.
112:10 The wicked man shall see it and be vexed; he shall gnash his teeth; his courage shall fail. The desire of the wicked shall come to nothing.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Happy the man who fears the Lord,
who take delight in all his commands.
2 His sons shall be powerful on earth;
the children of the upright are blessed.

3 Riches and wealth are in his house;
his justice stands firm for ever.
4 He is a light in the darkness for the upright:
he is generous, merciful and just.
5 The good man takes pity and lends,
he conducts his affairs with honor.
6 The just man will never waver:
he will be remembered for ever.
7 He has no fear of evil news;
with a firm heart he trusts in the Lord.
8 With a steadfast heart he will not fear;
he will see the downfall of his foes.
9 Openhanded, he gives to the poor;
his justice stands firm for ever.
His head will be raised in glory.
10 The wicked man sees and is angry,
grinds his teeth and fades away;
the desire of the wicked leads to doom.
text.NABRE:
112:1 Psalm 112 The Blessings of the Just - Hallelujah! Blessed the man who fears the LORD, who greatly delights in his commands.
112:2 His descendants shall be mighty in the land, a generation of the upright will be blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches shall be in his house; his righteousness shall endure forever.
112:4 Light shines through the darkness for the upright; gracious, compassionate, and righteous.
112:5 It is good for the man gracious in lending, who conducts his affairs with justice.
112:6 For he shall never be shaken; the righteous shall be remembered forever.
112:7 He shall not fear an ill report; his heart is steadfast, trusting the LORD.
112:8 His heart is tranquil, without fear, till at last he looks down on his foes.
112:9 Lavishly he gives to the poor; his righteousness shall endure forever; his horn shall be exalted in honor.
112:10 The wicked sees and is angry; gnashes his teeth and wastes away; the desire of the wicked come to nothing.
text.DRB:
112:1 Alleluia. Praise our Lord ye children: praise ye the name of our Lord.
112:2 Be the name of our Lord blessed, from henceforth now and for ever,
112:3 From the rising of the sun unto the going down, the name of our Lord is laudable.
112:4 Our Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
112:5 Who is as the Lord our God, that dwelleth on high,
112:6 and beholdeth the low things in heaven and in earth?
112:7 Raising up the needy from the earth, and lifting up the poor out of the dung:
112:8 To place him with Princes, with the Princes of his people.
112:9 Who maketh the barren woman to dwell in a house, a joyful mother of children.

text.NRSV:
113:1 Praise the Lord! Praise, O servants of the Lord; praise the name of the Lord.
113:2 Blessed be the name of the Lord from this time forth and forevermore.
113:3 From the rising of the sun to its setting the name of the Lord is to be praised.
113:4 The Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
113:5 Who is like the Lord our God, who is seated on high,
113:6 who looks far down on the heavens and the earth?
113:7 He raises the poor from the dust, and lifts the needy from the ash heap,
113:8 to make them sit with princes, with the princes of his people.
113:9 He gives the barren woman a home, making her the joyous mother of children. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
113:1 Praise the LORD, you servants; *
      O praise the Name of the LORD.
113:2 Blessed be the Name of the LORD, *
      from this time forth for evermore.
113:3 The LORD's Name is praised, *
      from the rising up of the sun unto the going down of the same.
113:4 The LORD is high above all heathen, *
      and his glory above the heavens.
113:5 Who is like unto the LORD our God, that has his dwelling so high, *
      and yet humbles himself to behold the things that are in heaven and earth?
113:6 He takes up the simple out of the dust, *
      and lifts the poor out of the mire;
113:7 That he may set him with the princes, *
      even with the princes of his people.
113:8 He makes the barren woman to keep house, *
      and to be a joyful mother of children.
text.KJV:
113:1 Praise yee the Lord. Praise, O yee seruants of the Lord: praise the name of the Lord.
113:2 Blessed be the name of the Lord: from this time forth and for euermore.
113:3 From the rising of the sunne vnto the going downe of the same: the Lords name is to be praised.
113:4 The Lord is high aboue all nations: and his glory aboue the heauens.
113:5 Who is like vnto the Lord our God: who dwelleth on high:
113:6 Who humbleth himselfe to behold the things that are in heauen, and in the earth?
113:7 He raiseth vp the poore out of the dust: and lifteth the needie out of the dung-hill:
113:8 That he may set him with princes: euen with the princes of his people.
113:9 He maketh the barren woman to keepe house; to be a ioyfull mother of children: Praise yee the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
113:1 Hallelujah. O servants of the LORD, give praise; praise the name of the LORD.
113:2 Let the name of the LORD be blessed now and forever.
113:3 From east to west the name of the LORD is praised.
113:4 The LORD is exalted above all nations; His glory is above the heavens.
113:5 Who is like the LORD our God, who, enthroned on high,
113:6 sees what is below, in heaven and on earth?
113:7 He raises the poor from the dust, lifts up the needy from the refuse heap
113:8 to set them with the great, with the great men of His people.
113:9 He sets the childless woman among her household as a happy mother of children. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Praise, O servants of the Lord,
praise the name of the Lord!
2 May the name of the Lord be blessed
both now and for evermore!
3 From the rising of the sun to its setting
praised be the name of the Lord!
4 High above all nations is the Lord,
above the heavens his glory.
5 Who is like the Lord, our God,
who has risen on high to his throne

to look down upon heaven and earth?

text.NABRE:
113:1 Psalm 113 Praise of God’s Care for the Poor - Hallelujah! I - Praise, you servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.
113:2 Blessed be the name of the LORD both now and forever.
113:3 From the rising of the sun to its setting let the name of the LORD be praised.
113:4 II - High above all nations is the LORD; above the heavens his glory.
113:5 Who is like the LORD our God, enthroned on high,
113:6 looking down on heaven and earth?
113:7 He raises the needy from the dust, lifts the poor from the ash heap,
113:8 Seats them with princes, the princes of the people,
113:9 Gives the childless wife a home, the joyful mother of children. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
113:1 Alleluia. In the coming forth of Israel out of Ægypt: of the house of Jacob from the barbarous people.
113:2 Jewry was made his sanctification, Israel his dominion.
113:3 The sea saw, and fled: Jordan was turned backward.
113:4 The mountains leaped as rams: and the little hills as the lambs of sheep.
113:5 What aileth thee o sea that thou didst fly: and thou o Jordan, that thou was turned backward?
113:6 Ye mountains leaped as rams, and ye little hills as the lambs of sheep.
113:7 At the face of our Lord was the earth moved, at the face of the God of Jacob.
113:8 Who turned the rock into pools of waters, and stony hill into fountains of waters.
113:9 Not to us O Lord, Not TO US: but to thy name give the glory.
113:10 For thy mercy, and thy truth: lest at any time the Gentiles say: Where is their God?
113:11 But our God is in heaven: he hath done all things what soever he would.
113:12 The idols of the gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
113:13 They have mouth, and shall not speak: they have eyes, an shall not see.
113:14 They have ears, and shall not hear: they have nostrils and shall not smell.
113:15 They have hands, and shall not handle: they have feet, and shall not walk: they shall not cry in their throat.
113:16 Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
113:17 The house of Israel hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
113:18 The house of Aaron hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
113:19 They that fear our Lord have hoped in our Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
113:20 Our Lord hath been mindful of us: and hath blessed us. He hath blessed the house of Israel: he hath blessed the house of Aaron.
113:21 He hath blessed all that fear our Lord, the little with the great.
113:22 Our Lord add upon you: upon you, & upon your children.
113:23 Blessed be you of our Lord, which made heaven and earth.
113:24 The heaven of heaven is to our Lord: but the earth he hath given to the children of men.
113:25 The dead shall not praise thee, o Lord: nor all they that go down into hell:
113:26 but we that live do bless our Lord, from this time, and for ever.

text.NRSV:
114:1 When Israel went out from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
114:2 Judah became God's sanctuary, Israel his dominion.
114:3 The sea looked and fled; Jordan turned back.
114:4 The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like lambs.
114:5 Why is it, O sea, that you flee? O Jordan, that you turn back?
114:6 O mountains, that you skip like rams? O hills, like lambs?
114:7 Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob,
114:8 who turns the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a spring of water.

text.NABRE:

114:2 Judah became God’s sanctuary, Israel, God’s domain.
114:3 The sea saw and fled; the Jordan turned back.

text.DRB:
114:1 Alleluia. I have loved, because our Lord will hear the voice of my prayer.
114:2 Because he hath inclined his ear to me: and in my days I will invocate.
114:3 The sorrows of death have compassed me: and the perils of hell have found me. I have found tribulation and sorrow:
114:4 and I invocated the name of our Lord. O Lord deliver my soul:
114:5 our Lord is merciful, and just, and our God doth mercy.
114:6 Our Lord keepeth little ones: I was humbled, and he hath delivered me.
114:7 Turn o my soul into thy rest: because our Lord hath done good to thee.
114:8 Because he hath delivered my soul from death: mine eyes from tears, my feet from sliding.
114:9 I will please our Lord in the country of the living.

text.NRSV:
115:1 Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness.
115:2 Why should the nations say, 'Where is their God?'
115:3 Our God is in the heavens; he does whatever he pleases.
115:4 Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
115:5 They have mouths, but do not speak; eyes, but do not see.
115:6 They have ears, but do not hear; noses, but do not smell.
115:7 They have hands, but do not feel; feet, but do not walk; they make no sound in their throats.
115:8 Those who make them are like them; so are all who trust in them.
115:9 O Israel, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
115:10 O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
115:11 You who fear the Lord, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
115:12 The Lord has been mindful of us; he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;
115:13 he will bless those who fear the Lord, both small and great.
115:14 May the Lord give you increase, both you and your children.
115:15 May you be blessed by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
115:16 The heavens are the Lord's heavens, but the earth he has given to human beings.
115:17 The dead do not praise the Lord, nor do any that go down into silence.
115:18 But we will bless the Lord from this time forth and forevermore. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
115:1 Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto your Name give the praise; *
      for your loving mercy, and for your truth's sake.
115:2 Wherefore shall the heathen say, *
      "Where is now their God?"
115:3 As for our God, he is in heaven; *
      he has done whatsoever pleased him.
115:4 Their idols are silver and gold, *
      even the work of men's hands.
115:5 They have mouths, and speak not; *
      eyes have they, and see not.
115:6 They have ears, and hear not; *
      noses have they, and smell not.
115:7 They have hands, and handle not; feet have they, and walk not; *
      neither speak they through their throat.
115:8 They that make them are like unto them; *
      and so are all such as put their trust in them.
115:9 But you, house of Israel, trust you in the LORD; *
      he is their succor and defense.
115:10 You house of Aaron, put your trust in the LORD; *
      he is their helper and defender.
115:11 You that fear the LORD, put your trust in the LORD; *
      he is their helper and defender.
115:12 The LORD has been mindful of us, and he shall bless us; *
      even he shall bless the house of Israel, he shall bless the house of Aaron.
115:13 He shall bless them that fear the LORD, *
      both small and great.
115:14 The LORD shall increase you more and more, *
      you and your children.
115:15 You are the blessed of the LORD, *
      who made heaven and earth.
115:16 All the whole heavens are the LORD's; *
      the earth has he given to the children of men.
115:17 The dead praise not you, O LORD, *
      neither all they that go down into silence.
115:18 But we will praise the LORD, *
      from this time forth for evermore. Praise the LORD.
text.KJV:
115:1 Not vnto vs, O Lord, not vnto vs, but vnto thy name giue glory: for thy mercy, and for thy truthes sake.
115:2 Wherefore should the heathen say: Where is now their God?
115:3 But our God is in the heauens: he hath done whatsoeuer he pleased.
115:4 Their idoles are siluer and gold: the worke of mens hands.
115:5 They haue mouths, but they speake not; eies haue they, but they see not.
115:6 They haue eares, but they heare not: noses haue they, but they smell not.
115:7 They haue hands, but they handle not, feete haue they, but they walke not: neither speake they through their throat.
115:8 They that make them are like vnto them: so is euery one that trusteth in them.
115:9 O Israel, trust thou in the Lord: he is their helpe and their shield.
115:10 O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord: he is their helpe & their shield.
115:11 Ye that feare the Lord trust in the Lord: he is their helpe and their shield.
115:12 The Lord hath bene mindfull of vs, he will blesse vs, he will blesse the house of Israel: he will blesse the house of Aaron.
115:13 Hee will blesse them that feare the Lord: both small and great.
115:14 The Lord shall increase you more and more: you and your children.
115:15 You are blessed of the Lord: which made heauen and earth.
115:16 The heauen, euen the heauens are the Lords: but the earth hath hee giuen to the children of men.
115:17 The dead praise not the Lord: neither any that go downe into silence.
115:18 But we will blesse the Lord, from this time foorth and for euermore. Praise the Lord.
text.JPS1985:

text.Rotherham:
115:1 Not unto us, O Yahweh, not unto us,—but, unto thine own Name, give glory, concerning thy lovingkindness, concerning thy faithfulness.
115:2 Wherefore should the nations say, Pray where is their God?
115:3 When, our God, is in the heavens, Whatsoever he pleased, hath he done.
115:4 Their idols, are silver and gold, the works of the hands of men,—
115:5 A mouth, have they, but they speak not, Eyes, have they, but they see not;
115:6 Ears, have they, but they hear not, A nose, have they, but they smell not:
115:7 Their hands! but they feel not Their feet! but they walk not, No sound make they in their throat.
115:8 Like unto them, shall be they who make them, Every one who trusteth in them.
115:9 O Israel! trust thou in Yahweh, Their help and their shield, is he!
115:10 O house of Aaron! trust ye in Yahweh, Their help and their shield, is he!
115:11 Ye that revere Yahweh! trust in Yahweh, Their help and their shield, is he!
115:12 Yahweh, hath remembered us, he will bless—He will bless the house of Israel, He will bless the house of Aaron;
115:13 He will bless them who revere Yahweh, the small with the great.
115:14 Yahweh multiply you, You, and your children.
115:15 Blessed are ye of Yahweh, who made the heavens and the earth:
115:16 As for the heavens, the heavens, belong to Yahweh, but the earth, hath he given to the sons of men.
115:17 The dead, cannot praise Yah, nor any that go down into silence;
115:18 But, we, will bless Yah, from henceforth even unto times age-abiding. Praise ye Yah.
text.NABRE:
115:1 Psalm 115 The Greatness of the True God I - Not to us, LORD, not to us but to your name give glory because of your mercy and faithfulness.
115:2 Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
115:3 Our God is in heaven and does whatever he wills.
115:4 II - Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
115:5 They have mouths but do not speak, eyes but do not see.
115:6 They have ears but do not hear, noses but do not smell.
115:7 They have hands but do not feel, feet but do not walk; they produce no sound from their throats.
115:8 Their makers will be like them, and anyone who trusts in them.
115:9 III - The house of Israel trusts in the LORD, who is their help and shield.
115:10 The house of Aaron trusts in the LORD, who is their help and shield.
115:11 Those who fear the LORD trust in the LORD, who is their help and shield.
115:12 The LORD remembers us and will bless us, will bless the house of Israel, will bless the house of Aaron,
115:13 Will bless those who fear the LORD, small and great alike.
115:14 May the LORD increase your number, yours and your descendants.
115:15 May you be blessed by the LORD, maker of heaven and earth.
115:16 The heavens belong to the LORD, but he has given the earth to the children of Adam.
115:17 The dead do not praise the LORD, not all those go down into silence.
115:18 It is we who bless the LORD, both now and forever. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
115:1 Alleluia. I believed, for which cause I spake: but I was humbled exceedingly.
115:2 Isaid in mine excess: Every man is a liar.
115:3 What shall I render to our Lord, for all things that he hath rendered to me?
115:4 I will take the chalice of salvation: and I will invocate the name of our Lord.
115:5 I will render my vows to our Lord before all his people:
115:6 precious in the sight of our Lord is the death of his Saints.
115:7 O Lord because I am thy servant: I am thy servant, and the son of thy handmaid. Thou hast broken my bonds:
115:8 I will sacrifice to thee the host of praise, and I will invocate the name of our Lord.
115:9 I will render my vows to our Lord in the sight of all his people:
115:10 in the courts of the house of our Lord, in the midst of thee, o Jerusalem.

text.NRSV:
116:1 I love the Lord, because he has heard my voice and my supplications.
116:2 Because he inclined his ear to me, therefore I will call on him as long as I live.
116:3 The snares of death encompassed me; the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me; I suffered distress and anguish.
116:4 Then I called on the name of the Lord: 'O Lord, I pray, save my life!'
116:5 Gracious is the Lord, and righteous; our God is merciful.
116:6 The Lord protects the simple; when I was brought low, he saved me.
116:7 Return, O my soul, to your rest, for the Lord has dealt bountifully with you.
116:8 For you have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I walk before the Lord in the land of the living.
116:10 I kept my faith, even when I said, 'I am greatly afflicted';
116:11 I said in my consternation, 'Everyone is a liar.'
116:12 What shall I return to the Lord for all his bounty to me?
116:13 I will lift up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the Lord,
116:14 I will pay my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people.
116:15 Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his faithful ones.
116:16 O Lord, I am your servant; I am your servant, the child of your serving girl. You have loosed my bonds.
116:17 I will offer to you a thanksgiving sacrifice and call on the name of the Lord.
116:18 I will pay my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people,
116:19 in the courts of the house of the Lord, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
116:1 I am well pleased, *
      that the LORD has heard the voice of my prayer;
116:2 That he has inclined his ear unto me; *
      therefore will I call upon him as long as I live.
116:3 The snares of death compassed me round about, *
      and the pains of hell gat hold upon me.
116:4 I shall find trouble and heaviness, and I will call upon the Name of the LORD; *
      "O LORD, I beseech you, deliver my soul."
116:5 Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; *
      yea, our God is merciful.
116:6 The LORD preserves the simple; *
      I was in misery, and he helped me.
116:7 Turn again then unto your rest, O my soul; *
      for the LORD has rewarded you.
116:8 And why? you have delivered my soul from death, *
      my eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.
116:9 I will walk before the LORD *
      in the land of the living.
116:10 I believed, and therefore will I speak; but I was sore troubled; *
      I said in my haste, "All men are liars."
116:11 What reward shall I give unto the LORD *
      for all the benefits that he has done unto me?
116:12 I will receive the cup of salvation, *
      and call upon the Name of the LORD.
116:13 I will pay my vows now in the presence of all his people; *
      right dear in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints.
116:14 Behold, O LORD, how that I am your servant; *
      I am your servant, and the son of your handmaid; you have broken my bonds in sunder.
116:15 I will offer to you the sacrifice of thanksgiving, *
      and will call upon the Name of the LORD.
116:16 I will pay my vows unto the LORD, in the sight of all his people, *
      in the courts of the LORD's house, even in the midst of you, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD.
text.KJV:
116:1 I loue the Lord: because hee hath heard my voice, & my supplications.
116:2 Because hee hath inclined his eare vnto mee: therefore will I call vpon him as long as I liue.
116:3 The sorrowes of death compassed me, and the paines of hell gate hold vpon me: I found trouble and sorrow.
116:4 Then called I vpon the Name of the Lord: O Lord, I beseech thee deliuer my soule.
116:5 Gracious is the Lord, and righteous: yea our God is mercifull.
116:6 The Lord preserueth the simple: I was brought low, and hee helped me.
116:7 Returne vnto thy rest, O my soule: for the Lord hath dealt bountifully with thee.
116:8 For thou hast deliuered my soule from death, mine eyes from teares, and my feete from falling.
116:9 I wil walke before the Lord: in the land of the liuing.
116:10 I beleeued, therfore haue I spoken: I was greatly afflicted.
116:11 I said in my haste: All men are lyers.
116:12 What shall I render vnto the Lord: for all his benefits towards mee?
116:13 I will take the cup of saluation: and call vpon the Name of the Lord.
116:14 I will pay my vowes vnto the Lord: now in the presence of all his people.
116:15 Precious in þ sight of the Lord: is the death of his Saints.
116:16 Oh Lord, truely I am thy seruant, I am thy seruant, and the sonne of thy handmayde: thou hast loosed my bonds.
116:17 I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thankes-giuing: and will call vpon the Name of the Lord.
116:18 I will pay my vowes vnto the Lord: now in the presence of all his people:
116:19 In the Courts of the Lords house, in the middes of thee, O Ierusalem. Praise ye the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
116:1 I love the LORD for He hears my voice, my pleas;
116:2 for He turns His ear to me whenever I call.
116:3 The bonds of death encompassed me; the torments of Sheol overtook me. I came upon trouble and sorrow
116:4 and I invoked the name of the LORD, “O LORD, save my life! ”
116:5 The LORD is gracious and beneficent; our God is compassionate.
116:6 The LORD protects the simple; I was brought low and He saved me.
116:7 Be at rest, once again, O my soul, for the LORD has been good to you.
116:8 You have delivered me from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I shall walk before the LORD in the lands of the living.
116:10 I trust [in the LORD]; out of great suffering I spoke
116:11 and said rashly, “All men are false.”
116:12 How can I repay the LORD for all His bounties to me?
116:13 I raise the cup of deliverance and invoke the name of the LORD.
116:14 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all His people.
116:15 The death of His faithful ones is grievous in the LORD’s sight.
116:16 O LORD, I am Your servant, Your servant, the son of Your maidservant; You have undone the cords that bound me.
116:17 I will sacrifice a thank offering to You and invoke the name of the LORD.
116:18 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all His people,
116:19 in the courts of the house of the LORD, in the midst of Jerusalem. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
ALLELUIA!
1 I love the Lord for he has heard
the cry of my appeal;
2 for he turned his ear to me
in the day when I called him.
3 They surrounded me, the snares of death,
with the anguish of the tomb;
they caught me, sorrow and distress.
4 I called on the Lord's name.
O Lord, my God, deliver me!
5 How gracious is the Lord, and just;
our God has compassion.
6 The Lord protects the simple hearts;
I was helpless so he saved me.
7 Turn back, my soul, to your rest
for the Lord has been good;
8 he has kept my soul from death,
(my eyes from tears)
and my feet from stumbling.
9 I will walk in the presence of the Lord
in the land of the living.

10 I trusted, even when I said:
"I am sorely afflicted,"
11 and when I said in my alarm:
"No man can be trusted."
12 How can I repay the Lord
for his goodness to me?

13 The cup of salvation I will raise;
I will call on the Lord's name.
14 My vows to the Lord I will fulfill
before all his people.
15 O precious in the eyes of the Lord
is the death of his faithful.
16 Your servant, Lord, your servant am I;
you have loosened my bonds.
17 A thanksgiving sacrifice I make;
I will call on the Lord's name.
18 My vows to the Lord I will fulfill
before all his people,
19 in the courts of the house of the Lord,
in your midst, O Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham:
116:1 I love Yahweh—because he heareth my voice, my supplications;
116:2 Because he hath bowed down his ear unto me, therefore, throughout my days, will I call.
116:3 The meshes of death encompassed me, and the distresses of hades, came upon me, Peril and sorrow, I found;
116:4 But, on the Name of Yahweh, I called—I beseech thee, Yahweh, deliver my soul.
116:5 Gracious is Yahweh and righteous, and, our God, is full of compassion.
116:6 Yahweh, preserveth the simple, I was brought low, when, to me, he granted salvation.
116:7 Return, O my soul, to thy rest, For, Yahweh, hath dealt bountifully with thee.
116:8 For thou hast rescued my soul from death,—mine eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I will walk to and fro before Yahweh, in the lands of life.
116:10 I believed that I should speak, I, was greatly depressed.
116:11 I, said in mine alarm, All men, are false!
116:12 How shall I give back to Yahweh, All his benefits unto me?
116:13 The cup of salvation, will I lift, and, on the Name of Yahweh, will I call:
116:14 My vows—to Yahweh, will I pay, Might it be in presence of all his people!
116:15 Costly in the eyes of Yahweh, is, death, for his men of lovingkindness.
116:16 I beseech thee, O Yahweh—for, I, am thy servant,—I, am thy servant, the son of thy handmaid, Thou hast loosened my bonds.
116:17 To thee, will I sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and, on the Name of Yahweh, will I call:
116:18 My vows—to Yahweh, will I pay, Might it be in the presence of all his people;—
116:19 In the courts of the house of Yahweh, In the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
116:1 Psalm 116 Thanksgiving to God Who Saves from Death I - I love the LORD, who listened to my voice in supplication,
116:2 Who turned an ear to me on the day I called.
116:3 I was caught by the cords of death; the snares of Sheol had seized me; I felt agony and dread.
116:4 Then I called on the name of the LORD, “O LORD, save my life!”
116:5 II - Gracious is the LORD and righteous; yes, our God is merciful.
116:6 The LORD protects the simple; I was helpless, but he saved me.
116:7 Return, my soul, to your rest; the LORD has been very good to you.
116:8 For my soul has been freed from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I shall walk before the LORD in the land of the living.
116:10 III - I kept faith, even when I said, “I am greatly afflicted!”
116:11 I said in my alarm, “All men are liars!”
116:12 How can I repay the LORD for all the great good done for me?
116:13 I will raise the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD.
116:14 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people.
116:15 Dear in the eyes of the LORD is the death of his devoted.
116:16 LORD, I am your servant, your servant, the child of your maidservant; you have loosed my bonds.
116:17 I will offer a sacrifice of praise and call on the name of the LORD.
116:18 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people,
116:19 In the courts of the house of the LORD, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
116:1 Alleluia. Praise our Lord all ye Gentiles: praise him all ye peoples.
116:2 Because his mercy is confirmed, upon us: and his truth remaineth for ever.

text.NRSV:
117:1 Praise the Lord, all you nations! Extol him, all you peoples!
117:2 For great is his steadfast love toward us, and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
117:1 O praise the LORD, all you heathen; *
      praise him, all you nations.
117:2 For his merciful kindness is ever more and more towards us; *
      and the truth of the LORD endures for ever. Praise the LORD.
text.KJV:
117:1 O praise the Lord, all ye nations: praise him all ye people.
117:2 For his merciful kindnesse is great toward vs: and the trueth of the Lord endureth for euer. Praise ye the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
117:1 Praise the LORD, all you nations; extol Him, all you peoples,
117:2 for great is His steadfast love toward us; the faithfulness of the LORD endures forever. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
ALLELUIA!
1 O praise the Lord, all you nations,
acclaim him all you peoples!
2 Strong is his love for us;
he is faithful for ever.
text.Rotherham:
117:1 Praise Yahweh, all ye nations, Laud him, all ye tribes of men;
117:2 For his lovingkindness, hath prevailed over us, and, the faithfulness of Yahweh, is to times age-abiding. Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
117:1 Psalm 117 The Nations Called to Praise - Praise the LORD, all you nations! Extol him, all you peoples!
117:2 His mercy for us is strong; the faithfulness of the LORD is forever. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
117:1 Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.
117:2 Let Israel now say that he is good: that his mercy is for ever.
117:3 Let the house of Aaron now say: that his mercy is for ever.
117:4 Let them now say which fear our Lord: that his mercy is for ever.
117:5 From tribulation I invocated our Lord: and our Lord heard me in largeness.
117:6 Our Lord is my helper: I will not fear what man can do to me.
117:7 Our Lord is my helper: and I will look over mine enemies.
117:8 It is good to hope in our Lord, rather than to hope in man.
117:9 It is good to hope in our Lord, rather than to hope in Princes.
117:10 All nations have compassed me: and in the name of our Lord am I revenged on them.
117:11 Compassing they have compassed me: and in the name of our Lord I was revenged on them.
117:12 They compassed me as bees, and were inflamed as fire in thorns: and in the name of our Lord I was revenged on them.
117:13 Being thrust I was overturned to fall: and our Lord received me.
117:14 Our Lord is my strength, and my praise: and he is made my salvation.
117:15 The voice of exultation, and of salvation in the tabernacles of the just.
117:16 The right hand of our Lord hath wrought strength: the right hand of our Lord hath exalted me, the right hand of our Lord hath wrought strength.
117:17 Ishall not die, but shall live: and I will tell the works of our Lord.
117:18 Our Lord chastising hath chastised me: and to death he hath not delivered me.
117:19 Open ye the gates of justice to me, being entered into them I will confess to our Lord:
117:20 this is the gate of our Lord, the just shall enter into it.
117:21 I will confess to thee because thou hast heard me: and art become my salvation.
117:22 The stone, which the builders rejected: the same is made into the head of the corner.
117:23 This was done by our Lord: and it is marvelous in our eyes.
117:24 This is the day, which our Lord made: let us rejoice, and be glad therein.
117:25 O Lord save me, o Lord give good success: Blessed be he that cometh in the name of our Lord.
117:26 We have blessed you of the house of our Lord:
117:27 our Lord is God, and he hath given light to us. Appoint a solemn day with thick boughs, even to the horn of the altar.
117:28 Thou art my God, and I will confess to thee: thou art my God, and I will exalt thee. I will confess to thee because thou hast heard me: and art become my salvation.
117:29 Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.

text.NRSV:
118:1 O give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his steadfast love endures forever!
118:2 Let Israel say, 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118:3 Let the house of Aaron say, 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118:4 Let those who fear the Lord say, 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118:5 Out of my distress I called on the Lord; the Lord answered me and set me in a broad place.
118:6 With the Lord on my side I do not fear. What can mortals do to me?
118:7 The Lord is on my side to help me; I shall look in triumph on those who hate me.
118:8 It is better to take refuge in the Lord than to put confidence in mortals.
118:9 It is better to take refuge in the Lord than to put confidence in princes.
118:10 All nations surrounded me; in the name of the Lord I cut them off!
118:11 They surrounded me, surrounded me on every side; in the name of the Lord I cut them off!
118:12 They surrounded me like bees; they blazed like a fire of thorns; in the name of the Lord I cut them off!
118:13 I was pushed hard, so that I was falling, but the Lord helped me.
118:14 The Lord is my strength and my might; he has become my salvation.
118:15 There are glad songs of victory in the tents of the righteous: 'The right hand of the Lord does valiantly;
118:16 the right hand of the Lord is exalted; the right hand of the Lord does valiantly.'
118:17 I shall not die, but I shall live, and recount the deeds of the Lord.
118:18 The Lord has punished me severely, but he did not give me over to death.
118:19 Open to me the gates of righteousness, that I may enter through them and give thanks to the Lord.
118:20 This is the gate of the Lord; the righteous shall enter through it.
118:21 I thank you that you have answered me and have become my salvation.
118:22 The stone that the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone.
118:23 This is the Lord's doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.
118:24 This is the day that the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
118:25 Save us, we beseech you, O Lord! O Lord, we beseech you, give us success!
118:26 Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord. We bless you from the house of the Lord.
118:27 The Lord is God, and he has given us light. Bind the festal procession with branches, up to the horns of the altar.
118:28 You are my God, and I will give thanks to you; you are my God, I will extol you.
118:29 O give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Coverdale:
118:1 O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is gracious; *
      because his mercy endures for ever.
118:2 Let Israel now confess that he is gracious, *
      and that his mercy endures for ever.
118:3 Let the house of Aaron now confess, *
      that his mercy endures for ever.
118:4 Yea, let them now that fear the LORD confess, *
      that his mercy endures for ever.
118:5 I called upon the LORD in trouble; *
      and the LORD heard me at large.
118:6 The LORD is on my side; *
      I will not fear what man does unto me.
118:7 The LORD takes my part with them that help me; *
      therefore shall I see my desire upon mine enemies.
118:8 It is better to trust in the LORD, *
      than to put any confidence in man.
118:9 It is better to trust in the LORD, *
      than to put any confidence in princes.
118:10 All nations compassed me round about; *
      but in the Name of the LORD will I destroy them.
118:11 They kept me in on every side, they kept me in, I say, on every side; *
      but in the Name of the LORD will I destroy them.
118:12 They came about me like bees, and are extinct even as the fire among the thorns; *
      for in the Name of the LORD I will destroy them.
118:13 You have thrust sore at me, that I might fall; *
      but the LORD was my help.
118:14 The LORD is my strength, and my song; *
      and is become my salvation.
118:15 The voice of joy and health is in the dwellings of the righteous; *
      the right hand of the LORD brings mighty things to pass.
118:16 The right hand of the LORD has the pre-eminence; *
      the right hand of the LORD brings mighty things to pass.
118:17 I shall not die, but live, *
      and declare the works of the LORD.
118:18 The LORD has chastened and corrected me; *
      but he has not given me over unto death.
118:19 Open me the gates of righteousness, *
      that I may go into them, and give thanks unto the LORD.
118:20 This is the gate of the LORD; *
      the righteous shall enter into it.
118:21 I will thank you, for you have heard me, *
      and are become my salvation.
118:22 The same stone which the builders refused, *
      is become the head-stone in the corner.
118:23 This is the LORD's doing, *
      and it is marvelous in our eyes.
118:24 This is the day which the LORD has made; *
      we will rejoice and be glad in it.
118:25 Help me now, O LORD; *
      O LORD, send us now prosperity.
118:26 Blessed is he that comes in the Name of the LORD; *
      we have wished you good luck, you that are of the house of the LORD.
118:27 God is the LORD, who has showed us light; *
      bind the sacrifice with cords, yea, even unto the horns of the altar.
118:28 You are my God, and I will thank you; *
      you are my God, and I will praise you.
118:29 O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is gracious; *
      and his mercy endures for ever.
text.KJV:
118:1 O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer.
118:2 Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
118:3 Let the house of Aaron now say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
118:4 Let them now that feare the Lord, say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
118:5 I called vpon the Lord in distresse: the Lord answered me, and set me in a large place.
118:6 The Lord is on my side, I will not feare: What can man doe vnto mee?
118:7 The Lord taketh my part with them that helpe me: therfore shall I see my desire vpon them that hate me.
118:8 It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in man.
118:9 It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in Princes.
118:10 All nations compassed me about: but in the Name of the Lord, will I destroy them.
118:11 They compassed mee about, yea they compassed mee about: but in the Name of the Lord, I will destroy them.
118:12 They compassed mee about like Bees, they are quenched as the fire of thornes: for in the Name of the Lord I wil destroy them.
118:13 Thou hast thrust sore at mee that I might fall: but the Lord helped mee.
118:14 The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation.
118:15 The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
118:16 The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
118:17 I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord.
118:18 The Lord hath chastened me sore: but he hath not giuen me ouer vnto death.
118:19 Open to mee the gates of righteousnesse: I will goe into them, and I will praise the Lord:
118:20 This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter.
118:21 I will praise thee, for thou hast heard mee: and art become my saluation.
118:22 The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner.
118:23 This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes.
118:24 This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will reioyce, and be glad in it.
118:25 Saue now, I beseech thee, O Lord: O Lord, I beseech thee, send now prosperitie.
118:26 Blessed be he that commeth in the Name of the Lord: wee haue blessed you out of the house of the Lord.
118:27 God is the Lord, which hath shewed vs light, bind the sacrifice with cords: euen vnto the horns of the Altar.
118:28 Thou art my God, and I will praise thee: thou art my God, I will exalt thee.
118:29 O giue thanks vnto the Lord, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for euer.
text.JPS1985:
118:1 Praise the LORD, for He is good, His steadfast love is eternal.
118:2 Let Israel declare, “His steadfast love is eternal.”
118:3 Let the house of Aaron declare, “His steadfast love is eternal.”
118:4 Let those who fear the LORD declare, “His steadfast love is eternal.”
118:5 In distress I called on the LORD; the Lord answered me and brought me relief.
118:6 The LORD is on my side, I have no fear; what can man do to me?
118:7 With the LORD on my side as my helper, I will see the downfall of my foes.
118:8 It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in mortals;
118:9 it is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in the great.
118:10 All nations have beset me; by the name of the LORD I will surely cut them down.
118:11 They beset me, they surround me; by the name of the LORD I will surely cut them down.
118:12 They have beset me like bees; they shall be extinguished like burning thorns; by the name of the LORD I will surely cut them down.
118:13 You pressed me hard, I nearly fell; but the LORD helped me.
118:14 The LORD is my strength and might; He has become my deliverance.
118:15 The tents of the victorious resound with joyous shouts of deliverance, “The right hand of the LORD is triumphant!
118:16 The right hand of the LORD is exalted! The right hand of the LORD is triumphant! ”
118:17 I shall not die but live and proclaim the works of the LORD.
118:18 The LORD punished me severely, but did not hand me over to death.
118:19 Open the gates of victory for me that I may enter them and praise the LORD.
118:20 This is the gateway to the LORD — the victorious shall enter through it.
118:21 I praise You, for You have answered me, and have become my deliverance.
118:22 The stone that the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone.
118:23 This is the LORD’s doing; it is marvelous in our sight.
118:24 This is the day that the LORD has made— let us exult and rejoice on it.
118:25 O LORD, deliver us! O LORD, let us prosper!
118:26 May he who enters be blessed in the name of the LORD; we bless you from the House of the LORD.
118:27 The LORD is God; He has given us light; bind the festal offering to the horns of the altar with cords.
118:28 You are my God and I will praise You; You are my God and I will extol You.
118:29 Praise the LORD for He is good, His steadfast love is eternal.
text.Grail1963:
ALLELUIA!
1 Give thanks to the Lord for he is good,
for his love endures for ever.
* * *
2 Let the sons of Israel say:
"His love endures for ever."
3 Let the sons of Aaron say:
"His love endures for ever."
4 Let those who fear the Lord say:
"His love endures for ever."
5 I called to the Lord in my distress;
he answered and freed me.
6 The Lord is at my side; I do not fear.
what can man do against me?
7 The Lord is at my side as my helper;
I shall look down on my foes.
8 It is better to take refuge in the Lord
than to trust in men;

9 it is better to take refuge in the Lord
than to trust in princes.
10 The nations all encompassed me;
in the Lord's name I crushed them.
11 They compassed me, compassed me about;
in the Lord's name I crushed them.
12 They compassed me about like bees;
they blazed like a fire among thorns.
In the Lord's name I crushed them.
13 I was thrust down, thrust down and falling,
but the Lord was my helper.
14 The Lord is my strength and my song;
he was my savior.
15 There are shouts of joy and victory
in the tents of the just.
The Lord's right hand has triumphed;
16 his right hand raised me.
The Lord's right hand has triumphed;
17 I shall not die, I shall live
and recount his deeds.
18 I was punished, I was punished by the Lord,
but not doomed to die.
19 Open to me the gates of holiness:
I will enter and give thanks.
20 This is the Lord's own gate
where the just may enter.
21 I will thank you for you have answered
and you are my savior.
22 The stone which the builders rejected
has become the corner stone.
23 This is the work of the Lord,
a marvel in our eyes.
24 This day was made by the Lord;
we rejoice and are glad.
25 O Lord, grant us salvation;
O Lord, grant success.
26 Blessed in the name of the Lord
is he who comes.
We bless you from the house of the Lord;
27 the Lord God is our light.
Go forward in procession with branches
even to the altar.
28 You are my God, I thank you.
My God, I praise you.
29 Give thanks to the Lord for he is good;
for his love endures for ever.
text.Rotherham:
118:1 Give ye thanks to Yahweh—For he is good, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
118:2 I pray you! let Israel, say, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
118:3 I pray you! let the house of Aaron, say, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
118:4 I pray you! let them who revere Yahweh, say, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
118:5 Out of a strait, called I on Yah, He answered me with enlargement.
118:6 Yahweh, is on my side, I will not fear, What can man, do unto me?
118:7 Yahweh, is on my side, with them who help me, I, therefore shall gaze upon them who hate me.
118:8 It is, better to seek refuge in Yahweh, than to put confidence in man:
118:9 It is, better to seek refuge in Yahweh, than to put confidence in nobles.
118:10 All nations, have compassed me about, In the Name of Yahweh, surely I will make them be circumcised;
118:11 They have compassed me about—yea compassed me about, In the Name of Yahweh, surely I will make them be circumcised;
118:12 They have compassed me about like wax bees, they have blazed up like the fire of thorns, In the Name of Yahweh, surely I will make them be circumcised.
118:13 Thou didst, thrust sore, at me that I might fall, But, Yahweh, hath helped me.
118:14 My might and melody, is Yah, and he hath become mine by salvation.
118:15 The voice of shouting and salvation, is in the tents of the righteous, The right hand of Yahweh, is doing valiantly:
118:16 The right hand of Yahweh, is exalted, The right hand of Yahweh, is doing valiantly.
118:17 I shall not die, but live, That I may recount the doings of Yah.
118:18 Yah, chastened me sore, But, unto death, did not deliver me.
118:19 Open to me the gates of righteousness, I will enter therein, I will give thanks unto Yah.
118:20 This, is the gate for Yahweh, Such as are righteous, shall enter therein.
118:21 I will thank thee, because thou hast answered me, And hast become mine, by salvation.
118:22 A stone the builders refused, hath become the head of the corner:
118:23 From Yahweh, hath this come to pass, The same, is marvellous in our eyes.
118:24 This is the day, which Yahweh hath made, We will exult, and be glad therein.
118:25 Ah now, Yahweh, do save, we beseech thee, Ah now, Yahweh, do send success, we beseech thee!
118:26 Blessed be he that entereth, In the Name of Yahweh, we have blessed you, out of the house of Yahweh.
118:27 Yahweh is GOD, and hath shed on us light,—Bind ye the festal sacrifice with cords, Up to the horns of the altar.
118:28 My GOD, thou art, and I will thank thee,—My Elohim, I will exalt thee.
118:29 Give ye thanks to Yahweh—For he is good, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
text.NABRE:
118:1 Psalm 118 Hymn of Thanksgiving I - Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, his mercy endures forever.
118:2 Let Israel say: his mercy endures forever.
118:3 Let the house of Aaron say, his mercy endures forever.
118:4 Let those who fear the LORD say, his mercy endures forever.
118:5 II - In danger I called on the LORD; the LORD answered me and set me free.
118:6 The LORD is with me; I am not afraid; what can mortals do against me?
118:7 The LORD is with me as my helper; I shall look in triumph on my foes.
118:8 Better to take refuge in the LORD than to put one’s trust in mortals.
118:9 Better to take refuge in the LORD than to put one’s trust in princes.
118:10 III - All the nations surrounded me; in the LORD’s name I cut them off.
118:11 They surrounded me on every side; in the LORD’s name I cut them off.
118:12 They surrounded me like bees; they burned up like fire among thorns; in the LORD’s name I cut them off.
118:13 I was hard pressed and falling, but the LORD came to my help.
118:14 The LORD, my strength and might, has become my savior.
118:15 IV - The joyful shout of deliverance is heard in the tents of the righteous: “The LORD’s right hand works valiantly;
118:16 the LORD’s right hand is raised; the LORD’s right hand works valiantly.”
118:17 I shall not die but live and declare the deeds of the LORD.
118:18 The LORD chastised me harshly, but did not hand me over to death.
118:19 V - Open the gates of righteousness; I will enter and thank the LORD.
118:20 This is the LORD’s own gate, through it the righteous enter.
118:21 I thank you for you answered me; you have been my savior.
118:22 The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
118:23 By the LORD has this been done; it is wonderful in our eyes.
118:24 This is the day the LORD has made; let us rejoice in it and be glad.
118:25 LORD, grant salvation! LORD, grant good fortune!
118:26 VI - Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD. We bless you from the house of the LORD.
118:27 The LORD is God and has enlightened us. Join in procession with leafy branches up to the horns of the altar.
118:28 VII - You are my God, I give you thanks; my God, I offer you praise.
118:29 Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, his mercy endures forever.
text.DRB:
118:1 Alleluia. Aleph. Doctrine. Blessed are the immaculate in the way: which walk in the law of our Lord.
118:2 Blessed are they that search his testimonies: that seek after him with all their heart.
118:3 For they that work iniquity, have not walked in his ways.
118:4 Thou hast very much commanded thy commandments to be kept.
118:5 Would God my ways might be directed, to keep thy justifications.
118:6 Then shall I not be confounded, when I shall look throughly in all thy commandments.
118:7 I will confess to thee in direction of heart: in that I have learned the judgments of thy justice.
118:8 I will keep thy justifications: forsake me not wholly.
118:9 Beth. House. Wherein doth a young man correct his way? In keeping thy words.
118:10 With my whole heart I have sought after thee: repel me not from thy commandments.
118:11 In my heart I have hid thy words: that I may not sin to thee.
118:12 Blessed art thou, o Lord: teach me thy justifications.
118:13 In my lips I have pronounced all the judgments of thy mouth.
118:14 I am delighted in the way of thy testimonies, as in all riches.
118:15 I will be exercised in thy commandments: and I will consider thy ways.
118:16 I will meditate in thy justifications: I will not forget thy words.
118:17 Gimel. Fullness. Render to thy servant, quicken me: and I shall keep thy words.
118:18 Reveal mine eyes: and I shall consider the marvelous things of thy law.
118:19 I am a sojourner in the land, hide not thy commandments from me.
118:20 My soul hath coveted to desire thy justifications, at all time.
118:21 Thou hast rebuked the proud: cursed are they that decline from thy commandments.
118:22 Take from me reproach, and contempt: because I have sought after thy testimonies.
118:23 For Princes sat, and they spake against me: but thy servant was exercised in thy justifications.
118:24 For both thy testimonies are my meditation: and thy justifications my counsel.
118:25 Daleth. Of Tables. My soul hath cleaved to the pavement: quicken me according to thy word.
118:26 I have uttered my ways, and thou hast heard me: teach me thy justifications.
118:27 Instruct me the way of thy justifications: and I shall be exercised in thy marvelous works.
118:28 My soul hath slumbered for tediousness: confirm me in thy words.
118:29 Remove from me the way of iniquity: and according to thy law, have mercy on me.
118:30 I have chosen the way of truth: I have not forgotten thy judgments.
118:31 I have cleaved to thy testimonies, o Lord: do not confound me.
118:32 I ran the way of thy commandments: when thou didst dilate my heart.
118:33 He. This thing. Set me a law, o Lord, the way of thy justifications: and I will seek after it always.
118:34 Give me understanding, and I will search thy law: and I will keep it with my whole heart.
118:35 Conduct me into the path of thy commandments: because I would it.
118:36 Incline my heart into thy testimonies: and not into avarice.
118:37 Turn away mine eyes that they see not vanity: in thy way quicken me.
118:38 Establish thy word to thy servant, in thy fear.
118:39 Take away my reproach, which I have feared: because thy judgments are pleasant.
118:40 Behold I have coveted thy commandments: in thine equity quicken me.
118:41 Vau. And. And let thy mercy come upon me, o Lord: thy salvation according to thy word.
118:42 And I shall answer a word to them that upbraid me: because I have hoped in thy words.
118:43 And take not away out of my mouth the word of truth utterly: because I have much hoped in thy judgments.
118:44 And I will keep thy law always: for ever, and for ever and ever.
118:45 And I walked in largeness: because I have sought after thy commandments.
118:46 And I spake of thy testimonies in the sight of kings: and was not confounded.
118:47 And I meditated in thy commandments, which I loved.
118:48 And I have lifted up my hands to thy commandments, which I loved: and I was exercised in thy justifications.
118:49 Zain. This. Be mindful of thy word to thy servant, wherein thou hast given me hope.
118:50 This hath comforted me in my humiliation: because thy word hath quickened me.
118:51 The proud did unjustly exceedingly: but I declined not from thy law.
118:52 I have been mindful of thy judgments from everlasting, o Lord: and was comforted.
118:53 Fainting possessed me, because of sinners forsaking thy Law.
118:54 Thy justifications were song by me, in the place of my peregrination.
118:55 I have been mindful in the night of thy name, o Lord: and have kept thy law.
118:56 This was done to me: because I sought after thy justifications.
118:57 Heth. Life. My portion, o Lord, I said to keep thy law.
118:58 I besought thy face, with all my heart: have mercy on me according to thy word.
118:59 I thought upon my ways: and converted my feet unto thy testimonies.
118:60 I am prepared, and am not troubled: to keep thy commandments.
118:61 The cords of sinners have wrapped me round about: and I have not forgotten thy law.
118:62 At mid-night I rose to confess to thee, for the judgments of thy justification.
118:63 I am partaker of all that fear thee: and that keep thy commandments.
118:64 The earth, o Lord, is full of thy mercy: teach me thy justifications.
118:65 Teth. Good. Thou hast done bounty with thy servant, o Lord: according to thy word.
118:66 Teach me goodness, and discipline, and knowledge: because I have believed thy commandments.
118:67 Before I was humbled I offended: therefore have I kept thy word.
118:68 Thou art good: and in thy goodness teach me thy justifications.
118:69 The iniquity of the proud is multiplied upon me: but I in all my heart will search thy commandments.
118:70 Their heart is curded together as milk: but I have meditated thy law.
118:71 It is good for me that thou hast humbled me: that I may learn thy justifications.
118:72 The law of thy mouth is good unto me, above thousands of gold and silver.
118:73 Iod. Beginning. Thy hands have made me, and formed me: give me understanding, and I will learn thy commandments.
118:74 They that fear thee shall see me, & shall rejoice: because I have much hoped in thy words.
118:75 I know, o Lord, that thy judgments are equity: and in thy truth thou hast humbled me.
118:76 Let thy mercy be done to comfort me, according to thy word unto thy servant.
118:77 Let thy commiserations come to me, and I shall live: because thy law is my meditation.
118:78 Let the proud be confounded, because they have done unjustly toward me: but I will be exercised in thy commandments.
118:79 Let them be converted to me that fear thee: and that know thy testimonies.
118:80 Let my heart be made immaculate in thy justifications, that I be not confounded.
118:81 Caph. Hand, or Palm of the hand. My soul hath fainted for thy salvation: and I have much hoped in thy word.
118:82 Mine eyes have failed for thy word, saying: When wilt thou comfort me?
118:83 Because I am made as a bottle in the hoar frost: I have not forgotten thy justifications.
118:84 How many are the days of thy servant: when wilt thou do judgment on them that persecute me?
118:85 The unjust have told me fables: but not as thy law.
118:86 All thy commandments are truth: they have unjustly persecuted me, help me.
118:87 They have well near made end of me in the earth: but I have not forsaken thy commandments.
118:88 According to thy mercy quicken me: and I shall keep the testimonies of thy mouth.
118:89 Lamed. Discipline. For ever Lord thy word is permanent in heaven.
118:90 Thy truth in generation and generation: thou hast founded the earth, and it is permanent.
118:91 By thy ordinance the day continueth: because all things serve thee.
118:92 But that thy law is my meditation: I had then perhaps perished in my humiliation.
118:93 I will not forget thy justifications for ever: because in them thou hast quickened me.
118:94 I am thine, save me: because I have sought out thy justifications.
118:95 Sinners have expected me to destroy me: I understood thy testimonies.
118:96 Of all consummation I have seen the end: thy commandment is exceeding large.
118:97 Mem. Of them. How have I loved thy law, o Lord! All the day it is my meditation.
118:98 Above mine enemies thou hast made me wise by thy commandment: because it is to me for ever.
118:99 Above all that taught me have I understood: because thy testimonies are my meditation.
118:100 Above ancients have I understood: because I have sought thy commandments.
118:101 I have stayed my feet from all evil way: that I may keep thy words.
118:102 I have not declined from thy judgments: because thou hast set me a law.
118:103 How sweet are thy words to my jaws. More than honey to my mouth!
118:104 By thy commandments I have understood: therefore have I hated all the way of iniquity.
118:105 Nun. Everlasting. Thy word is a lamp to my feet, and a light to my paths.
118:106 I sware, and have determined to keep the judgments of thy justice.
118:107 I am humbled exceedingly, o Lord: quicken me according to thy word.
118:108 The voluntaries of my mouth make acceptable, o Lord: and teach me thy judgments.
118:109 My soul is in my hands always: and I have not forgotten thy law.
118:110 Sinners laid a snare for me: and I have not erred from thy commandments.
118:111 For inheritance I have purchased thy testimonies for ever: because they are the joy of my heart.
118:112 I have inclined my heart to do thy justifications for ever, for reward.
118:113 Samech. Help. I have hated the unjust: and I have loved thy law.
118:114 Thou art my helper, and protector: and upon thy word I have much hoped.
118:115 Depart from me ye malignant: and I will search the commandments of my God.
118:116 Receive me according to thy word, and I shall live: and confound me not of mine expectation.
118:117 Help me, and I shall be saved: and I will meditate in thy justifications always.
118:118 Thou hast despised all that revolt from thy judgments: because their cogitation is unjust.
118:119 All the sinners of the earth I have reputed prevaricators: therefore have I loved thy testimonies.
118:120 Pierce my flesh with thy fear: for I am afraid of thy judgments.
118:121 Ain. A fountain, or an eye. I have done judgment and justice: deliver me not to them that calumniate me.
118:122 Receive thy servant unto good: let not the proud calumniate me.
118:123 Mine eyes have failed after thy salvation: and for the word of thy justice.
118:124 Do with thy servant according to thy mercy: and teach me thy justifications.
118:125 I am thy servant: give me understanding, that I may know thy testimonies.
118:126 It is time to do, o Lord: they have dissipated thy law.
118:127 Therefore have I loved thy commandments, above gold and topazius.
118:128 Therefore was I directed to all thy commandments: all wicked way I have hated.
118:129 Phe. Mouth. Thy testimonies are marvelous: therefore hath my soul searched them.
118:130 The declaration of thy words doth illuminate: and giveth understanding to little ones.
118:131 I opened my mouth, and drew breath: because I desired thy commandments.
118:132 Look upon me, and have mercy on me, according to the judgment of them that love thy name.
118:133 Direct my steps according to thy word: and let not any injustice have domination over me.
118:134 Redeem me from the calumnies of men: that I may keep thy commandments.
118:135 Illuminate thy face upon thy servant: and teach me thy justifications.
118:136 Mine eyes have gushed forth issues of waters: because they have not kept thy law.
118:137 Sade. Justice. Thou art just, o Lord: and thy judgment is right.
118:138 Thou hast commanded justice thy testimonies: and thy verity exceedingly.
118:139 My zeal hath made me to pine away: because mine enemies have forgotten thy words.
118:140 Thy word is fired exceedingly: and thy servant hath loved it.
118:141 I am a youngman, and contemned: I have not forgotten thy justifications.
118:142 Thy justice is justice for ever: and thy law is verity.
118:143 Tribulation and distress have found me: thy commandments are my meditation.
118:144 Thy testimonies are equity for ever give me understanding, and I shall live.
118:145 Coph. Vocation. I have cried in my whole heart, hear me, o Lord: I will seek after thy justifications.
118:146 I have cried to thee, save me: that I may keep thy commandments.
118:147 I have prevented in maturity, and have cried: because I hoped much in thy words.
118:148 Mine eyes have prevented early unto thee: that I might meditate thy words.
118:149 Hear my voice according to thy mercy, o Lord: and according to thy judgment quicken me.
118:150 They that persecute me have approached to iniquity: but from thy law they are made far off.
118:151 Thou art nigh, o Lord: and all thy ways are truth.
118:152 From the beginning I knew of thy testimonies: that thou hast founded them for ever.
118:153 Res. Head. See my humiliation, and deliver me: because I have not forgotten thy law.
118:154 Judge my judgment, & redeem me: for thy word quicken thou me.
118:155 Salvation is far from sinners: because they have not sought after thy justifications.
118:156 Thy mercies are many, o Lord: according to thy judgment quicken me.
118:157 There are many that persecute me, and afflict me: I have not declined from thy testimonies.
118:158 Isaw the prevaricators, and I pined away: because they kept not thy words.
118:159 See that I have loved thy commandments, o Lord: in thy mercy quicken me.
118:160 The beginning of thy words is truth: all the judgments of thy justice are for ever.
118:161 Sin. Tooth. Princes have persecuted me without cause: and my heart hath been afraid of thy words.
118:162 I will rejoice at thy words: as he that findeth many spoils.
118:163 I have heard iniquity, and abhorred it: but thy law I have loved.
118:164 Seven times in the day I have said praise to thee, for the judgments of thy justice.
118:165 There is much peace to them that love thy law: & there is no scandal to them.
118:166 I expected thy salvation, o Lord: and have loved thy commandments.
118:167 My soul hath kept thy testimonies: & hath loved them exceedingly.
118:168 I have kept thy commandments, and thy testimonies: because all my ways are in thy sight.
118:169 Tau. Sign. Let my petition approach in thy sight, o Lord: according to thy word give me understanding.
