happy the people whose God is the Lord.
text.Rotherham:
144:1 Blessed be Yahweh, my Rock, who teacheth my hands to war, my fingers to fight:
144:2 My lovingkindness and my stronghold, my high tower and my deliverer—mine! My buckler, and he in whom I have sought refuge,—He that subdueth my people under me.
144:3 O Yahweh! what is the earthborn, And yet thou hast acknowledged him,—the son of a mortal, And yet thou hast taken account of him:
144:4 The earthborn, resembleth, a vapour, his days, are like a passing shadow.
144:5 O Yahweh! bow thy heavens and come down, Touch the mountains, that they smoke:
144:6 Flash forth lightning, that thou mayest scatter them, Send out thine arrows, that thou mayest confound them:
144:7 Put forth thy hands from on high:—Snatch me away and rescue me out of mighty waters, out of the hand of the sons of the alien,
144:8 Whose mouth, hath spoken deceit, and, whose right hand, is a right hand of falsehood.
144:9 O God! a new song, will I sing unto thee,—On a harp of ten strings, will I make music to thee:
144:10 Who giveth victory unto kings—Who snatcheth away David his servant, from the calamitous sword.
144:11 Snatch me away and rescue me out of he hand of the sons of the alien,—whose mouth hath spoken deceit, and, whose right hand, is a right hand of falsehood:—
144:12 That, our sons, may be like plants well grown while yet young,—Our daughters, like corner pillars,—carved, in the construction of a palace:
144:13 Our garners, full, pouring out from one kind to another; Our flocks, multiplying by thousands—by myriads, in our open fields:
144:14 Our oxen, well-laden; no breaking in and no departing,—and no loud lament in our places of concourse:—
144:15 How happy the people that is in such a case! How happy the people that hath Yahweh for its God!
text.NABRE:
144:1 Psalm 144 A Prayer for Victory and Prosperity - Of David. I - Blessed be the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for battle, my fingers for war;
144:2 My safeguard and my fortress, my stronghold, my deliverer, My shield, in whom I take refuge, who subdues peoples under me.
144:3 II - LORD, what is man that you take notice of him; the son of man, that you think of him?
144:4 Man is but a breath, his days are like a passing shadow.
144:5 LORD, incline your heavens and come down; touch the mountains and make them smoke.
144:6 Flash forth lightning and scatter my foes; shoot your arrows and rout them.
144:7 Reach out your hand from on high; deliver me from the many waters; rescue me from the hands of foreign foes.
144:8 Their mouths speak untruth; their right hands are raised in lying oaths.
144:9 O God, a new song I will sing to you; on a ten-stringed lyre I will play for you.
144:10 You give victory to kings; you delivered David your servant. From the menacing sword
144:11 deliver me; rescue me from the hands of foreign foes. Their mouths speak untruth; their right hands are raised in lying oaths.
144:12 III - May our sons be like plants well nurtured from their youth, Our daughters, like carved columns, shapely as those of the temple.
144:13 May our barns be full with every kind of store. May our sheep increase by thousands, by tens of thousands in our fields; may our oxen be well fattened.
144:14 May there be no breach in the walls, no exile, no outcry in our streets.
144:15 Blessed the people so fortunate; blessed the people whose God is the LORD.
text.DRB:
144:1 Praising, to David himself. I will exalt thee my God the King: and I will bless thy name for ever and for ever.
144:2 Every day will I bless thee: and will praise thy name for ever, and for ever and ever.
144:3 Great is our Lord and exceeding laudable, and of his greatness there is no end.
144:4 Generation and generation shall praise thy works: and they shall pronounce thy power.
144:5 They shall speak the magnificence of the glory of thy holiness: and shall tell thy marvelous works.
144:6 And they shall tell the force of thy terrible things: and shall declare thy greatness:
144:7 They shall utter the memory of the aboundance of thy sweetness: and in thy justice they shall rejoice.
144:8 Our Lord is pitiful and merciful: patient and very merciful.
144:9 Our Lord is sweet to all: and his commiserations are over all his works.
144:10 Let all thy works, o Lord, confess to thee: and let thy Saints bless thee.
144:11 They shall tell the glory of thy kingdom: and shall speak thy might.
144:12 That they may make thy might known to the children of men: and the glory of the magnificence of thy kingdom.
144:13 Thy kingdom is a kingdom of all worlds: and thy dominion in all generation and generation. Our Lord is faithful in all his words, and holy in all his works.
144:14 Our Lord lifteth up all that fall: and setteth up all that are bruised.
144:15 The eyes of all hope in thee, o Lord: and thou givest their meat in time convenient.
144:16 Thou openest thy hand: and fillest every living creature with blessing.
144:17 Our Lord is just in all his ways: and holy in all his works.
144:18 Our Lord is near to all that invocate him: to all that invocate him in truth.
144:19 He will do the will of them that fear him, and will hear their prayer, and save them.
144:20 Our Lord keepeth all that love him: and he will destroy all sinners.
144:21 My mouth shall speak the praise of our Lord: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever, and for ever and ever.

text.NRSV:
145:1 I will extol you, my God and King, and bless your name forever and ever.
145:2 Every day I will bless you, and praise your name forever and ever.
145:3 Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; his greatness is unsearchable.
145:4 One generation shall laud your works to another, and shall declare your mighty acts.
145:5 On the glorious splendor of your majesty, and on your wondrous works, I will meditate.
145:6 The might of your awesome deeds shall be proclaimed, and I will declare your greatness.
145:7 They shall celebrate the fame of your abundant goodness, and shall sing aloud of your righteousness.
145:8 The Lord is gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.
145:9 The Lord is good to all, and his compassion is over all that he has made.
145:10 All your works shall give thanks to you, O Lord, and all your faithful shall bless you.
145:11 They shall speak of the glory of your kingdom, and tell of your power,
145:12 to make known to all people your mighty deeds, and the glorious splendor of your kingdom.
145:13 Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and your dominion endures throughout all generations. The Lord is faithful in all his words, and gracious in all his deeds.
145:14 The Lord upholds all who are falling, and raises up all who are bowed down.
145:15 The eyes of all look to you, and you give them their food in due season.
145:16 You open your hand, satisfying the desire of every living thing.
145:17 The Lord is just in all his ways, and kind in all his doings.
145:18 The Lord is near to all who call on him, to all who call on him in truth.
145:19 He fulfills the desire of all who fear him; he also hears their cry, and saves them.
145:20 The Lord watches over all who love him, but all the wicked he will destroy.
145:21 My mouth will speak the praise of the Lord, and all flesh will bless his holy name forever and ever.
text.Coverdale:
145:1 I will magnify you, O God, my King; *
      and I will praise your Name for ever and ever.
145:2 Every day will I give thanks unto you, *
      and praise your Name for ever and ever.
145:3 Great is the LORD, and marvellous, worthy to be praised; *
      there is no end of his greatness.
145:4 One generation shall praise your works unto another, *
      and declare your power.
145:5 As for me, I will be talking of your worship, *
      your glory, your praise, and wondrous works;
145:6 So that men shall speak of the might of your marvelous acts; *
      and I will also tell of your greatness.
145:7 The memorial of your abundant kindness shall be showed; *
      and men shall sing of your righteousness.
145:8 The LORD is gracious, and merciful, *
      long-suffering, and of great goodness.
145:9 The LORD is loving unto every man, *
      and his mercy is over all his works.
145:10 All your works praise you, O LORD; *
      and your saints give thanks unto you.
145:11 They show the glory of your kingdom, *
      and talk of your power;
145:12 That your power, your glory, and mightiness of your kingdom, *
      might be known unto men.
145:13 Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, *
      and your dominion endures throughout all ages.
145:14 The LORD upholds all such as fall, *
      and lifts up all those who are down.
145:15 The eyes of all wait upon you, O Lord; *
      and you give them their meat in due season.
145:16 You open your hand, *
      and fillest all things living with plenteousness.
145:17 The LORD is righteous in all his ways, *
      and holy in all his works.
145:18 The LORD is nigh unto all them that call upon him; *
      yea, all such as call upon him faithfully.
145:19 He will fulfill the desire of them that fear him; *
      he also will hear their cry, and will help them.
145:20 The LORD preserves all them that love him; *
      but scatters abroad all the ungodly.
145:21 My mouth shall speak the praise of the LORD; *
      and let all flesh give thanks unto his holy Name for ever and ever.
text.KJV:
145:1 I will extoll thee, my God, O King: and I will blesse thy name for euer and euer.
145:2 Euery day wil I blesse thee: and I will praise thy Name for euer and euer.
145:3 Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised: and his greatnes is vnsearchable.
145:4 One generation shall praise thy works to another, and shal declare thy mightie actes.
145:5 I will speake of the glorious honour of thy maiestie: and of thy wonderous workes.
145:6 And men shall speake of the might of thy terrible acts: and I wil declare thy greatnesse.
145:7 They shall abundantly vtter the memory of thy great goodnesse: and shall sing of thy righteousnesse.
145:8 The Lord is gracious and full of compassion: slow to anger, and of great mercy.
145:9 The Lord is good to all: and his tender mercies are ouer all his workes.
145:10 All thy workes shall praise thee, O Lord: and thy Saints shal blesse thee.
145:11 They shall speake of the glory of thy kingdome: and talke of thy power.
145:12 To make knowen to the sonnes of men his mightie actes: and the glorious Maiestie of his kingdome.
145:13 Thy kingdome is an euerlasting kingdome: and thy dominion endureth throughout all generations.
145:14 The Lord vpholdeth all that fall: and raiseth vp all those that bee bowed downe.
145:15 The eyes of all waite vpon thee: and thou giuest them their meat in due season.
145:16 Thou openest thine hand: and satisfiest the desire of euery liuing thing.
145:17 The Lord is righteous in all his wayes: and holy in all his works.
145:18 The Lord is nigh vnto all them that call vpon him: to all that call vpon him in trueth.
145:19 Hee will fulfill the desire of them that feare him: he also will heare their cry, and will saue them.
145:20 The Lord preserueth all them that loue him: but all the wicked will he destroy.
145:21 My mouth shall speake the praise of the Lord: and let all flesh blesse his holy Name for euer and euer.
text.JPS1985:
145:1 A song of praise. Of David. I will extol You, my God and king, and bless Your name forever and ever.
145:2 Every day will I bless You and praise Your name forever and ever.
145:3 Great is the LORD and much acclaimed; His greatness cannot be fathomed.
145:4 One generation shall laud Your works to another and declare Your mighty acts.
145:5 The glorious majesty of Your splendor and Your wondrous acts will I recite.
145:6 Men shall talk of the might of Your awesome deeds, and I will recount Your greatness.
145:7 They shall celebrate Your abundant goodness, and sing joyously of Your beneficence.
145:8 The LORD is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in kindness.
145:9 The LORD is good to all, and His mercy is upon all His works.
145:10 All Your works shall praise You, O LORD, and Your faithful ones shall bless You.
145:11 They shall talk of the majesty of Your kingship, and speak of Your might,
145:12 to make His mighty acts known among men and the majestic glory of His kingship.
145:13 Your kingship is an eternal kingship; Your dominion is for all generations.
145:14 The LORD supports all who stumble, and makes all who are bent stand straight.
145:15 The eyes of all look to You expectantly, and You give them their food when it is due.
145:16 You give it openhandedly, feeding every creature to its heart’s content.
145:17 The LORD is beneficent in all His ways and faithful in all His works.
145:18 The LORD is near to all who call Him, to all who call Him with sincerity.
145:19 He fulfills the wishes of those who fear Him; He hears their cry and delivers them.
145:20 The LORD watches over all who love Him, but all the wicked He will destroy.
145:21 My mouth shall utter the praise of the LORD, and all creatures shall bless His holy name forever and ever.
text.Grail1963:
1 I will give you glory, O God my king,
I will bless your name for ever.
2 I will bless you day after day
and praise your name for ever.
3 The Lord is great, highly to be praised,
his greatness cannot be measured.
4 Age to age shall proclaim your works,
shall declare your mighty deeds,
5 shall speak of your splendor and glory,
tell the tale of your wonderful works.
6 They will speak of your terrible deeds,
recount your greatness and might.
7 They will recall your abundant goodness;
age to age shall ring out your justice.
8 The Lord is kind and full of compassion,
slow to anger, abounding in love.
9 How good is the Lord to all,
compassionate to all his creatures.
10 All your creatures shall thank you, O Lord,
and your friends shall repeat their blessing.
11 They shall speak of the glory of your reign
and declare your might, O God,
12 to make known to all your mighty deeds
and the glorious splendor of your reign.
13 Yours is an everlasting kingdom;
your rule lasts from age to age.
The Lord is faithful in all his words
and loving in all his deeds.
14 The Lord supports all who fall
and raise up all who are bowed down.
15 The eyes of all creatures look to you
and you give them their food in due time.
16 You open wide your hand,
grant the desires of all who live.
17 The Lord is just in all his ways

and loving in all his deeds.
18 He is close to all who call him,
who call on him from their hearts.
19 He grants the desires of those who fear him,
he hears their cry and he saves them.
20 The Lord protects all who love him ;
the wicked he will utterly destroy.
21 Let me speak the praise of the Lord,
let all peoples bless his holy name
for ever, for ages unending.
text.Rotherham:
145:1 I will extol thee, my God, O King, and will bless thy Name, to times age-abiding and beyond:
145:2 Every day, will I bless thee, and praise thy Name, to times age-abiding and beyond.
145:3 Great is Yahweh—and worthy to be heartily praised, And, his greatness, is unsearchable.
145:4 Generation unto generation, shall celebrate thy works, and, thy mighty deeds, shall they tell:
145:5 The splendour of the glory of thy majesty, shall they speak, and, thy wonders, will I utter.
145:6 And, the might of thy terrible acts, shall men speak, and, as for thy greatness, I will recount it.
145:7 The memory of thy great goodness, shall men pour forth, and, thy righteousness, shall they shout aloud.
145:8 Gracious and compassionate, is Yahweh,—slow to anger, and of great lovingkindness.
145:9 Good is Yahweh to all, and, his tender compassions, are over all his works.
145:10 All thy works, O Yahweh, will give thanks unto thee,—and, thy men of lovingkindness, will bless thee:
145:11 The glory of thy kingdom, will they tell,—and, thy power, will they speak.
145:12 To make known to the sons of men, his mighty deeds, and the splendid glory of his kingdom.
145:13 Thy kingdom, is a kingdom of all ages,—and, thy dominion, is over generation after generation.
145:14 Yahweh is ready to uphold all who are falling, and to raise all who are laid prostrate.
145:15 The eyes of all, for thee, do wait, and, thou, givest them their food in its season.
145:16 Thou, openest thy hand, and fillest every living thing with gladness.
145:17 Righteous is Yahweh in all his ways, and kind in all his works.
145:18 Near is Yahweh to all who call upon him,—to all them who call upon him in faithfulness.
145:19 The desire of them who revere him, will he fulfil, and, their cry, will he hear, and will save them.
145:20 Yahweh preserveth all who love him, but, all the lawless, will he destroy.
145:21 The praise of Yahweh, my mouth shall speak, That all flesh may bless his holy Name, Unto times age-abiding and beyond.
text.NABRE:
145:1 Psalm 145 The Greatness and Goodness of God - Praise. Of David. I will extol you, my God and king; I will bless your name forever and ever.
145:2 Every day I will bless you; I will praise your name forever and ever.
145:3 Great is the LORD and worthy of much praise, whose grandeur is beyond understanding.
145:4 One generation praises your deeds to the next and proclaims your mighty works.
145:5 They speak of the splendor of your majestic glory, tell of your wonderful deeds.
145:6 They speak of the power of your awesome acts and recount your great deeds.
145:7 They celebrate your abounding goodness and joyfully sing of your justice.
145:8 The LORD is gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in mercy.
145:9 The LORD is good to all, compassionate toward all your works.
145:10 All your works give you thanks, LORD and your faithful bless you.
145:11 They speak of the glory of your reign and tell of your mighty works,
145:12 Making known to the sons of men your mighty acts, the majestic glory of your rule.
145:13 Your reign is a reign for all ages, your dominion for all generations. The LORD is trustworthy in all his words, and loving in all his works.
145:14 The LORD supports all who are falling and raises up all who are bowed down.
145:15 The eyes of all look hopefully to you; you give them their food in due season.
145:16 You open wide your hand and satisfy the desire of every living thing.
145:17 The LORD is just in all his ways, merciful in all his works.
145:18 The LORD is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.
145:19 He fulfills the desire of those who fear him; he hears their cry and saves them.
145:20 The LORD watches over all who love him, but all the wicked he destroys.
145:21 My mouth will speak the praises of the LORD; all flesh will bless his holy name forever and ever.
text.DRB:
145:1 Alleluia, of Aggæus and Zacharie.
145:2 My soul praise thou our Lord, I will praise our Lord in my life: I will sing to my God as long as I shall be. Put not confidence in Princes:
145:3 in the sons of men: in whom there is no salvation.
145:4 His spirit shall go forth, and he shall return into his earth: in that day all their cogitations shall perish.
145:5 Blessed is he, whose helper is the God of Jacob, his hope in our Lord his God:
145:6 who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all things that are in them.
145:7 Which keepeth truth for ever, doth judgment for them that suffer wrong: giveth food to the hungry. Our Lord looseth the fettered:
145:8 our Lord illuminateth the blind. Our Lord lifteth up the bruised, our Lord loveth the just.
145:9 Our Lord keepeth strangers, the pupil and widow he will receive: and the ways of sinners he will destroy.
145:10 Our Lord will reign for ever thy God, o Sion in generation & generation.

text.NRSV:
146:1 Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord, O my soul!
146:2 I will praise the Lord as long as I live; I will sing praises to my God all my life long.
146:3 Do not put your trust in princes, in mortals, in whom there is no help.
146:4 When their breath departs, they return to the earth; on that very day their plans perish.
146:5 Happy are those whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the Lord their God,
146:6 who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them; who keeps faith forever;
146:7 who executes justice for the oppressed; who gives food to the hungry. The Lord sets the prisoners free;
146:8 the Lord opens the eyes of the blind. The Lord lifts up those who are bowed down; the Lord loves the righteous.
146:9 The Lord watches over the strangers; he upholds the orphan and the widow, but the way of the wicked he brings to ruin.
146:10 The Lord will reign forever, your God, O Zion, for all generations. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
146:1 Praise the LORD, O my soul; while I live will I praise the LORD; *
      yea, as long as I have any being, I will sing praises unto my God.
146:2 O put not your trust in princes, nor in any child of man; *
      for there is no help in them.
146:3 For when the breath of man goes forth he shall turn again to his earth, *
      and then all his thoughts perish.
146:4 Blessed is he that has the God of Jacob for his help, *
      and whose hope is in the LORD his God;
146:5 Who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that therein is; *
      who keeps his promise for ever;
146:6 Who helps them to right that suffer wrong; *
      who feeds the hungry.
146:7 The LORD looses men out of prison; *
      the LORD gives sight to the blind.
146:8 The LORD helps them that are fallen; *
      the LORD cares for the righteous.
146:9 The LORD cares for the strangers; he defends the fatherless and widow; *
      as for the way of the ungodly, he turns it upside down.
146:10 The LORD your God, O Zion, shall be King for evermore, *
      and throughout all generations.
text.KJV:
146:1 Praise yee the Lord: prayse the Lord, O my soule.
146:2 While I liue, will I praise the Lord: I will sing praises vnto my God, while I haue any being.
146:3 Put not your trust in Princes: nor in the sonne of man, in whom there is no helpe.
146:4 His breath goeth foorth, he returneth to his earth: in that very day his thoughts perish.
146:5 Happy is he that hath the God of Iacob for his helpe: whose hope is in the Lord his God:
146:6 Which made heauen and earth, the Sea, and all that therein is: which keepeth trueth for euer:
146:7 Which executeth iudgement for the oppressed, which giueth food to the hungry: the Lord looseth the prisoners.
146:8 The Lord openeth the eyes of the blinde, the Lord raiseth them that are bowed downe: the Lord loueth the righteous.
146:9 The Lord preserueth the strangers, he relieueth the fatherlesse and widow: but the way of the wicked he turneth vpside downe.
146:10 The Lord shall reigne for euer, euen thy God, O Zion, vnto all generations: Praise ye the Lord.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
My soul, give praise to the Lord;
2 I will praise the Lord all my days,
make music to my God while I live.
3 Put no trust in princes,
In mortal men in whom there is no help.
4 Take their breath, they return to clay
and their plans that day come to nothing.
5 He is happy who is helped by Jacob's God,
whose hope is in the Lord his God,
6 who alone made heaven and earth,
the seas and all they contain.
It is the Lord who keeps faith for ever,
7 who is just to those who are oppressed.
It is he who gives bread to the hungry,
the Lord, who sets prisoners free,
8 the Lord who gives sight to the blind,
who raises up those who are bowed down,
9 the Lord, who protects the stranger
and upholds the widow and orphan.
8c It is the Lord who loves the just
9c but thwarts the path of the wicked.
10 The Lord will reign for ever,
Zion's God, from age to age.
ALLELUIA!
text.Rotherham:
146:1 Praise ye Yah, Praise, O my soul, Yahweh.
146:2 I will praise Yahweh while I live! I will make melody to my God while I continue!
146:3 Do not ye trust in nobles, in a son of man who hath no deliverance:
146:4 His spirit, goeth forth, he returneth to his ground, In that very day, his thoughts perish.
146:5 How happy is he that hath the GOD of Jacob as his help, whose hope, is on Yahweh his God:—
146:6 Who made The heavens and the earth, The sea and all that is therein, Who keepeth faithfulness to times age-abiding:
146:7 Who executeth justice for the oppressed, who giveth food to the famishing, Yahweh, who liberateth prisoners;
146:8 Yahweh, who opened [the eyes of] the blind, Yahweh, who raiseth the prostrate, Yahweh, who loveth the righteous;
146:9 Yahweh, who preserveth sojourners, The fatherless and widows, he relieveth,—but, the way of the lawless, he overturneth.
146:10 Yahweh, will reign, to times age-abiding, Thy God, O Zion, to generation after generation. Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
146:1 Psalm 146 Trust in God the Creator and Redeemer - Hallelujah!
146:2 Praise the LORD, my soul; I will praise the LORD all my life, sing praise to my God while I live.
146:3 I - Put no trust in princes, in children of Adam powerless to save.
146:4 Who breathing his last, returns to the earth; that day all his planning comes to nothing.
146:5 II - Blessed the one whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD, his God,
146:6 The maker of heaven and earth, the seas and all that is in them, Who keeps faith forever,
146:7 secures justice for the oppressed, who gives bread to the hungry. The LORD sets prisoners free;
146:8 the LORD gives sight to the blind. The LORD raises up those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous.
146:9 The LORD protects the resident alien, comes to the aid of the orphan and the widow, but thwarts the way of the wicked.
146:10 The LORD shall reign forever, your God, Zion, through all generations! Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
146:1 Alleluia. Praise ye our Lord because Psalm is good: to our God let there be pleasant and comely praise.
146:2 Our Lord building up Jerusalem: will gather together the dispersions of Israel.
146:3 Who healeth the contrite of heart: and bindeth up their sores.
146:4 Who numbereth the multitude of stars: & giveth names to them all.
146:5 Great is our Lord, and great is his strength: and of his wisdom there is no number.
146:6 Our Lord receiving the meek: & humbling sinners even to the ground.
146:7 Sing ye to our Lord in confession: sing ye to our God on harp.
146:8 Who covereth the heaven with clouds: and prepareth rain for the earth. Who bringeth forth grass in the mountains: and herb for the service of men.
146:9 Who giveth to beasts their food: and to the young ravens that call upon him.
146:10 He shall not have pleasure in the strength of an horse: nor in the legs of a man shall he be well pleased.
146:11 Our Lord is well pleased toward them that fear him: and in them that hope upon his mercy.

text.NRSV:
147:1 Praise the Lord! How good it is to sing praises to our God; for he is gracious, and a song of praise is fitting.
147:2 The Lord builds up Jerusalem; he gathers the outcasts of Israel.
147:3 He heals the brokenhearted, and binds up their wounds.
147:4 He determines the number of the stars; he gives to all of them their names.
147:5 Great is our Lord, and abundant in power; his understanding is beyond measure.
147:6 The Lord lifts up the downtrodden; he casts the wicked to the ground.
147:7 Sing to the Lord with thanksgiving; make melody to our God on the lyre.
147:8 He covers the heavens with clouds, prepares rain for the earth, makes grass grow on the hills.
147:9 He gives to the animals their food, and to the young ravens when they cry.
147:10 His delight is not in the strength of the horse, nor his pleasure in the speed of a runner;
147:11 but the Lord takes pleasure in those who fear him, in those who hope in his steadfast love.
147:12 Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
147:13 For he strengthens the bars of your gates; he blesses your children within you.
147:14 He grants peace within your borders; he fills you with the finest of wheat.
147:15 He sends out his command to the earth; his word runs swiftly.
147:16 He gives snow like wool; he scatters frost like ashes.
147:17 He hurls down his crystals of ice like crumbs— who can stand before his cold?
147:18 He sends out his word, and melts them; he makes his wind blow, and the waters flow.
147:19 He declares his word to Jacob, his statutes and ordinances to Israel.
147:20 He has not dealt thus with any other nation; they do not know his ordinances. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
147:1 O praise the LORD, for it is a good thing to sing praises unto our God; *
      yea, a joyful and pleasant thing it is to be thankful.
147:2 The LORD does build up Jerusalem, *
      and gather together the outcasts of Israel.
147:3 He heals those who are broken in heart, *
      and gives medicine to heal their sickness.
147:4 He tells the number of the stars, *
      and calls them all by their names.
147:5 Great is our Lord, and great is his power; *
      yea, and his wisdom is infinite.
147:6 The LORD sets up the meek, *
      and brings the ungodly down to the ground.
147:7 O sing unto the LORD with thanksgiving; *
      sing praises upon the harp unto our God;
147:8 Who covers the heaven with clouds, and prepares rain for the earth, *
      and makes the grass to grow upon the mountains, and herb for the use of men;
147:9 Who gives fodder unto the cattle, *
      and feeds the young ravens that call upon him.
147:10 He has no pleasure in the strength of an horse; *
      neither delights he in any man's legs.
147:11 But the LORD's delight is in them that fear him, *
      and put their trust in his mercy.
147:12 Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem; *
      praise your God, O Zion.
147:13 For he has made fast the bars of your gates, *
      and has blessed your children within you.
147:14 He makes peace in your borders, *
      and fills you with the flour of wheat.
147:15 He sends forth his commandment upon earth, *
      and his word runs very swiftly.
147:16 He gives snow like wool, *
      and scatters the hoar-frost like ashes.
147:17 He casts forth his ice like morsels; *
      who is able to abide his frost?
147:18 He sends out his word, and melts them; *
      he blows with his wind, and the waters flow.
147:19 He shows his word unto Jacob, *
      his statutes and ordinances unto Israel.
147:20 He has not dealt so with any nation; *
      neither have the heathen knowledge of his laws.
text.KJV:
147:1 Praise ye the Lord: for it is good to sing praises vnto our God: for it is pleasant, and praise is comely.
147:2 The Lord doeth build vp Ierusalem: he gathereth together the out-casts of Israel.
147:3 Hee healeth the broken in heart: and bindeth vp their wounds.
147:4 He telleth the number of the stars: he calleth them all by their names.
147:5 Great is our Lord, and of great power: his vnderstanding is infinite.
147:6 The Lord lifteth vp the meeke: hee casteth the wicked downe to the ground.
147:7 Sing vnto the Lord with thankesgiuing: sing prayse vpon the harpe vnto our God:
147:8 Who couereth the heauen with cloudes, who prepareth raine for the earth: who maketh grasse to growe vpon the mountaines.
147:9 He giueth to the beast his foode: and to the yong rauens which crie.
147:10 Hee delighteth not in the strength of the horse: he taketh not pleasure in the legs of a man.
147:11 The Lord taketh pleasure in them that feare him: in those that hope in his mercie.
147:12 Praise the Lord, O Ierusalem: praise thy God, O Zion.
147:13 For hee hath strengthened the barres of thy gates: hee hath blessed thy children within thee.
147:14 He maketh peace in thy borders: and filleth thee with the finest of the wheate.
147:15 He sendeth forth his commandement vpon earth: his word runneth very swiftly.
147:16 He giueth snow like wooll: he scattereth the hoare frost like ashes.
147:17 He casteth forth his yce like morsels: who can stand before his cold?
147:18 He sendeth out his word, and melteth them: he causeth his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
147:19 He sheweth his word vnto Iacob: his statutes and his iudgements vnto Israel.
147:20 He hath not dealt so with any nation: and as for his iudgements, they haue not knowen them. Praise yee the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
147:1 Hallelujah. It is good to chant hymns to our God; it is pleasant to sing glorious praise.
147:2 The LORD rebuilds Jerusalem; He gathers in the exiles of Israel.
147:3 He heals their broken hearts, and binds up their wounds.
147:4 He reckoned the number of the stars; to each He gave its name.
147:5 Great is our Lord and full of power; His wisdom is beyond reckoning.
147:6 The LORD gives courage to the lowly, and brings the wicked down to the dust.
147:7 Sing to the LORD a song of praise, chant a hymn with a lyre to our God,
147:8 who covers the heavens with clouds, provides rain for the earth, makes mountains put forth grass;
147:9 who gives the beasts their food, to the raven’s brood what they cry for.
147:10 He does not prize the strength of horses, nor value the fleetness of men;
147:11 but the LORD values those who fear Him, those who depend on His faithful care.
147:12 O Jerusalem, glorify the LORD; praise your God, O Zion!
147:13 For He made the bars of your gates strong, and blessed your children within you.
147:14 He endows your realm with well-being, and satisfies you with choice wheat.
147:15 He sends forth His word to the earth; His command runs swiftly.
147:16 He lays down snow like fleece, scatters frost like ashes.
147:17 He tosses down hail like crumbs— who can endure His icy cold?
147:18 He issues a command—it melts them; He breathes—the waters flow.
147:19 He issued His commands to Jacob, His statutes and rules to Israel.
147:20 He did not do so for any other nation; of such rules they know nothing. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
ALLELUIA!
1 Praise the Lord for he is good;

sing to our God for he is loving:
to him our praise is due.
2 The Lord builds up Jerusalem
and brings back Israel's exiles,
3 he heals the brokenhearted,
he binds up all their wounds.
4 He fixes the number of the stars;
he calls each one by its name.
5 Our Lord is great and almighty;
his wisdom can never be measured.
6 The Lord raises the lowly;
he humbles the wicked to the dust.
7 O sing to the Lord giving thanks;
sing psalms to our God with the harp.
8 He covers the heavens with clouds;
he prepares the rain for the earth,
making mountains sprout with grass
and with plants to serve man's needs.
9 He provides the beasts with their food
and young ravens that call upon him.
10 His delight is not in horses
nor his pleasure in warriors' strength.
11 The Lord delights in those who revere him,
in those who wait for his love.

12 O praise the Lord, Jerusalem!
Zion praise your God!
13 He has strengthened the bars of your gates
he has blessed the children within you.
14 He established peace on your borders,
he feeds you with finest wheat.
15 He sends out his word to the earth
and swiftly runs his command.
16 He showers down snow white as wool,
he scatters hoarfrost like ashes.
17 He hurls down hailstones like crumbs.
The waters are frozen at his touch;
18 he sends forth his word and it melts them:
at the breath of his mouth the waters flow.
19 He makes his word known to Jacob,
to Israel his laws and decrees.
20 He has not dealt thus with other nations;
he has not taught them his decrees.
ALLELUIA!
text.Rotherham:
147:1 Praise ye Yah, For it is good, to make melody to our GOD,—For it is delightful, seemly is praise!
147:2 Yahweh, is building Jerusalem, The outcasts of Israel, will he gather together.
147:3 He is healing the broken in heart,—and binding up their hurts.
147:4 Counting the number of the stars, To all of them—names, he giveth.
147:5 Great is our Lord, and of abounding strength, And, his knowledge, cannot be expressed.
147:6 Yahweh, relieveth the humbled! casting the lawless down to the earth.
147:7 Respond to Yahweh with thanksgiving, Make melody to our God with the lyre.
147:8 Who covereth the heavens with clouds, preparing rain for the earth, who causeth the mountains to sprout grass:
147:9 Giving, to the beast, its food, to the young ravens, when they cry.
147:10 Not in the strength of the horse, doth he delight, Not in the legs of a man, hath he pleasure:
147:11 Yahweh, hath pleasure, in them who revere him, in them who wait for his lovingkindness.
147:12 Laud thou, O Jerusalem, Yahweh, Praise thy God, O Zion:
147:13 For he hath strengthened the bars of thy gates, He hath blessed thy children in thy midst:
147:14 Who maketh thy boundaries to be peace, With the marrow of wheat, doth he satisfy thee:
147:15 Who sendeth his utterance to the earth, How swiftly, runneth his word!
147:16 Who giveth snow like wool, Hoar-frost—like ashes, he scattereth:
147:17 Casting forth his ice like crumbs, Before his cold, who can stand?
147:18 He sendeth forth his word and melteth them, He causeth his wind to blow, the waters, stream along:
147:19 Declaring his word unto Jacob, His statutes and his regulations, unto Israel.
147:20 He hath not dealt so with any nation, and, his regulations, he maketh not known to them. Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
147:1 Psalm 147 God’s Word Restores Jerusalem - Hallelujah! I - How good to sing praise to our God; how pleasant to give fitting praise.
147:2 The LORD rebuilds Jerusalem, and gathers the dispersed of Israel,
147:3 Healing the brokenhearted, and binding up their wounds.
147:4 He numbers the stars, and gives to all of them their names.
147:5 Great is our Lord, vast in power, with wisdom beyond measure.
147:6 The LORD gives aid to the poor, but casts the wicked to the ground.
147:7 II - Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; with the lyre make music to our God,
147:8 Who covers the heavens with clouds, provides rain for the earth, makes grass sprout on the mountains,
147:9 Who gives animals their food and young ravens what they cry for.
147:10 He takes no delight in the strength of horses, no pleasure in the runner’s stride.
147:11 Rather the LORD takes pleasure in those who fear him, those who put their hope in his mercy.
147:12 III - Glorify the LORD, Jerusalem; Zion, offer praise to your God,
147:13 For he has strengthened the bars of your gates, blessed your children within you.
147:14 He brings peace to your borders, and satisfies you with finest wheat.
147:15 He sends his command to earth; his word runs swiftly!
147:16 Thus he makes the snow like wool, and spreads the frost like ash;
147:17 He disperses hail like crumbs. Who can withstand his cold?
147:18 Yet when again he issues his command, it melts them; he raises his winds and the waters flow.
147:19 He proclaims his word to Jacob, his statutes and laws to Israel.
147:20 He has not done this for any other nation; of such laws they know nothing. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
147:1 Alleluia. O Jerusalem praise our Lord: praise thy God, o Sion.
147:2 Because he hath strengthened the locks of thy gates: he hath blessed thy children in thee.
147:3 Who hath set thy borders peace: and filleth thee with the fat of corn.
147:4 Who sendeth forth his speech to the earth: his word runneth swiftly.
147:5 Who giveth snow as wool: scattereth mist as ashes.
147:6 He casteth his crystal as morsels: before the face of his cold who shall endure?
147:7 He shall send forth his word, and shall melt them: his spirit shall blow, and waters shall flow.
147:8 Who declareth his word to Jacob: his justices and judgments to Israel.
147:9 He hath not done in like manner to any nation: and his judgements he hath not made manifest to them. Alleluia.

text.NRSV:
148:1 Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise him in the heights!
148:2 Praise him, all his angels; praise him, all his host!
148:3 Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all you shining stars!
148:4 Praise him, you highest heavens, and you waters above the heavens!
148:5 Let them praise the name of the Lord, for he commanded and they were created.
148:6 He established them forever and ever; he fixed their bounds, which cannot be passed.
148:7 Praise the Lord from the earth, you sea monsters and all deeps,
148:8 fire and hail, snow and frost, stormy wind fulfilling his command!
148:9 Mountains and all hills, fruit trees and all cedars!
148:10 Wild animals and all cattle, creeping things and flying birds!
148:11 Kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all rulers of the earth!
148:12 Young men and women alike, old and young together!
148:13 Let them praise the name of the Lord, for his name alone is exalted; his glory is above earth and heaven.
148:14 He has raised up a horn for his people, praise for all his faithful, for the people of Israel who are close to him. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
148:1 O praise the LORD of heaven, *
      praise him in the height.
148:2 Praise him, all you angels of his; *
      praise him, all his host.
148:3 Praise him, sun and moon; *
      praise him, all you stars and light.
148:4 Praise him, all you heavens, *
      and you waters that are above the heavens.
148:5 Let them praise the Name of the LORD; *
      for he spake the word, and they were made; he commanded, and they were created.
148:6 He has made them fast for ever and ever; *
      he has given them a law which shall not be broken.
148:7 Praise the LORD upon earth, *
      you dragons and all deeps;
148:8 Fire and hail, snow and vapors, *
      wind and storm, fulfilling his word;
148:9 Mountains and all hills; *
      fruitful trees and all cedars;
148:10 Beasts and all cattle; *
      worms and feathered fowls;
148:11 Kings of the earth and all people; *
      princes and all judges of the world;
148:12 Young men and maidens, old men and children, praise the Name of the LORD; *
      for his Name only is excellent, and his praise above heaven and earth.
148:13 He shall exalt the horn of his people; all his saints shall praise him; *
      even the children of Israel, even the people that serves him.
text.KJV:
148:1 Praise yee the Lord. Praise ye the Lord from the heauens: praise him in the heights.
148:2 Praise yee him all his Angels: praise ye him all his hosts.
148:3 Praise ye him Sunne and Moone: praise him all ye starres of light.
148:4 Praise him ye heauens of heauens: and ye waters that be aboue the heauens.
148:5 Let them praise the Name of the Lord: for he commanded, and they were created.
148:6 Hee hath also stablished them for euer and euer: he hath made a decree which shall not passe.
148:7 Praise the Lord from the earth: ye dragons and all deepes.
148:8 Fire and haile, snow and vapour: stormie wind fulfilling his word.
148:9 Mountaines and all hilles: fruitfull trees, and all cedars.
148:10 Beastes and all cattell: creeping things, and flying foule.
148:11 Kings of the earth, and all people: Princes, and all Iudges of the earth.
148:12 Both young men and maidens: olde men and children.
148:13 Let them praise the Name of the Lord, for his Name alone is excellent: his glory is aboue the earth and heauen.
148:14 Hee also exalteth the horne of his people, the praise of all his Saints; euen of the children of Israel, a people neere vnto him. Praise ye the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
148:1 Hallelujah. Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise Him on high.
148:2 Praise Him, all His angels, praise Him, all His hosts.
148:3 Praise Him, sun and moon, praise Him, all bright stars.
148:4 Praise Him, highest heavens, and you waters that are above the heavens.
148:5 Let them praise the name of the LORD, for it was He who commanded that they be created.
148:6 He made them endure forever, establishing an order that shall never change.
148:7 Praise the LORD, O you who are on earth, all sea monsters and ocean depths,
148:8 fire and hail, snow and smoke, storm wind that executes His command,
148:9 all mountains and hills, all fruit trees and cedars,
148:10 all wild and tamed beasts, creeping things and winged birds,
148:11 all kings and peoples of the earth, all princes of the earth and its judges,
148:12 youths and maidens alike, old and young together.
148:13 Let them praise the name of the LORD, for His name, His alone, is sublime; His splendor covers heaven and earth.
148:14 He has exalted the horn of His people for the glory of all His faithful ones, Israel, the people close to Him. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Praise the Lord from the heavens,
praise him in the heights.
2 Praise him, all his angels,
praise him, all his host.
3 Praise him, sun and moon,
praise him, shining stars.
4 Praise him, highest heavens
and the waters above the heavens.
5 Let them praise the name of the Lord.
He commanded: they were made.
6 He fixed them for ever,
gave a law which shall not pass away.
7 Praise the Lord from the earth,
sea creatures and all oceans,
8 fire and hail, snow and mist,
stormy winds that obey his word;
9 all mountains and hills,
all fruit trees and cedars,
10 beasts, wild and tame,
reptiles and birds on the wing;
11 all earth's kings and peoples,
earth's princes and rulers,
12 young men and maidens,
the old men together with children.
13 Let them praise the name of the Lord
for he alone is exalted.
The splendor of his name
reaches beyond heaven and earth.
14 He exalts the strength of his people.
He is the praise of all his saints,
of the sons of Israel,
of the people to whom he comes close.
ALLELUIA!
text.DRB:
148:1 Alleluia. Praise ye our Lord from the heavens: praise ye him in the high places.
148:2 Praise ye him all his Angels: praise ye him all his hosts:
148:3 Praise ye him sun and moon: praise him all ye stars, and light.
148:4 Praise him ye heavens of heavens: and the waters that are above the heavens,
148:5 let them praise the name of our Lord. Because he said, and they were made: he commanded, and they were created.
148:6 He established them for ever, and for ever and ever: he put a precept, and it shall not pass.
148:7 Praise our Lord from the earth: ye dragons, and all depths.
148:8 Fire, hail, snow, ice, spirit of storms: which do his word:
148:9 Mountains, and all little hills: trees that bear fruit, and all cedars.
148:10 Beasts, and all cattle: serpents, and feathered fowls:
148:11 Kings of the earth, & all peoples: Princes, and all judges of the earth.
148:12 Young men and virgins: old with young let them praise the name of our Lord:
148:13 because the name of him alone is exalted.
148:14 The confession of him above heaven and earth: and he hath exalted the horn of his people. An hymn to all his saints: to the children of Israel a people approaching unto him. Alleluia.

text.NRSV:
149:1 Praise the Lord! Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise in the assembly of the faithful.
149:2 Let Israel be glad in its Maker; let the children of Zion rejoice in their King.
149:3 Let them praise his name with dancing, making melody to him with tambourine and lyre.
149:4 For the Lord takes pleasure in his people; he adorns the humble with victory.
149:5 Let the faithful exult in glory; let them sing for joy on their couches.
149:6 Let the high praises of God be in their throats and two-edged swords in their hands,
149:7 to execute vengeance on the nations and punishment on the peoples,
149:8 to bind their kings with fetters and their nobles with chains of iron,
149:9 to execute on them the judgment decreed. This is glory for all his faithful ones. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
149:1 O sing unto the LORD a new song; *
      let the congregation of saints praise him.
149:2 Let Israel rejoice in him that made him, *
      and let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.
149:3 Let them praise his Name in the dance; *
      let them sing praises unto him with tabret and harp.
149:4 For the LORD has pleasure in his people, *
      and helps the meek-hearted.
149:5 Let the saints be joyful with glory; *
      let them rejoice in their beds.
149:6 Let the praises of God be in their mouth; *
      and a two-edged sword in their hands;
149:7 To be avenged of the heathen, *
      and to rebuke the people;
149:8 To bind their kings in chains, *
      and their nobles with links of iron.
149:9 That they may be avenged of them, as it is written, *
      Such honor have all his saints.
text.KJV:
149:1 Praise yee the Lord: Sing vnto the Lord a new song: and his prayse in the Congregation of Saints.
149:2 Let Israel reioyce in him that made him: let the children of Zion bee ioyfull in their King.
149:3 Let them praise his Name in the dance: let them sing praises vnto him with the timbrell and harpe.
149:4 For the Lord taketh pleasure in his people: hee will beautifie the meeke with saluation.
149:5 Let the Saints be ioyfull in glory: let them sing aloude vpon their beddes.
149:6 Let the high praises of God be in their mouth: and a two edged sword in their hand:
149:7 To execute vengeance vpon the heathen: and punishments vpon the people.
149:8 To binde their Kings with chaines: and their Nobles with fetters of yron.
149:9 To execute vpon them the iudgement written: This honour haue all his Saints. Praise ye the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
149:1 Hallelujah. Sing to the LORD a new song, His praises in the congregation of the faithful.
149:2 Let Israel rejoice in its maker; let the children of Zion exult in their king.
149:3 Let them praise His name in dance; with timbrel and lyre let them chant His praises.
149:4 For the LORD delights in His people; He adorns the lowly with victory.
149:5 Let the faithful exult in glory; let them shout for joy upon their couches,
149:6 with paeans to God in their throats and two-edged swords in their hands,
149:7 to impose retribution upon the nations, punishment upon the peoples,
149:8 binding their kings with shackles, their nobles with chains of iron,
149:9 executing the doom decreed against them. This is the glory of all His faithful. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Sing a new song to the Lord,
his praise in the assembly of the faithful.

2 Let Israel rejoice in its Maker,
let Zion's sons exult in their king.
3 Let them praise his name with dancing
and make music with timbrel and harp.
4 For the Lord takes delight in his people.
He crowns the poor with salvation.
5 Let the faithful rejoice in their glory,
shout for joy and take their rest.
6 Let the praise of God be on their lips
and a twoedged sword in their hand,
7 to deal out vengeance to the nations
and punishment on all the peoples;
8 to bind their kings in chains
and their nobles in fetters of iron;
9 to carry out the sentence preordained:
this honor is for all his faithful.
ALLELUIA!
text.Rotherham:
149:1 Praise ye Yah, Sing to Yahweh a song that is new, his praise in the convocation of the men of lovingkindness.
149:2 Let Israel rejoice in him that made him, Let the sons of Zion exult in their king;
149:3 Let them praise his Name in the dance, with timbrel and lyre, Let them make music to him.
149:4 For Yahweh is taking pleasure in his people, He will beautify humbled ones with victory.
149:5 Let the men of lovingkindness exult as they glory, Let them shout aloud upon their beds:
149:6 The high songs of GOD be in their throat, and a two-edged sword in their hand:
149:7 To execute an avenging among the nations, rebukes among the peoples:
149:8 To bind their kings with fetters, and their honoured ones with iron bands:
149:9 To execute upon them the sentence written, An honour, shall it be to all his men of lovingkindness. Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
149:1 Psalm 149 Praise God with Song and Sword - Hallelujah! Sing to the LORD a new song, his praise in the assembly of the faithful.
149:2 Let Israel be glad in its maker, the people of Zion rejoice in their king.
149:3 Let them praise his name in dance, make music with tambourine and lyre.
149:4 For the LORD takes delight in his people, honors the poor with victory.
149:5 Let the faithful rejoice in their glory, cry out for joy on their couches,
149:6 With the praise of God in their mouths, and a two-edged sword in their hands,
149:7 To bring retribution on the nations, punishment on the peoples,
149:8 To bind their kings in shackles, their nobles in chains of iron,
149:9 To execute the judgments decreed for them— such is the glory of all God’s faithful. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
149:1 Alleluia. Sing ye to our Lord a new song: let his praise be in the Church of saints.
149:2 Let Israel be joyful in him, that made him: and let the children of Sion rejoice in their King.
149:3 Let them praise his name in choir: on timbrel and psalter let them sing to him.
149:4 Because our Lord is well pleased in his people: and he will exalt the meek unto salvation.
149:5 The saints shall rejoice in glory: they shall be joyful in their beds.
149:6 The exaltations of God in their throat: and two edged swords in their hands.
149:7 To do revenge in the nations: chastisements among the peoples.
149:8 To bind their kings in fetters: and their nobles in iron manacles.
149:9 That they may do in them the judgement that is written: this glory is to all his saints. Alleluia.

text.NRSV:
150:1 Praise the Lord! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty firmament!
150:2 Praise him for his mighty deeds; praise him according to his surpassing greatness!
150:3 Praise him with trumpet sound; praise him with lute and harp!
150:4 Praise him with tambourine and dance; praise him with strings and pipe!
150:5 Praise him with clanging cymbals; praise him with loud clashing cymbals!
150:6 Let everything that breathes praise the Lord! Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
150:1 O praise God in his holiness; *
      praise him in the firmament of his power.
150:2 Praise him in his noble acts; *
      praise him according to his excellent greatness.
150:3 Praise him in the sound of the trumpet; *
      praise him upon the lute and harp.
150:4 Praise him in the cymbals and dances; *
      praise him upon the strings and pipe.
150:5 Praise him upon the well-tuned cymbals; *
      praise him upon the loud cymbals.
150:6 Let every thing that has breath *
      praise the LORD.
text.KJV:
150:1 Praise ye the Lord. Praise God in his Sanctuarie: Praise him in the firmament of his power.
150:2 Praise him for his mightie actes: Praise him according to his excellent greatnesse.
150:3 Praise him with the sound of the Trumpet: Prayse him with the Psalterie and Harpe.
150:4 Praise him with the timbrell and dance: praise him with stringed instruments, and Organes.
150:5 Praise him vpon the loud cymbals: praise him vpon the high sounding cymbals.
150:6 Let euery thing that hath breath, praise the Lord. Praise yee the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
150:1 Hallelujah. Praise God in His sanctuary; praise Him in the sky, His stronghold.
150:2 Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him for His exceeding greatness.
150:3 Praise Him with blasts of the horn; praise Him with harp and lyre.
150:4 Praise Him with timbrel and dance; praise Him with lute and pipe.
150:5 Praise Him with resounding cymbals; praise Him with loud-clashing cymbals.
150:6 Let all that breathes praise the LORD. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Praise God in his holy place,
praise him in his mighty heavens.
2 Praise him for his powerful deeds,
praise his surpassing greatness.
3 O praise him with sound of trumpet,
praise him with lute and harp.
4 Praise him with timbrel and dance,
praise him with strings and pipes.
5 O praise him with resounding cymbals,
praise him with clashing of cymbals.
6 Let everything that lives and that breathes
give praise to the Lord.
ALLELUIA!
text.Rotherham:
150:1 Praise ye Yah, Praise ye GOD in his sanctuary, Praise him, in his strong expanse:
150:2 Praise him, for his mighty deeds, Praise him, according to his exceeding greatness:
150:3 Praise him, with the blast of a horn, Praise him, with the harp and lyre:
150:4 Praise him, with timbrel and dance,—Praise him, with stringed instrument and flute,
150:5 Praise him, with cymbals of clear tone,—Praise him, with cymbals of loud clang:
150:6 Let, every breathing thing, praise Yah, Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
150:1 Psalm 150 Final Doxology - Hallelujah! Praise God in his holy sanctuary; give praise in the mighty dome of heaven.
150:2 Give praise for his mighty deeds, praise him for his great majesty.
150:3 Give praise with blasts upon the horn, praise him with harp and lyre.
150:4 Give praise with tambourines and dance, praise him with strings and pipes.
150:5 Give praise with crashing cymbals, praise him with sounding cymbals.
150:6 Let everything that has breath give praise to the LORD! Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
150:1 Alleluia. Praise ye our Lord in his holies: praise ye him in the firmament of his strength.
150:2 Praise ye him in his powers: praise ye him according to the multitude of his greatness.
150:3 Praise ye him in the sound of trumpet: praise ye him on psalter and harp.
150:4 Praise ye him on timbrel and choir: praise ye him on strings and organ.
150:5 Praise ye him on well sounding cymbals: praise ye him on cymbals of jubilation:
150:6 let every spirit praise our Lord. Alleluia.

text.Orthodox:
151:1 I was small among my brothers and the youngest in my father's house. I tended my father's sheep.
151:2 My hands made a harp; my fingers fashioned a lyre.
151:3 And who will tell my Lord? The Lord himself; it is he who hears.
151:4 It was he who sent his messenger and took me from my father's sheep, and anointed me with his holy oil.
151:5 My brothers were handsome and tall, but the Lord was not pleased with them.
151:6 I went out to meet the Philistine, and he cursed me by his idols.
151:7 But I drew his own sword; I beheaded him, and took away reproach from the people of Israel.
text.Grail1963:
Psalm (151)
This Psalm is ascribed to David and is outside the number. When he slew Goliath in single combat.
(This Title given in the Septuagint)
1 Little was I among my brethren:
A younger brother in my father's house.
2 My hands, they made an instrument of music:
My fingers, they prepared a psaltery.
3 And who shall bring back tidings to my master?
The Lord Himself, Himself gives ear.
4 Himself sent forth His messenger:
And took me from among my father's sheep;

And with the oil of His annointing He annointed me.
5 Comely my brethren were and tall:
And yet they found not favour with the Lord.
6 But I, I sallied forth to meet the alien:
And he reviled me by all his idols.
7 But I drew forth the sword that was beside him:
I cut his head off with it,
And from the sons of Israel removed reproach.

text.Orthodox:
152:1 When I was a youth, I was chosen of the Lord, and he took me and anointed me.
152:2 I was a shepherd of my father's flock, and I kept them in the wilderness.
152:3 A lion came out of the forest and a wolf out of the desert, and they were devouring my sheep.
152:4 But I went out against them and I slew them, and I delivered my flock from their mouth.
152:5 Glory to the Lord, who gave me strength to overcome the beasts of the field!

153:1 O Lord, deliver me from the hands of the enemy, and save me from the mouth of the lion.
153:2 For the lion is strong and the enemy is cruel, and they have compassed me about on every side.
153:3 But you, O Lord, are my strength and my helper; you have delivered me from the peril of death.
153:4 Praise be to you, O Lord, for your mercy is great and your goodness is for ever.

154:1 Glorify God with a loud voice; proclaim His glory in the assembly of many.
154:2 In the multitude of the righteous glorify His praise; and speak of His glory with the righteous.
154:3 Join your souls with the good and with the perfect, to glorify the Most High.
154:4 Assemble yourselves together to make known His salvation; and be not slow in showing forth His strength and His majesty to all the simple.
154:5 That the honor of the Lord may be known, wisdom hath been given; and to tell of His works it was made known to men: to declare to the simple His righteousness, to make the ignorant understand His greatness; to set before the humble the knowledge of His majesty.
154:6 Draw nigh then, ye that fear the Lord, and hear His word.
154:7 Receive the wisdom of the Lord, and walk in it, and walk not in the way of unrighteousness.
154:8 Let him that feareth God receive doctrine, and they that can understand obtain it.
154:9 Let him that is willing be obedient to the commandments, and they that are well-pleasing to Him shall find everlasting life.

155:1 O Lord, I have cried unto Thee; hearken unto me.
155:2 I have lifted up my hands to Thy holy dwelling-place; incline Thine ear unto me.
155:3 Grant me my request; withhold not from me my petition.
155:4 Build up my soul, and destroy it not; and lay it not bare before the wicked.
155:5 May the judge of truth turn away from me the rewards of evil, O Lord.
155:6 O Lord, judge me not in Thy wrath, neither chasten me in Thine anger.
155:7 For Thy chastening is upon all, O Lord, and Thy judgment is over all the world.
